controls.risk: Tone-at-the-top / ethics breaches enable override of controls and fraud.

controls.risk: Errors in results undetected by management.
controls.description: Management review of actuals vs budget/forecast with documented investigation of variances — system-supported and EVIDENCED. The budget-vs-actual report (GET /api/ledger/budget-vs-actual) flags MATERIAL variances per line (|variance| >= THB 1,000 AND >= 10% of budget, or unbudgeted actual spend) with material/requires_review flags and a review summary (material_count, requires_review_count, unfavorable_total). Management records a REVIEW SIGN-OFF (POST /api/ledger/budget-review/sign-off — a required follow-up note; captures the material-variance count + unfavourable total at review time) which is retained as append-only evidence (budget_reviews) and surfaced back on the report as last_signoff; the review history (GET /api/ledger/budget-reviews) is the sample-able audit trail of monthly variance review + follow-up.
controls.code_reference: budget.service.ts budgetVsActual(material/requires_review flags + review summary)/signOffReview/listReviews; budget.controller.ts (budget-review/sign-off, budget-reviews); schema/budgets.ts budget_reviews + migration 0144; cutover/budget.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the materiality flagging on budget-vs-actual + the recorded sign-off (reviewer, material count, follow-up note) + the review history.
controls.toe: Re-perform: post an actual that breaches a budget materially, confirm the line is flagged material/requires_review and the summary counts it; sign off with a note, confirm the sign-off records the material count + unfavourable total and the report then shows last_signoff and the history lists it (re-performed by the budget harness).
controls.evidence: Budget-vs-actual material-variance report + review sign-off log

controls.risk: Financial-condition deterioration (liquidity, margin, receivables ageing, leverage) goes unnoticed between close cycles because management has no single, comparative view of the key financial KPIs.
controls.description: Management analytical review of financial KPIs — a single, canonical CFO scorecard (GET /api/finance/metrics/pack) computes ~31 finance KPIs (liquidity, working-capital efficiency/CCC, profitability, leverage, growth & cash, and receivables/payables health) DIRECTLY from the posted GL + sub-ledgers, each with prior-period / prior-year / budget comparatives and a RAG (green/amber/red) rating against a defined threshold. Every KPI drills through to the account-group rows behind it (GET /api/finance/metrics/:id/drill) so a movement can be investigated to the ledger, and a monthly trend (…/:id/trend) evidences the review over time. One definition (finance-metrics-constants METRICS) feeds the scorecard, the CFO dashboard, the scheduled KPI pack and the exec_scorecard, so the reviewed number cannot drift by surface. Read-only aggregation — no posting authority, no new SoD conflict.
controls.code_reference: finance/finance-metrics.service.ts (pack/trend/drill + execFinance); finance-metrics-constants.ts (METRICS registry + RAG bands + FIN_STATEMENT_MAP account groups); finance-metrics.controller.ts (GET /api/finance/metrics/pack|:id/trend|:id/drill, perms exec/fin_report/dashboard); bi.service.ts execScorecard (reads the canonical finance leg); cutover/finance-kpi.ts (ToE); docs/35-finance-oracle-grade-analytics-plan.md
controls.tod: Inspect the KPI scorecard: each KPI's value, comparatives (prior-period/prior-year/budget), RAG rating, and that a red KPI drills to the GL account rows behind it.
controls.toe: Re-perform: on a known seeded ledger every KPI equals its hand-computed value, RAG turns red past its threshold (e.g. overdue-AR %), the drill rows tie to the sub-ledger, and a non-finance role is denied the scorecard (finance-kpi harness).
controls.evidence: CFO KPI scorecard (finance/metrics/pack) + drill/trend

controls.risk: Session token stealable via XSS; weak/stale credentials; sessions never expire.
controls.description: Web session is an httpOnly JWT cookie (token unreadable by JS → XSS can't exfiltrate it) + CSRF double-submit on cookie-authenticated mutations + a web Content-Security-Policy; plus password policy, forced first-login change (a HARD API gate — a must_change_password account gets 403 PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED on every endpoint except change-password/logout/me/refresh, so a seeded or admin-reset credential can transact nothing until rotated; docs/27 R0-3), and JWT/session expiry. Seeding refuses NODE_ENV=production without ALLOW_PROD_SEED=1 and REQUIRES the operator-supplied SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD — no generated default and no credential ever written to a log (CodeQL js/clear-text-logging remediated). Bearer/API-key clients are CSRF-exempt (no ambient cookie).
controls.code_reference: common/cookies.ts; common/guards.ts (cookie auth + CSRF); auth.controller.ts (login/logout set/clear cookies); apps/web/next.config.mjs (CSP); 0045_must_change_password; guards.ts PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED hard gate + database/seed.ts random-credential/prod-refusal (R0-3); cutover/cookie-auth.ts + onboarding.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect cookie flags (HttpOnly/SameSite), CSRF enforcement, and CSP.
controls.toe: Sample: cookie auth works; cookie mutation w/o X-CSRF-Token → 403; Bearer exempt; logout clears; expired token rejected (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Auth config; cookie-auth ToE

controls.code_reference: pos-fiscal/journal.service.ts (prevHash→hash chain, FOR UPDATE); appended by portal/portal.pos.service.ts (SALE), payments.service.ts (REFUND/VOID) and restaurant/dine-in.service.ts checkout (SALE — dine-in, diner-QR and register sales, which previously appended nothing at all); GET /api/pos/journal/verify; cutover/restaurant.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)

controls.risk: Secrets hardcoded/shared/un-rotated → compromise; the encryption-at-rest key cannot be rotated without invalidating stored ciphertext (TOTP seeds, webhook/SSO/messaging secrets, encrypted PII columns).
controls.description: Secrets (JWT_SECRET, APP_ENC_KEY, PSP_WEBHOOK_SECRET, DB creds) held in the deployment secret store; rotated; no dev fallbacks in prod (fail-closed boot gate). Encryption-at-rest key ROTATION (4.3): common/crypto.ts supports a VERSIONED KEYRING — APP_ENC_KEYRING (JSON kid→secret, HKDF-SHA256 with salt + per-kid label separation, replacing the legacy bare-sha256 derivation for new keys) + APP_ENC_ACTIVE_KID select the write key; ciphertext embeds its key id (v2:<kid>:… wire format; legacy v1 = kid 1) so every generation stays decryptable during rotation, and an unknown kid FAILS CLOSED (never returns ciphertext as plaintext). The idempotent key_rotation_sweep job (BI scheduler) re-encrypts all 17 at-rest ciphertext columns (PII columns + TOTP seeds + webhook/SSO/messaging secrets) under the active kid, bounded 500 rows/column/run, converging to 0 on re-run; DEFAULT-INERT with no keyring configured (byte-identical v1 behaviour). External-KMS custody of the root secrets remains an infra dependency (documented in docs/ops/secrets.md §5) — the keyring provides the versioning + re-encrypt machinery a KMS would drive.
controls.code_reference: common/crypto.ts (keyring/HKDF/activeKeyId/needsRotation, v2 wire format, fail-closed); bi.service.ts key_rotation_sweep (17-column inventory); apps/api/test/key-rotation.test.ts (7 unit tests incl. label separation + fail-closed); cutover/bi.ts (ToE: inert/rotate/idempotent/rotate-back); env.validation.ts (fail-closed boot gate); docs/ops/secrets.md
controls.tod: Inspect the keyring derivation (HKDF + label separation), the embedded-kid wire format, the fail-closed unknown-kid path, and the sweep's column inventory.
controls.toe: Re-perform: with no keyring the sweep rotates 0 (inert); with an active kid 2 a v1 blob is re-encrypted to v2:2 and still decrypts; a re-run rotates 0 (idempotent); rotate-back to kid 1 restores v1; a blob relabelled to another kid fails GCM auth (label separation); a missing-kid blob throws (fail closed) — re-performed by the bi harness + unit tests. Confirm no plaintext secrets in repo/CI; prod boot blocked without secrets.
controls.evidence: Keyring config + rotation-sweep run results + unit/ToE evidence

controls.risk: A compromised or shared session cannot be terminated — a stolen/forwarded JWT stays valid for its full lifetime, and a deactivated user's existing token keeps working, so there is no incident-containment kill-switch.
controls.description: Session revocation: JWTs carry a jti so a session can be killed before expiry. Logout adds the token's jti to a denylist (revoked_tokens) which the global auth guard checks every request; an admin can revoke ALL of a user's sessions (users.tokens_valid_from watermark — any JWT issued before it is rejected) for incident response; a role/permission change via user-admin AUTO-bumps the same watermark so an authorization narrowing takes effect immediately instead of lagging the token TTL (docs/27 R2-2); and account DEACTIVATION is enforced live (the guard re-checks is_active each request, so a disabled account's token stops working immediately rather than lasting up to 8h). Brute-force protection: per-account login lockout (login_attempts atomic UPSERT — threshold/window/lock env-tunable; shared by password and PIN login) layered on the per-IP edge rate limiter; the store deliberately FAILS OPEN on infra error (login availability wins) and that degradation raises a throttled ops alert `login_lockout_store_unavailable` (docs/27 R2-1) so the fail-open window is visible, never silent; an account LOCK transition alerts `login_lockout`.
controls.code_reference: modules/auth/login-attempt.store.ts (lockout + fail-open alerting); apps/api/test/login-attempt-failopen.test.ts; auth.service.ts revokeToken/revokeAllSessions (+ jti on issued JWTs); auth.controller.ts (auth/logout revokes jti, auth/users/:username/revoke-sessions); common/guards.ts (jti-denylist + is_active + tokens_valid_from checks); schema/users.ts revoked_tokens + users.tokens_valid_from + migration 0144; cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the jti-denylist + revoke-all watermark + the guard's per-request revocation/deactivation checks.
controls.toe: Re-perform: log in, logout → the token is rejected (TOKEN_REVOKED); deactivate an account → its existing token is rejected (USER_DEACTIVATED); revoke-all → pre-existing tokens rejected (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Revocation denylist + SoD/deactivation test

controls.risk: The primary financial audit trail is append-only but not tamper-EVIDENT — immutability is enforced only by a DB trigger it shares a trust boundary with, so a superuser (or out-of-band DB access) bypassing the trigger could alter/delete history undetectably.
controls.description: Audit hash chain (tamper-evidence): every audit_log row carries a per-(tenant) monotonic seq + prev_hash + hash, each hash binding the previous hash + the row's content (actor/tenant/action/ip/request_id/status/meta) — exactly the pos_journal (ITGC-AC-11) construction. The append is serialised by a FOR UPDATE lock on the latest row so concurrent writes can't fork the chain. Re-walking the chain (GET /api/admin/audit/verify, gated by 'users', RLS-scoped) recomputes every hash and reports the first row where the sequence gaps or a hash mismatches — so ANY edit/delete/insert of a past row (even one that bypassed the append-only trigger) is DETECTED. Complements ITGC-AC-10 (immutability) with detectability.
controls.code_reference: common/audit.interceptor.ts (auditRowHash + per-tenant FOR UPDATE chain append); audit-viewer.service.ts verifyChain + audit-viewer.controller.ts (GET /api/admin/audit/verify); schema/system.ts audit_log.seq/prev_hash/hash + migration 0148; cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the chain construction (seq + prev_hash + content hash, FOR UPDATE serialisation) + the verify walk.
controls.toe: Re-perform: verify returns ok over the accumulated chain; then alter a past row behind a DISABLEd append-only trigger and re-verify → ok=false with a hash mismatch at the tampered seq (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Chain verify (intact + tamper-detected) test

controls.risk: A fast front-of-house PIN login could become a back door — a short, shared or guessable PIN, or a PIN assigned to a privileged account, would let a 4–6 digit code bypass the password + MFA controls that gate financial functions.
controls.description: POS-PIN quick-login is scoped to LOW-PRIVILEGE tills only: a per-user PIN is scrypt-hashed (never stored in clear; same KDF as the password) and any role whose effective permissions require MFA is HARD-BLOCKED from both SETTING and USING a PIN — the second factor can't be downgraded to a 4–6 digit code. PIN login shares the ITGC-AC-07 per-account lockout (a PIN brute-force trips the same throttle), set/rotate is step-up-gated (self: re-enter current password; admin: 'users' access-admin authority), and the combined 'login + เปิดกะ' action still gates opening a cash shift on pos_till (SoD R08) — a plain cashier only signs in.
controls.code_reference: auth.service.ts loginWithPin (lockout + privileged block + scrypt verify) / setOwnPin / setPinFor / clearPinFor (assertPinAllowed); auth.controller.ts (POST /api/login/pin, /api/auth/me/pin, POST+DELETE /api/auth/users/:username/pin); password.service.ts (scrypt KDF reused); permissions.ts requiresMfa; schema/users.ts pin_hash/pin_set_at + migration 0181; payments.controller.ts till/current; cutover/pos-pin.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect that the PIN is hashed (not clear), that a privileged role can neither set nor use a PIN, the shared lockout, and the step-up on set/rotate.
controls.toe: Re-perform: cashier PIN login → 200 (+ permissions); wrong PIN → 401; a privileged account with a valid PIN → 401 PIN_NOT_ALLOWED; self set-PIN requires the current password; admin set-PIN refused for a privileged target; clearing a PIN disables PIN login (re-performed by the pos-pin harness).
controls.evidence: POS-PIN restriction ToE (pos-pin)

controls.risk: HQ 'Admin' bypasses Row-Level Security globally — under a multi-company deployment one over-privileged Admin would see EVERY independent tenant's data, defeating tenant isolation.
controls.description: Hybrid org-scoped tenancy: TENANCY_MODE selects the Admin RLS-bypass scope. Default 'single-company' keeps the legacy HQ-sees-all-branches model (correct for one company, many outlets) and now AUDIT-LOGS every bypassed request. 'multi-company' org-scopes an Admin to its own org's tenants via an app.org_id GUC + an org clause in the RLS 'tenant_isolation' policy on every tenant_id table (installed by 0196, RE-APPLIED by 0232 after a broad RLS re-loop in the 0218 index-backfill had reverted it on data tables) — an Admin in org A sees its org's sibling tenants' data but cannot read org B, and a missing org_id fails closed (sees only its own tenant). Two preventive hardenings on the signup attack surface: the public POST /api/auth/signup — which mints a tenant + an Admin — is FAIL-CLOSED in production (enabled only via PUBLIC_SIGNUP_ENABLED; 403 SIGNUP_DISABLED otherwise), and each signup assigns the new company its OWN org (org_id = the new tenant's id, on the tenant AND its Admin) so a newly-created Admin is org-isolated to just that company by default. Controlled onboarding: instead of opening public signup, a configured PLATFORM owner (PLATFORM_ADMIN_USERNAMES — a cross-tenant operator, not a per-tenant Admin) provisions a company from an authenticated session via POST /api/admin/tenants (@PlatformAdmin); the PlatformAdminGuard authorises it and grants a server-set, one-shot RLS bypass (never from client input), and the cross-tenant write is audit-logged. Empty config ⇒ nobody can (secure default). Platform owner = 'god' (cross-org super-user): a PLATFORM_ADMIN_USERNAMES member is granted a GLOBAL RLS bypass on EVERY route (not only the @PlatformAdmin management endpoints), so an ops-designated owner can see/operate across ALL tenants while a per-tenant Admin stays org-scoped — deliberately gated on the ops-controlled env var (NOT an assignable DB role) so an in-app Admin who manages users cannot escalate into cross-org visibility, and every cross-tenant read/write it performs is flagged to the audit interceptor (rls_bypass meta).
controls.code_reference: common/tenant-tx.interceptor.ts (mode strategy + app.org_id); common/guards.ts (live org_id source); common/env.validation.ts (TENANCY_MODE); drizzle/0196_hybrid_org_tenancy.sql (org_id + org-scoped policy) + drizzle/0232_reapply_org_rls.sql (re-applies the org clause to every tenant_id table after 0218's plain RLS re-loop dropped it); common/audit.interceptor.ts (rls_bypass/rls_org_scope meta); modules/billing/billing.service.ts (isSignupAllowed prod gate + org_id-per-signup + provisionTenant + invite issue/validate/consume); modules/billing/billing.controller.ts (POST /api/admin/tenants + POST/GET /api/admin/signup-invites + POST /api/auth/signup-requests + GET/approve/reject /api/admin/signup-requests + suspend/reactivate /api/admin/tenants/:id); common/guards.ts (JwtAuthGuard TENANT_SUSPENDED block, platform owners exempt); drizzle/0233_signup_invites.sql + database/schema/signup-invites.ts (single-use invite, hash-only); drizzle/0234_signup_requests.sql + database/schema/signup-requests.ts (approval queue, hashed password, no tenant_id); drizzle/0235_tenant_lifecycle.sql (tenants.suspended_at); common/guards.ts (PlatformAdminGuard); common/decorators.ts (PlatformAdmin/isPlatformAdmin); cutover/pg-smoke.ts (real-Postgres raw-RLS ToE); cutover/pg-core.ts (full HTTP-stack ToE: login→guard→interceptor→RLS); apps/api/test/signup-gate.test.ts; apps/api/test/platform-admin.test.ts; cutover/onboarding.ts (org_id + create-company ToE); docs/ops/tenancy-model.md
controls.tod: Inspect the mode strategy, the org-scoped RLS policy, the signup gate, the platform-admin create path, and the bypass audit metadata.
controls.toe: Re-perform on real Postgres: an org-A Admin sees only org-A tenants; a tenant-scoped user is FORCE-RLS isolated; global bypass (single-company) still sees all + emits a bypass audit row (pg-smoke). Full-stack over HTTP (pg-core, both backends): a fresh-signup-style Admin (org_id=NULL) sees ONLY its own tenant, a single-tenant company Admin only itself, an org-scoped Admin is isolated from OTHER companies, and the single-company contrast sees ALL — so per-company isolation is proven end-to-end. Signup hardening: the gate is fail-closed in prod unless PUBLIC_SIGNUP_ENABLED (signup-gate unit matrix), and a real signup sets org_id = tenant id on the tenant + its Admin (onboarding harness). Controlled onboarding: POST /api/admin/tenants is 403 PLATFORM_ADMIN_REQUIRED for a non-platform caller and provisions a fully-isolated company (org_id=tenant id, 12 periods, admin can log in) for a PLATFORM_ADMIN_USERNAMES caller (onboarding harness + platform-admin unit matrix). Invite-link onboarding: a platform owner issues a single-use invite (403 PLATFORM_ADMIN_REQUIRED for a non-platform caller); a bogus token → 400 INVALID_INVITE, a valid token provisions the company (even when public signup is disabled), and re-using the consumed token → 400 (single-use); the invite list marks it used (onboarding harness). Approval queue: a PUBLIC request creates a PENDING row (no tenant provisioned; dup pending → 409); the queue is platform-admin only (403 for a non-owner); approve provisions the company with the requester's hashed password (admin can then log in) and re-approving → 409 REQUEST_NOT_PENDING; reject marks rejected (onboarding harness). Company lifecycle: a non-platform caller cannot suspend (403); a platform owner suspends a company and its Admin is then blocked on every request (403 TENANT_SUSPENDED), and reactivate restores access — platform owners are exempt from the block (onboarding harness). Cross-account org SHARING (an org-scoped Admin seeing a sibling tenant's DATA rows in its own org) is proven: pg-core hard-asserts org1===2 — an org-A Admin sees BOTH org-A sibling tenants' background_jobs rows, never org-B's — on both backends; the org clause 0218 had reverted on data tables is re-applied by 0232. See docs/ops/tenancy-model.md §6. Platform-owner 'god': pg-core hard-asserts that a PLATFORM_ADMIN_USERNAMES member (same role + org as a non-god Admin) sees ALL companies across every org (god===4) while the same-org non-god Admin sees only its org (org1===2) — proving the global bypass is env-gated to the platform owner and does not leak to per-tenant Admins. See docs/ops/tenancy-model.md §2bis.
controls.evidence: Org-scoped tenancy + signup/onboarding-hardening ToE (pg-smoke + pg-core + signup-gate + platform-admin + onboarding)

controls.risk: Customer/employee/vendor PII (Thai citizen/tax ID, SSO number, bank account, free-text notes) is stored in plaintext — a DB snapshot leak or an RLS bypass exposes personal data, failing PDPA/ISO-27001.
controls.description: Field-level PII encryption at rest extends the AES-256-GCM scheme (ITGC-AC-04) from secrets to PII columns via a transparent Drizzle 'encryptedText' type (encrypt-on-write/decrypt-on-read, versioned v1: with legacy-plaintext passthrough). Applied to customer_master.tax_id/notes, employees.national_id/sso_no/bank_account, payslips.national_id (per-slip snapshot), vendors.tax_id/bank_account (docs/27 R0-1). Aggregations that keyed on these values in SQL were rewritten to group DECRYPTED values in app code (PND1A per-employee summary; ghost-vendor duplicate-tax-ID detector) since random-IV ciphertext never collides. Legacy plaintext rows are re-encrypted by the idempotent db:backfill:pii script; exact-match lookups use a keyed blind index (HMAC-SHA256). Remaining lookup-keyed fields are scaffolded in docs/ops/pii-encryption-rollout.md. PII is ALSO redacted before any transmission to the AI provider (AIG-02).
controls.code_reference: database/encrypted-column.ts (encryptedText + blindIndex); common/crypto.ts (AES-256-GCM); schema/customer-master.ts, schema/payroll.ts, schema/procurement.ts (encrypted columns); payroll.service.ts pnd1a + controls.service.ts scan (app-code grouping); database/backfill-encrypt-pii.ts; apps/api/test/pii-encrypt.test.ts; cutover/hcm.ts + ext.ts (at-rest ToE); docs/ops/pii-encryption-rollout.md
controls.tod: Inspect the column type + blind-index helper + which fields are encrypted; confirm fail-closed key gate; confirm value-keyed aggregations group decrypted values in app code.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a written citizen ID / bank account / vendor tax ID is ciphertext at rest (v1:…) and decrypts on read (hcm + ext harnesses); PND1A still aggregates one line per employee; ghost-vendor still fires on a shared tax ID; legacy plaintext still reads (unit test pii-encrypt).
controls.evidence: PII field-encryption ToE (hcm/ext)

controls.risk: An issued API key never expires — a leaked machine credential stays valid until someone notices and manually revokes it (unbounded blast radius).
controls.description: Optional API-key expiry: a key may be issued with a TTL (ttl_days) → expires_at; verify() rejects an expired key exactly like a revoked one. Default NULL = non-expiring (back-compat); bounding the lifetime caps a leaked credential's exposure window.
controls.code_reference: platform/api-key.service.ts (issue ttl_days → expires_at; verify expiry reject); platform/platform.controller.ts (ttl_days body); schema/system.ts api_keys.expires_at + drizzle/0196_hybrid_org_tenancy.sql
controls.tod: Inspect the TTL on issue + the expiry check on verify.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a key past expires_at is rejected (treated as revoked); a NULL-expiry key keeps working.
controls.evidence: API-key expiry

controls.risk: An SoD conflict is blocked at grant time (ITGC-AC-09) but a consciously-ACCEPTED conflict has no standing register — there is no whole-population view of the CURRENT conflicts, and an accepted exception is a one-time approval that is never re-reviewed, so residual SoD risk drifts undetected (a compensating control lapses, an owner leaves, an acceptance never expires).
controls.description: SoD-conflict register + compensating-control governance (detective + preventive-governance) — the layer that completes the SoD story ALONGSIDE the preventive block. A standing dashboard scans the whole tenant user population, runs detectSodConflicts() on each user's EFFECTIVE permissions and groups the CURRENT conflicts by rule (who holds both sides), cross-referenced to any governance decision. A conflict a company consciously ACCEPTS is recorded in the register and MUST carry a documented compensating control, an accountable owner and an expiry date (422 COMPENSATING_CONTROL_REQUIRED otherwise), records who accepted the residual risk, and cannot be raised for a user who does not actually hold the conflict (NO_SUCH_CONFLICT). A detective worklist flags accepted conflicts past expiry OR overdue for re-review (quarterly cadence), and a periodic re-review stamps last_reviewed_at and clears the flag. Read-only aggregation + a governance register — no change to SOD_RULES / detectSodConflicts / the grant-time enforcement.
controls.code_reference: permissions.ts (SOD_RULES R01..R21, detectSodConflicts — unchanged); modules/sod-register (sod-register.service.ts conflicts/accept/review/expired; sod-register.controller.ts GET/POST /api/admin/sod/conflicts|dispositions[/:id/review|/expired] — reads gate users/exec, acceptor recorded); schema/sod-dispositions.ts (sod_conflict_dispositions) + drizzle/0338_sod_conflict_dispositions.sql (tenant-scoped RLS); cutover/sod-register.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the standing conflict dashboard (current conflicts by rule) + the accepted-conflict register (compensating control, owner, expiry, acceptor).
controls.toe: Re-perform: the dashboard surfaces a user holding both sides of a rule; accepting without a compensating control/owner/expiry is rejected (422); an accepted conflict past expiry or overdue for re-review is flagged on the detective worklist; a re-review stamps last_reviewed_at and clears it; the register is RLS-isolated per tenant (sod-register harness).
controls.evidence: SoD-conflict register + compensating-control worklist

controls.risk: The periodic access review (ITGC-AC-08) is a BLANKET period sign-off — one attestation row with counts, and per-user keep/revoke exists only as blank columns in an offline CSV, so there is no in-app line-item disposition and, critically, no follow-through: a decision to REMOVE a user's access is never actually applied (access creep and terminated-user access persist despite the review).
controls.description: Line-item Access Recertification Campaign with CLOSED-LOOP revocation (GRC-2). Opening a campaign snapshots every in-scope user's effective permissions into access_review_items as `pending`; the reviewer keeps or revokes EACH user in-app (decision keep|revoke + note); certification asserts every line is decided (ITEMS_PENDING otherwise) and, for each `revoke`, ACTUALLY removes that user's user_permissions grants and bumps the token watermark (immediate effect, docs/27 R2-2) — recording actioned=true. The campaign is then frozen (status open → in_review → certified; a certified campaign rejects further edits, CAMPAIGN_CERTIFIED) and every line is retained as line-item evidence with the disposing reviewer. Tenant-scoped (RLS); the legacy blanket certify + CSV export remain for back-compat. Deepens ITGC-AC-08 from an attestation into an enforced recertification.
controls.code_reference: admin-users.service.ts (openCampaign/getCampaign/decideItem/certifyCampaign — closed-loop revoke); admin-users.controller.ts (POST access-review/campaign[/:id/items/:username|/certify], GET .../campaign/:id); schema/access-review.ts (access_review_items + access_reviews.status/items_total/items_revoked) + drizzle/0337_access_review_items.sql (tenant-scoped RLS); cutover/access-recert.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the campaign lifecycle, the all-lines-decided gate, and the auto-revoke on certification.
controls.toe: Re-perform: opening a campaign snapshots the population as pending; certify is BLOCKED while any line is pending (ITEMS_PENDING); a 'revoke' decision on certification actually removes the user's permission grants (asserted against the DB — the closed loop) and stamps actioned=true; a certified campaign is frozen; RLS isolates a campaign to its tenant — re-performed by the access-recert harness.
controls.evidence: Recertification campaign + closed-loop revoke ToE (access-recert)

controls.risk: An AI assistant directly posting financial transactions (GL/PO) could book unauthorized or hallucinated entries with no human check.
controls.description: Human-in-the-loop on AI write-ops: the agent can only PROPOSE (propose_journal_entry / propose_purchase_order) — a PENDING ai_action_request that a DIFFERENT authorized human must approve (SoD: approver ≠ proposer + must hold the action's permission, e.g. gl_post). On approval it executes through the normal service + GL + audit trail. The AI never mutates the ledger directly.
controls.code_reference: modules/ai/ai-action.service.ts (propose/approve SoD + permission); agent.service.ts (propose_* tools file PENDING); schema/ai-actions.ts + migration 0063; cutover/ai-actions.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the propose→approve flow + the SoD/permission gate on approve.
controls.toe: Re-perform: AI proposes a JE (PENDING, not posted); the proposer cannot approve it (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL); a different permitted user approves → it posts balanced with audit (ai-actions harness).
controls.evidence: Agentic write-op HITL ToE (ai-actions)

controls.risk: Tenant PII (email, phone, Thai national ID, LINE id, address) is transmitted to the third-party AI model provider, creating a PDPA disclosure.
controls.description: PII redaction before AI transmission: tool results returned to the model are scrubbed of contact identifiers (email/phone/national-id/address/LINE) by default (AI_PII_REDACTION on). doc-ai intake is intentionally unredacted (its purpose is field extraction) and is therefore additionally governed by the production DPA gate (AIG-05) + per-tenant opt-out.

controls.risk: An AI cost run-away — a tenant consumes unlimited model tokens under a flat subscription, eroding gross margin; or an unbounded prompt-injected loop.
controls.description: Per-tenant daily AI token budget with FINITE caps (no unlimited tier): checkBudget resolves the plan's ai_tokens_daily to a finite included cap (a missing value → conservative default; legacy -1 → a finite enterprise ceiling) and blocks at AI_BUDGET_EXCEEDED; tokens beyond the cap are metered as overage for billing. Plus a 15-turn agent loop bound, per-tool timeout, and prompt-caching/model-tiering cost controls.
controls.code_reference: agent.service.ts (checkBudget finite caps + recordUsage overage; MAX_LOOP_TURNS; TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS); common/ai-models.ts (tiering); schema/ai-usage.ts overage_tokens + drizzle/0197_ai_token_overage.sql; billing.service.ts PLAN_SEED ai_tokens_daily; docs/ops/pricing-and-ai-cogs.md
controls.tod: Inspect the cap resolution (no unlimited path) + the overage metering + the loop/timeout bounds.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a tenant at its cap is blocked (AI_BUDGET_EXCEEDED); over-cap tokens accrue to overage_tokens; model tiering never selects Opus (ai-eval).
controls.evidence: AI budget + overage ToE

controls.risk: Prompt injection — instructions embedded in retrieved data (a supplier name, a customer note, a KB document) are followed by the model as if they were user/system commands.
controls.description: Prompt-injection defense: the system prompt declares all tool/RAG results to be UNTRUSTED DATA that must never be executed as instructions, and each tool result is wrapped with explicit untrusted-data provenance before it reaches the model. The human-approval write-gate (AIG-01) remains the hard backstop so even a successful injection cannot post a transaction.
controls.code_reference: agent.service.ts (SYSTEM_TH untrusted-data clause + runToolBlocks provenance wrapper); common/llm-client.ts (provider seam — scripted fake drives the real loop); cutover/ai-eval.ts (ToE incl. scored fake-LLM benchmark)

controls.toe: Re-perform: the system prompt carries the injection-defense clause; the scored fake-LLM benchmark (docs/27 R4-4) drives the REAL agent loop over a seeded DB and asserts every tool result reaches the model wrapped as untrusted_data, the figures are correct end-to-end, and Voided rows stay excluded — 100% gate (ai-eval).
controls.evidence: Prompt-injection defense + scored-loop ToE (ai-eval)

controls.risk: AI features transmit tenant data to the model provider in production BEFORE the Data Processing Agreement is executed — a contractual + PDPA violation.
controls.description: Production AI legal gate (fail-closed): when an Anthropic API key is set in production but AI_DPA_ACKNOWLEDGED is unset, all AI endpoints return AI_DPA_REQUIRED and the AI-assisted services degrade to their deterministic (no-transmission) path. The flag is set only after the DPA + Anthropic addendum are executed (counsel TODO tracked in the DPA).

controls.tod: Inspect the fail-closed gate + that fallback services degrade without transmitting.
controls.toe: Re-perform: with a prod key but no DPA ack, the gate blocks (AI_DPA_REQUIRED) and degrades; once acknowledged it allows (ai-cost unit test).

controls.risk: A data subject's rights request (access / rectification / portability / objection / erasure) is not tracked or is missed past the 30-day statutory deadline — a PDPA compliance + reputational exposure.
controls.description: DSAR register + workflow: POST /api/pdpa/dsar files a request (subject_type member/customer/employee/user x request_type access/rectification/erasure/portability/objection), stamped with the statutory 30-day due_date and a status lifecycle (received->in_progress->completed/rejected). Access/portability is fulfilled by POST /api/pdpa/dsar/:id/export which assembles the subject's data bundle (profile + per-purpose consents + points ledger + member-submitted receipt-upload photos, LYL-17 + the consent-gated guest dining profile/companions of the fine-casual guest CRM) and closes the request; reject records a reason. Gated by 'users' (the DPO/AccessAdmin duty, same as the access review + audit viewer) and tenant-scoped by RLS (a tenant sees only its own requests).
controls.code_reference: pdpa/pdpa.service.ts (createDsar sets 30-day due_date; exportSubject bundles profile+consents+ledger; listDsar/getDsar/rejectDsar); pdpa/pdpa.controller.ts (/api/pdpa/dsar*, perm 'users'); schema/pdpa.ts dsar_requests + migration 0180 (RLS); cutover/pdpa.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the DSAR lifecycle, the 30-day due-date stamp, the export bundle contents, and the RLS scoping.
controls.toe: Re-perform: file an access DSAR (received + ~30-day due), export (profile/consents/ledger returned, completed), reject (rejected); a DPO in another tenant cannot read the request (404) - re-performed by the pdpa harness.
controls.evidence: DSAR register + 30-day SLA test

controls.risk: Honouring erasure (right to be forgotten) conflicts with the immutable, hash-chained financial audit trail (ITGC-AC-10/AC-16): deleting/editing audit rows to remove PII would break tamper-evidence, yet leaving PII in place violates PDPA.
controls.description: Erasure with read-time pseudonymisation: POST /api/pdpa/dsar/:id/erase redacts the subject's PII in the operational store (pos_members name/phone/email/card/LINE nulled, consents withdrawn, account deactivated - id + member_code kept for referential integrity; loyalty_receipt_submissions receipt_image/store_name/note also redacted in place - purchase_amount/status/ref_doc are kept as transactional facts already reflected in the points ledger; member_dining_profiles + member_companions guest-CRM preference rows are HARD-DELETED - pure consent-based profiling data with no accounting value) and records an append-only pdpa_erasures ledger row (subject + erased PII values + a stable pseudonym). EMPLOYEE data subjects are covered too (docs/27 AUD-LGL-03): access/portability returns the decrypted ITGC-AC-19 identifiers (citizen ID / SSO no / bank account) + payslip history; erasure redacts the employee-master identifiers and deactivates the account while payslips/payruns are RETAINED under the Revenue Code / Accounting Act statutory periods (PDPA legal-obligation exemption), recorded in the DSAR result. The audit_log itself is NEVER mutated - its bytes and hash chain stay intact - instead the audit viewer/exports apply the ledger at READ time, replacing any erased PII with the pseudonym. So PDPA erasure and audit tamper-evidence both hold simultaneously.
controls.code_reference: pdpa/pdpa.service.ts (eraseSubject redacts pos_members + loyalty_receipt_submissions + hard-deletes member_dining_profiles/member_companions + withdraws consents + writes pdpa_erasures; maskAuditRows applies the ledger); audit-viewer.service.ts (query/exportCsv call maskAuditRows); schema/pdpa.ts pdpa_erasures + migration 0180 (RLS); schema/loyalty-receipt-submissions.ts; pdpa.service.ts collectEmployee/eraseEmployee (employee subject); cutover/pdpa.ts (ToE incl. employee access/erasure/statutory-retention)
controls.tod: Inspect that erasure redacts the source PII (incl. receipt-upload photos), records the pseudonym ledger, and that the stored audit row is unchanged while views mask it.
controls.toe: Re-perform: erase a member -> PII redacted (name=[erased], phone null) + a receipt submission's image/store/note redacted (purchase_amount/status kept) + pseudonym issued; the audit viewer shows the pseudonym (no PII) while the raw stored audit row still contains the original PII (immutability preserved) - re-performed by the pdpa harness.
controls.evidence: Erasure + audit pseudonymisation test

controls.risk: There is no maintained Record of Processing Activities (PDPA มาตรา 39 / GDPR Art.30): the company cannot demonstrate to the regulator/auditor what personal data it processes, for what purpose, on what legal basis, who receives it (recipients + sub-processors), how long it is kept, and on what basis it crosses borders — a PDPA accountability gap and an audit finding.
controls.description: RoPA register: a tenant-scoped, RLS-isolated inventory of processing activities (ropa_activities, migration 0282). Each activity records name, purpose, LEGAL BASIS (validated to the PDPA/GDPR set: consent/contract/legal_obligation/legitimate_interest/vital_interest/public_task — an invalid basis is rejected), data categories, data subjects, recipients, SUB-PROCESSORS (e.g. Anthropic/Stripe/Sentry), retention period, cross-border transfer basis (PDPA s.28-29 / GDPR Ch.V), and security measures. Managed by the DPO (POST/GET /api/pdpa/ropa[/:id], gated 'users' — the same DPO/AccessAdmin duty as the DSAR console) with an active/retired flag so the register is a living document reviewed periodically by the DPO.
controls.code_reference: pdpa/pdpa.service.ts (listRopa/getRopa/createRopa/updateRopa + legal-basis validation); pdpa/pdpa.controller.ts (GET/POST /api/pdpa/ropa[/:id]); schema/pdpa.ts ropa_activities + migration 0282 (canonical org-scoped RLS); cutover/pdpa.ts (RoPA ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the RoPA CRUD, the legal-basis validation, the sub-processor/cross-border capture, and the RLS isolation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a RoPA activity (legal basis captured, sub-processors listed); an invalid legal basis is rejected (400); the register lists it; update (retention + deactivate) persists; the active-only filter excludes a retired activity; another tenant's DPO cannot read it (404 RLS) — re-performed by the pdpa harness.
controls.evidence: RoPA register (processing-activity inventory) + RoPA CRUD ToE

controls.risk: Personal data is kept forever: loyalty-member PII whose retention period has elapsed is never anonymized (PDPA data-minimization/retention-limitation breach), or an automated purge mis-fires and destroys data still under statutory hold / still active.
controls.description: Opt-in PII retention sweep, DEFAULT-OFF and fail-safe: a per-tenant pdpa_retention_policies row (migration 0283, canonical RLS; UNIQUE per tenant+subject) sets an inactivity window with a SERVICE-ENFORCED 12-MONTH FLOOR (RETENTION_TOO_SHORT) — no row / enabled=false means the sweep touches NOTHING. The pii_retention_sweep job (BI scheduler report type; also POST /api/pdpa/retention/sweep with a dry_run mode) anonymizes loyalty members whose LATEST activity (newest points-ledger txn, else last_updated, else enrolled_at; all-NULL activity is never swept — fail-safe on missing data) is older than the window, via the SAME redaction path as DSAR erasure (redactMember): operational PII redacted, consents withdrawn, receipt photos deleted, an append-only pdpa_erasures pseudonym row recorded (dsar_id NULL marks it retention-swept) — the immutable audit trail is pseudonymised at READ time, never mutated, and transactional facts (points ledger, balances) are KEPT. Idempotent (an [erased] member is never a candidate) and bounded (500/policy/run).
controls.code_reference: pdpa/pdpa.service.ts (getRetentionPolicies/setRetentionPolicy floor + runRetentionSweep + shared redactMember); pdpa/pdpa.controller.ts (GET/PUT /api/pdpa/retention + POST retention/sweep); bi.service.ts REPORT_TYPES pii_retention_sweep (PdpaModule wired into BiModule); schema/pdpa.ts pdpa_retention_policies + migration 0283 (canonical org-scoped RLS); cutover/pdpa.ts + cutover/bi.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the default-off/opt-in gate, the 12-month floor, the latest-activity computation (fail-safe on NULL), the shared redaction path, and the dry-run.
controls.toe: Re-perform: with no policy the sweep is a no-op; retain_months<12 is rejected (RETENTION_TOO_SHORT); dry-run lists the aged member without redacting; the run anonymizes the aged member (pseudonym ledger row, dsar_id NULL) while a recently-active member is untouched; a re-run sweeps 0 (idempotent); the BI-scheduled job runs opt-in-only — re-performed by the pdpa + bi harnesses.
controls.evidence: Retention-policy register + sweep run results + pdpa_erasures rows (dsar_id NULL)

controls.risk: Member PII is shared with an external ads platform / CDP without a legal basis: an audience export runs with no recorded processing activity, includes members who never consented (or withdrew), ships RAW phone/email off-platform, or pushes to an attacker-controlled/internal destination (SSRF) — a PDPA s.19/24 breach with regulatory and reputational exposure.
controls.description: Consent-gated hashed audience export (G3, docs/45): the audience_export_sync BI job is FAIL-CLOSED twice over. (1) It refuses to run without an ACTIVE ropa_activities row named 'audience_export' with legal_basis='consent' (ROPA_MISSING — the processing register IS the permission to process), recording a 'blocked' row in the append-only audience_exports register (migration 0371, canonical RLS). (2) The payload builder (CrmService.exportForCustomerMatch) includes ONLY members with a LIVE marketing consent in member_consents (granted, not withdrawn) — NO fallback to the legacy marketingOptIn flag (the ledger is the legal basis; a member with no row is EXCLUDED) — and emits ONLY SHA-256 hashes of the normalized email (trim/lowercase) and phone (E.164 digits, Thai 0x→66x), the exact Meta Custom Audiences / Google Customer Match ingest format: raw PII never reaches the wire (no names, no codes, no traits). The push routes through pushHashedAudience with the L-6 SSRF gate (assertPublicUrl: public https only; the legacy pushToCdp gained the same gate); unconfigured destination = deterministic mock. Every attempt — success, failed, or blocked — lands in audience_exports (counts, target, consent basis, the ROPA id it ran under, actor) plus an ITGC-AC-10 egress row in audit_log; GET /api/crm/audience-export/preview (marketing/exec) lets the marketer SEE the consent gate + hash-only payload before scheduling.
controls.code_reference: crm/crm.service.ts (exportForCustomerMatch consent filter + sha256 normalization; audienceExportRegister); bi/bi-generate.service.ts (audienceExportSync fail-closed ROPA gate + register writes); common/cdp-sync.ts (pushHashedAudience + assertPublicUrl SSRF gates); crm/crm.controller.ts (audience-export/preview + /register, marketing/exec); schema/audience-exports.ts + migration 0371 (canonical org-scoped RLS, tenant-leading index); cutover/pdpa.ts (PDPA-05 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the ROPA fail-closed gate, the member_consents-only filter (no flag fallback), the sha256 normalization, the SSRF gate on the push, and the append-only register.
controls.toe: Re-perform: the preview returns hash-only rows for ONLY the consented member (a no-consent-row member with marketingOptIn=true and a withdrawn member are both excluded) with byte-exact normalized sha256 email/phone and no raw PII anywhere in the payload; a run without the audience_export ROPA activity FAILS CLOSED (ROPA_MISSING) with a 'blocked' register row; after the DPO records the ROPA activity the run succeeds and the register row carries consented/pushed counts, the mock target, and the ROPA id — re-performed by the pdpa harness (PDPA-05 checks).
controls.evidence: audience_exports register rows (blocked + success) tied to the ROPA activity; audit_log CRM.AUDIENCE_EXPORT egress rows

controls.risk: Developer self-deploys unreviewed change to prod.
controls.description: Segregation: developer ≠ prod deployer. The control of record is the GitHub `production` Environment's REQUIRED-REVIEWER approval (a human other than the author must approve before the deploy job runs). An automated deploy-time SoD assertion additionally re-performs the check every deploy: it compares GITHUB_ACTOR (who triggered the deploy) against the deployed commit's git author (identity-normalised so a GitHub login matches its noreply-email form) and flags a self-deploy — report-only by default (the reviewer gate is the hard control), promotable to a hard gate via DEPLOY_SOD_ENFORCE=1.
controls.code_reference: .github/workflows/deploy.yml (production Environment required reviewers + the 'Deploy SoD check' step); tools/ci/check-deploy-sod.mjs (deployer≠author assertion, --selftest self-tests)
controls.tod: Inspect the deploy approval gate + role separation + the automated deployer≠author step in deploy.yml.
controls.toe: Sample deploys: approver ≠ author (Environment reviewer log); the automated check re-performs deployer≠author each deploy and its self-tests (node tools/ci/check-deploy-sod.mjs --selftest) prove a matching identity fails (exit 1) and a distinct identity passes (exit 0).
controls.evidence: Deploy approvals + automated deployer≠author check

controls.risk: Failures/incidents undetected — the API runs 'blind' in production with error reporting/tracing left unconfigured, and slow paths or pool pressure are invisible until a customer reports an outage.
controls.description: Monitoring + alerting with on-call. The API ALWAYS emits monitorable signals — structured pino logs (requestId+tenant), the append-only audit_log, /healthz + /readyz probes, per-request slow-transaction timing (SLOW_TX_MS), and an admin-gated ops-metrics endpoint (DB-pool saturation / slow-request counts / job backlog) — so prod is never 'silently blind'. External error-aggregation (Sentry) + distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) are RECOMMENDED enhancements, not a boot requirement; an operator MANDATES them as a fail-closed boot gate via REQUIRE_OBSERVABILITY_BACKENDS=1 (the gate then REFUSES to start when either is unset, still overridable with a conscious ALLOW_NO_OBSERVABILITY=1 — a loud, auditable decision). A reusable ops-alert sink (captureOpsAlert) emits a structured error log + a Sentry capture for operator-facing conditions.
controls.code_reference: common/env.validation.ts (observability posture — recommended by default, enforceable via REQUIRE_OBSERVABILITY_BACKENDS); observability/instrumentation.ts (OTel + Sentry + captureOpsAlert); observability/runtime-metrics.ts; common/tenant-tx.interceptor.ts (slow_request log + in-flight gauge); modules/jobs/jobs.controller.ts (GET /api/jobs/ops-metrics); apps/api/test/ops-observability.test.ts; docs/ops/observability-incident.md
controls.tod: Inspect the always-on built-in signals (logs/audit/health/ops-metrics), the optional enforce gate, the ops-alert sink, and slow-path logging.
controls.toe: Re-perform (unit): prod BOOTS when Sentry/OTel unset (recommended, not required); with REQUIRE_OBSERVABILITY_BACKENDS set boot THROWS unless wired or opted out; the in-flight/slow-tx gauges count correctly.
controls.evidence: Observability posture (built-in signals + opt-in enforce gate) + ops-metrics ToE

controls.risk: Scheduled/background financial jobs (recurring journals, lease/prepaid amortization, billing, dunning) fail silently — a job that exhausts its retries is dead-lettered with no signal, and a job whose worker crashes mid-run is left 'running' forever (a zombie the claim path never re-picks), so a missed posting goes unnoticed.
controls.description: Background-job failure alerting + dead-letter recovery. A job that exhausts its retries raises an operational alert (captureOpsAlert → structured error log + Sentry) instead of silently landing in 'failed'. A reaper (piggybacked on the worker poll loop, also harness-callable) detects jobs stuck in 'running' past a stale-lock threshold and requeues them if retries remain, else dead-letters + alerts. Cross-tenant counts (queued/running/failed/stuck) are exposed on the admin ops-metrics endpoint as the input to an external page-on-backlog rule. Additionally the scheduled-report/action layer (BI subscriptions: recurring journals, lease/prepaid amortization, AI-overage billing, dunning) emits the SAME ops alert + an operator-facing in-app notification when a run fails — closing the swallow gap where executeSubscription catches the error (so the worker would otherwise mark the background job 'done' and the dead-letter alert would never fire) — and every failed run is recorded for review (GET /api/bi/runs).
controls.code_reference: modules/jobs/job-queue.service.ts (markFailed dead-letter alert; reapStuck; opsCounts); job-worker.service.ts (maybeReap on the poll loop + reap()); observability/instrumentation.ts (captureOpsAlert); modules/jobs/jobs.controller.ts (ops-metrics); modules/bi/bi.service.ts executeSubscription (scheduled-job-failure ops alert + operator notification + failed-run record); schema/background-jobs.ts; cutover/async-jobs.ts + cutover/bi.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the dead-letter alert, the stuck-job reaper, the backlog metrics, and the scheduled-job failure alert path.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a retry-exhausted job → 'failed' + ops alert; a stale 'running' job is requeued (retries left) or dead-lettered (exhausted); a fresh 'running' job is left alone; opsCounts reports the backlog (async-jobs); a failed scheduled subscription → failed run recorded + an operator ops-notification raised + reviewable in GET /api/bi/runs (bi harness).
controls.evidence: Dead-letter + reaper + scheduled-job alert ToE (async-jobs, bi)

controls.risk: Cash over/short never hits the books, or a cashier clears their own material drawer discrepancy — a shortage (theft/skimming) is buried because book-cash (1000) is never reconciled to the physical count, and no independent review gates a large variance.
controls.description: Till-close cash over/short GL posting + material-variance maker-checker (SoD): expected cash = opening float + CASH tenders (amount + tip: a cash tip physically enters the drawer and the sale's GL debits 1000 for total+tip, while payments.amount excludes it; tips on non-cash tenders never reach the drawer) + paid-in - paid-out - drops - cash refunds. On closeTill the variance (counted - expected) is POSTED to GL — short → Dr 5830 Cash Over/Short / Cr 1000 Cash; over → Dr 1000 / Cr 5830 — so the cash control account always tracks the physical count (idempotent per till via source=TILL_CLOSE). A variance at/above the materiality threshold (THB 100) posts the over/short as a DRAFT JE and parks the session in PendingApproval: a DIFFERENT user (manager) must approve (POST /api/payments/till/variance/:sessionNo/approve) before it is effective — self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin); reject voids the draft. Sub-threshold variances post immediately. The till still closes either way (the cash has left the drawer); only the GL clearing of a material discrepancy is gated.
controls.code_reference: payments.service.ts (closeTill posts 5830↔1000, approveVariance/rejectVariance via ledger.approveEntry/rejectEntry SoD); payments.controller.ts (till/variance/:sessionNo/approve|reject, pos_till); schema/payments.ts till_sessions.variance_status/variance_journal_no + migration 0140 (account 5830); ledger COA 5830 Cash Over/Short; cutover/cashreport.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the over/short JE direction + the materiality gate + the approver≠closer check + that a material variance posts a Draft (excluded from balances) until approved.
controls.toe: Re-perform: close with a small short → over/short posts to 5830 immediately (NotRequired); close with a material short → Draft JE + PendingApproval, self-approve blocked (SOD_VIOLATION), a different user approves → JE Posted; trial balance stays balanced throughout (re-performed by the cashreport harness).
controls.evidence: Cash over/short JEs (5830); till variance approval trail

controls.risk: The close-of-day Z-tape can be altered after the fact — a shift's recorded sales/cash totals are mutated to conceal skimming, and there is no immutable, attested record an auditor can rely on to prove the day's takings as originally counted.
controls.description: Signed, tamper-evident Z-report archive (POS-07): closing a till computes the shift totals (gross sales, by-method, expected vs counted cash, over/short — see REV-13); a manager then SIGNS the Z-report (POST /api/payments/till/:sessionNo/z-report/sign, gated pos_close — distinct from the cashier's pos_till) which SNAPSHOTS those totals + the denomination count into an immutable record (xz_reports/xz_report_denominations) carrying a content_hash = sha256 over the canonical totals. Signing is only allowed once the till is Closed (TILL_NOT_CLOSED otherwise) and is idempotent per till (a second sign returns the existing record — no duplicate Z-tape). On read, the stored hash is RE-COMPUTED from the persisted totals and any mismatch is surfaced as hash_valid=false, so a later edit to the archived row is detectable. A sell-only Cashier (pos_sell) cannot sign — the attestation is a manager duty.
controls.code_reference: payments.service.ts (signZReport snapshots aggregateTill totals + denominations, content_hash via node:crypto, idempotent per till; getXzReport recomputes the hash → hash_valid; listXzReports); payments.controller.ts (till/:sessionNo/z-report/sign pos_close, xz-reports[/:id]); schema/payments.ts xz_reports + xz_report_denominations + migration 0157; permissions.ts pos_close (PosSupervisor); cutover/cashreport.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the sign→SIGNED+content_hash flow, the closed-till precondition, the idempotency, and the hash recompute → hash_valid tamper flag.
controls.toe: Re-perform: close a till, sign the Z (SIGNED + 64-hex hash, denominations snapshotted), re-sign (already=true, same hash), fetch (hash_valid=true), tamper a persisted total (hash_valid=false), sign an open till (TILL_NOT_CLOSED), Cashier sign (403) — re-performed by the cashreport harness.
controls.evidence: Signed Z-report archive + tamper-detection test

controls.risk: A PromptPay QR taking is captured at the till but the money never actually lands in the store's bank account (or lands short/late) and no one notices — the QR tender sits as 'settled' on the POS while the bank inflow is missing, so skimmed/failed/duplicated e-payments are not detected because the till's QR sales are never tied out to the bank statement per store per day.
controls.description: PromptPay store-level auto-reconciliation (POS-08): POST /api/pos/promptpay-recon/run matches the store's PromptPay tenders (method PromptPay/QR or gateway=promptpay) for a business day against the imported bank-statement INFLOWS on the store's mapped settlement account — REUSING the bank reconciliation auto-match engine (modules/bank/match-engine.ts: amount within a cent, date within the ±5-day tolerance window, payer-ref containment), so there is ONE matcher scoped to the settlement account, not a second one. A matched inflow line is recorded (reconciled + matched_payment_no) exactly as the GL bank auto-match does; a tender left UNMATCHED is surfaced as a till/cash EXCEPTION (promptpay_till_exceptions, Open) mirroring the till-variance exception surface — it stays on the open worklist (GET …/exceptions?status=Open) until a manager (pos_close/exec) clears it, and a re-run that finds a late inflow auto-resolves the exception. The settlement-account map is a finance-config duty (PUT …/settlement-account, recon_prep/exec); running the reconciliation is recon_prep/pos_close/exec (a sell-only cashier is denied). Read-model over the tenders + statement lines — posts no GL, adds no posting authority (no SoD change).
controls.code_reference: bank/promptpay-recon.service.ts (setSettlementAccount/reconcile/listExceptions/clearException — reconcile reuses match-engine.greedyMatch); bank/match-engine.ts (shared amount/date/ref scoring, also used by bank.service.ts autoMatch); bank/promptpay-recon.controller.ts (/api/pos/promptpay-recon settlement-account|run|exceptions[/:id/clear], recon_prep/pos_close/exec); schema/promptpay-recon.ts pos_settlement_accounts + promptpay_till_exceptions + migration 0301 (RLS 0232); cutover/cash-banking.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reuse of the single bank match engine scoped to the settlement account, the matched-line recording, the unmatched→exception surface, and the recon_prep/pos_close/exec gating (a pos_sell cashier denied).
controls.toe: Re-perform (cash-banking): map a settlement account; seed 2 PromptPay tenders (500 with a bank inflow, 300 without); run recon → 1 matched (the inflow line reconciled to the payment), 1 unmatched → an Open exception for the 300; import a late inflow + re-run → the 300 matches and its exception auto-resolves; a manager clears a fresh exception → Resolved; a pos_sell cashier is denied (403).
controls.evidence: PromptPay recon match log; open till-exception worklist

controls.risk: A card tip added after the authorization is manipulated — a server inflates the tip after the guest leaves (capturing more than the guest wrote on the slip), edits the tip after the charge has already settled, or the captured total balloons past the authorized hold — so the guest is over-charged, the tip pass-through (2300) is overstated, and there is no trail tying the charged tip back to the slip.
controls.description: Tip-adjust-after-auth window (POS-10, migration 0309): the US-restaurant 'authorize now, add the tip, capture later' card flow. A card tender is AUTHORIZED for the bill amount at checkout (POST /api/payments {authorize:true} → status Authorized, no funds captured); staff then set the tip the guest wrote on the slip (POST /api/payments/:no/tip-adjust) and finally capture bill + tip (POST /api/payments/:no/capture). TWO preventive controls bound the adjustment: (1) PRE-CAPTURE ONLY — the tip can be set only while the tender is Authorized; a Captured/Settled/Voided/Refunded tender is immutable (TIP_ADJUST_CLOSED), so a settled charge can never be re-tipped. (2) POLICY CEILING — the tip may not exceed TIP_ADJUST_MAX_PCT of the authorized amount (default 25%, env-overridable) at BOTH adjust and capture time (TIP_OVER_LIMIT), so the captured total stays within the pre-auth cushion. EVERY adjustment is written to the append-only pos_tip_adjustments log (old→new tip, delta, the auth amount + ceiling in force, who/when) so the charged tip always ties back to the slip. On capture the tip posts to 2300 Tips Payable (Dr 1000 / Cr 2300, idempotent per payment — re-capture is a no-op), feeding the existing tip-pool/distribution flow (TIP-01) unchanged. The bill's revenue/VAT are recognised at the sale checkout, so only the added tip books here.
controls.code_reference: payments.service.ts (recordTender authorize-only branch; adjustTip pre-capture + ceiling guards + pos_tip_adjustments audit insert under row lock; capture re-asserts the ceiling, PSP capture for bill+tip, Dr1000/Cr2300 tip post idempotent per payment, listTipAdjustments); payments.controller.ts (:no/tip-adjust|:no/capture pos_sell/ar, :no/tip-adjustments read; TenderBody authorize flag); gateways.ts (authorize/capture on Mock/Stripe manual-capture/Opn preauth surface — reuses OmiseProvider preauth+capture, no new processor); schema/payments.ts pos_tip_adjustments + migration 0309 (RLS 0232, tenant-leading index); COA 1000/2300; cutover/tips.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the authorize→adjust→capture state machine, the pre-capture-only + %-ceiling guards at both adjust and capture, the immutable pos_tip_adjustments trail, and the idempotent Dr1000/Cr2300 tip post on capture.
controls.toe: Re-perform (tips harness): authorize a 200 card bill → Authorized; adjust the tip above the 25% ceiling (60>50) → TIP_OVER_LIMIT; adjust to 40 within the window → logged; capture → Captured, total 240, tip posts +40 to 2300 and flows into the tip pool; adjust after capture → TIP_ADJUST_CLOSED; re-capture is idempotent (no double 2300 post); trial balance stays balanced.
controls.evidence: pos_tip_adjustments audit log; 2300 tip accrual; over-limit/post-capture rejects

controls.risk: An uncollectible receivable is written off the books without independent review — one person both declares the receivable uncollectible and posts the write-off, concealing a misappropriated customer collection (lapping) or removing a still-collectible balance.
controls.description: AR bad-debt write-off maker-checker (SoD): POST /api/finance/ar/write-off books the write-off (Dr 5720 Bad Debt Expense / Cr 1100 AR) as a DRAFT via the ledger maker-checker (GL-05) — excluded from the trial balance until a DIFFERENT user approves it (POST /api/ledger/journal/:entryNo/approve); a self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin), and the pending write-off surfaces in the pending-approvals monitor automatically (it is a Draft JE). A non-positive amount → BAD_AMOUNT and a missing reason is rejected. The write-off register (GET /api/finance/ar/write-offs) lists every write-off Draft(pending)/Posted(approved)/Voided(rejected) with its amount for controller review.
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts writeOffAr/listWriteOffs; finance.controller.ts (ar/write-off, ar/write-offs); ledger COA 5720 Bad Debt Expense; reuses ledger.approveEntry maker-checker (GL-05); cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the write-off → Draft (excluded from balances) → approve flow + the approver≠maker check + that nothing posts until approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: request a write-off (Draft, 5720 unchanged), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user; confirm 5720 bad-debt expense + 1100 relief only after approval; the register lists it (re-performed by the basics harness).
controls.evidence: Write-off register + SoD test

controls.risk: No single customer-of-record — B2C loyalty members and B2B accounts live in disjoint silos, so revenue-by-customer, a 360 view, and AR-by-customer cannot be reconciled to one identity (incomplete/duplicated customer reporting; AR mis-attributed).
controls.description: Unified customer master: a single registry (customer_master) is the customer-of-record, LINKING the pre-existing silos — member_id → pos_members (B2C loyalty) and account_code → the B2B customer tenant (orders + AR) — without disturbing them. A 360 view (GET /api/customer-master/:no/360) joins both: loyalty tier/points + the B2B orders/stats and AR balance, and the 360 AR-outstanding TIES to the AR sub-ledger for that account (revenue/AR resolve to one record). Tenant-scoped (RLS). Create/link/search are gated by crm/exec/ar.
controls.code_reference: customers.module.ts CustomerMasterService (create/list/get/link/view360 — reuses CustomersService.detail for the B2B side); customer-master controller (POST/GET /api/customer-master, :no/360, :no/link); schema/customer-master.ts customer_master + migration 0149; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the master registry + the 360 join across loyalty + AR + that 360 AR-outstanding ties to the AR sub-ledger.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a customer-of-record linking a B2B account + a B2C member; GET 360 → loyalty tier/points present and AR-outstanding equals the AR sub-ledger for the account; search finds the record (re-performed by the basics harness).
controls.evidence: Customer master register + 360 AR tie-out

controls.risk: A cashier issues a large refund without any independent review — refunding to themselves/an accomplice or reversing a real sale to skim the cash (refund fraud), because the refund moves money the moment one person requests it.
controls.description: Refund maker-checker (SoD): a STANDALONE refund (POST /api/payments/refunds) at/above the materiality threshold (THB 1,000) is parked as a REQUEST (refund_requests) and MOVES NO MONEY — no payment_refunds row, the payment is not flipped — until a DIFFERENT user approves it (POST /api/payments/refund-requests/:id/approve); self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION; reject voids the request. Sub-threshold refunds run immediately, and a refund that is part of a goods-return is never gated (the return is the authorizing document, REV-07). The pending request auto-surfaces in the GOV-01 pending-approvals monitor and an inline approve/reject sits on the /approvals screen. The over-refund guard (REV-06) still binds at execution.
controls.code_reference: payments.service.ts refund (threshold gate → requestRefund), approveRefund/rejectRefund (SoD by username), listRefundRequests; payments.controller.ts (refund-requests/:id/approve|reject, pos_refund/ar/exec); schema/payments.ts refund_requests + migration 0151; finance.pendingApprovals (GOV-01 9th source); /approvals inline action; cutover/refund-approval.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the threshold gate, the request-posts-nothing property, the approver≠requester check, and that the goods-return path is not gated.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a small refund runs immediately; a large refund parks PendingApproval (no payment_refunds row) + surfaces in GOV-01; self-approve → SOD_VIOLATION; a different user approves → the refund executes; reject moves no money (re-performed by the refund-approval harness).
controls.evidence: Refund-request worklist + SoD test

controls.risk: Sales pipeline ungoverned — no lead-to-opportunity motion or controlled stage progression, so the revenue forecast is unreliable (opportunities skip stages, closed deals are silently re-opened, lost deals carry no reason), and converted leads don't attach to a customer-of-record.
controls.description: CRM sales pipeline on the customer-of-record — UNIFIED spine (CRM-1, migration 0295): leads → qualify → CONVERT (creates/attaches a customer_master + a CRM account/primary contact + an opportunity; a converted/lost lead can't be re-converted) → ONE opportunity table (crm_opportunities) serves BOTH route families (/api/crm/pipeline and the legacy /api/pipeline adapters) and runs a controlled stage machine over the tenant-configurable pipeline_stages master (defaults seeded per tenant; probability tracks the stage row) where won/lost are TERMINAL on every route (OPP_CLOSED) and lost REQUIRES a reason on the governed route (LOST_REASON_REQUIRED). EVERY stage transition (incl. creation) writes the append-only crm_stage_history audit. Duplicate governance on the party model: creating a crm_account/crm_contact that matches an existing record on normalized tax-id/email/phone/company-name is refused 409 DUPLICATE_SUSPECT (steward force override); a confirmed duplicate is merged survivor-pattern (children repointed, soft-retired merged_into/by/at) and the merge is MAKER-CHECKED when children reassign (caller ≠ duplicate creator → SOD_VIOLATION). The weighted pipeline forecast = Σ open-opportunity amount × probability, with won/lost totals + win-rate — the revenue forecast finance relies on. Activities are logged per lead/opportunity. Tenant-scoped (RLS); gated crm/exec/ar (merge: crm/exec/masterdata).
controls.code_reference: crm-pipeline.service.ts (unified spine: stage machine over pipeline_stages, crm_stage_history, pipelineSummary, /api/pipeline adapter methods); pipeline.service.ts (thin adapter); crm/accounts/crm-accounts.module.ts (accounts/contacts, DUPLICATE_SUSPECT, audited merge); schema/crm-pipeline.ts crm_leads/crm_opportunities/crm_activities/crm_accounts/crm_contacts/crm_stage_history + migrations 0152/0294; cutover/basics.ts + cutover/pipeline.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the lead-conversion → customer-of-record/account link, the unified stage machine (won/lost terminal on both routes, lost-reason required, stage-history audit), and the duplicate-detection + maker-checked merge.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a lead, qualify, convert (→ CUS- + ACC- + OPP-, re-convert blocked), advance to won (terminal on BOTH routes — re-stage blocked OPP_CLOSED), lost without a reason blocked; stage history lists every transition; a duplicate account create → DUPLICATE_SUSPECT then force; the duplicate creator's merge with children → SOD_VIOLATION, a distinct user merges and children repoint; the weighted forecast + won total compute (re-performed by the basics + pipeline harnesses).
controls.evidence: Pipeline stage-machine + stage-history + duplicate-governance tests

controls.risk: Staff tips are skimmed or mis-paid — tips collected from guests are never paid out (the 2300 liability grows unexplained), more is paid out than was collected (theft via inflated tip-outs), or the cashier who rings sales also pays the tips (to self).
controls.description: Tip pooling / distribution with SoD: tips ride into 2300 Tips Payable on checkout (a staff pass-through liability, not revenue/VAT). A distribution (POST /api/restaurant/tips/distribute) pays the pooled tips out to staff for a period — Dr 2300 / Cr 1000 (cash) — clearing the liability; the amount is HARD-CAPPED at the available pool (collected − already distributed, bounded by the 2300 GL outstanding) so tips can never be over-paid (TIP_OVER_DISTRIBUTE). 2300 always reconciles to what is still owed to staff. SoD: distributing is a manager/finance duty (order_mgt/exec), segregated from the cashier who rings sales (pos_sell) — a cashier cannot pay tips to themselves (403).
controls.code_reference: restaurant/tip.service.ts (pool/distribute/list, outstanding2300 reconciliation, over-distribute guard); restaurant.controller.ts (tips/pool|distribute|list; distribute @order_mgt/exec); schema/restaurant.ts tip_distributions + tip_distribution_lines + migration 0146; payments.tip → 2300 on checkout; cutover/tips.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the pool computation, the 2300↔collected reconciliation, the over-distribute cap, and the cashier-vs-manager SoD on the distribute route.
controls.toe: Re-perform: ring sales with tips (2300 accrues), a cashier's distribute → 403, a manager distributes by hours (Dr 2300 / Cr 1000, 2300 clears to 0, shares correct), a second over-distribute → TIP_OVER_DISTRIBUTE; trial balance balanced (re-performed by the tips harness).
controls.evidence: Tip distribution register; 2300 reconciliation

controls.fsli: Accounts Receivable; Bad-debt expense; Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (1190)
controls.risk: AR is carried at gross with no provision for expected credit losses — receivables that are partly/wholly uncollectible are overstated (assets/equity overstated, bad-debt expense understated and untimely), and a single person could both set the provision and post it (manage earnings via the allowance).
controls.description: AR Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (ECL): a periodic, aging-driven provision — each open-AR bucket (current / 1-30 / 31-60 / 61-90 / 91-120 / 120+) carries a loss rate and the allowance = Σ(outstanding × rate) (a flat-percentage method is also supported). Computation upserts an UNPOSTED row (one per tenant/as-of-date); posting is MAKER-CHECKED — the poster must DIFFER from the computer (computer self-post → SOD_SELF_POST, binds even Admin) — and journals only the DELTA vs the prior posted allowance (increase: Dr 5720 Bad-Debt Expense / Cr 1190 contra-asset; decrease reverses) so 1190 carries the cumulative provision while 1100 gross AR is untouched. Posting routes through LedgerService.postEntry, so it respects the period lock (PERIOD_LOCKED) and a posted allowance can't be re-posted (ALREADY_POSTED).
controls.code_reference: finance/ar-allowance.service.ts (computeAllowance aging buckets/rates; postAllowance delta + SoD computer≠poster); finance.controller.ts (/api/finance/ar-allowance/compute|:id/post|GET, gated creditors/ar/gl_post/exec); schema/ar-allowance.ts ar_allowance + migration 0166; COA 1190; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the aging-bucket rates, the allowance computation, the delta posting (Dr 5720 / Cr 1190), and the compute-vs-post SoD.
controls.toe: Re-perform: compute an allowance over seeded aged AR (buckets × rates → allowance), the computer's self-post → SOD_SELF_POST, a different user posts the delta → 5720/1190 move by the provision, a re-post → ALREADY_POSTED (re-performed by the basics harness).
controls.evidence: AR allowance register; provision JE; SoD test

controls.fsli: Revenue; Contract Liability / Deferred Revenue (2410); Refund Liability (2420)
controls.risk: Service/subscription/project revenue is recognized in the wrong period — booked up-front (or never deferred) instead of as the performance obligations are satisfied, and a multi-element contract's price is not split by standalone selling price — so revenue and the contract-liability are misstated (TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 non-compliance; revenue overstated/understated, deferred revenue mis-carried, expected refunds unprovided).
controls.description: TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 five-step revenue recognition engine (the 'real ERP' deferred-revenue model; the restaurant POS keeps immediate recognition). A contract carries one or more performance obligations; the transaction price is ALLOCATED across them in proportion to standalone selling price (allocateBySSP — Σ allocated == total_price EXACTLY, the rounding residual lands on the largest-SSP PO; Σssp≤0 → INVALID_ALLOCATION). On activation the full price raises the contract liability (Dr 1100 AR / Cr 2410 Deferred Revenue, viaSubledger so the AR control is respected). buildSchedule lays out the recognition plan — over_time POs straight-line their allocated price across the months start..end, point_in_time POs a single row at the satisfaction date — and is idempotent (rebuilds only UNRECOGNIZED rows; recognized rows are never touched). recognize() releases revenue for all schedule rows due in/through a period (Dr 2410 / Cr 4300 Recognized Revenue), marks the row + bumps the PO satisfied_pct/status, and is idempotent (an already-recognized row is skipped — no double post). accrueRefundLiability provisions expected returns (Dr 4300 contra revenue / Cr 2420 Refund Liability) posting only the DELTA vs the prior provision. All posting routes through LedgerService.postEntry so the period lock (PERIOD_LOCKED, GL-15/16) and the GL-17 audit trail bind; recognition is tenant-scoped (HQ/Admin must name a tenant → TENANT_REQUIRED).
controls.code_reference: revenue/revrec.service.ts (createContract; allocateBySSP residual handling; activate Dr1100/Cr2410; buildSchedule straight-line/point-in-time idempotent; recognize Dr2410/Cr4300; accrueRefundLiability delta Dr4300/Cr2420); revenue/revrec.controller.ts (/api/revenue/contracts POST|GET|:id|:id/allocate|:id/schedule|:id/activate|recognize|:id/refund-liability, gated exec/ar/fin_report); schema/revrec-contracts.ts rev_contracts/performance_obligations/revrec_schedules/refund_liability + migration 0172; COA 2410/2420; cutover/revrec.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the SSP allocation (sums exactly to price), the activation/recognition/refund postings, the straight-line vs point-in-time schedule, and the idempotency + period-lock + tenant-scoping guards.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a contract (total 1000, SSP 800 over_time / 400 point_in_time) → allocate sums to 1000 with residual on the larger PO; activate → Dr1100/Cr2410 1000; build schedule (4+1 rows); recognize 2040-01 → 2410 falls 500 / 4300 rises 500; recognize again → 0 new; refund liability 10% → Dr4300/Cr2420 50 (re-performed by the revrec harness).
controls.evidence: Contract register; SSP allocation; deferred-revenue roll-forward; recognition + refund JEs

controls.risk: A voucher/coupon campaign goes live without independent review (self-issued margin giveaway), a code is redeemed twice or after expiry/void under concurrency, an under-minimum sale takes the discount, or a voided code leaves no audit trail — revenue is understated by unauthorized or duplicated markdowns (POS-3, docs/41).
controls.description: Voucher lifecycle control (migration 0292: voucher_campaigns + voucher_codes, RLS). (1) ACTIVATION IS MAKER-CHECKER: a campaign is created 'PendingApproval' and its codes redeem ONLY once a DIFFERENT user activates it via POST /api/vouchers/campaigns/:id/approve (creator self-approval → 403 SOD_VIOLATION — mirrors the price-rule G6 gate); the checkout redemption path reads status='Active' campaigns only. (2) ATOMIC SINGLE REDEMPTION: redemption at checkout (dine-in buildSale, POST /api/restaurant/orders/:orderNo/checkout voucher_code) runs a guarded UPDATE (WHERE state='issued' AND use_count < per_code_max_uses) inside the sale transaction — a concurrent second redemption claims 0 rows → 409 VOUCHER_ALREADY_REDEEMED and its sale rolls back; the campaign-wide cap increments under its own guard (VOUCHER_EXHAUSTED). Validity window (VOUCHER_EXPIRED/NOT_STARTED), min-spend (VOUCHER_MIN_SPEND) and channel gates are enforced server-side; the loyalty member-coupon wallet redeems through the SAME surface (guarded active→used; ALREADY_USED). The code is consumed only when its discount actually applies, and sale_ref/redeemed_by/redeemed_at record the redeeming sale. (3) VOID AUDITED: POST /api/vouchers/codes/:code/void flips only an issued code (redeemed → 409 CANNOT_VOID) stamping voided_by/voided_at/void_reason. Codes are crypto-random (unique per tenant); generation/maintenance is gated to the promo/marketing duties (R10 split — segregated from selling); a returned sale does NOT auto-release the code (consistent with promo usage; manual re-issue via bulk generate).
