controls.code_reference: campaigns/vouchers.service.ts (createCampaign PendingApproval; approveCampaign SOD_VIOLATION; generateCodes crypto-random unique retry; voidCode guarded + audited; previewForCheckout validity/min-spend/channel/cap checks incl. member_coupons; redeemAtCheckout guarded UPDATE + campaign-cap increment); campaigns/vouchers.controller.ts (/api/vouchers/campaigns[…]/approve|reject|end|codes|codes.csv|redemptions, codes/:code/void, validate — maintenance gated promos/pricelist/marketing/exec, validate open to sellers); restaurant/dine-in.service.ts buildSale voucher_code path (best-discount slot, consume-only-if-applied, atomic in-sale-tx redeem); schema/vouchers.ts + migration 0292 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes); web /loyalty/campaigns admin + /pos/register checkout voucher field; cutover/pricing.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the maker-checker gate (pending campaign codes rejected at checkout; self-approval 403), the guarded-UPDATE redemption (state/use_count predicates), the validity/min-spend enforcement, and the void audit stamps.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a 10% voucher campaign (min spend 100) as one user → codes do NOT redeem (VOUCHER_NOT_ACTIVE); creator self-approve → 403 SOD_VIOLATION; a distinct user approves → a generated code redeems at checkout (discount + balanced GL) and sale_ref recorded; the SAME code again → 409 VOUCHER_ALREADY_REDEEMED; an expired campaign's code → VOUCHER_EXPIRED; an under-minimum sale → VOUCHER_MIN_SPEND; void an issued code → audited, then redeem → rejected (re-performed by the pricing harness).
controls.evidence: voucher_campaigns register (status/created_by/approved_by); voucher_codes state + sale_ref/redeemed_by + void stamps; redemption report GET /api/vouchers/campaigns/:id/redemptions

controls.fsli: Cash; Accounts Receivable (1100); Unapplied Customer Receipts (2220)
controls.risk: Customer cash is mis-applied or its application is untracked — a receipt covering several invoices is forced onto one (or keyed twice), an invoice is over-applied, unallocated cash disappears into the wrong account (or is shuffled between customers/invoices to conceal lapping), and an application can be silently un-done — so AR open balances, aging, dunning and the customer statement misstate what each customer owes.
controls.description: AR cash application with on-account tracking + threshold maker-checker: a worksheet (POST /api/finance/ar/cash-application) applies ONE receipt across MANY invoices (partial allowed) with fail-closed guards — Σ applications ≤ the receipt (APPLY_EXCEEDS_RECEIPT), each invoice's open balance never overshot INCLUDING pending applications (OVER_APPLIED), cross-customer application rejected (CUSTOMER_MISMATCH). The unapplied remainder parks ON-ACCOUNT — tracked per receipt (ar_receipts.unapplied_amount) and on GL 2220 Unapplied Customer Receipts (Dr 1000 / Cr 1100 applied / Cr 2220 remainder) — and is applied later via POST /api/finance/ar/apply-on-account (Dr 2220 / Cr 1100; INSUFFICIENT_UNAPPLIED guard incl. pending draws). An application batch at/over the threshold (THB 100,000, mirroring REV-16) parks PendingApproval — the cash banks fully on-account and NO invoice moves — until a DIFFERENT user approves (self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION; pending batches surface in the GOV-01 monitor). A reversal REQUIRES a reason and is audited in place (reversed flag + who/when/why; the cash returns on-account, Dr 1100 / Cr 2220). An Issued AR-linked credit note (ใบลดหนี้, TAX-07) is applicable as a credit line — re-aligning the AR sub-ledger to the 1100 relief the note posted at approval (CN_NOT_ISSUED / CN_NOT_AR_LINKED / CN_OVER_APPLIED guards) — and applied credit notes + on-account cash flow through the customer statement, aging (on_account/net_total) and the collections worklist. A deterministic auto-suggest (exact single-invoice match, else oldest-due-first) feeds the worksheet.
controls.code_reference: finance/ar-cash-application.service.ts (createCashApplication guards + threshold park; applyOnAccount; approveBatch/rejectBatch SoD; reverseApplication reason-required audit; openItems worksheet feed; suggest deterministic FIFO); finance.controller.ts (/api/finance/ar/cash-application[|/suggest|/:batchNo/approve|reject|/:applicationNo/reverse], ar/open-items, ar/apply-on-account — gated ar / approvals+gl_close); schema/finance.ts ar_receipt_applications + ar_receipts.unapplied_amount + migration 0295; COA/CF_CLASSIFY 2220; finance.service.ts arAging on_account + customerStatement credit-note lines + pendingApprovals REV-21 source; collections worklist on_account; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the worksheet guards (receipt cap, per-invoice open-balance cap incl. pending, customer match), the on-account tracking (receipt unapplied ↔ GL 2220), the threshold park + approver≠poster check, and the reversal audit (reason, who, when).
controls.toe: Re-perform: apply one receipt across two invoices with an on-account remainder → invoices move, 2220 carries the remainder, TB balanced; over-apply → OVER_APPLIED; another customer's invoice → CUSTOMER_MISMATCH; Σ lines > receipt → APPLY_EXCEEDS_RECEIPT; apply-on-account later clears 2220 into 1100; a batch ≥ THB 100k parks (invoices unmoved) — self-approve → SOD_VIOLATION, a different user approves → invoices + GL move; reverse with a reason → invoice reopens + cash returns on-account, audit row carries reason/who (re-performed by the basics harness).
controls.evidence: Cash-application register; on-account (2220) tie-out; SoD + reversal audit tests

controls.risk: New leads and open deals are not followed up on time — an inbound lead sits untouched past the response SLA (and goes cold / to a competitor), a deal rots with no activity, or an overdue follow-up task is forgotten — so the pipeline forecast is unreliable and revenue is lost to neglect rather than to a real loss reason; and lead prioritisation is ad-hoc (no consistent, explainable scoring).
controls.description: CRM sales-automation discipline (CRM-4) on the unified pipeline spine (migration 0307): every lead is scored by an EXPLAINABLE, versioned rules formula (source / size / contactability / engagement-recency → grade A–D; coefficients are code-reviewed in LEAD_SCORE_COEFFS, each score stamped LEAD_SCORE_VERSION with a persisted per-factor breakdown) so prioritisation is consistent and auditable. A tenant-configurable follow-up policy (crm_followup_settings: sla_hours, rotting_days, round-robin owners — change gated crm/exec) drives a DETECTIVE follow-up center (GET /api/crm/pipeline/follow-up) that surfaces, severity-ranked: NEW leads untouched (no activity logged) past the SLA, open follow-up tasks past their due date, and OPEN deals idle for rotting_days — the control: a lead must be touched within the SLA. New leads are auto-assigned round-robin across the configured owners. A schedulable daily digest (BI report type crm_followup_digest) re-runs the center, fires lead.stagnant into the automation engine and drops a notification on the alerts rail. The pipeline emits lead.created / opp.stage_changed / deal.won / deal.lost into the SAME no-code automation-rules engine (reusable by existing rules), and a rep can send email/LINE from the deal timeline (MessagingService + {{merge-field}} substitution) logged as a timeline activity. Read-only detective aggregation — posts nothing to the GL, no new SoD conflict.
controls.code_reference: crm-pipeline.service.ts (scoreLead/computeLeadScore + LEAD_SCORE_COEFFS/VERSION; followUpCenter SLA/rotting/overdue detective aggregation; getFollowupSettings/putFollowupSettings; nextRoundRobinOwner/assignLead; runFollowUpSweep digest; sendComms merge-field email/LINE; emitEvent lead.created/opp.stage_changed/deal.won/deal.lost); crm-pipeline.controller.ts (leads/:no/score, follow-up[/settings|/run], leads/:no/assign, opportunities/:no/comms, comms/merge-fields); automation.service.ts (CRM-4 event catalog); schema/crm-pipeline.ts crm_lead_scores/crm_followup_settings + migration 0307; bi report crm_followup_digest; cutover/pipeline.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the explainable lead-score formula (coefficients + version + breakdown), the follow-up center's SLA-breach / overdue-task / rotting-deal detection against the tenant policy, the round-robin assignment, and the pipeline events reaching the automation engine.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a referral lead with company+email+phone scores grade A with a versioned breakdown; a cold contactless lead grades D; an aged untouched 'new' lead surfaces as an SLA breach and logging an activity clears it; new leads auto-assign round-robin across owners; the daily digest sweep reports breaches and fires lead.stagnant; closing a deal Won fires deal.won into an automation rule; sending comms from a deal resolves {{merge fields}} and logs a timeline activity (re-performed by the pipeline harness).
controls.evidence: Lead-score register + follow-up SLA/rotting worklist + automation-event executions + comms activity log

controls.risk: A trading partner is BOTH a customer (open AR) and a vendor (open AP), yet the two are settled in full both ways — cash paid out to the vendor while the same counterparty's receivable is chased — needlessly inflating gross AR and AP (mis-stating working capital and liquidity), tying up cash, and creating the collection/payment risk the offset would eliminate; or an offset is booked by one person with no independent review (a fictitious/over-stated netting could conceal an uncollectible receivable or an unauthorised write-down of a payable).
controls.description: AR/AP netting & contra settlement (docs/41 FIN-8): for a counterparty that is both a customer and a vendor, an open AR balance is offset against an open AP balance with a SINGLE contra JE (Dr 2000 AP / Cr 1100 AR) that clears both sub-ledgers up to the netted amount, leaving the residual open. Fail-closed + maker-checked: a per-counterparty NETTING AGREEMENT must exist and be enabled (NETTING_NOT_AGREED / NETTING_DISABLED — the agreement is the counterparty↔vendor mapping authorising the offset), a per-counterparty THRESHOLD caps any one settlement's net (NETTING_EXCEEDS_THRESHOLD), a mandatory reason is required (REASON_REQUIRED), and nothing-to-net is rejected (NOTHING_TO_NET, needs BOTH open AR and open AP). The settlement is PROPOSED PendingApproval (no GL, no sub-ledger movement) and applied only when a DIFFERENT user approves (POST …/netting/settlements/:no/approve; proposer self-approval → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin; pending settlements surface in the GOV-01 monitor). At approval the net = min(open AR, open AP) [capped by the threshold / requested amount] is recomputed from CURRENT balances, allocated oldest-due-first across the customer's invoices and our bills (both absorb exactly the net, so the contra JE balances and the AR/AP controls stay tied to GL), and the netting statement (header + AR/AP offset lines) records exactly what was offset. Reject leaves everything untouched.
controls.code_reference: finance/arap-netting.service.ts (upsertAgreement counterparty mapping + enable/threshold; preview open AR/AP + proposed net; propose NETTING_NOT_AGREED/NETTING_DISABLED/NETTING_EXCEEDS_THRESHOLD/REASON_REQUIRED/NOTHING_TO_NET, PendingApproval no-GL park; approve SoD + recompute + contra JE Dr2000/Cr1100 + oldest-first AR/AP relief; getSettlement netting statement); finance.controller.ts (/api/finance/netting/agreements[|preview|/settlements[|/:no[|/approve|/reject]]] — gated creditors/exec / approvals+gl_close); schema/finance.ts netting_agreements/netting_settlements/netting_settlement_lines + migration 0309; finance.service.ts pendingApprovals REV-23 (ar_ap_netting) source; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the counterparty agreement gate (must exist + enabled), the per-counterparty threshold cap, the mandatory reason, the PendingApproval park (no GL until approval), the approver≠proposer check, and the recompute + oldest-first allocation that keeps the contra JE balanced and both sub-ledgers tied to GL.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed a counterparty with open AR 1300 and open AP 900; preview shows proposed net 900 (residual AR 400 / AP 0); propose without a reason → REASON_REQUIRED; propose parks PendingApproval (net 900, no GL / sub-ledger movement) and surfaces in the GOV-01 monitor; self-approve → SOD_VIOLATION; a different user approves → contra JE Dr 2000 +900 / Cr 1100 −900, both AP bills Paid, the AR residual (INV-NET2, 400) stays open, and the netting statement lists the offset (AR 800+100, AP 300+600); propose without an agreement → NETTING_NOT_AGREED, against a disabled agreement → NETTING_DISABLED, above the threshold → NETTING_EXCEEDS_THRESHOLD, with no open AP → NOTHING_TO_NET (re-performed by the basics harness).
controls.evidence: Netting-settlement register + netting statement; contra-JE (2000/1100) tie-out; SoD + agreement/threshold guards

controls.fsli: Contract Asset / Unbilled Receivable (1265); Contract Liability / Deferred Revenue (2410); Accounts Receivable (1100); Recognized Revenue (4300)
controls.risk: Revenue recognized on a contract is NOT distinguished from what has been billed — the engine can only ever carry a contract liability (billing the whole contract price up-front on activation), so revenue earned AHEAD of invoicing (an unbilled receivable / contract asset) is invisible, the balance-sheet split of contract asset vs contract liability is misstated, and when an invoice is finally raised the earned unbilled receivable is not reclassified to trade AR — breaching TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §105-107 (contract-asset / contract-liability presentation) and mis-stating both current assets and deferred-revenue liabilities.
controls.description: Contract-asset / contract-liability split with an INDEPENDENT billing schedule decoupled from recognition (Track D Wave 1, TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §105-107). Billing is separated from the REV-19 recognition engine: revenue is recognized as performance obligations are satisfied while invoices are raised on their own milestone/period schedule. (1) The REV-19 activation is made OPTIONAL — bill_upfront (default TRUE) preserves today's Dr 1100 AR / Cr 2410 up-front billing (back-compat); set FALSE to decouple. (2) recognize() is now asset-aware: it first RELEASES any contract liability already billed in advance (Dr 2410) and the surplus recognized ahead of billing builds a CONTRACT ASSET / unbilled receivable (Dr 1265 / Cr 4300) — for an up-front-billed contract the liability always covers the amount so it reduces to the legacy Dr 2410 / Cr 4300. (3) A MAKER defines the billing milestones (POST …/billing-schedule; Σ planned ≤ contract price → SCHEDULE_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT). (4) A DIFFERENT user bills a milestone (POST …/bill) — the invoice RECLASSES the earned contract asset 1265 → 1100 AR and parks any billing-in-excess as a contract liability 2410; the biller may NOT be the milestone's maker (SOD_SELF_BILLING) and cumulative billing may never exceed the contract price (BILL_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT). (5) A detective /position tie-out reconciles cumulative recognized vs billed to the derived contract_asset = max(0, Σrecognized − Σbilled) / contract_liability = max(0, Σbilled − Σrecognized). All posting routes through LedgerService.postEntry so the period lock (PERIOD_LOCKED, GL-15/16) + GL-17 audit bind; tenant-scoped (RLS). No new COA (1100/1265/2410/4300 already exist + CF-classified operating).
controls.code_reference: revrec-billing/rev-billing.service.ts (defineBillingSchedule maker + SCHEDULE_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT; bill checker reclass Dr1100/Cr1265 + Cr2410 excess, SOD_SELF_BILLING + BILL_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT; position tie-out); rev-billing.controller.ts (/api/revenue/contracts/:id/billing-schedule POST|GET, :id/bill POST, :id/position GET — gated exec/ar/fin_report); revenue/revrec.service.ts (activate bill_upfront optional + billed_amount; recognize asset-aware Dr2410 then Dr1265 / Cr4300); schema/revrec-billing.ts rev_billing_schedules + rev_contracts.billed_amount + migration 0358 (0232-form RLS, leading (tenant_id, contract_id) index); COA 1100/1265/2410/4300; cutover/revrec-billing.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the recognize asset-aware split (release 2410 first, build 1265 for the surplus), the billing-schedule maker + Σ-planned ≤ contract-price guard, the bill-side reclass (1265→1100, excess→2410) with the maker≠biller SoD and cumulative-billing cap, and the /position tie-out.
controls.toe: Re-perform (revrec-billing harness): activate a 1200 contract bill_upfront=false → billed 0; recognize 3 months (300) → contract asset 1265 rises 300, no 2410 moved; /position shows recognized 300 / billed 0 / asset 300; define a billing schedule (maker) → the maker billing it → 403 SOD_SELF_BILLING; a different user bills 200 → 1265→1100 reclass (asset 100 left); bill 400 → 100 clears the asset and 300 parks contract liability 2410; /position ties recognized 300 / billed 600 / liability 300 / asset 0; a milestone beyond the contract price → SCHEDULE_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT; a contract activated up-front (bill_upfront default) still releases 2410 on recognition (REV-19 back-compat); a second tenant sees 0 of the first tenant's contract/schedule (RLS).
controls.evidence: Contract-asset / contract-liability roll-forward; billing-schedule maker-checker log; 1265↔1100 reclass tie-out; /position report

controls.fsli: Recognized Revenue (4300); Contract Liability / Deferred Revenue (2410); Contract Asset / Unbilled Receivable (1265)
controls.risk: A contract with VARIABLE consideration (rebates, refunds, performance bonuses/penalties, price concessions, usage/volume tiers) is recognized at a fixed price only — the variable amount is either ignored or booked in full with NO constraint, so revenue is OVER-stated by an amount that is not highly probable to survive (a subsequent reversal restates prior revenue), the estimate is never revisited as facts change, and a change in the estimate does not TRUE-UP the revenue already recognized on satisfied obligations — breaching TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §50-59 (estimating variable consideration by expected value or most-likely amount, constraining it to the amount highly probable not to reverse, re-estimating each period and catching up prior revenue). A single person could also set AND bless the estimate (manage earnings via an aggressive, unreviewed judgement).
controls.description: Variable-consideration estimate + the constraint, as a maker-checker management judgement (Track D Wave 2, TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §50-59). Extends the REV-19 fixed-price engine (does not rebuild it). (1) A MAKER records an estimate (POST …/variable-consideration) computed by EXPECTED VALUE (Σ probability × amount; probabilities must sum to 1 → INVALID_PROBABILITIES) OR MOST-LIKELY amount (§53). (2) THE CONSTRAINT (§56-58) is the reviewed control: only the CONSTRAINED amount (highly probable not to reverse) — never the gross estimate — drives the recognizable transaction price, and a constrained amount ABOVE the estimate is rejected as over-recognition (CONSTRAINT_EXCEEDS_ESTIMATE). (3) The estimate is a JUDGEMENT, so approval is MANDATORY before it drives revenue: a DIFFERENT user must approve it (POST …/variable-consideration/:vcId/approve; the estimator self-approving → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) and only an Approved estimate can be applied. (4) RE-ESTIMATE each period (POST …/reestimate) recomputes the transaction price (fixed base + constrained variable), re-allocates by SSP, rebuilds only the UNRECOGNIZED schedule, and TRUES-UP already-recognized revenue via a cumulative catch-up (§88) — building a contract asset (Dr 1265) when recognized ahead of billing, or releasing the contract liability (Dr 2410) when billed up-front — and is idempotent (a re-run with no new approved estimate posts nothing; also guarded by LedgerService.alreadyPosted). All posting routes through LedgerService.postEntry so the period lock (PERIOD_LOCKED, GL-15/16) + GL-17 audit bind; tenant-scoped (RLS). No new COA (2410/1265/4300 already exist + CF-classified).
controls.code_reference: revrec-variable/rev-variable.service.ts (recordEstimate expected_value/most_likely + CONSTRAINT_EXCEEDS_ESTIMATE / INVALID_PROBABILITIES; approveEstimate SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; reestimate recompute price + re-allocateBySSP + buildSchedule + cumulative catch-up Dr2410/Dr1265 / Cr4300, idempotent); rev-variable.controller.ts (/api/revenue/contracts/:id/variable-consideration POST|GET, :id/variable-consideration/:vcId/approve POST, :id/reestimate POST — gated exec/ar/fin_report); reuses revenue/revrec.service.ts (allocateBySSP/buildSchedule/sumRecognized); schema/revrec-variable.ts rev_variable_estimates + migration 0359 (0232-form RLS, leading (tenant_id, contract_id) index); COA 2410/1265/4300; cutover/revrec-variable.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the two estimation methods (expected value vs most-likely) and the probability-sum check, the constraint gate that caps the recognizable amount at the estimate (CONSTRAINT_EXCEEDS_ESTIMATE), the estimator≠approver maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) with approval mandatory before recognition, and the re-estimate cumulative catch-up (price recompute → re-allocate → rebuild unrecognized → true-up already-recognized revenue) and its idempotency.
controls.toe: Re-perform (revrec-variable harness): recognize 400 of a 1000 fixed-price contract; record an expected-value estimate {200@50%,0@50%}=100 constrained to 80; reestimate before approval is a no-op (approval mandatory); the estimator self-approving → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a different user approves; reestimate → price 1080 (constrained 80, not gross 100), catch-up 32 builds contract asset 1265 and lifts 4300; a second reestimate posts nothing (idempotent); most-likely {100@30%,300@70%}=300 with a constrained amount above the estimate → CONSTRAINT_EXCEEDS_ESTIMATE; on an up-front-billed contract the catch-up releases 2410 instead of building 1265; a second tenant sees 0 of the first tenant's estimates (RLS).
controls.evidence: Variable-consideration estimate register (expected-value/most-likely + constrained amount); estimate maker-checker approval log; re-estimate true-up (catch-up delta) posting; period re-estimate history

controls.fsli: Recognized Revenue (4300); Contract Liability / Deferred Revenue (2410); Contract Asset / Unbilled Receivable (1265)
controls.risk: A contract MODIFICATION (added/changed goods or services, or a price change) is MIS-CLASSIFIED or applied on an ad-hoc basis — the three TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §18-21 outcomes are conflated, so revenue is mis-stated: added distinct goods at their standalone selling price are folded into the existing contract (understating a genuinely separate contract) instead of a NEW contract (§20); a distinct-but-not-at-SSP modification is either ignored or catch-up-adjusted (§21a is PROSPECTIVE — re-allocate the remaining transaction price over the remaining obligations, NO catch-up — so a wrongful catch-up over/under-states prior revenue); or a NOT-distinct modification (§21b) is treated prospectively and the REQUIRED cumulative catch-up on the already-satisfied portion is NEVER booked (revenue mis-stated in the period of change). A wrong 'separate_contract' call in particular HIDES a required catch-up. A single person could also set AND bless the classification (an aggressive, unreviewed judgement manages earnings).
controls.description: Contract-modification classification & re-allocation as a maker-checker management judgement (Track D Wave 3, TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §18-21). Extends the REV-19 fixed-price engine (does not rebuild it; reuses createContract/allocateBySSP/buildSchedule/sumRecognized). A MAKER records a modification (POST …/modify) with the added/changed obligations, the added price, and the management judgements distinct_flag + at_ssp_flag; the service CLASSIFIES it into exactly one of three and this classification IS the control: (1) separate_contract (§20 — distinct AND at SSP) → a NEW independent contract is created and the ORIGINAL is left untouched (no re-allocation, no catch-up); (2) prospective (§21a — distinct but NOT at SSP) → the remaining (unrecognized) transaction price + the added price is RE-ALLOCATED over the remaining performance obligations by relative SSP with NO catch-up (already-recognized revenue is frozen); (3) cumulative_catchup (§21b — NOT distinct) → the transaction price is recomputed, re-allocated by SSP, and a cumulative CATCH-UP is posted on the already-satisfied portion (Dr 2410/1265 ↔ Cr 4300, same asset-aware split as recognition; a downward modification reverses). The modification is a JUDGEMENT, so approval is MANDATORY before it drives revenue: a Pending modification changes nothing, and a DIFFERENT user must approve it (POST …/modifications/:modId/approve; the maker self-approving → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; re-approving an applied modification → MODIFICATION_NOT_PENDING). Approval APPLIES the classified effect; the GL post is guarded by LedgerService.alreadyPosted (idempotent) so the period lock (PERIOD_LOCKED, GL-15/16) + GL-17 audit bind; tenant-scoped (RLS). No new COA (2410/1265/4300 already exist + CF-classified).
controls.code_reference: revrec-modifications/rev-modification.service.ts (modify classifies §18-21 from distinct_flag/at_ssp_flag → separate_contract | prospective | cumulative_catchup, records Pending with a preview effect, drives nothing; approve SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + MODIFICATION_NOT_PENDING then applies: applySeparate creates a new linked contract via createContract/allocateBySSP/buildSchedule, applyProspective inserts distinct POs + re-allocates the remaining consideration over remaining POs by SSP + rebuilds the unrecognized schedule with no catch-up, applyCumulative re-allocateBySSP + scales recognized rows + buildSchedule + posts the catch-up Dr2410/Dr1265 / Cr4300); rev-modification.controller.ts (/api/revenue/contracts/:id/modify POST, :id/modifications/:modId/approve POST, :id/modifications GET — gated exec/ar/fin_report); reuses revenue/revrec.service.ts (createContract/allocateBySSP/buildSchedule/sumRecognized); schema/revrec-modifications.ts rev_contract_modifications + migration 0360 (0232-form RLS, leading (tenant_id, contract_id) index); COA 2410/1265/4300; cutover/revrec-modifications.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the §18-21 classification (distinct×at-SSP → the three outcomes), that separate_contract leaves the original untouched and creates a new contract, that prospective re-allocates the remaining price over remaining POs with no catch-up, that cumulative_catchup posts a catch-up on the satisfied portion, and the maker≠approver maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) with approval mandatory before it drives revenue.
controls.toe: Re-perform (revrec-modifications harness): recognize 400 of a 1000 contract; a NOT-distinct +200 modification classifies cumulative_catchup and (after a distinct approver — self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) posts an 80 catch-up (Dr 1265 / Cr 4300) lifting recognized revenue to 480; a distinct-not-at-SSP +300 modification classifies prospective, re-allocates the remaining 900 over the remaining POs (Support 600→540, Add-on 360, satisfied Setup frozen at 400) with catch_up_delta 0 and no revenue movement; a distinct-at-SSP +500 modification classifies separate_contract, creating a new linked contract (total 500, PO 500) while the original stays total 1000 / recognized 400; a Pending modification drives nothing and re-approving an applied one → MODIFICATION_NOT_PENDING; a second tenant sees 0 of the first tenant's modifications (RLS).
controls.evidence: Contract-modification register (type/added_price/distinct_flag/at_ssp_flag/effect_amount + new_contract_id link); modification maker-checker approval log (created_by/approved_by); cumulative catch-up (REVREC-MOD) posting; re-allocation schedule rebuild

controls.fsli: Contract Liability / Deferred Revenue (2410); Contract Asset / Unbilled Receivable (1265); Significant Financing Component Interest Income (4650); Interest Expense (5900); Recognized Revenue (4300)
controls.risk: A contract whose payment timing gives a MATERIAL financing benefit is recognized at the undiscounted (face) price — the significant financing component is ignored, so revenue is mis-stated (over-stated when the customer prepays, understated when payment is deferred) and the interest element is never separated from the revenue element, breaching TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §60-65 (adjust the promised consideration to its cash-selling-price present value and recognize the financing component as interest over the contract). Separately, the §120 DISCLOSURES are incomplete or do not tie to the ledger: the contract-liability rollforward (opening → billings → recognized → closing) does not reconcile to the GL contract-liability (2410) / contract-asset (1265) control accounts, and the remaining-performance-obligation (RPO / backlog) is not disclosed — so a reader cannot see how much revenue is deferred, how it moved, or how much is still to be delivered. The DISCOUNT RATE is a management judgement a single person could set AND bless (an aggressive/omitted rate manages the revenue↔interest split).
controls.description: Significant-financing-component rate as a maker-checker judgement + a GL-reconciled revenue-disclosure pack (Track D Wave 4, TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 §60-65 + §120). Extends the REV-19 recognition engine + Wave 1 billing (does not rebuild them; reuses the contract-asset 1265 / contract-liability 2410 balances). (A) Financing component (§60-65): a MAKER flags a material financing component and sets the discount rate (POST …/financing-component); the service discounts the transaction price to its cash-selling-price PRESENT VALUE and schedules the interest UNWIND by the effective-interest method (the same EIR primitive the lease engine uses, LSE-01) so Σ interest == face − PV exactly. The rows land Pending and DRIVE NOTHING until a DIFFERENT user approves the rate (POST …/financing-component/approve; the maker self-approving → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL); run-financing on an un-approved component → FINANCING_NOT_APPROVED. Only an APPROVED component posts (POST …/run-financing, idempotent via LedgerService.alreadyPosted): advance (customer prepays — the entity BORROWS from the customer) Dr 5900 / Cr 2410 (interest expense; the contract liability accretes from PV toward face); arrears (deferred payment — the entity LENDS to the customer) Dr 1265 / Cr 4650 (interest income; the contract asset accretes from PV toward face). (B) Disclosure pack (§120): the contract-liability rollforward (GET …/disclosure/contract-liability-rollforward) reconstructs opening → additions (billings) → recognized → closing over 2410/1265 DIRECTLY from the GL journal lines so it RECONCILES to GL by construction (opening + additions − reductions = closing = GL); the RPO / backlog (GET …/disclosure/rpo) = Σ unrecognized allocated price, banded by expected timing (≤12m / >12m). All GL routes through LedgerService.postEntry so the period lock (PERIOD_LOCKED, GL-15/16) + GL-17 audit bind; tenant-scoped (RLS). New COA 4650 (added to the canonical COA + CF_CLASSIFY operating); the advance case reuses 5900.
controls.frequency: Per financing component / per period disclosure
controls.code_reference: revrec-disclosure/rev-financing.service.ts (setFinancingComponent discounts to PV + builds the EIR unwind schedule so Σ interest = face − PV, records Pending; approveFinancingComponent SOD_SELF_APPROVAL flips Pending→Approved; runFinancing posts advance Dr5900/Cr2410 or arrears Dr1265/Cr4650, FINANCING_NOT_APPROVED guard, idempotent) + rev-disclosure.service.ts (contractLiabilityRollforward decomposes the GL 2410/1265 movements by source and reconciles closing to the GL balance; rpo sums unrecognized revrec_schedules planned amounts with a 12-month timing band); rev-disclosure.controller.ts (/api/revenue/contracts/:id/financing-component POST + /approve POST + GET, :id/run-financing POST, /api/revenue/disclosure/contract-liability-rollforward GET, /disclosure/rpo GET — gated exec/ar/fin_report); schema/revrec-disclosure.ts rev_financing_schedules + migration 0367 (0232-form RLS, leading (tenant_id, contract_id) index); ledger-constants.ts COA 4650 + CF_CLASSIFY; posting-events.ts REVFIN.INCOME/REVFIN.EXPENSE; bi report types contract_liability_rollforward + rpo_backlog; cutover/revrec-disclosure.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect that a material financing component discounts the transaction price to PV and unwinds the interest so Σ interest equals face − PV, that the discount-rate judgement is maker-checker (maker ≠ approver, SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) with an un-approved component unable to post (FINANCING_NOT_APPROVED), that advance (customer prepays) posts interest expense to 5900 with the contract liability 2410 accreting and arrears (deferred payment) posts interest income to 4650 with the contract asset 1265 accreting, and that the §120 contract-liability rollforward reconciles to the GL 2410/1265 control accounts and the RPO equals the unrecognized allocated price.
controls.toe: Re-perform (revrec-disclosure harness): a 1200 face at 12%/yr over 12 months discounts to PV 1064.94 and Σ interest 135.06 = face − PV, last closing balance = 1200; the maker self-approving → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL and run-before-approval → FINANCING_NOT_APPROVED; a different checker approves and run-financing posts 12 periods of interest EXPENSE to 5900 with the contract liability 2410 accreting toward face (idempotent re-run posts 0); an arrears component accretes the contract asset 1265 against interest income 4650; the contract-liability rollforward reads opening 0 + billings 300 − recognized 100 = closing 200 = GL 2410; the RPO = 900 unrecognized allocated price banded within 12 months; a second tenant sees 0 of the first tenant's financing/RPO (RLS).
controls.evidence: Financing-component schedule (PV/face/interest + EIR unwind) with the discount-rate maker-checker approval log (created_by/approved_by); REVFIN interest postings (4650/5900); §120 contract-liability rollforward reconciled to GL; RPO / backlog by contract with timing band

controls.risk: B2B account relationships are ungoverned — a company's parent/subsidiary structure is not modelled (so pipeline cannot be rolled up to the buying group, and re-parented accounts can loop), a deal is worked without documenting WHO decides (no buying committee, so the forecast rests on a single unverified contact), and there is no account plan (owner, target, product-line coverage) — so strategic accounts are pursued ad-hoc, whitespace (un-targeted product lines) is invisible, and the account-based revenue forecast is unreliable.
controls.description: B2B account/contact 360 DEPTH (CRM-7, migration 0365) on the REV-17 CRM spine. Account HIERARCHY: crm_accounts gains a self-referential parent_account_id; PATCH /api/crm/accounts/:no/parent rejects a self-parent (SELF_PARENT) and any link that would close a loop (HIERARCHY_CYCLE), and GET /api/crm/accounts/:no/hierarchy returns the ancestor chain + direct children + a SUBTREE pipeline roll-up (Σ open weighted amount across the account and all descendants) so pipeline ties to the buying group; a survivor-merge re-parents the duplicate's child accounts so the hierarchy stays intact. Buying COMMITTEE: crm_opportunity_contacts records which contacts sit on a deal, each with a role (decision_maker/champion/influencer/evaluator/blocker/user) + influence weight — a committee contact must belong to the deal's account (CONTACT_ACCOUNT_MISMATCH), a contact appears at most once per deal (COMMITTEE_DUP), and at most one is_primary. Account PLANS: crm_account_plans is a GOVERNED draft -> active -> closed plan carrying an owner, objective, strategy, target revenue and target product categories validated against item_categories (UNKNOWN_CATEGORY); a plan activates only when it has an owner AND an objective (PLAN_INCOMPLETE), the lifecycle is enforced (PLAN_NOT_DRAFT / PLAN_NOT_ACTIVE, a closed plan is immutable PLAN_CLOSED), and GET /api/crm/accounts/:no/whitespace surfaces the product categories the account is NOT yet being pursued for (active item_categories minus the union of active-plan targets). All three tables tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy, tenant-leading indexes); the surface gates crm/exec/ar. No GL post.
controls.code_reference: modules/crm/account-depth/crm-account-depth.module.ts (setParent SELF_PARENT/HIERARCHY_CYCLE cycle guard; hierarchy ancestors+children+subtree open-weighted roll-up; addCommitteeMember CONTACT_ACCOUNT_MISMATCH/COMMITTEE_DUP + single is_primary; listCommittee/removeCommitteeMember; createPlan APL- + target-category validation UNKNOWN_CATEGORY; updatePlan PLAN_CLOSED guard; activatePlan PLAN_NOT_DRAFT/PLAN_INCOMPLETE; closePlan PLAN_NOT_ACTIVE; whitespace active-plan targets vs item_categories); crm/accounts/crm-accounts.module.ts (merge re-parents child accounts); schema/crm-pipeline.ts crm_accounts.parent_account_id + crm_opportunity_contacts + crm_account_plans + migration 0365 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes); cutover/pipeline.ts (ToE, CRM-7 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the account-hierarchy cycle guard + subtree pipeline roll-up, the buying-committee account-membership + uniqueness + single-primary rules, the account-plan draft->active->closed lifecycle + category validation, and the whitespace derivation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: link a subsidiary under a parent, then parenting the parent under its own child -> HIERARCHY_CYCLE; the hierarchy read rolls a child's open deal into the parent's subtree weighted pipeline; add a buying-committee member (is_primary) then re-add the same contact -> COMMITTEE_DUP and a contact from another account -> CONTACT_ACCOUNT_MISMATCH; create an account plan (APL-) with a valid target category, an unknown category -> UNKNOWN_CATEGORY, activate a complete draft -> active, re-activate -> PLAN_NOT_DRAFT, activate an incomplete draft -> PLAN_INCOMPLETE, close -> closed; whitespace reports the targeted category as covered and the rest as whitespace; a T1 user sees none of HQ's account plans (RLS) (re-performed by the pipeline harness CRM-7 checks).
controls.evidence: Account hierarchy + subtree pipeline roll-up; buying-committee register (role/influence/primary); governed account-plan lifecycle + whitespace coverage read

controls.risk: A strategic B2B account slips toward CHURN with no early warning — declining engagement (deals go quiet, no activity logged), open support cases pile up (escalated / SLA-breached), the pipeline dries up — and no one notices until the account has effectively lapsed; and renewal/expansion revenue is not tracked as its own forward pipeline, so a due renewal is simply missed (a won account with no renewal in flight silently drops off). The account-based retention forecast is unreliable and revenue is lost to neglect rather than to a competitor on the merits.
controls.description: B2B account HEALTH / churn surveillance + renewal-expansion pipeline (CRM-15, migration 0370) — a read-only DETECTIVE layer on the REV-17 CRM spine. A per-account health SCORE is computed by an EXPLAINABLE, code-reviewed weighted formula (base 60, clamped 0..100, with a persisted per-factor breakdown — same posture as the CRM-4 lead score, no trained model) from ACCOUNT-SPECIFIC signals: engagement recency (days since the last activity logged across the account's deals), open pipeline (Σ open weighted amount), open / escalated (P1-P2) / SLA-breached support cases (service_cases, SVC-4/5, grouped by account), and win/loss balance; the score is BANDED healthy (≥70) / watch (40-69) / at_risk (<40) and GET /api/crm/account-health returns the CHURN WATCHLIST ranked worst-first with band counts (filterable by band). RENEWAL/EXPANSION opportunities are tagged crm_opportunities.deal_type (new|renewal|expansion; PATCH /api/crm/opportunities/:no/deal-type) and GET /api/crm/account-health/renewals is the forward pipeline (Σ weighted) PLUS the renewal GAP queue — accounts with a won deal but NO open renewal (the churn-risk population). A schedulable daily SNAPSHOT (POST /api/crm/account-health/snapshot / BI report crm_account_health) persists the score to crm_account_health_snapshots (upsert per account/day, mirrors project_health_snapshots) for trend (GET …/health/history). The company AR/credit position (creditStatus) is surfaced as CONTEXT (tenant-level, company_level=true) but deliberately NOT folded into the per-account score. Tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy, tenant-leading indexes); the surface gates crm/exec/ar (snapshot crm/exec). No GL post.
controls.owner: Sales Manager / Account Manager / Customer Success
controls.code_reference: modules/crm/account-health/crm-account-health.module.ts (computeHealth explainable weighted score + band + breakdown; gatherSignals 4 grouped queries — open pipeline / last-activity join opps⋈activities / open-escalated-breached cases by account; accountHealth; portfolio churn watchlist ranked worst-first + band filter + counts; renewalPipeline forward pipeline + renewal-gap queue; setDealType new/renewal/expansion; captureAllHealth idempotent snapshot per account/day; healthHistory trend); schema/crm-pipeline.ts crm_opportunities.deal_type + crm_account_health_snapshots + migration 0370 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes); service_cases (tenant_id, account_id) index; bi report-registry crm_account_health + bi-generate crm_account_health branch (captureAllHealth action job); cutover/pipeline.ts (ToE, CRM-15 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the explainable per-account health formula (engagement / pipeline / support-case / win-loss factors + banding), the churn watchlist ranking, the renewal/expansion pipeline + renewal-gap detection, and the idempotent snapshot + trend.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an engaged account (recent activity + open deal) scores in the healthy band with an explainable breakdown; an account with open escalated + SLA-breached cases and no open pipeline scores in the at_risk band; tagging a deal renewal surfaces it in the renewal pipeline (weighted); a won account with no open renewal is flagged a renewal gap; the portfolio watchlist ranks at_risk first and filters by band; a snapshot captures a dated score per account (re-run same day is idempotent) and appears in the account's health history; the crm_account_health report type is registered; a second tenant cannot read the first tenant's account health (RLS) (re-performed by the pipeline harness CRM-15 checks).
controls.evidence: Per-account health score register (band + explainable breakdown) + churn watchlist; renewal/expansion forward pipeline + renewal-gap queue; daily health snapshot + trend

controls.risk: The sales forecast reported to management is UNGOVERNED — it rests on a single system-weighted number with no rep->manager accountability (a rep's own commit is never captured or reconciled, so over-optimism or sandbagging is invisible), it is never SNAPSHOTTED so forecast-vs-actual accuracy cannot be measured after the fact, and pipeline COVERAGE (open pipeline vs the commit target) is not tracked — so a thin, unbacked forecast or a quota with too little pipeline behind it goes unnoticed until the period closes short.
controls.description: Sales FORECASTING depth (CRM-12, migration 0378) — a governance layer over the REV-17 pipeline forecast. Rep->manager OVERRIDE roll-up: crm_forecast_submissions records per (period, owner) a rep's own commit / best-case / pipeline number under a governed draft->submitted status (POST /api/crm/forecast/submission, upsert per period/owner); GET /api/crm/forecast/depth is the MANAGER ROLL-UP — each rep's submitted forecast reconciled against the system-weighted forecast (commit at full value + best-case/pipeline risk-weighted) with the VARIANCE surfaced, plus totals, a category WATERFALL (commit -> best-case -> pipeline build-up of the forecast) and pipeline COVERAGE (open pipeline / the submitted-commit target, >=3x healthy). Dated SNAPSHOTS: a schedulable period snapshot (POST /api/crm/forecast/snapshot / BI report crm_forecast_snapshot) persists the forecast + the period's ACTUAL won to crm_forecast_snapshots (idempotent per period/day, mirrors crm_account_health_snapshots), so GET /api/crm/forecast/history is the forecast-vs-actual ACCURACY trend (actual / forecast per period). Both tables tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy, tenant-leading indexes); the surface gates crm/exec/ar (submission + snapshot crm/exec). No GL post.
controls.owner: Sales Manager / Sales Operations / VP Sales
controls.code_reference: modules/crm/forecast/crm-forecast.module.ts (systemByOwner weighted forecast by rep; depth manager roll-up — system vs submitted forecast + variance + totals; forecastOf commit-full + best-case/pipeline weighted; waterfall category build-up; coverage open-pipeline / submitted-or-system commit target; submit rep override upsert draft->submitted; listSubmissions; captureSnapshot idempotent per period/day + actual-won-in-period; accuracy/history forecast-vs-actual trend); schema/crm-pipeline.ts crm_forecast_submissions + crm_forecast_snapshots + migration 0378 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes); bi report-registry crm_forecast_snapshot + bi-generate crm_forecast_snapshot branch (captureSnapshot action job); cutover/pipeline.ts (ToE, CRM-12 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the rep->manager submission roll-up + system-vs-submitted variance, the pipeline-coverage ratio, the category waterfall, and the idempotent period snapshot + forecast-vs-actual accuracy trend.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a rep submits a period commit/best-case (draft->submitted) and it appears in the manager roll-up reconciled against the system-weighted forecast with the variance; the coverage ratio = open pipeline / the submitted-commit target; the waterfall's commit->best-case->pipeline stages build up to the forecast total; capturing a snapshot persists the forecast + the period's actual won (re-run same day is idempotent) and the history shows the forecast-vs-actual accuracy %; the crm_forecast_snapshot report type is registered; a second tenant cannot read the first tenant's submissions/snapshots (RLS) (re-performed by the pipeline harness CRM-12 checks).
controls.evidence: Rep->manager forecast-override roll-up + system-vs-submitted variance; pipeline-coverage + category waterfall; dated forecast snapshot + forecast-vs-actual accuracy trend

controls.risk: A sales rep builds a quote (CPQ) applying discounts with NO cost, NO margin and NO discount-approval workflow — so a quote can be priced below cost or over-discounted and then accepted straight to GL revenue (Dr 1100 / Cr 4000) with only a weak same-person check on accept. Margin leakage / selling below the authorised floor goes undetected until after the sale is booked.
controls.description: CPQ margin-floor & discount-approval maker-checker (SVC-1): each quote line carries a unit_cost, so on SEND the quote's effective discount% (gross list to net after discounts) and margin% (net vs cost) are computed and checked against a per-tenant FLOOR (cpq_settings: min_margin_pct default 20 / max_discount_pct default 15). A quote that breaches the floor (discount% > max OR margin% < min) is BLOCKED from send/accept: it parks in PendingApproval and opens a quote_approvals row (floor snapshot + breaching actuals) — it cannot reach Accepted (and cannot recognise revenue) until a DIFFERENT authorised user approves it (POST …/quotes/:id/approve; author self-approval -> 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, and the checker duty cpq_approve is segregated from the author duty cpq — SoD R20). Reject returns the quote to Draft for re-work (approver != author). A within-floor quote sends normally (unchanged path). The existing accept-side maker-checker (acceptor != author, SOD_VIOLATION -> GL) is retained.
controls.code_reference: cpq.service.ts (metricsFromLines discount%/margin%; getFloor per-tenant cpq_settings; sendQuote floor gate -> PendingApproval + quote_approvals; approveDiscount / rejectQuote SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; getSettings/updateSettings); cpq.controller.ts (/api/cpq/quotes/:id/[send|approve|reject], /api/cpq/settings, /api/cpq/approvals — gated exec/cpq / exec/cpq_approve); schema/cpq.ts quote_lines.unit_cost + quotes.discount_pct/margin_pct/requires_approval + cpq_settings + quote_approvals, migration 0328; permissions.ts cpq/cpq_approve + SoD R20; cutover/cpq-approval.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the per-line unit_cost + the send-time discount%/margin% computation, the per-tenant floor lookup, the PendingApproval park + quote_approvals audit row on breach, the block on accepting an un-approved quote, and the author!=approver self-approval guard on approve/reject.
controls.toe: Re-perform (cpq-approval harness): set floor 20% margin / 5% discount; an in-floor quote (margin 40%, discount 0) sends -> Sent; a thin-margin quote (margin 10%) -> PendingApproval and accept -> INVALID_TRANSITION; author self-approve -> SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a different user approves -> Sent, then accept -> Accepted + AR 50000 posted with an approved quote_approvals row; a 10%-discount quote -> PendingApproval, author self-reject -> SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, a different user rejects -> Draft; a second tenant sees 0 of the HQ quotes/approvals (RLS).
controls.evidence: Quote margin/discount register; quote_approvals maker-checker log; PendingApproval to approve/reject audit; SoD guard

controls.risk: A service contract silently lapses at end_date with no renewal action (recurring revenue lost, service continuity broken), OR it is renewed at an arbitrarily inflated price by the same person who proposes it — a renewal-price uplift beyond the authorised ceiling, or an auto-renew that raises price, is applied with no independent sign-off, so the contract's recurring value grows uncontrolled (revenue/AR mis-stated on an unapproved basis) and the renewal is both raised and approved by one hand.
controls.description: Service-contract renewal & expiry control (SVC-3, docs Track-2): a renewal PROPOSAL computes the successor value from the base × (1 + uplift_pct/100). A within-ceiling proposal (uplift ≤ the per-tenant threshold contract_renewal_settings.max_auto_uplift_pct, default 5%, and NOT an auto-renew that would raise price) auto-approves and creates the successor service_contracts row at once (linked via renewed_to_contract_id, old contract renewal_status='renewed'). A proposal that exceeds the ceiling, OR any auto-renew with a price rise, is parked `pending` and the successor is created ONLY when a DIFFERENT user approves (approved_by ≠ requested_by → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) — the proposer cannot approve their own uplift. Reject leaves the old contract untouched (renewal_status='declined'); an already-renewed contract cannot be re-renewed (CONTRACT_ALREADY_RENEWED) and only one renewal is in flight at a time (RENEWAL_IN_FLIGHT). Detective side: GET /api/service/contracts/expiring?days=N lists Active contracts nearing end_date with NO renewal in flight (renewal_status not pending/renewed) so an expiring contract is actioned before it lapses. The uplift ceiling is change-gated to exec (PUT /api/service/renewal-settings). Never touches the SLA-event / subscription-billing paths.
controls.code_reference: service/contract-renewal.service.ts proposeRenewal (new_value = base×(1+uplift); threshold gate; auto-approve+createSuccessor within ceiling; UPLIFT_INVALID/CONTRACT_ALREADY_RENEWED/RENEWAL_IN_FLIGHT) / approveRenewal (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, RENEWAL_NOT_PENDING, createSuccessor + old renewal_status='renewed') / rejectRenewal (renewal_status='declined') / expiring (near-end_date, no renewal in flight) / get|putSettings (max_auto_uplift_pct, exec); contract-renewal.controller.ts POST /api/service/contracts/:id/renew [+ /renewals/:id/approve|reject], GET /api/service/contracts/expiring, /api/service/renewals, GET|PUT /api/service/renewal-settings; schema/service.ts service_contracts renewal cols + contract_renewals + contract_renewal_settings (migration 0331); cutover/contract-renewal.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the uplift computation (new_value = base×(1+uplift)), the auto-approve-within-ceiling vs park-pending-over-ceiling gate, the maker-checker approve (approver ≠ proposer), the successor-contract creation + old-contract renewed link, and the expiring detective read (excludes renewed / in-flight).
controls.toe: Re-perform: a within-threshold (3%) renewal auto-approves and creates the successor at base×1.03; an over-threshold (20%) renewal parks pending with no successor; the proposer self-approving → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a distinct approver approves → successor contract created + old one renewal_status='renewed'; reject leaves the old contract (declined); an auto-renew with a small uplift is still gated; the expiring worklist lists a near-expiry contract with no renewal in flight and excludes renewed ones; a T1 tenant cannot see/approve an HQ renewal (RLS) (re-performed by the contract-renewal harness).
controls.evidence: Renewal register (pending/approved/rejected) + successor-contract link; expiring-contract worklist; SoD self-approval block

controls.risk: Free warranty service or replacement goods are granted without entitlement — a serialized unit whose warranty has EXPIRED (or a fault the coverage type does not cover) is repaired/replaced free of charge, or one person both requests and authorizes a zero-charge claim, so unauthorized free service/goods leak out (revenue/cost mis-stated, inventory consumed) with no independent check that the unit was actually in coverage.
controls.description: Warranty & entitlement registry with coverage-authorization maker-checker (SVC-2, migration 0329): a per-tenant warranty_terms catalogue (coverage_months + coverage_type parts/labor/full) and an installed_base serialized-unit registry (serial unique per tenant, tied to a customer + item + term) whose warranty_end is COMPUTED at registration (sold_date + coverage_months). A warranty_claims claim is COVERAGE-CHECKED at raise — in coverage = reported_date within [warranty_start, warranty_end] AND the unit's coverage_type covers the claim kind ('full' covers any; else the kinds must match. An in-coverage claim AUTO-AUTHORIZES free (charge 0, disposition repair) — it is contractually covered. An OUT-OF-COVERAGE claim (expired, or a kind the term does not cover) parks status='pending' and moves nothing: it can be actioned only by authorizeClaim/rejectClaim, which enforce the SVC-01 control — the authorizer/rejector MUST differ from the requester (authorized_by ≠ requested_by → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin), so no single person can grant themselves free service/goods on a non-covered unit; a non-pending claim re-decided → CLAIM_NOT_PENDING; a reject needs a reason. A distinct authorizer may set a real charge (paid repair) or deliberately authorize charge 0 (a free-service override). Two DETECTIVE reads: GET /api/service/warranty/expiring?days=N (active units whose warranty_end falls within N days — a renewal / proactive-service worklist) and GET /api/service/warranty/coverage-exceptions (the OVERRIDE REGISTER — claims authorized FREE that were actually out of coverage, exactly the population an auditor samples). Tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy); reads gate exec/marketing, catalogue/registry writes masterdata, claim raise exec, authorize/reject approvals. No GL post in v1 (a service-order cost posting is future work).
controls.code_reference: modules/service-warranty/service-warranty.service.ts (createTerm; registerUnit warranty_end = sold + coverage_months; createClaim coverage check → in-coverage auto-authorize free / out-of-coverage park pending; authorizeClaim SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + charge; rejectClaim reason-required + SoD; expiring; coverageExceptions override register); service-warranty.controller.ts (/api/service/warranty/terms|units[/:id]|claims[/:id/authorize|reject]|expiring|coverage-exceptions — gated exec/marketing read, masterdata write, exec raise, approvals authorize/reject); schema/service-warranty.ts warranty_terms/installed_base/warranty_claims + migration 0329 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes, serial/claim unique per tenant); cutover/warranty.ts (ToE, 20 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the computed warranty_end, the coverage check (window + kind), the auto-authorize-free vs park-pending split, the authorizer≠requester maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), and the expiring + coverage-exceptions detective reads.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a 12-month full term; register a unit sold today → warranty_end = sold + 12mo; an in-coverage claim auto-authorizes free (charge 0); a claim on an expired unit parks pending; a parts-only unit + a labor claim is out of coverage (kind mismatch); the requester self-authorizing → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a DISTINCT authorizer approves with a charge (paid) or free (override); the free out-of-coverage override appears in coverage-exceptions (the in-coverage + paid ones do not); expiring(30d) surfaces a soon-expiring unit; a distinct user rejects with a reason; RLS isolates a second tenant (re-performed by the warranty harness).
controls.evidence: Warranty-term catalogue + installed-base register (computed warranty_end); claim coverage snapshot; SOD_SELF_APPROVAL test; coverage-exceptions override register; expiring worklist

controls.fsli: Service revenue; Customer-service obligations / SLA response (no direct GL in v1)
controls.risk: A customer request or complaint that arrives by email is DROPPED or lost — no support case is created, so a service obligation (and any warranty / revenue / liability exposure behind it) goes untracked and unresolved; OR the inbound email webhook is unauthenticated, so a forged or replayed message injects or mutates cases; OR a case is advanced through an ad-hoc, ungoverned status lifecycle so its resolution/closure state and the record of customer communication are unreliable and incomplete.
controls.description: Service Cloud — Support Cases + Email-to-Case (SVC-4, migration 0350): a per-tenant service_cases register (case_no unique per tenant, a governed status lifecycle new → open → pending → resolved → closed with reopen → open, priority P1–P4, owner/assignee, optional CRM contact link) and an append-only case_email_messages trail (each inbound customer→us / outbound us→customer message, deduped per tenant on the provider Message-ID). The COMPLETENESS control is Email-to-Case: a @Public @NoTx webhook (POST /api/service/email-to-case/inbound/:tenantCode) receives the provider-parsed email and is AUTHENTICATED by the per-tenant email shared secret / HMAC over the raw body (verifyInboundWebhook — a configured HMAC secret REQUIRES a valid signature, an optional timestamp enforces a 300s freshness window against replay → WEBHOOK_STALE, a bad secret → BAD_INBOUND_SECRET, and in production an unconfigured secret is FAIL-CLOSED → INBOUND_UNVERIFIED); the tenant is resolved from the path code (UNKNOWN_TENANT). Every authenticated inbound is then matched — (1) a per-case thread token embedded in the reply (subject/body/In-Reply-To/References) threads onto the exact case; (2) else the sender address threads onto their most-recent OPEN case; (3) else a NEW case is OPENED — so no inbound customer email is ever dropped (completeness), and a reply onto a resolved/closed case REOPENS it. Redeliveries are idempotent (Message-ID dedupe). The lifecycle is GOVERNED: assign (→open), pending, resolve, close and reopen enforce valid transitions (CASE_NOT_ACTIVE / CASE_ALREADY_CLOSED / CASE_NOT_CLOSED / CASE_CLOSED), and an outbound reply embeds the case thread token so the customer's reply threads back. Tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy, tenant-leading indexes); the authenticated surface gates exec/marketing. No GL post in v1 (a service-order / billable-time posting is future work).
controls.code_reference: modules/service-cases/service-cases.service.ts (handleInbound: resolve tenant by code → assertSecret verifyInboundWebhook (HMAC/static/stale/unconfigured) → Message-ID dedupe → matchCase thread-token/contact-open-case → thread-or-create, reopen on reply; createCase case_no + thread token; assignCase/setPending/resolveCase/closeCase/reopenCase governed transitions; addReply outbound + caseThreadMark); service-cases.controller.ts (ServiceCasesController /api/service/cases[/:id[/assign|pending|resolve|close|reopen|reply]] gated exec/marketing; CaseEmailInboundController @Public @NoTx POST /api/service/email-to-case/inbound/:tenantCode); case-thread.ts (svct_ token mint/mark/parse); schema/service-cases.ts service_cases + case_email_messages + migration 0350 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes, case_no unique per tenant, Message-ID unique per tenant); cutover/service.ts (ToE, SVC-4 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the inbound-webhook authentication (HMAC-over-rawBody, timestamp freshness/replay, prod fail-closed), the tenant resolution from the path code, the three-way match (thread token → open-case-by-sender → new case) that guarantees no dropped email, the Message-ID idempotency, and the governed status transitions.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an inbound email with no matching case OPENS a new case (source=email, thread token minted); a reply carrying the case thread token threads onto the same case; a reply from the same sender with no token threads onto their open case; a redelivered Message-ID is ignored (idempotent); a reply onto a resolved case reopens it; an inbound with a bad secret → BAD_INBOUND_SECRET and a stale timestamp → WEBHOOK_STALE; the lifecycle transitions enforce (resolve a closed case → CASE_NOT_ACTIVE, reopen an active case → CASE_NOT_CLOSED); a second tenant cannot see the first tenant's cases (RLS) (re-performed by the service harness SVC-4 checks).
controls.evidence: Support-case register (governed lifecycle) + append-only inbound/outbound email trail; Email-to-Case webhook HMAC/tenant-resolution rejects; Message-ID idempotency; no-dropped-email completeness (new-case-on-unmatched)

controls.fsli: Service revenue; Customer-service SLA commitments / service obligations (no direct GL in v1)
controls.risk: A contracted service-level commitment on a support case silently lapses — a case has no computed first-response or resolution target, so the desk cannot tell whether a customer entitlement is being met, a breach goes unnoticed and unremediated (service liability / churn / SLA-credit exposure), and no worklist surfaces the cases that are past due, so the completeness of the SLA obligation over the case population is unmonitored.
controls.description: Service Cloud — per-case Entitlement / SLA tracking (SVC-5, migration 0352): the SVC-4 service_cases object is extended with an SLA ENTITLEMENT — a tier (Standard/Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum, mirroring the #666 contract SLA tiers) whose first-response + resolution targets (hours) are COMPUTED into first_response_due_at / resolution_due_at, anchored on the case open time, at creation and whenever the entitlement is (re)set (POST /api/service/cases/:id/entitlement — recomputes the due times off the original open time and re-evaluates the breach flags). The first outbound reply (addReply) is the FIRST RESPONSE: it stamps first_responded_at and sets response_breached = responded after first_response_due_at; resolveCase stamps resolution_breached = resolved after resolution_due_at. The DETECTIVE control is GET /api/service/cases/sla/breaches — the worklist of OPEN cases (new/open/pending) whose first-response (no response yet, past due) or resolution (past due, not resolved) SLA is currently breached, each tagged breach_kind response/resolution/both — exactly the population a service manager must action so no commitment lapses unseen. Additive columns on the already-RLS-scoped service_cases (no new table); tenant-scoped; the authenticated surface gates exec/marketing. No GL post in v1 (an SLA-credit / service-order posting is future work).
controls.code_reference: modules/service-cases/service-cases.service.ts (CASE_SLA tier→hours map; slaDue(tier, anchor) computes first_response_due_at/resolution_due_at; createCase applies the entitlement at open; setEntitlement recomputes off opened_at + re-evaluates breaches; addReply stamps first_responded_at + response_breached; resolveCase stamps resolution_breached; slaBreaches detective worklist over open past-due cases, breach_kind response/resolution/both); service-cases.controller.ts (POST /api/service/cases/:id/entitlement, GET /api/service/cases/sla/breaches, sla_tier accepted on create — gated exec/marketing); schema/service-cases.ts service_cases SLA columns + migration 0352 (additive on the RLS-scoped table); cutover/service.ts (ToE, SVC-5 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the tier→hours due-time computation (anchored on open time), the first-response stamp + breach on the first outbound reply, the resolution breach on resolve, the entitlement recompute + breach re-evaluation, and the breaches detective worklist (open past-due population).
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a case with a Gold entitlement → resolution_due_at = opened + 8h, first_response_due_at = opened + 2h; re-set the entitlement to Bronze → due times recompute off the original open time (resolution = opened + 72h); a first reply stamps first_responded_at + response_breached vs the due; resolve after the resolution due → resolution_breached=true; a case opened with a due time in the past appears in GET /cases/sla/breaches with breach_kind; a responded/resolved case drops out of the worklist; a second tenant cannot see the first tenant's breaches (RLS) (re-performed by the service harness SVC-5 checks).
controls.evidence: Per-case SLA entitlement (tier + computed first-response/resolution due times); first-response & resolution breach flags; SLA-breach worklist (breach_kind)

controls.fsli: Service revenue; Customer-service quality / self-service knowledge (no direct GL in v1)
controls.risk: Knowledge published to customers is unreviewed — a knowledge-base article reaches customers (and drives their self-service decisions) without any independent review, so inaccurate or unauthorized guidance is published by a single hand (the author self-publishes); AND the effectiveness of self-service is unmeasured — there is no record of whether the knowledge base actually deflected a case or the customer still had to open one, so case-deflection / self-service performance is invisible and unmanaged.
controls.description: Service Cloud — Knowledge Base + Case Deflection (SVC-6, migration 0355): a per-tenant kb_articles register (article_no unique per tenant) with a GOVERNED publish lifecycle draft → published → archived. An article is authored as a draft (editable only while draft — a published article is immutable, ARTICLE_NOT_DRAFT), and the SVC-06 MAKER-CHECKER is the publish: draft → published ONLY by a user who is NOT the author (published_by ≠ author → 403 SOD_SELF_PUBLISH), so no one publishes their own unreviewed knowledge; archive is published → archived (ARTICLE_NOT_PUBLISHED otherwise). Self-service is GET /api/service/kb/search?q= over PUBLISHED articles only (title/body/tags, view-count incremented on the hits) with a helpful/not_helpful feedback signal. The DETECTIVE side is the case-deflection log kb_deflections — POST /api/service/kb/deflect records each KB-assisted interaction (query, article, deflected=true self-served / false a case was opened, optional case link) — and GET /api/service/kb/deflection-stats returns the deflection rate + top articles, the population a service manager reads to gauge self-service effectiveness. Both tables tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy, tenant-leading indexes); the surface gates exec/marketing. No GL post.
controls.code_reference: modules/service-kb/service-kb.service.ts (createArticle draft + author; updateArticle draft-only ARTICLE_NOT_DRAFT; publishArticle SVC-06 maker-checker published_by ≠ author → SOD_SELF_PUBLISH; archiveArticle; search published-only + view bump; feedback helpful/not_helpful; logDeflection kb_deflections; deflectionStats rate + top articles); service-kb.controller.ts (/api/service/kb/articles[/:id[/publish|archive|feedback]], /search, /deflect, /deflection-stats — gated exec/marketing); schema/service-kb.ts kb_articles + kb_deflections + migration 0355 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes, article_no unique per tenant); cutover/service.ts (ToE, SVC-6 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the draft→published→archived lifecycle, the draft-only edit guard, the publish maker-checker (published_by ≠ author → SOD_SELF_PUBLISH), the published-only search surface, and the deflection log + deflection-rate detective read.
controls.toe: Re-perform: author a draft article; the author self-publishing → 403 SOD_SELF_PUBLISH; a DIFFERENT user publishes → status='published', published_by set; editing a published article → ARTICLE_NOT_DRAFT; search returns the published article (and not a draft), incrementing its views; a helpful vote increments the counter; a deflected interaction and a case-opened interaction are logged and deflection-stats returns the rate (deflected/total) + top articles; archive a published article; a second tenant cannot see the first tenant's articles/deflections (RLS) (re-performed by the service harness SVC-6 checks).
controls.evidence: KB article register (governed publish lifecycle) + author≠publisher SOD_SELF_PUBLISH test; case-deflection log + deflection-rate detective read; published-only self-service search

controls.fsli: Quality / rework & scrap cost; inventory (defective goods); warranty & recall exposure (no direct GL in v1)
controls.risk: Quality problems are dispositioned one-off (mfg-depth quality disposition, supplier gr_claims) with NO MANAGED corrective-action loop: a root cause is never formally identified, an action plan is never tracked to completion, and — most critically — a corrective/preventive action is CLOSED with no INDEPENDENT check that it actually worked, OR it is closed by the very person who owned it (self-sign-off). So recurring defects persist, the root cause is never truly fixed, and a CAPA can be marked 'effective/closed' by one hand with the underlying problem still live.
controls.description: CAPA (Corrective & Preventive Action) lifecycle with effectiveness sign-off (QMS-2, migration 0332): a first-class per-tenant CAPA register (capas: capa_no unique per tenant, a generic NULLABLE source_type/source_ref link to an NCR/gr_claim/complaint/audit/manual origin — deliberately NOT a FK, so it stands alone; problem_statement, root_cause, action_type corrective/preventive/both, owner, target_date) with child action items (capa_actions, status pending/done, completed_by/at). The lifecycle is open → in_progress (first action added) → pending_verification (submit — blocked with NO_ACTIONS if no action plan exists) → closed | cancelled. The QC-02 CONTROL is the effectiveness verification: a CAPA reaches 'closed' ONLY via verify by a user who is NEITHER the owner NOR the creator (verified_by ≠ owner/created_by → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin) AND ONLY when every child action is 'done' (else ACTIONS_INCOMPLETE), recording effectiveness_result. An 'ineffective' verification REOPENS the CAPA (in_progress) rather than closing it — the root cause was not resolved. A distinct verifier may also reject the verification (evidence insufficient, REASON_REQUIRED) sending it back to in_progress; a non-pending CAPA re-verified → NOT_PENDING_VERIFICATION. DETECTIVE read: GET /api/quality/capa/overdue?days=N surfaces open (not closed/cancelled) CAPAs whose target_date has passed — the corrective-action loop is slipping. Tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy); reads gate quality/quality_approve/exec, create/own/actions/submit quality/exec (owner duty), verify/reject quality_approve/exec (independent verifier duty). New SoD rule R21 (quality vs quality_approve). No GL post in v1.
controls.code_reference: modules/quality-capa/capa.service.ts (createCapa capa_no + open; addAction seq + open→in_progress; completeAction pending→done; submit → pending_verification, NO_ACTIONS guard; verify QC-02: NOT_PENDING_VERIFICATION → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (verified_by ≠ owner/created_by) → ACTIONS_INCOMPLETE → effective=closed / ineffective=in_progress; reject REASON_REQUIRED + SoD; cancel; overdue detective); capa.controller.ts (/api/quality/capa[/:id[/actions[/:actionId/complete]|submit|verify|reject|cancel]]|overdue — reads quality/quality_approve/exec, own quality/exec, verify/reject quality_approve/exec); schema/quality-capa.ts capas/capa_actions + migration 0332 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes, capa_no unique per tenant); permissions.ts quality/quality_approve + SoD R21; cutover/quality-capa.ts (ToE, 20 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the CAPA lifecycle states, the submit NO_ACTIONS guard, and the verify() maker-checker: the verified_by ≠ owner/created_by self-approval block, the all-actions-done gate (ACTIONS_INCOMPLETE), the effective→closed vs ineffective→reopen split, and the overdue detective read.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a CAPA (defaults owner=creator, links a gr_claim source) + two actions (open→in_progress); submit → pending_verification; verify while actions pending → ACTIONS_INCOMPLETE; the owner self-verifying → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; complete all actions; a DISTINCT verifier marks effective → closed (effectiveness_result + verified_by recorded); re-verifying a closed CAPA → NOT_PENDING_VERIFICATION; an 'ineffective' verification reopens to in_progress; reject without a reason → REASON_REQUIRED and owner self-reject → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; submit with no actions → NO_ACTIONS; cancel → cancelled; overdue(0d) lists a past-target open CAPA and excludes the closed one; a T1 tenant cannot see/verify an HQ CAPA (RLS) (re-performed by the quality-capa harness, 20 checks).
controls.evidence: CAPA register (lifecycle + effectiveness_result) + child-action completion log; SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + ACTIONS_INCOMPLETE tests; overdue-CAPA worklist

controls.fsli: Loyalty Points Liability (2250); Loyalty Points Expense (5700)
controls.risk: The loyalty points sub-ledger (pos_member_ledger) is not tied out to the GL, or the liability accrual double-posts / omits a fair-value re-measurement — the TFRS 15/IFRS 15 contract liability is misstated.
controls.description: Points-liability tie-out (`GET /api/loyalty/liability`) reports outstanding points × fair value (`baht_per_point`) against control account 2250, exposing `posted_liability` vs `unposted_value`. `POST /api/loyalty/liability/post` reconciles 2250 to the target by posting only the delta since the last run (`Dr 5700 / Cr 2250` on a net grant; reversed on a net redemption/forfeiture) — watermarked on `pos_member_ledger.id` and idempotent (deterministic `sourceRef` + a unique `(tenant_id, watermark_id)` on `loyalty_posting_runs`), so a re-run with no new movement posts nothing. Explicitly tenant-scoped (an HQ/Admin caller must pass `tenant_id`). Basis = ALL members (not just active), so deactivation never desyncs the GL.
controls.code_reference: loyalty/member.service.ts (liability, postLiability); ledger/ledger.service.ts (accrueLiability); loyalty/loyalty.controller.ts (GET /api/loyalty/liability, POST /api/loyalty/liability/post, gated loyalty/marketing/exec/gl_post); schema/loyalty-runs.ts loyalty_posting_runs + migration 0102; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-03 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the tie-out calculation, the watermarked/idempotent accrual posting, and the tenant-scoping guard.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an earn/redeem movement is posted, the tie-out reports outstanding × fair value on 2250; POST .../post posts the delta (Dr 5700/Cr 2250), the TB stays balanced, and a re-run with no new movement posts nothing (idempotent); an HQ-tenant run with no points finds none (tenant-scoped) — re-performed by the LYL-03 harness checks (8 assertions).
controls.evidence: Liability tie-out report; loyalty_posting_runs; GL JE (source=LOYALTY)

controls.fsli: Loyalty Points Liability (2250); Loyalty Points Expense (5700)
controls.risk: Points that should have expired (per `expiry_days`) are never written off, overstating the outstanding points liability indefinitely (breakage never recognised).
controls.description: `POST /api/loyalty/expire` computes each member's redeemable balance (earned within `expiry_days`, net of redemptions/adjustments) under a per-member `FOR UPDATE` lock and writes the excess off as an append-only `Expire` ledger row (negative points), decrementing the balance. Idempotent — a second run finds the balance already at the redeemable floor and expires nothing more. The next liability accrual then releases the matching GL amount (`Dr 2250 / Cr 5700`, breakage).
controls.frequency: Per expiry run (daily via the maintenance sweep, LYL-06)
controls.code_reference: loyalty/member.service.ts (expirePoints, expireForTenant); schema/loyalty-members.ts pos_member_ledger txn_type='Expire'; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-04 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the redeemable-balance calculation, the FOR UPDATE lock, and the idempotency guard.
controls.toe: Re-perform: aged points expire (breakage) under lock and release the GL liability (Dr 2250/Cr 5700); a second run with no further aging expires 0 more points — re-performed by the LYL-04 harness check.
controls.evidence: pos_member_ledger Expire rows; accrual delta

controls.fsli: Loyalty Points Liability (2250); Loyalty Points Expense (5700)
controls.risk: The loyalty liability is not accrued before a period locks, so a closed period's trial balance omits points earned/redeemed/expired within it (understated/overstated liability at close).
controls.description: `closePeriod`/`closeYear` (ledger.service.ts) call `accrueLiability` for every tenant BEFORE locking the period, dated within the closing period; at year-end the resulting 5700 expense closes to Retained Earnings while 2250 remains on the balance sheet. Best-effort — a loyalty-accrual hiccup is recorded but never blocks the financial close.
controls.code_reference: ledger/ledger.service.ts (closePeriod/closeYear → accrueLiability); cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-05 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the close-period hook invoking the loyalty accrual before the lock.
controls.toe: Re-perform: close a period with an active points balance → loyalty_accrual.posted=true and the delta ties to the outstanding liability — re-performed by the LYL-05 harness check.
controls.evidence: closePeriod response loyalty_accrual block

controls.risk: Expiry + accrual rely on someone remembering to call them manually — a missed run lets the sub-ledger and GL drift apart indefinitely.
controls.description: `POST /api/loyalty/maintenance/run` runs the whole cycle per tenant (expire aged points, then accrue/release the liability) in one call; an Admin caller sweeps all tenants (RLS bypass), a tenant user sweeps its own, `tenant_id` limits it to one. Best-effort per tenant — one tenant's closed period/error is recorded in `results[]` and never aborts the others. Driven by an external scheduler (a daily GitHub Actions workflow, opt-in) since the API has no in-process scheduler (multi-instance-safe).
controls.code_reference: loyalty/member.service.ts (sweepMaintenance); loyalty/loyalty.controller.ts (POST /api/loyalty/maintenance/run, gated exec/gl_post/masterdata); .github/workflows/loyalty-maintenance.yml; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-06 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the sweep's per-tenant expire→accrue sequencing and the scheduled trigger.
controls.toe: Re-perform: the sweep expires aged points then re-accrues the liability per tenant, tallying totals across tenants — re-performed by the LYL-06 harness check.
controls.evidence: Sweep results[] log; workflow run history
