controls.risk: A member (or a colluding operator) burns points for a reward without a hard stock/eligibility/limit check, or a redemption code is reused (double-dip on the same reward).
controls.description: `POST /api/loyalty/rewards/:id/redeem` burns points under a `FOR UPDATE` member lock, validating sufficient balance, `tier_min`, an atomic `stock>0` guard, and `per_member_limit` before decrementing the balance and writing a Redeem ledger row (releasing the GL liability). Issues a single-use `loyalty_redemptions` code (`RDM-YYYYMMDD-NNN`); `POST /api/loyalty/redemptions/:code/use` is a one-way `issued→used` transition under `FOR UPDATE` (a second use → `ALREADY_USED`). All reward/redemption/coupon reads & writes are explicitly tenant-scoped (RLS is bypassed for Admin).
controls.code_reference: rewards/rewards.service.ts; schema/loyalty-rewards.ts loyalty_rewards/loyalty_redemptions/member_coupons + migration 0103; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-07 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the FOR UPDATE lock, stock/limit guards, the single-use code transition, and tenant scoping.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a reward burn issues a single-use code and releases the GL liability; a second use of the same code → ALREADY_USED; a cross-tenant code use/read is blocked — re-performed by the LYL-07 harness checks.
controls.evidence: loyalty_redemptions register; GL liability delta

controls.risk: A member's tier is stale/manually gameable, or a mission/stamp-card reward is claimed more than once (double-granted bonus points or duplicate coupon).
controls.description: `POST /api/loyalty/tiers/recompute` (and the maintenance sweep) recomputes each member's tier from lifetime points against the `loyalty_tiers` ladder under a per-member `FOR UPDATE` lock, appending a `loyalty_tier_history` audit row on any change. Missions (`loyalty_missions`/`loyalty_mission_progress`) record progress at the till (serialized per member under `FOR UPDATE`) and grant their reward — bonus points via an Adjust ledger row, or a coupon — exactly once: `claimedAt` is set under `FOR UPDATE` and a second claim → `ALREADY_CLAIMED`; an incomplete mission → `MISSION_INCOMPLETE`.
controls.code_reference: gamification/gamification.service.ts; loyalty/member.service.ts (recomputeTiers, recomputeTiersForTenant, tierJourney); schema/loyalty-gamification.ts loyalty_tier_history/loyalty_missions/loyalty_mission_progress + migration 0104; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-08 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the tier-recompute audit trail and the single-claim lock on mission rewards.
controls.toe: Re-perform: lifetime points cross a tier threshold → tier changes + a history row; a completed mission claim grants the bonus once, a second claim → ALREADY_CLAIMED — re-performed by the LYL-08 harness check.
controls.evidence: loyalty_tier_history; loyalty_mission_progress claimedAt

controls.risk: A member self-refers or refers the same member twice to farm bonus points, or a referral reward is granted more than once.
controls.description: `POST /api/loyalty/referrals` records a referral (self-referral blocked by id and by phone; a member can be referred at most once — a partial unique on `referred_member_id` → `ALREADY_REFERRED`). `POST /api/loyalty/referrals/:id/reward` grants both sides their bonus points exactly once — `status` flips to `rewarded` under a `FOR UPDATE` lock on the referral row (a second call → `ALREADY_REWARDED`); the two member rows are locked in deterministic ascending-id order to prevent an ABBA deadlock under concurrent rewards. Explicitly tenant-scoped.
controls.code_reference: referrals/referrals.service.ts; schema/loyalty-referrals.ts loyalty_referrals + migration 0105; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-09 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the refer-once partial-unique guard, self-referral blocks, and the single-reward FOR UPDATE transition.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a referral rewards both members once; a second reward call → ALREADY_REWARDED; a cross-tenant reward attempt → not found — re-performed by the LYL-09 harness check.
controls.evidence: loyalty_referrals status/rewarded_at

controls.risk: The consumer member app's authentication (phone-OTP) is brute-forceable, leaks whether a phone is enrolled, or a member token can reach staff-only functions / another member's data.
controls.description: `POST /api/member/auth/request-otp` resolves the member by explicit shop code + phone (RLS bypassed on this public route) and issues a single-use, 5-minute, scrypt-hashed 6-digit OTP (rate-limited to one live code/60s), always returning `{sent:true}` so it never leaks phone-enrolment status. `POST /api/member/auth/verify-otp` checks the code under expiry + a ≥5-attempt bound — the counter is an auto-committing atomic guarded UPDATE (`@NoTx`) so a wrong-code 401 cannot roll back the increment — and mints a member JWT `{role:'Member', tenantId, memberId, permissions:[]}`. Every `/api/member/*` route is `MemberGuard`-gated (rejects any non-member token) and self-scoped (the member is taken from the token — no member-id input), so a member token is rejected by every `@Permissions`-gated staff route and can only ever act on itself; PDPA per-purpose consent self-service (`source='self'`) is likewise self-scoped.
controls.code_reference: member/member-auth.service.ts; member/member.guard.ts; member/member.controller.ts; schema/member-otp.ts member_otps + migration 0106; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-10, LYL-10b, LYL-10c checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the OTP hashing/expiry/attempt-bound, the MemberGuard self-scoping, and the consent self-service scoping.
controls.toe: Re-perform: OTP login mints a member token, a staff route (/api/loyalty/members) rejects it (403); a member self-manages PDPA consent (self-scoped); 5 wrong guesses lock the code (the correct code is then also rejected) — re-performed by the LYL-10/10b/10c harness checks.
controls.evidence: member_otps attempt counter; member token claims

controls.risk: A lucky-draw outcome is client-influenceable or unaudited (rigged draw), a prize is oversold beyond its stock, or the spin cost is not actually charged before a prize is granted.
controls.description: `POST /api/loyalty/wheels/:id/spin` (and the member self-spin) draws exactly one segment server-side using a cryptographic (`node:crypto`) weighted RNG over the in-stock, positive-weight segments only — the member cannot influence the outcome, and every spin is recorded in `loyalty_spins` (provably fair). Under one `FOR UPDATE` member lock: a daily free spin costs nothing, otherwise `cost_points` is burned (a Redeem ledger row, releasing the GL liability) after an insufficiency check; a points prize is an Adjust row, a coupon prize issues a `member_coupons` row; limited stock is decremented by an atomic guarded UPDATE (`stock>0`) so a prize cannot be oversold (a lost race → `PRIZE_OUT_OF_STOCK`, no charge).
controls.code_reference: wheels/wheels.service.ts; schema/loyalty-wheels.ts loyalty_wheels/loyalty_wheel_segments/loyalty_spins + migration 0107; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-11 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the weighted-RNG draw, the cost-burn-before-prize ordering under the member lock, and the atomic stock guard.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a free spin costs nothing, a paid spin burns cost_points before the prize is granted, and a stocked-out segment is never awarded twice (atomic guard) — re-performed by the LYL-11 harness check.
controls.evidence: loyalty_spins outcome log; ledger Redeem/Adjust rows

controls.risk: A segmented campaign is sent to a member who opted out of marketing (PDPA violation), or a scheduled/manual send race double-delivers the same campaign (duplicate spend/annoyance, unaudited).
controls.description: `loyalty_campaigns` targets all / an RFM segment / a tier / today's birthdays, sent now or at `schedule_at` (fired by a dedicated `@NoTx` cron route, `run-due`). Every targeted member is resolved tenant-scoped; a `marketing_opt_in=false` member is never delivered to — logged `skipped` (PDPA) — and every recipient (sent/skipped/failed) is audited in `message_log`. Sending is claim-first: the campaign is atomically flipped `draft|scheduled→sent` and COMMITTED (under `@NoTx`) BEFORE any message goes out, so a crash/rollback (esp. the cron's former whole-sweep transaction) can never re-open it for a duplicate blast — at-most-once delivery is durable, not dependent on the transaction surviving.
controls.code_reference: campaigns/campaigns.service.ts; schema/loyalty-campaigns.ts loyalty_campaigns + migration 0108; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-12, LYL-12b checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the opt-out filter, the per-recipient audit log, and the claim-first commit-before-delivery ordering.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a segmented send skips opted-out members and audits every recipient; a re-send of an already-sent campaign → ALREADY_SENT; a scheduled campaign fires exactly once across repeated run-due calls — re-performed by the LYL-12/12b harness checks.
controls.evidence: message_log per-recipient audit; campaign status transition

controls.risk: A single role holds both duties of a CRM single-duty conflict (e.g. configure a reward AND redeem at the till) enabling self-dealing loyalty value, because a coarse legacy permission bundles both.
controls.description: Four standalone granular permissions (`crm_member`, `crm_points_adjust`, `crm_reward`, `crm_campaign`) and three SoD rules — R14 `crm_reward ✗ pos_sell`, R15 `crm_points_adjust ✗ crm_member`, R16 `crm_campaign ✗ crm_points_adjust` — are enforced by the same preventive `assertNoSodConflict` gate used for every other role assignment (ITGC-AC-09): assigning a conflicting set is blocked (422 `SOD_CONFLICT`) unless justified with `allow_sod_override`+`reason`. Config endpoints are gated `crm_* OR` the coarse legacy permission, so existing coarse roles keep working while a single-duty CRM role stays SoD-clean.
controls.code_reference: packages/shared/src/permissions.ts (crm_member/crm_points_adjust/crm_reward/crm_campaign; SOD_RULES R14–R16); admin-users.service.ts (assertNoSodConflict); cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-13 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the crm_* permission definitions, the R14–R16 rule set, and the endpoint gating.
controls.toe: Re-perform: assigning crm_reward+pos_sell together → 422 SOD_CONFLICT (R14); a single-duty crm_reward-only role is assigned cleanly — re-performed by the LYL-13 harness check.
controls.evidence: SoD conflict-report; role-assignment test

controls.risk: A member claims a partner privilege they don't qualify for (tier too low), a claim code is reused at the partner, or a privilege is claimed beyond its stock/per-member limit.
controls.description: A member claims a privilege (tier-gated by a minimum lifetime-points threshold, same gating as rewards) under a `FOR UPDATE` lock on the privilege row — eligibility re-checked, stock decremented by an atomic `stock>0` guard, per-member limit counted over non-void claims — issuing a single-use `PRV-…` code. The partner redeems it via a one-way `claimed→used` transition under `FOR UPDATE` (`ALREADY_USED` on reuse); the code is globally unique and the redeem is tenant-scoped, so one tenant cannot redeem another's code.
controls.code_reference: partners/partners.service.ts; schema/loyalty-partners.ts loyalty_partners/loyalty_privileges/loyalty_privilege_claims + migration 0109; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-14 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the tier gate, the atomic stock/limit guards, and the single-use claimed→used transition.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a qualifying member claims a privilege (single-use code), the partner redeems it once (2nd use → ALREADY_USED); a below-tier member's claim → TIER_TOO_LOW; a limit/stock-exhausted claim is blocked — re-performed by the LYL-14 harness check.
controls.evidence: loyalty_privilege_claims status

controls.risk: A cross-tenant aggregate leak in the loyalty analytics dashboard exposes one tenant's member/financial data to another, or the reported liability doesn't reconcile to the posted GL figure (misleading monitoring).
controls.description: `GET /api/loyalty/analytics` returns, for ONE tenant only (HQ/Admin must pass `?tenant_id` — no cross-tenant aggregate), member counts + tier mix, points movements, the redemption funnel, the points-liability fair value vs the posted GL 2250, the breakage rate, and churn risk (dormant ≥90d members holding points). Every aggregate query is explicitly tenant-scoped. Read-only — a detective monitoring control supporting MKT-05/06.
controls.code_reference: loyalty-analytics/loyalty-analytics.service.ts; loyalty-analytics/loyalty-analytics.controller.ts; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-15 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the tenant-scoping on every aggregate and the liability-vs-GL reconciliation figure.
controls.toe: Re-perform: the analytics overview returns liability/funnel/churn figures scoped to the caller's tenant only; an HQ caller with no tenant_id is required to name one — re-performed by the LYL-15 harness check.
controls.evidence: Analytics overview response; churn drill-down list

controls.risk: A forged/unsigned LINE identity token mints a member session for the wrong member, or a dev/mock login bypass is reachable in production, enabling account takeover via LINE.
controls.description: `POST /api/member/auth/line` (and the staff enrol-line/link-line routes) verifies the LIFF idToken via the shared `verifyLineIdToken` — production calls LINE's `oauth2/v2.1/verify` against `LINE_LOGIN_CHANNEL_ID`; a `mock:<userId>` token is accepted ONLY when no channel id is configured (non-prod). The verified `lineUserId` resolves the member WITHIN the requested tenant (`pos_members.line_user_id`, a one-LINE-↔-one-member-per-tenant partial unique — `LINE_ALREADY_LINKED` on conflict); an unlinked LINE account → `LINE_NOT_LINKED`, never silently creates or misattributes a session.
controls.code_reference: loyalty/line-auth.ts (verifyLineIdToken); loyalty/loyalty.controller.ts (staff enroll-line/link-line); member/member.controller.ts (auth/line, link-line); schema/loyalty-members.ts line_user_id + migration 0105_member_line; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-16 check)
controls.tod: Inspect the idToken verification call, the non-prod-only mock gate, and the tenant+line_user_id resolution.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a linked LINE id mints a member token scoped to the correct member; an unlinked id → 401 LINE_NOT_LINKED; a member links their LINE account (one-per-tenant enforced) — re-performed by the LYL-16 harness check.
controls.evidence: pos_members.line_user_id; login response

controls.risk: A member (or a colluding submitter) claims loyalty points for a receipt from a purchase made outside our own POS with no independent review, or re-submits the same receipt to claim points twice — granting unearned or duplicated points liability.
controls.description: Receipt-upload-for-points review queue: a member submits a photo of the receipt + the claimed amount (self-service `/m` app; the member token carries no `crm_points_adjust` permission, so a member can never approve their own submission) into `loyalty_receipt_submissions` (`status='Pending'`). A staff user holding `crm_points_adjust` reviews the photo and approves or rejects; approval re-validates the row is still `Pending` (else `RECEIPT_ALREADY_REVIEWED`) and grants points through the SAME `earnInTx` FOR-UPDATE-locked path POS checkout uses (member.service.ts) — no separate GL logic; the existing points-liability accrual (LYL-03) picks up the resulting `Earn` ledger row on its next run. A partial-unique index on `(tenant_id, member_id, purchase_date, purchase_amount)` (excluding rejected rows) blocks the same receipt being claimed twice while it is pending or approved.
controls.code_reference: loyalty/receipt-submissions.service.ts (submit/queue/approve/reject); loyalty/member.service.ts (earnInTx, reused unchanged); member/member.controller.ts (POST/GET /api/member/receipts, MemberGuard); loyalty/loyalty.controller.ts (GET /api/loyalty/receipts, POST .../:id/approve|reject, gated crm_points_adjust/loyalty/exec); schema/loyalty-receipt-submissions.ts + migration 0207 (partial-unique dup guard); cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-17 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the submit/approve/reject endpoints' permission gates, the FOR-UPDATE re-validation on approval, and the duplicate-claim unique index.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a member submits a receipt (Pending, no points yet); a staff user with crm_points_adjust approves → points post via earnInTx and the row flips Approved; a second approve/reject on the same id → RECEIPT_ALREADY_REVIEWED; reject leaves the balance untouched; a second submission with the identical member/date/amount while the first is live → DUPLICATE_RECEIPT (re-performed by the LYL-17 harness check).
controls.evidence: Receipt submission queue; posMemberLedger Earn row tie-out; LYL-17 harness

controls.risk: A member-to-member point transfer double-spends or loses points (one side of the move commits without the other), drains an account past its balance, launders value in bulk past any daily limit, or silently changes the tenant's points liability — corrupting the 2250 sub-ledger tie-out.
controls.description: P2P point transfer (docs/27 W1): `POST /api/member/points/transfer` (member self-service — the sender is ALWAYS the authenticated member, no member_id parameter) and the staff counterpart `POST /api/loyalty/members/:id/transfer` (gated `crm_points_adjust`/`loyalty`/`exec`) execute an ATOMIC two-row ledger move — one 'Transfer' row at −points on the sender and one at +points on the recipient in a single DB transaction, both member rows locked FOR UPDATE in ascending-id order (deadlock-free for opposite-direction transfers). Guards inside the transaction: sender balance must cover the points (INSUFFICIENT_POINTS); recipient must be an active member of the SAME tenant (RECIPIENT_NOT_FOUND — no cross-tenant leak); self-transfer rejected (SELF_TRANSFER); the sender's outgoing total per Bangkok day is capped at `loyalty_config.transfer_day_cap` (TRANSFER_CAP; 0 disables the feature entirely, TRANSFER_DISABLED). The move nets to ZERO on outstanding points, so the TFRS-15 liability accrual (LYL-03) is unchanged by construction — the obligation merely changes owner; `movements.transfer_net_points` on the liability tie-out surfaces the (always-zero) net for the reviewer. Lifetime points are untouched on both sides, so tier standing cannot be gifted; inbound transferred points age from their own transfer date in the expiry model, outbound consume like redemptions.
controls.code_reference: loyalty/member.service.ts (transferPoints; Transfer handling in the expiry model); member/member.controller.ts (POST /api/member/points/transfer, MemberGuard); loyalty/loyalty.controller.ts (POST /api/loyalty/members/:id/transfer); schema/marketing.ts loyalty_config.transfer_day_cap + migration 0221; cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-18 ToE); cutover/loyalty.ts (W1 P2P checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the single-transaction two-row move, the ascending-id FOR-UPDATE lock order, the in-tx day-cap re-check, and the same-tenant recipient resolution.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a staff transfer moves points atomically (two ledger rows netting 0) and the liability tie-out's outstanding_points is unchanged; a member token cannot use the staff route (403) but moves its OWN points via /api/member/points/transfer; self-transfer → SELF_TRANSFER; over-balance → INSUFFICIENT_POINTS; a transfer past the daily cap → TRANSFER_CAP; cap 0 → TRANSFER_DISABLED; a recipient in another tenant → RECIPIENT_NOT_FOUND (re-performed by the LYL-18 compliance ToE + the loyalty-harness W1 section).
controls.evidence: posMemberLedger paired Transfer rows; liability tie-out transfer_net_points; LYL-18 harness

controls.risk: A coalition (multi-shop) point movement books points on the wrong shop's ledger or moves value between shops with no offsetting entry — one shop's 2250 liability grows with no compensation while another books revenue it did not fund; or a partner shop resolves/reads another shop's full member PII; or shop staff configure the network themselves.
controls.description: Coalition network (docs/27 W2): earn/burn at a partner shop always lands on the member's HOME ledger through the SAME locked earnInTx/redeemInTx path (`POST /api/coalition/earn|redeem`, staff `loyalty`/`pos`) — the ledger row's tenant is the member's home shop, so each shop's TFRS-15 accrual (LYL-03) keeps deriving from ITS OWN roster by construction. Every cross-shop movement posts a BALANCED intercompany clearing entry at fair value (category 'loyalty-clearing', createIcInternal: home Dr 1150 due-from / Cr 5700; partner Dr 5700 / Cr 2150 due-to — reversed for burns), atomically with the point move (a closed partner period rejects the WHOLE movement: no points without the clearing entry); periodic `settleIc` nets the balances and the IC reconciliation proves elimination. Cross-shop member resolution (`GET /api/coalition/resolve?phone=`) is COALITION-SCOPED (both shops must share an ACTIVE coalition; outsiders → 404, existence never revealed) and PDPA-MINIMAL (code/name/tier/points/home-shop only — never phone/email/birthday/consents). Coalition configuration is HQ-only (perm `users`/`exec` + the Admin-role COALITION_HQ_ONLY guard). Cross-tenant reads/writes run in a deliberate, validated bypass context (`runInTenantContext`) entered ONLY after the shared-active-coalition check; the manual /api/intercompany endpoint cannot create 'loyalty-clearing' entries (dto enum unchanged).
controls.code_reference: coalition/coalition.service.ts (resolve/earn/redeem/validateCross, hqOnly); coalition/coalition.controller.ts (@NoTx partner-till routes); intercompany/intercompany.service.ts (createIcInternal, 'loyalty-clearing' MAP); schema/coalition.ts + migration 0222 (tables + ic_category value + RLS); cutover/coalition.ts (21-check harness, CI gate); cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-19 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the home-ledger routing, the atomic clearing-entry posting, the coalition-membership validation before every bypass block, and the PDPA-minimal resolve payload.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an earn at partner shop B books the Earn row under the member's home tenant A and creates a balanced 'loyalty-clearing' IC (A due-from / B due-to at points × fair value); a burn reverses the direction; both shops' liability tie-outs stay per-shop-true; a non-coalition shop gets 404 on resolve/earn; a non-Admin exec cannot configure the network (COALITION_HQ_ONLY); the resolve payload carries no contact/consent PII; IC reconciliation eliminates after settlement (re-performed by the coalition harness + the LYL-19 compliance ToE).
controls.evidence: ic_transactions loyalty-clearing rows + reconciliation; home-tenant pos_member_ledger rows; coalition harness

controls.risk: A dissatisfied customer's low NPS score triggers a notification that nobody acts on — no owner, no deadline, no record of whether the member was ever contacted — so churn-signal detection exists but service recovery silently fails.
controls.description: Service-recovery cases (docs/29 V2): every detractor answer (score ≤ 6 on the tokenized single-use NPS survey) AUTO-OPENS exactly one recovery case in `recovery_cases` (idempotent unique (source, source_ref); the atomic single-use answer guard makes a duplicate response impossible) with a 24-hour response SLA (`response_due_at`). The worklist (`GET /api/recovery/cases`, perms crm/loyalty/marketing; web `/loyalty/recovery`) drives ACTOR-STAMPED transitions: `POST .../contact` (Open → Contacted, stamps contacted_by/at; a repeat → CASE_NOT_OPEN) and `POST .../resolve` (requires a resolution note — NOTE_REQUIRED without one; stamps resolved_by/at). An Open case past its SLA reads `overdue` on the worklist, on `GET /api/nps/summary` (`recovery.open/overdue`), and the member 360 flags the open case — an unhandled detractor is surfaced everywhere, never silently dropped. The automation event (`loyalty.nps_detractor` → journeys/notifications) still fires; the case is the accountable record and its creation is NOT best-effort (a write failure fails the submit so the member can retry).
controls.code_reference: nps/recovery.service.ts (openForDetractor/list/contact/resolve/overdueCount); nps/nps.service.ts (submit detractor branch); nps/nps.controller.ts (RecoveryController); crm/crm.service.ts (360 open-case flag); schema/nps.ts recovery_cases + migration 0224 (unique source_ref, RLS, tenant-leading index); cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-20 ToE); cutover/crm.ts (V2 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the auto-open in the submit path (non-best-effort), the unique source_ref, the actor-stamped guarded transitions, and the overdue surfacing on worklist/summary/360.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a detractor answer opens exactly one Open case with a 24h due; a second answer on the same token is rejected (single-use); resolve without a note → 400; contact/resolve stamp the acting user and a repeated transition → 409; an Open case past its SLA reads overdue on the worklist AND the NPS summary (re-performed by the LYL-20 compliance ToE + the crm-harness V2 checks).
controls.evidence: recovery_cases rows + actor stamps; overdue counts on /api/nps/summary; LYL-20 harness

controls.risk: A paid VIP membership fee is recognized as revenue up-front instead of over the service period (TFRS 15 violation), a re-run double-recognizes a month, or a member whose membership lapsed keeps the paid tier forever — free VIP status and misstated revenue.
controls.description: Paid VIP membership (docs/29 V4): the sale (`POST /api/loyalty/memberships/sell`, pos/loyalty; plan config marketing/exec) books Dr 1000 cash / Cr **2410 Contract Liability** for the full fee — revenue is NEVER taken up-front. Monthly recognition (`POST /api/loyalty/memberships/recognize`, gl_post/exec; also the schedulable BI job `membership_revenue_recognize`) releases 2410 → 4300 straight-line (price ÷ period_months; the final month takes the rounding remainder), IDEMPOTENT per (membership, month) via the journal's source_ref dedup — a re-run posts nothing. One ACTIVE membership per member (partial unique → MEMBERSHIP_ACTIVE). The plan's tier is granted with a `loyalty_tier_history` reason 'vip' row; the nightly maintenance sweep EXPIRES lapsed memberships (status → Expired + 'vip-expired' audit row) BEFORE the tier recompute runs, so the member falls back to the EARNED ladder rung automatically — no perpetual free VIP.
controls.frequency: Per sale / monthly recognition / nightly lapse sweep
controls.code_reference: loyalty/membership.service.ts (upsertPlan/sell/recognizeDue/expireLapsed/forMember); loyalty/loyalty.controller.ts (membership routes); loyalty/member.service.ts (sweep order: lapse before recompute); bi.service.ts membership_revenue_recognize; schema/membership.ts + migration 0225 (partial-unique one-active, RLS, tenant-leading indexes); cutover/loyalty.ts (V4 checks); cutover/compliance.ts (LYL-21 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the deferral entry, the straight-line release with the source_ref idempotency guard, the one-active partial unique, and the sweep's lapse-before-recompute ordering.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a sale books Dr 1000 / Cr 2410 for the full fee and grants the tier with a 'vip' history row; with N months elapsed the recognition posts exactly N straight-line releases (2410 → 4300) and a re-run posts 0; a second sale to the same member → MEMBERSHIP_ACTIVE; a lapsed membership expires on the sweep and the tier falls back to the earned rung with a 'vip-expired' row (re-performed by the loyalty-harness V4 checks + the LYL-21 compliance ToE).
controls.evidence: member_memberships + VIP/VIP-REC journal entries; loyalty_tier_history vip/vip-expired rows; LYL-21 harness

controls.risk: The SAME source document earns or redeems loyalty points TWICE — a retried checkout, a store-hub replay (BRANCH-04), or an integration retry — so the member balance and the 2250 points liability are overstated or a customer is charged points twice for one meal.
controls.description: Points-ledger replay guard: earnInTx/redeemInTx first look for an existing pos_member_ledger row for (tenant, member, ref_doc, txn_type) under the member row lock they already hold; when one exists the call returns the points ALREADY booked and mutates nothing. Migration 0315 adds the hard backstop: a PARTIAL UNIQUE index uq_member_ledger_doc on (tenant_id, member_id, ref_doc, txn_type) WHERE ref_doc IS NOT NULL AND txn_type IN ('Earn','Redeem'). Adjust/Transfer/Expire rows and rows without a source document stay repeatable by design.
controls.code_reference: loyalty/member.service.ts (priorLedgerPoints + earnInTx/redeemInTx guards); migration 0315 (uq_member_ledger_doc); schema/loyalty-members.ts; cutover/loyalty.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the pre-check under the member row lock and the partial unique index.
controls.toe: Re-perform: earn against a sale, replay it (same points, balance unchanged, one ledger row); redeem and replay (no second deduction); a different document still books; a hand-written duplicate row is rejected (loyalty harness).
controls.evidence: pos_member_ledger rows per source document; points-liability (2250) roll-forward

controls.risk: A lifecycle journey (multi-step drip) spams a member (no frequency cap), messages an opted-out member (PDPA breach), or a crashed/re-run step delivers the same message twice — eroding consent compliance and brand trust with no audit trail.
controls.description: Lifecycle journeys (Phase G1, docs/25): a journey is a LINEAR list of steps (wait N days → send channel/body unless a whitelisted skip-rule matches). The runner (POST /api/loyalty/journeys/run-due, @NoTx; also the schedulable BI job journey_runner) CLAIMS each due enrollment-step with an atomic guarded UPDATE (next_run_at NULLed) BEFORE delivery — a concurrent/re-run claims 0 rows, so each step fires AT MOST ONCE (a crash mid-send strands the step rather than duplicating it, mirroring MKT-10's claim-first). Every send routes through MessagingService: an opted-out member is recorded 'skipped' (never contacted) and each outcome is audited in message_log (campaign = journey:<code>:<step>). A per-journey FREQUENCY CAP (max N journey messages per member per window) skips-and-audits over-cap sends ('frequency cap'). Enrolment is once-per-member (unique key); config/run is marketing/exec; every query is explicitly tenant-scoped (the cron/Admin path bypasses RLS).
controls.code_reference: journeys/journeys.service.ts (runDue claim + executeStep consent/cap; enroll once-per-member); journeys/journeys.controller.ts (/api/loyalty/journeys*, run-due @NoTx, gated marketing/exec); schema/journeys.ts journeys/journey_steps/journey_enrollments + migration 0212 (RLS); bi.service.ts journey_runner; automation.service.ts enroll_journey action; cutover/compliance.ts (MKT-12 check) + cutover/crm.ts (5 journey checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the claim-first guarded UPDATE, the consent-respecting send path, the frequency-cap check + audited skip, and the tenant scoping.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an opted-out member enrols, the step is 'skipped' + audited in message_log and a re-run fires nothing (at-most-once); an over-cap step is skipped with error 'frequency cap'; a T2 user cannot see/enrol into a T1 journey (404) — re-performed by the MKT-12 compliance check + the crm harness journey checks.
controls.evidence: message_log journey:<code>:<step> rows; journey_enrollments claim trail

controls.risk: A marketplace buyer (Grab/LINE MAN/foodpanda/Robinhood) is profiled or marketed to WITHOUT consent, raw platform identifiers (phone/customer id) accumulate as unmanaged PII (PDPA breach), or a stolen link QR silently binds someone else's platform account to an attacker's member profile — poisoning CRM attribution.
controls.description: Marketplace-to-member identity capture (G1, docs/45): the aggregator ingest paths (channel-adapter ingestWebhook + restaurant ingestThirdParty) persist ONLY a SHA-256 hash of the platform's stable buyer identifier in channel_customer_refs (raw id/phone is NEVER copied out of the audited webhook payload — data minimization) plus counters. LINKING is consent-gated and identity-bound: the member self-link (POST /api/member/channel-link) requires an authenticated member token (the HMAC QR capability only NAMES which ref — possession alone links nothing, and a cross-tenant token is rejected TENANT_MISMATCH) and a REQUIRED explicit marketing_opt_in decision recorded to member_consents (source='self') in the SAME request tx; the staff link (POST /api/loyalty/channel-refs/:id/link, crm_member/loyalty duty) equally refuses to link without the attested consent boolean (source='pos'). One ref = one member: a second member claiming a linked ref is rejected REF_ALREADY_LINKED (409). Once linked, later ingests auto-attach dine_in_orders.member_id so attribution accrues only along the consented mapping.
controls.code_reference: channel-adapter/channel-customer-refs.service.ts (hashCustomerRef data-minimization; captureOnIngest; linkMember/staffLink single-winner); channel-adapter/link-token.util.ts (HMAC capability); channel-adapter/mappers.ts (extCustomerRef); restaurant/channel-order.service.ts + channel-adapter.service.ts (ingest hooks); loyalty/member/member.controller.ts + loyalty.controller.ts (consent-in-same-tx endpoints); schema/channel-customer-refs.ts + migration 0367 (RLS, tenant-leading index); cutover/channel.ts (MKT-13 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the hash-only persistence at both ingest sites, the member-token + required-consent link path, the REF_ALREADY_LINKED single-winner, and the tenant-mismatch rejection.
controls.toe: Re-perform: ingest two orders with the same buyer ref → ONE ref row, order_count=2, raw identifier absent from channel_customer_refs; link via member token + marketing_opt_in → consent row written and the next ingest auto-attaches member_id; a second member linking the same ref → 409 REF_ALREADY_LINKED; a token minted for another tenant → 403 TENANT_MISMATCH (re-performed by the channel-harness MKT-13 checks).
controls.evidence: channel_customer_refs (hashed refs + link audit trail); member_consents channel:<platform> rows

controls.risk: Pay supplier for goods not ordered/received or wrong price; or the same person who ran the match force-overrides a variance to push their own invoice through.
controls.description: 3-way match (PO ↔ GR ↔ Invoice) within tolerance gates AP payment; a variance blocks the invoice until it passes or is overridden. The override is MAKER-CHECKED: the person who ran the match cannot override its variance — a DIFFERENT user must (overrider ≠ matchedBy → SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin) — and the override is reset by any re-match so a stale override can't keep a now-failing invoice payable.
controls.code_reference: match/three-way-match.service.ts (match gate; override SoD overrider≠matcher); match.controller.ts (override gated creditors/approvals/gl_close); cutover/match.ts (ToE); cutover/compliance.ts (ICFR ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect match + tolerance + payment gate + the override SoD check.
controls.toe: Sample invoices: payment blocked until matched; the matcher's self-override is rejected SOD_VIOLATION, an independent override unblocks, a re-match clears it (re-performed by the match + compliance harnesses).
controls.evidence: Match results; override SoD test

controls.risk: Payment to blocklisted/unapproved vendor.
controls.description: Supplier approval-status / blocklist check blocks PO & payment to blocked vendors.
controls.code_reference: procurement.service.ts (SUPPLIER_BLOCKED)
controls.tod: Inspect vendor-status gate.
controls.toe: Attempt PO to blocked vendor → 422.
controls.evidence: Vendor gate test

controls.risk: PR/PO raised or received without authorization.
controls.description: PR/PO approval workflow (maker-checker) — the approval-workflow engine routes by amount threshold (DoA matrix, ELC-03) to an approver ≠ requester (and enforces SoD); and goods receipt is HARD-GATED on PO approval (a GR against a Pending/Draft/Rejected/Cancelled PO is blocked, PO_NOT_APPROVED) so an unapproved PO can't trigger a receipt + AP liability.
controls.code_reference: workflow/workflow.service.ts (amount-threshold routing + maker-checker + SoD); procurement.service.ts createGr (PO-approval gate); cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect approval routing + thresholds + the GR-on-approval gate.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a PR/PO is approved by an authorized approver ≠ requester; a GR against an unapproved PO is blocked (403 PO_NOT_APPROVED).
controls.evidence: Approval trail + GR gate ToE

controls.risk: Match tolerance loosened to force payment.
controls.description: Match-tolerance configuration change restricted to 'creditors' permission.
controls.code_reference: match.controller.ts (PUT tolerance @Permissions creditors)
controls.tod: Inspect permission on tolerance change.
controls.toe: Attempt change w/o perm → 403; log changes.
controls.evidence: Config-change log

controls.risk: Employee/maintenance spend recorded in GL but not as a payable (escapes AP aging; AP sub-ledger ≠ GL control).
controls.description: ESS expense approval and EAM work-order completion raise an AP payable via createApTxn (Dr 5100/5710 / Cr 2000) — not a bare GL post — so the liability appears in AP aging, settles through the AP pay flow, and keeps the AP sub-ledger ↔ GL 2000 reconciled (REC-01). Reimbursements vat-exempt; SoD approver ≠ claimant retained.
controls.code_reference: ess.service.ts (approveExpense → createApTxn); eam.service.ts (updateWorkOrderStatus → createApTxn, acct 5710); finance.service.ts (createApTxn expense_account/tenant_id); cutover/ess.ts + basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect approval→AP routing + reconciliation.
controls.toe: Approve a claim / complete a WO → AP payable raised, paid via AP, sub-ledger ties to GL 2000 (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: AP sub-ledger; reimbursement/maintenance payables

controls.risk: Vendor payment disbursed without independent approval — one person both books and pays a bill.
controls.description: AP disbursement maker-checker — a payment is REQUESTED by a 'creditors' holder and APPROVED by a DIFFERENT user (approvals/gl_close); the bill's paid_amount and the cash-disbursement GL move only on approval. Booking a bill pre-paid in one call is blocked.
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts (requestApPayment / approveApPayment requester≠approver / rejectApPayment); finance.controller.ts (creditors requests, approvals|gl_close approves); schema/finance.ts (ap_payments) + migration 0115; cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect AP payment approval routing + SoD guard.
controls.toe: Sample payments: approver ≠ requester; no cash/GL effect until approved; self-approval → 403 SOD_VIOLATION; pre-paid creation → 400 (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: AP payment approvals

controls.risk: Equipment not maintained; maintenance uncontrolled; preventive maintenance missed; maintenance cost mis-stated or not tracked per asset.
controls.description: EAM maintenance work orders against the asset register with a guarded lifecycle (open→in_progress→completed/cancelled; BAD_TRANSITION); preventive-maintenance schedules (time/meter) with an idempotent due-generation sweep (cron / daily scheduled job eam_pm_generate). Work-order labor/parts cost lines roll up to the WO actual cost (so the AP posting reflects real spend, not the estimate); per-asset reliability & cost KPIs (corrective failures, MTBF, downtime, total maintenance spend) support maintenance budgeting and repair-vs-replace decisions.
controls.code_reference: eam.service.ts (work orders, PM schedules, runPmDue, meters, WO cost lines, reliability); eam.controller.ts; bi.service.ts (eam_pm_generate); cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect WO lifecycle guard + PM due logic; inspect cost-line roll-up into actual cost and reliability KPI computation.
controls.toe: Raise/complete a WO; PM sweep raises due preventive WOs (time + meter) and is idempotent; cost lines roll up to actual cost and drive the AP posting; reliability KPIs computed (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Work-order log; PM schedule + sweep run log; WO cost lines; reliability KPI report

controls.description: Bin-stock decrement under FOR UPDATE inside a transaction — concurrent picks serialize (no oversell).
controls.code_reference: wms.service.ts (pick)
controls.tod: Inspect lock + sufficiency check in tx.
controls.toe: Concurrent last-unit test → one PICK_SHORT.
controls.evidence: Concurrency test

controls.tod: Inspect movement logging.

controls.risk: Book vs physical stock diverges; counts adjusted without independent review.
controls.description: Periodic stocktake / cycle count → variance computed → posted with an independent review (maker-checker, SoD R11): the person who COUNTED (counted_by) may not post/approve their own count, so the variance is reviewed before it adjusts on-hand + GL. Posting is permission-gated and audit-logged.
controls.code_reference: stock-ops/stock-ops.service.ts postStocktake (counter ≠ poster gate, SOD_SELF_APPROVAL); cutover/stock-ops.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the count → variance → independent-review/post flow.

controls.risk: A branch stock-out is fulfilled by BUYING new stock while a sibling branch holds idle surplus (capital tied up / over-ordering); or stock is moved between branches without attribution or segregation of duties.
controls.description: Branch-aware replenishment routes transfer-before-buy: a low (branch,item) is first covered by an inter-branch TRANSFER from a sibling branch with surplus (tenant-scoped branch_stock move + branch-attributed cust_stock_log, executed under the warehouse-custody duty), and only the residual the transfers cannot cover raises a purchase requisition through the maker-checker buy flow (EXP-03). Transfer execution (wh_custody) is segregated from PR/PO approval (procurement / approvals).
controls.code_reference: wms/replenishment.service.ts (suggest transfer/buy split, autoTransfer, autoPr); wms.controller.ts (auto-transfer @wh_custody vs auto-pr @procurement); schema branch_stock + item_supplier + migration 0130; cutover/wms.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect transfer-before-buy allocation, branch attribution, and leg segregation.
controls.toe: Deplete branch A below its reorder point with surplus at branch B -> suggest splits into a transfer (from B) + a buy (residual); auto-transfer moves branch_stock and logs both branches; auto-PR raises a PR for the residual; branch_stock reconciles (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Branch replenishment run log; inter-branch transfer log; residual PR

controls.risk: Perpetual stock VALUE drifts from the GL inventory control account; receipts/issues/adjustments uncosted or not posted to GL → inventory & COGS misstated.
controls.description: Perpetual valued sub-ledger (inv_moves / inv_balances): every receipt/issue/adjustment updates the item's cost (moving-average, or FIFO/FEFO cost layers in inv_cost_layers) AND posts a balanced JE (Dr/Cr 1200 Inventory ↔ 2000 AP / 5000 COGS / 5810 variance). Reconciliation ties sub-ledger value to GL 1200; posting is idempotent on ref (no double-count); negative stock is blocked. FEFO consumes soonest-expiry layers first (perishable waste control). The sub-ledger and the costing module (FIFO/AVG/STD) are mutually exclusive per item (CONFLICTING_COSTING guard on both sides) so inventory is never double-capitalized to 1200.
controls.code_reference: inventory-ledger.service.ts; stock-ops.service.ts (bridge); 0131 inv_moves/inv_balances; 0132 inv_cost_layers (FIFO/FEFO); ledger.postEntry
controls.tod: Walkthrough receipt→issue→adjust + a stock-ops issue/transfer/stocktake on a tracked item, in both moving-avg and FEFO: inspect cost recompute / layer consumption, the balanced JE, and reconcile().
controls.toe: Sample receipt/issue/adjust + stock-ops moves → sub-ledger value ties to GL 1200 (reconciled); FEFO COGS = actual layer cost; over-issue blocked; duplicate ref deduped.
controls.evidence: Reconciliation report; layers report; basics + compliance + stock-ops harness

controls.risk: Stock written off the books without independent review — one person posts a shrinkage/spoilage write-off to conceal a shortage they caused (theft of inventory).
controls.description: Inventory write-off maker-checker (SoD): a NEGATIVE ad-hoc stock adjustment (a write-off) on POST /api/inventory/adjustments is a REQUEST that posts NOTHING — no variance JE, no FIFO/FEFO layer consumption, no balance change — until a DIFFERENT wh_adjust holder approves (POST /api/inventory/writeoffs/:id/approve). On approval the real valued adjustment runs atomically against current state (Dr 5810 / Cr 1200). Self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin); one write-off pending per item/location; reject leaves stock untouched. A positive adjustment (overage/found) and a stocktake count-variance (the count is itself the authorizing document) post immediately and are out of scope.
controls.code_reference: inventory-ledger.service.ts requestWriteOff/approveWriteOff/rejectWriteOff/listWriteOffs; inventory-ledger.controller.ts (writeoffs/:id/approve|reject, wh_adjust); schema/inventory.ts inv_writeoff_requests + migration 0136; cutover/basics.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the request→approve flow + the approver≠requester check + that the request posts nothing until approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: request a write-off (on-hand unchanged), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user; confirm the variance JE + balance move only after approval (re-performed by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Write-off request register + SoD test

controls.risk: Stock mis-located or stored beyond a bin's physical capacity — overflow held in an unrecorded location, breaking the perpetual location records and concealing where inventory physically is (loss / shrink risk; inaccurate pick paths).
controls.description: Bin-capacity / location integrity: every storage bin carries a physical position + size + capacity, and putaway (POST /api/wms/putaway) enforces it — a receipt that would fill a bin past its defined capacity is rejected (422 BIN_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED) and no bin_stock moves. Bins without a capacity are unconstrained (back-compat). The warehouse layout (GET /api/wms/layout) reports live utilisation (on-hand ÷ capacity) and flags over-capacity bins; GET /api/wms/locate gives the exact bin(s) holding any item for spatial control.
controls.code_reference: wms.service.ts putaway (capacity guard) / warehouseLayout / locateItem / setBinLayout; wms.controller.ts (layout, locate, bins/:code/layout); schema/wms.ts bins.pos_*/dim_*/capacity + migration 0138; cutover/wms.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the putaway capacity guard + the layout utilisation/locate read model.
controls.toe: Re-perform: set a bin capacity, putaway within capacity (accepted), putaway beyond it (422 BIN_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED, stock unchanged); confirm layout reports utilisation and locate finds the item (re-performed by the wms harness).
controls.evidence: Bin capacity test + layout/locate read

controls.risk: Available-to-promise mis-stated by leaked stock reservations — allocations that are never released or consumed accumulate so ATP drifts permanently low (false stock-outs), or a reservation oversells stock it never had.
controls.description: ATP reservation integrity: stock reservations carry a lifecycle Open -> Fulfilled (goods shipped/issued) | Cancelled (order released), and ATP nets only OPEN reservations. allocate() is idempotent per (tenant, ref_doc, item) -- re-reserving the same document adjusts the one open row rather than stacking duplicates (a retried order cannot leak the float) -- and the additional reservation may not exceed current ATP (INSUFFICIENT_ATP), so a reservation can never oversell. release() frees a cancelled order's reservation back to ATP; fulfill() retires a shipped reservation from the open pool (the on-hand reduction is posted by the issue path, so the swap is ATP-neutral and never double-counted). A register exposes every reservation for review.
controls.code_reference: costing/atp.service.ts allocate (idempotent + ATP guard) / releaseAllocation / fulfillAllocation / listAllocations / atp (nets Open only); costing.controller.ts (allocate, allocations/:ref/release|fulfill, allocations); schema/costing.ts stock_allocations.status; cutover/costing.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reservation lifecycle + that ATP nets only Open + the idempotency/over-reserve guards.
controls.toe: Re-perform: allocate (ATP drops), re-allocate same ref (idempotent -- no leak), over-reserve (422 INSUFFICIENT_ATP), release (frees ATP), fulfill + on-hand drop (ATP-neutral) (re-performed by the costing harness).
controls.evidence: Reservation register + ATP drift test

controls.risk: Kitchen/ingredient waste is invisible — spoilage/over-production is not captured, so food-cost loss can't be measured or controlled; OR a perpetual-tracked item is written off as 'waste' to bypass the independent write-off approval (INV-07).
controls.description: Waste / spoilage logging with a write-off boundary: POST /api/inventory/waste records reason-coded ingredient waste (damage/expiry/spoilage/overproduction/prep_error/other), decrements the ingredient stock (customer_inventory + cust_stock_log) and, when a unit cost is supplied, posts Dr 5810 Scrap/Waste Loss / Cr 1200 Inventory (idempotent per WASTE- doc — mirrors recipe COGS, which credits 1200 on consumption). A by-reason analytics view (total cost, cost per reason) is the food-cost lever. **Boundary control:** a PERPETUAL-tracked item (an inv_balances row) is REJECTED here (USE_WRITEOFF) and must go through the INV-07 maker-checker write-off — so no one can dodge the independent write-off approval by mislabelling shrinkage as 'waste', and inventory value is never decremented twice.
controls.code_reference: inventory/waste.service.ts logWaste (perpetual-item guard, GL 5810/1200, stock decrement) + list (by-reason analytics); waste.controller.ts (/api/inventory/waste, warehouse/pos/order_mgt); schema/portal.ts waste_log + migration 0149; cutover/waste.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reason-coded capture + the 5810/1200 GL on costed waste + the perpetual-item USE_WRITEOFF boundary + the by-reason analytics.
controls.toe: Re-perform: costed waste posts Dr 5810 / Cr 1200 + decrements stock; uncosted waste logs with no GL; a perpetual-tracked item is rejected (USE_WRITEOFF); by-reason totals reconcile; trial balance balanced (re-performed by the waste harness).
controls.evidence: Waste log + by-reason food-cost analytics

controls.risk: Theoretical-vs-actual food-cost variance is a single undifferentiated number — a manager sees that COGS ran over the recipe but not WHY or WHERE, so the over-usage can't be acted on (over-portioning vs spoilage vs theft go unaddressed and recur).
controls.description: Reason-coded food-cost variance review: each EOD-count variance (cust_variance — theoretical vs actual ingredient use, valued at cost) is tagged with a normalized reason_code (WASTE/OVERSTOCK/SPOILAGE/PORTIONING/THEFT/OTHER, enum-validated at POST /api/portal/variance) and an optional station. GET /api/menu/food-cost/variance values the variance at ingredient cost and rolls it up three ways — per ingredient (with High/Medium anomaly bands), by_reason and by_station — netting unfavourable/favourable, so 'Sauce station is 8% over theoretical (portioning) → retrain' is actionable. Yield/waste factors on the recipe BoM (INV-03) feed the theoretical baseline so the variance isn't inflated by un-costed trim loss. Tenant-scoped (RLS); read gated by exec/order_mgt.
controls.code_reference: menu/food-cost.service.ts foodCostVariance (per-item + by_reason + by_station roll-ups, anomaly bands); portal.service.ts createVariance (persists reason_code/station); portal.controller.ts VarianceBody (reason_code enum); schema/bom.ts cust_variance.reason_code/station + migration 0161; /food-cost variance tab (by-reason/by-station tables); cutover/restaurant.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reason_code enum capture, the valued by_reason/by_station roll-ups, the anomaly bands, and tenant isolation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed counts with reason/station; the variance values per ingredient (NOODLE +฿30 High), nets +30/−50 to −20, by_reason PORTIONING +30 / WASTE −50, by_station Sauce +30 (20%); another tenant's counts are excluded (re-performed by the restaurant harness).
controls.evidence: Reason-coded variance roll-up + by-station test

controls.risk: Landed costs (freight, import duty, insurance, broker fees) on goods received are expensed as period costs instead of being capitalised into inventory unit cost — so on-hand inventory is understated, COGS is distorted (the charge hits P&L in the wrong period), and unit cost is understated for pricing/valuation; OR a landed-cost voucher is posted by the same person who prepared it (no independent check on the capitalised amount / accrued liability), OR the charge is retroactively loaded onto stock that has already been sold (overstating on-hand inventory that no longer exists).
controls.description: Landed-cost allocation voucher: freight/duty/insurance/broker charges are attached to one or more posted goods receipts and APPORTIONED into inventory unit cost by a chosen basis (value/qty/weight), summing to 100% of the charges (rounding remainder pinned so Σalloc = total). On post, the STILL-ON-HAND share of each line's allocation is capitalised into the perpetual sub-ledger — raising the moving-average value and the open FIFO/FEFO cost layers so FUTURE issues carry the loaded cost (Dr 1200) — while the ALREADY-ISSUED residual is expensed to the costing variance account (Dr 5500, mirroring STD-costing PPV; issued qty is never retroactively re-costed), crediting the landed-cost accrual liability (Dr 1200 + Dr 5500 / Cr 2010). Posting is MAKER-CHECKER: the poster must differ from the preparer (403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL). The capitalisation JE is inside the INV-06 reconcile scope (source INV-LC) so the sub-ledger still ties to GL 1200 after a voucher posts. Tenant-scoped (RLS).
controls.code_reference: landed-cost/landed-cost.service.ts create/allocate/post (apportion, on-hand vs issued split, maker-checker); landed-cost.controller.ts (/api/costing/landed-cost[/:no][/allocate|/post]); schema/landed-cost.ts landed_cost_vouchers/landed_cost_allocations + migration 0341; ledger-constants.ts account 2010; inventory-ledger.service.ts INV_SOURCES (INV-LC); posting-events.ts LANDEDCOST.CAPITALIZE; cutover/landed-cost.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Walkthrough create voucher → allocate preview (ties to 100%) → post: inspect the on-hand-vs-issued capitalisation split, the balanced Dr1200/Dr5500/Cr2010 JE, the maker-checker block, and that a future issue carries the loaded cost.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a voucher, preview apportionment (sums to total), self-post is blocked (403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), a distinct approver posts (Dr 1200 / Cr 2010 balanced), a subsequent issue carries the raised moving-average / FIFO-layer cost, and the already-issued residual lands in 5500 — never re-costing sold stock; sub-ledger reconciles to GL 1200 (re-performed by the landed-cost harness).
controls.evidence: Landed-cost voucher register + apportionment/maker-checker ToE

controls.risk: Per-branch reorder points are STATIC numbers — set once and never revisited — so a branch whose run-rate has grown carries too small a buffer and stocks out (lost sales), while a slow branch over-orders and ties up cash/spoils stock; neither is flagged.
controls.description: Demand-driven par-level recommendation (layered on the INV-05 branch min-max + transfer-before-buy): for each (branch,item) the system derives the average daily usage from the trailing window of branch-tagged consumption (cust_stock_log Sale/Consume) and recommends reorder_point = ceil(avg_daily x lead_time_days x safety 1.25), flagging rows whose static reorder_point is below that demand-driven buffer (under_buffered). GET /api/replenishment/par-recommendations is read-only; a planner applies a recommendation per row (POST .../apply writes the reorder_point onto branch_stock, planner duty). lead_time_days is per (branch,item) (migration 0163). Tenant-scoped (RLS); gated planner/procurement.
controls.code_reference: wms/replenishment.service.ts parRecommendations (avg-daily-usage x lead x safety, under_buffered flag) + applyPar (writes reorder_point, planner); wms.controller.ts (/api/replenishment/par-recommendations[/apply]); schema/portal.ts branch_stock.lead_time_days + migration 0163; /replenishment par-recommendations section; cutover/wms.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the avg-daily-usage derivation from consumption logs, the lead-time x safety reorder-point formula, the under_buffered flag, and the planner-gated apply.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed branch consumption (10/day) at lead 3 -> recommend ROP 38 (under-buffered vs static 10); apply writes 38 onto branch_stock; tenant-isolated (re-performed by the wms harness).
controls.evidence: Par recommendation list + apply test

controls.risk: Stock allocated to a project is double-allocated or issued without valuation: the same on-hand qty is reserved for two projects (both draw it, one stocks out), or material is moved to a project with no cost captured, so project WIP is understated and inventory doesn't reconcile.
controls.description: Stock reservation → issue-to-project (M3, docs/32): a reservation soft-allocates on-hand stock to a project so available-to-issue = on_hand(inv_balances) − Σ(held reservations); the reserve check is ATOMIC (a FOR UPDATE lock on the item+location's held rows) so two concurrent reservations can't over-allocate (INSUFFICIENT_STOCK otherwise). A held reservation is released (frees the stock) or issued to the project — issue-to-project relieves inventory at moving-average / consumed-layer cost and posts Dr 1260 project WIP (project_id) / Cr 1200 Inventory via the valued sub-ledger, so the value is CAPITALISED into project WIP (not expensed to COGS) and inventory 1200 stays tied to the sub-ledger; the issued value is booked as a consumed BoQ-line commitment. Prevents double-allocation and un-valued project draws.
controls.code_reference: reservations.service.ts reserve (FOR UPDATE lock, available = on_hand − held → INSUFFICIENT_STOCK) / release / issueToProject (→ inventory ledger + consumed commitment) / available; reservations.controller.ts (/api/reservations[/:id/issue|release], available); inventory-ledger.service.ts issueToProject (Dr 1260 project_id / Cr 1200, neg-stock guard, moving-avg/FIFO); schema/inventory.ts stock_reservations (migration 0240); cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the available = on_hand − held computation, the FOR UPDATE atomicity, the release/consume lifecycle, and the Dr 1260(project)/Cr 1200 issue-to-project posting.
controls.toe: Re-perform: receive stock; reserve part of it (available drops); a reservation beyond available → INSUFFICIENT_STOCK; issue the reservation → inventory relieved, project WIP 1260 debited with project_id, balanced; release restores availability (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Available-to-issue (on_hand − held); the issue-to-project WIP posting

controls.risk: A menu item stays sellable on the delivery aggregators after its ingredients deplete — the store keeps accepting Grab/LINE MAN/Foodpanda/Robinhood orders the kitchen can no longer cook (the #1 aggregator complaint → cancellations, refunds, store-rating penalties), because on-hand availability is not synced OUT to the partners.
controls.description: Auto-86 out-of-stock sync to aggregators (POS-7, migration 0299): the reactive auto-86 (LockingService.recomputeAvailability — run after every stock-depleting sale and on demand via POST /api/pos/scale/availability/recompute) flips a recipe-backed dish is_available=false the moment an ingredient can't cover one serving, and back to true on restock. Each such transition is now MIRRORED to every connected (enabled) aggregator via the outbound channel-adapter provider's setItemAvailability(store_ref, sku, available) op — a depletion PAUSES ('86') the item on the partners, a restock RESUMES ('un-86') it. IDEMPOTENT: the last-pushed per-(tenant,platform,sku) state is held in channel_item_availability, so a transition is pushed ONLY when the desired state differs from what the aggregator already has (a no-op recompute never spams the partner API). AUDITED: every 86/un-86 actually pushed is recorded (reason, push outcome/ref, actor) in the append-only channel_item_86_log, read at GET /api/channels/auto-86. Best-effort — a partner outage records push_ok=false but never poisons the sale. Both tables tenant-scoped (canonical 0232 RLS, tenant-leading indexes).
controls.code_reference: pos/scale/locking.service.ts recomputeAvailability (local 86 flip both directions → channelSync.syncAuto86); channel-adapter/channel-adapter.service.ts syncAuto86 (per-channel state diff → push, log) + listAuto86; channel-adapter/providers.ts setItemAvailability (Mock + Http); channel-adapter.controller.ts GET /api/channels/auto-86; schema/pos-scale.ts channel_item_availability + channel_item_86_log (migration 0299); cutover/channel-adapter.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the deplete→86 / restock→un-86 mirroring, the channel_item_availability idempotency guard (no push when state unchanged), and the channel_item_86_log transition audit.
controls.toe: Re-perform: deplete a recipe ingredient → recompute 86s the dish and POSTs setItemAvailability(available=false) to the aggregator (bearer + store_ref), state+log recorded; a second recompute with unchanged stock pushes NOTHING new (idempotent); restock → recompute un-86s the dish and POSTs available=true, state resumed, transition audited (re-performed by the channel-adapter harness).
controls.evidence: channel_item_availability state; channel_item_86_log 86/un-86 audit trail

controls.risk: A cancelled/voided FIRED ticket line (the dish was already prepped/cooked before the void) writes off no ingredients, so its cost vanishes from the food-cost picture; waste is captured only by an undifferentiated reason with no record of WHAT happened to the wasted stock (discarded vs donated vs staff-meal vs re-worked); and the gap between the recipe's theoretical usage and the kitchen's ACTUAL depletion is never quantified, so over-production/spoilage recurs unmeasured.
controls.description: Waste-ledger closing-the-loop (POS-5a, extends INV-10, migration 0301): the SINGLE waste ledger (waste_log) gains (1) a reason + DISPOSITION taxonomy — reason_code WHY (…/void_fire) × disposition WHAT-happened (discard/compost/donate/staff_meal/rework/return_supplier, enum-validated → BAD_DISPOSITION), rolled up in the by_disposition analytics + a ?disposition= filter; (2) VOID-FIRED-ITEM capture — POST /api/inventory/waste/void-fire explodes a voided fired dish's recipe (menu_recipes/menu_recipe_lines, gross qty = qty_per ÷ (yield−waste) ÷ batch yield × dishes) into per-ingredient waste rows (source=void_fire, ref_doc=the voided ticket), decrements the ingredient stock and posts ONE aggregated Dr 5810 Scrap/Waste Loss / Cr 1200 Inventory (idempotent per WASTE- doc); NO_RECIPE/NO_RECIPE_LINES guards; and (3) a THEORETICAL-vs-ACTUAL usage-variance report — GET /api/inventory/waste/variance nets the recipe-COGS theoretical depletion (cust_stock_log 'Consume' rows the recipe deduction writes on every sale) against ACTUAL depletion (theoretical + logged 'Waste'), valuing the waste-explained gap at ingredient cost per item with High/Medium anomaly bands, so a manager sees the baht impact and % of usage that is waste. The INV-07 perpetual-write-off boundary (USE_WRITEOFF) still holds — kitchen ingredients (customer_inventory) only. Tenant-scoped (RLS); gated warehouse/pos/order_mgt.
controls.frequency: Per void / waste event; per shift review
controls.code_reference: inventory/waste.service.ts logWaste (disposition) + voidFire (recipe explosion, aggregated 5810/1200 JE) + usageVariance (Consume-vs-Waste depletion) + list (by_disposition); waste.controller.ts (/api/inventory/waste[/void-fire][/variance]); schema/portal.ts waste_log disposition/source/ref_doc + migration 0301; cutover/waste.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reason×disposition taxonomy + the void-fire recipe explosion → ingredient waste + aggregated 5810/1200 JE + the theoretical-vs-actual usage-variance nets Consume vs Waste.
controls.toe: Re-perform: log costed waste with disposition=donate (echoed, by_disposition rolls it up); void-fire a recipe'd dish → 2 ingredient waste lines, one Dr 5810/Cr 1200 JE, ingredient stock decremented; unknown sku → NO_RECIPE; usage-variance shows theoretical (Consume) vs actual (Consume+Waste) with the valued waste gap; trial balance balanced (re-performed by the waste harness, 19 checks).
controls.evidence: Waste ledger + by_disposition + void-fire audit + usage-variance report

controls.risk: Inventory moved between warehouses/branches is mis-stated at period end: a single instant transfer either records the stock at BOTH locations (double count) or at NEITHER (existence gap) while goods are physically in transit, and the value in transit sits in no identifiable account — so an auditor cannot cut off in-transit inventory at the balance-sheet date (existence / completeness of inventory).
controls.description: Two-step ship→receive TRANSFER ORDER with in-transit ownership + GL (INV-2, migration 0341): an inter-warehouse/branch transfer is created Draft (document only), SHIPPED (relieves the source location's valued stock at current cost — moving-average, or consumed FIFO/FEFO layers carried forward — and books Dr 1255 Goods-in-Transit / Cr 1200 Inventory), and RECEIVED by a DIFFERENT custodian (lands the stock at the destination and books Dr 1200 Inventory / Cr 1255). Between ship and receive the value is isolated in the Goods-in-Transit control account (1255, seeded in the COA + the SCF CF_CLASSIFY operating bucket; distinct from 1250 WIP), so it is neither double-counted nor lost. Custody segregation (SoD): the shipper may not receive their own transfer (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin). A period-end in-transit AGING / cutoff report (GET …/in-transit/aging) lists every still-Shipped order with its days-in-transit + value bucketed (0-7 / 8-30 / 31+) — the population an auditor tests for cutoff. The perpetual sub-ledger stays tied to GL 1200 throughout (INV-GIT included in the reconcile source set). The legacy instant value-neutral stock-ops transfer is unchanged. Tenant-scoped (canonical 0232 RLS); custody duty wh_custody, mirroring the instant transfer.
controls.owner: Warehouse / Inventory Controller (segregated ship vs receive)
controls.code_reference: stock-ops/transfer-order.service.ts create/ship/receive/inTransitAging + inventory/inventory-ledger.service.ts shipToInTransit/receiveFromInTransit (Dr/Cr 1255); transfer-order.controller.ts (/api/stock-ops/transfer-orders[/:no/ship|/receive][/in-transit/aging]); schema/inventory.ts transfer_orders + transfer_order_lines + migration 0341; ledger-constants.ts 1255 COA + CF_CLASSIFY; cutover/transfer-orders.ts (ToE, 21 checks)
controls.tod: Walkthrough create→ship→receive: inspect the ship JE (Dr 1255 / Cr 1200), the source relief, the in-transit aging read model, the receive JE (Dr 1200 / Cr 1255) + dest landing, the shipper≠receiver SoD, and that the perpetual sub-ledger reconciles to GL 1200 across the round-trip.
controls.toe: Re-perform: ship a transfer order → source qty down, Dr 1255 / Cr 1200 balanced, aging lists it; shipper receiving their own order is blocked (403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL); an independent user receives → dest qty up, Dr 1200 / Cr 1255, in-transit cleared, sub-ledger ties to GL 1200 (re-performed by the transfer-orders harness, 21 checks).
controls.evidence: In-transit aging / cutoff report + ship/receive JE pair + SoD test

controls.risk: A defective or recalled lot cannot be traced or contained: there is no genealogy linking a batch back to its supplier/goods-receipt or forward to the customers/sales it was issued into (so a recall can't scope who received it), and a suspect lot stays PICKABLE — the FEFO suggestion and the WMS wave keep allocating it — so non-conforming material keeps shipping while the investigation runs.
controls.description: Lot recall / genealogy traceability + lot hold (INV-5, migration 0341): every lot (lot_ledger) gains a two-way GENEALOGY trace — GET /api/lots/:lotNo/trace resolves BACKWARD (lot → goods_receipt(s) via lot_ledger.gr_no → goods_receipts → PO → supplier/vendor) and FORWARD (lot → issue rows qty_out>0 → ref_doc pick → pick_lists → cust_pos_sales → customer/branch), so a recall investigation can scope both the source supplier and every downstream sale from one call. A one-click QUARANTINE — POST /api/lots/:lotNo/hold (reason-coded, HOLD- doc) — places a lot on hold (lot_holds, status Held); a held lot is EXCLUDED from BOTH the FEFO pick-suggestion (GET /api/lots/fefo/:itemId, excluded_held count returned) AND the WMS wave bin-allocation (wms.service suggestPickBin skips any bin whose lot has an active Held row), so a recalled/suspect lot physically cannot be picked, shipped or sold. POST /api/lots/:lotNo/release lifts the hold (audit trail retained — released_by/released_at/release_reason) and re-enables picking; LOT_NOT_HELD if not held. Hold/release gated to the inventory-control duties (lots/warehouse/wh_adjust); the trace read to lots/warehouse. lot_holds is tenant-scoped (canonical 0232 RLS, tenant-leading index); lot_ledger (no tenant_id) is never rewritten.
controls.code_reference: modules/lots/lots.service.ts (trace backward/forward genealogy; hold/release quarantine; fefo + heldLotNos exclusion); lots.controller.ts (/api/lots/:lotNo/trace|hold|release); modules/wms/wms.service.ts suggestPickBin (Held-lot exclusion in wave allocation); schema/inventory.ts lot_holds + migration 0341 (RLS, tenant-leading index, HOLD- doc); permissions.ts lots/warehouse/wh_adjust; cutover/lot-trace.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the two-way trace (backward GR→supplier, forward pick/sale→customer), the hold/release lifecycle, and that a Held lot is excluded from BOTH the FEFO suggestion and the WMS wave allocation.
controls.toe: Re-perform (lot-trace harness): seed GR→lot→issue→sale; the backward trace returns the supplier + GR; the forward trace returns the customer + sale; holding the lot excludes it from FEFO (excluded_held>0) and from the WMS wave bin-allocation; releasing it re-includes the lot; releasing a non-held lot → LOT_NOT_HELD.
controls.evidence: Two-way lot genealogy trace; lot hold/quarantine excluded from FEFO + WMS pick

controls.risk: Inventory existence is not assured between wall-to-wall counts: ad-hoc stocktakes (INV-04) have no schedule and no risk-based cadence, so high-velocity / high-value items — where existence and shrink risk concentrate — are not necessarily counted more often, and shrink can accumulate undetected until year-end. Separately, a counter who can see the SYSTEM (book) quantity can simply write the book number down as the 'physical' count, so a shortage they caused is never surfaced.
controls.description: Governed cycle-count program with ABC classification + BLIND counts (INV-3, migration 0341): (1) ABC classification — POST /api/stock-ops/abc/recompute ranks the tenant's items by annual CONSUMPTION VALUE (Σ goods-issue qty × moving-average unit cost) and Pareto-bands them A (top ~80% of cumulative value) / B (next ~15%) / C (last ~5%), persisted per (tenant,item) in item_abc_class (rank + cum_pct + computed_at/by); GET /api/stock-ops/abc returns the classification. (2) Cadence — cycle_count_plans holds the days-between-counts per class (seeded A=30 / B=90 / C=180 on first recompute; tunable), so class drives frequency. (3) Due worklist — GET /api/stock-ops/cycle-counts/due lists items whose next count (last POSTED count date + class cadence) is due or overdue, never-counted first. (4) BLIND count — POST /api/stock-ops/cycle-counts captures the system/book qty SERVER-SIDE into a Draft stocktake but NEVER returns it to the counter (the generate response carries only the item list); the counter submits an independent physical count via .../cycle-counts/:taskNo/count. (5) Posting REUSES the existing stocktake path unchanged — POST /api/stocktake/:stNo/post enforces the INV-04 counter≠poster maker-checker (SoD R11: SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) and books the valued GL adjustment (inventory-ledger.postCountVariance, Dr/Cr 5810↔1200). No posting logic is duplicated: the program only SCHEDULES and BLINDS, then feeds the INV-04/INV-06 post path. All three tables tenant-scoped (canonical 0232 RLS, tenant-leading indexes).
controls.code_reference: modules/stock-ops/cycle-count.service.ts (recomputeAbc — consumption-value Pareto A/B/C; dueWorklist — cadence honoured off the last POSTED count; generateTask — blind Draft stocktake, system_qty captured but withheld; submitCount — independent physical entry) + cycle-count.controller.ts (/api/stock-ops/abc[/recompute], /api/stock-ops/cycle-counts[/due][/:taskNo/count] — recompute wh_adjust, count wh_count); reuses stock-ops.service.ts postStocktake (INV-04 counter≠poster + valued GL); schema/inventory.ts item_abc_class/cycle_count_plans/cycle_count_tasks + migration 0341 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes); cutover/cycle-counts.ts (ToE, 15 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the ABC consumption-value ranking + Pareto banding, the per-class cadence in cycle_count_plans, the due-worklist next-due computation, that the generate response withholds system_qty (blind), and that posting routes through the unchanged INV-04 counter≠poster gate + valued adjustment.
controls.toe: Re-perform (cycle-counts harness): consume X/Y/Z at 5000/500/50 value → recompute classes them A/B/C; the due worklist lists all three (never counted, A first); generate a blind count for X → the response carries the item list but NO system_qty while the server captured book 500; the counter enters 480 → variance −20; a DIFFERENT user posts via the existing /api/stocktake/:no/post → Posted, valued on-hand corrected to 480 (@10 = 4800); the freshly-counted A item drops off the due worklist (cadence honoured); the counter posting their OWN count → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL.
controls.evidence: ABC classification snapshot; cadence-driven due worklist; blind count task + linked stocktake; INV-04 self-approval block + valued adjustment

controls.risk: Received or produced lots exist (lot_ledger) but their quality is not evidenced by a Certificate of Analysis, and an out-of-spec lot (a measured characteristic outside its acceptable range) can be RELEASED into stock/production with no independent sign-off — one person both records the failing result and decides to release it, so non-conforming material enters the flow (defective finished goods, customer rejects, recalls / warranty cost) with no documented deviation approval and no audit trail of who authorised the exception.
controls.description: Certificate-of-Analysis capture + out-of-spec release maker-checker (QMS-3, migration 0333): a per-item quality_specs catalogue defines an acceptable [min_value, max_value] range per measured characteristic. A coa_certificates CoA is raised against a lot (source incoming/production) and its coa_results measured values are captured; on EVALUATE the CoA's overall_result is computed = fail if ANY characteristic's actual falls outside its [min,max] window, else pass. A PASS (in-spec) CoA can be released by its recorder (routine). A FAIL (out-of-spec) lot can be RELEASED only as a documented DEVIATION: the releaser must (a) hold the quality-approver duty quality_approve/exec (DEVIATION_APPROVER_REQUIRED otherwise), (b) be a DIFFERENT user than the CoA recorder (released_by ≠ created_by → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin), and (c) supply a mandatory deviation_reason (DEVIATION_REASON_REQUIRED) — so no single person can release failing material on their own authority. Release before evaluation is blocked (COA_NOT_EVALUATED); a decided CoA cannot be re-actioned (COA_NOT_HELD); reject holds the lot (release_status='rejected', never released). DETECTIVE side: GET /api/quality/coa/out-of-spec is the deviation register — CoAs that FAILED spec yet were released — exactly the population an auditor samples. The recorder duty (quality) is segregated from the release-approver duty (quality_approve) — SoD R21. Tenant-scoped (canonical 0232 RLS); references lot_no as text (the read-only lot ledger is never rewritten).
controls.code_reference: modules/quality/coa.service.ts (createSpec range guard; createCoa; addResults per-row pass/fail vs [spec_min,spec_max]; evaluate overall_result=fail if any characteristic out of spec; release QC-03 gate — DEVIATION_APPROVER_REQUIRED / SOD_SELF_APPROVAL / DEVIATION_REASON_REQUIRED / COA_NOT_EVALUATED / COA_NOT_HELD; reject; outOfSpecRegister); coa.controller.ts (/api/quality/specs, /api/quality/coa[/:id/results|evaluate|release|reject], /api/quality/coa/out-of-spec — reads gated quality/exec, record quality/exec, out-of-spec release quality_approve/exec); schema/quality-coa.ts quality_specs/coa_certificates/coa_results + migration 0333 (RLS, tenant-leading indexes, coa_no/spec_no unique per tenant); permissions.ts quality/quality_approve + SoD R21; cutover/quality-coa.ts (ToE, 16 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the per-item spec range, the per-characteristic actual-vs-[min,max] pass/fail computation, the evaluate overall_result rule (any fail → fail), the out-of-spec release gate (approver duty + released_by ≠ created_by + mandatory deviation_reason), and the out-of-spec deviation register.
controls.toe: Re-perform (quality-coa harness): create a spec (min 10 / max 14); an in-spec CoA (actual 12) evaluates pass and its recorder releases it; an out-of-spec CoA (actual 18) evaluates fail; the recorder self-releasing the fail → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a distinct approver releasing without a reason → 400 DEVIATION_REASON_REQUIRED; the distinct approver releasing WITH a reason → released (deviation); reject holds a fail lot; the out-of-spec register lists only the released fail; a second tenant sees none of the HQ CoAs and cannot read one (RLS).
controls.evidence: Quality-spec catalogue; CoA + measured-result register; out-of-spec deviation register (released fails); SoD self-approval block

controls.risk: A POS sale is not attributed to its originating branch, so the consolidated branch roll-up is incomplete and cannot be tied to GL revenue.
controls.description: Branch sale-tagging + HQ consolidation: every POS sale carries cust_pos_sales.branch_id; GET /api/branches/consolidated aggregates per branch (excluding Voided) and surfaces untagged sales as branch '(none)' — a deliberate completeness flag the controller investigates and clears before tying the consolidated total to GL revenue.
controls.code_reference: branches.service.ts (consolidated, branch tagging); branches.controller.ts; cust_pos_sales.branch_id; cutover/branch.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect branch tagging completeness + the '(none)' surfacing + the GL tie.
controls.toe: Sample period: tagged sales group by branch, an untagged sale surfaces as '(none)', consolidated total ties to posted revenue.
controls.evidence: Consolidation report; '(none)' investigation log

controls.risk: An offline terminal sells at a stale or wrong price/promotion because the cached catalog drifted from the master.
