controls.description: Offline master-bundle export: GET /api/branches/master-bundle returns ONLY the active price-list + active promotions, time-stamped (generated_at). The offline terminal sells from this bundle, so it cannot ring a stale/withdrawn price or promo.
controls.code_reference: branches.service.ts masterBundle (active price-list + active promos + generated_at); branches.controller.ts; cutover/branch.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the bundle exports only active prices/promos with a timestamp.
controls.toe: Re-perform: export the bundle; confirm it carries only active price-list/promotions + generated_at.
controls.evidence: Bundle metadata; bundle vs price-master diff

controls.risk: An offline-captured sale is LOST or DUPLICATED when connectivity returns — revenue understated (lost) or double-counted (duplicated), and the GL double-posted.
controls.description: Offline-to-online exactly-once sync: offline-captured sales replay idempotently on (tenant, client_uuid) via the pos_offline_sync dedup ledger. Two paths share the ledger — the portal/inventory POS (POST /api/portal/pos/offline-sync, item_id lines) and the touch register (POST /api/restaurant/offline-sync, menu sku lines, re-running the restaurant order→checkout so the server re-prices + 86-checks). A re-sent batch returns 'duplicate' (no second sale/GL); a failed op is a RETRYABLE tombstone (sale_no NULL) that never blocks a later replay, so a transient error is not a lost sale; each op runs in its own savepoint so one bad sale never poisons the batch.
controls.code_reference: portal/offline-sync.service.ts (item_id path); restaurant/offline-sync.service.ts (register sku path, reuses dineIn create+checkout); pos_offline_sync dedup ledger; web lib/offline-pos.ts + lib/register-offline.ts; cutover/offline-sync.ts + restaurant-offline.ts (ToE) + e2e/register-offline.spec.ts
controls.tod: Inspect the (tenant, client_uuid) dedup gate (sale_no NOT NULL), the per-op savepoint isolation, and the retryable-failure tombstone.
controls.toe: Re-perform: sync a batch (all synced, distinct SALE-), replay it (all duplicate, no double sale/GL), a bad op fails while neighbours sync, same uuid twice in a batch posts once, a transient failure replays to synced after the cause clears (re-performed by the offline-sync + restaurant-offline harnesses).
controls.evidence: Sync recon report; terminal vs ledger counts; idempotency tests

controls.risk: A sale captured on an in-store hub (LAN-first store server) never reaches the central cloud ledger, reaches it twice, or reaches it altered — central revenue/GL incomplete, double-counted, or unfaithful to what the store rang.
controls.description: Hub→cloud replay reconciliation (docs/41 Phase 2a): the hub pusher (db:hub:push) reconstructs each hub-captured restaurant sale from its originating order (lines + modifiers + discount/tip/service-charge), signs the batch HMAC-SHA256 with the shared HUB_SYNC_SECRET, and delivers to POST /api/hub/ingest — which verifies the signature timing-safe (fail-closed when the secret is unset) and replays through the SAME idempotent register offline-sync path (BRANCH-03 dedup on (tenant, client_uuid); the client_uuid is DETERMINISTIC hub:{tenant}:{hub_sale_no}, so any re-push lands 'duplicate' — exactly-once even after a lost push-log). The cloud re-prices authoritatively and posts GL on the cloud ledger (book of record). Every outcome is recorded in the hub-side hub_push_log; a sale the pusher cannot faithfully replay (buffet tier, loyalty redemption, no order linkage) is logged 'skipped_unsupported' WITH its reason — a visible exception queue, never a silent drop. GET /api/hub/reconciliation ties hub-ingested ops and their cloud sale values back per device/period for review.
controls.code_reference: modules/hub/hub-sync.service.ts (ingest + reconciliation); database/hub-push.ts (pusher, hub_push_log, migration 0293); restaurant/offline-sync.service.ts (replay target); cutover/hub-snapshot.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the HMAC verification (fail-closed, timing-safe), the deterministic client_uuid derivation, and the skipped_unsupported exception rows.
controls.toe: Re-perform: ring sales on a hub, push (cloud sale + GL posted, TB balanced), delete the hub push-log and re-push (all duplicate, zero new cloud ops), tamper the batch (403 HUB_SYNC_BAD_SIGNATURE), an unsupported sale surfaces as skipped_unsupported with a reason, and a buffet-tier session rung on the hub replays with the charge re-priced from the cloud package master (re-performed by the hub-snapshot harness).
controls.evidence: Hub push log; reconciliation report; idempotency + tamper tests

controls.risk: A cash session (till/Z-report) captured on an in-store hub is certified against an INCOMPLETE or hub-asserted revenue population — the drawer variance is meaningless, a cash shortfall is hidden, or the over/short never reaches the central ledger.
controls.description: Hub till/Z-report ingest with cloud-side re-computation (docs/41 Phase 2c): the hub pushes the session envelope (float, physical closing count, cash movements, denominations) PLUS the hub sale numbers rung during the session; it never sends its own expected-cash figure. The cloud resolves EVERY listed sale through the BRANCH-04 dedup ledger and REFUSES the session (400 TILL_SALES_NOT_SYNCED, listing the missing sales) unless all of them have already replayed — a variance is never certified over a partial revenue population. It then computes expected = opening_float + SUM(cash tender amount + tip) + paid_in - paid_out - drops - cash_refunds from its OWN rows: the TENDER (recorded by the replayed checkout with the real method) says what entered the drawer, so a card/PromptPay sale in the session cannot inflate the expectation, and the tip is added back because payments.amount excludes it while the drawer receives it (matching the 1000 Cash debit), derives variance = counted - expected, and posts the over/short to 5830 on the SAME materiality line as a native close (REV-13): a variance above the threshold posts a DRAFT journal and parks the session PendingApproval for a DIFFERENT user to approve (GL-05 maker-checker); sub-threshold posts immediately. Idempotent on session_no (re-push returns duplicate; no second JE). Blocked/failed sessions stay in hub_push_log with their reason. Phase 4a: each hub reports a signed heartbeat (liveness, un-replayed backlog, failed/skipped counts, clock skew measured against the cloud clock); GET /api/hub/fleet flags a stale or backlogged box so un-replayed cash cannot sit unnoticed.
controls.code_reference: modules/hub/hub-sync.service.ts (ingestTill, heartbeat, fleet); database/hub-push.ts (pushHubTills, sendHubHeartbeat); hub_heartbeats + hub_push_log.doc_type (migration 0296); payments.service.ts CASH_VARIANCE_THRESHOLD (shared materiality); cutover/hub-snapshot.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the TILL_SALES_NOT_SYNCED completeness gate, the cloud-side expected-cash re-computation (hub figures never trusted), the shared materiality threshold + maker-checker parking, and the heartbeat staleness/backlog flags.
controls.toe: Re-perform: close a till on a hub while one covered sale is un-replayed (session BLOCKED, logged with reason), resolve and re-push (session ingests; expected cash re-derived from the cloud ledger; immaterial short posts 5830 immediately; TB balanced), re-push (idempotent, no second JE), tamper the envelope (403), and confirm the heartbeat surfaces the backlog and is tenant-isolated (re-performed by the hub-snapshot harness).
controls.evidence: Hub push log (doc_type='till'); till_sessions + 5830 postings; hub fleet/heartbeat report

controls.risk: Kitchen waste recorded on an in-store hub never reaches the central ledger: the expense (5810) and the inventory relief (1200) exist only on the box, so central COGS is understated, the inventory position drifts, and the shrinkage signal an HQ controller most wants — reason-coded waste by store — is invisible.
controls.description: Hub waste replay (docs/41 Phase 2c-2): the hub pusher sends each waste_log document (item, qty, reason code, unit cost) signed HMAC-SHA256 to POST /api/hub/ingest-waste, which verifies the signature timing-safe (fail-closed when HUB_SYNC_SECRET is unset) and replays it through the SAME WasteService the cloud uses natively — so every native guard applies: positive qty, a known reason code, and the INV-07 perpetual-item guard (a perpetual-tracked item must go through the approved write-off, so such a document fails LOUDLY and stays visible in hub_push_log rather than relieving inventory off-control). The hub's waste_no IS the document identity on both ledgers, so a re-push returns the stored row (duplicate) and neither decrements stock nor posts GL twice. Valuation: unit_cost is the hub kitchen's ingredient cost; the cloud has no independent basis to re-derive it for a non-perpetual ingredient, so it is accepted and carried into the GL (stated, not silently assumed).
controls.code_reference: modules/hub/hub-sync.service.ts (ingestWaste); database/hub-push.ts (pushHubWaste, hub_push_log doc_type='waste'); inventory/waste.service.ts (logWaste with an existing waste_no, idempotent); cutover/hub-snapshot.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the HMAC verification, the waste_no idempotency short-circuit, and that the ingest re-runs the native WasteService guards (incl. INV-07).
controls.toe: Re-perform: log waste on a hub (Dr 5810 / Cr 1200 locally), push it (same waste_no + qty + cost on the cloud, GL posted, TB balanced), re-push (nothing collected; a forced re-send is a duplicate that posts nothing), tamper the envelope (403) — re-performed by the hub-snapshot harness.
controls.evidence: hub_push_log (doc_type='waste'); waste_log + 5810/1200 postings; waste-by-reason report

controls.risk: A physical count posted on an in-store hub never reaches the central inventory records, or a machine replay LAUNDERS a self-approved count into them: the counter who found the variance also posted it, and the segregation the control depends on is erased by the sync principal.
controls.description: Hub stocktake replay with SoD preserved (docs/41 Phase 2c-2): only POSTED count sheets are pushed (a Draft count is not yet evidence), each carrying BOTH humans — counted_by and posted_by (persisted by migration 0318, since the native post previously stored only the counter). The signed document goes to POST /api/hub/ingest-stocktake, which verifies the HMAC timing-safe (fail-closed on HUB_SYNC_SECRET) and REFUSES the sheet when the two names are equal or missing (403/400 STOCKTAKE_NOT_SEGREGATED) — the machine principal hub-sync can never post a count it would have been forbidden to post as a human (SoD R11 / INV-04). Accepted sheets are recreated with the hub's st_no and produce the same Stock In/Out variance movements a native post produces; idempotent on (tenant, st_no) so a re-push creates no second movement. Blocked sheets stay in hub_push_log with STOCKTAKE_NOT_SEGREGATED for review — never silently dropped, never silently accepted.
controls.code_reference: modules/hub/hub-sync.service.ts (ingestStocktake); stock-ops/stock-ops.service.ts (ingestHubStocktake, postStocktake records posted_by); database/hub-push.ts (pushHubStocktakes, hub_push_log doc_type='stocktake'); migration 0318; cutover/hub-snapshot.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the two-name requirement + the equality refusal, the st_no idempotency, and that the replay creates the same variance movements as a native post.
controls.toe: Re-perform: on a hub post a sheet counted by A and posted by B, and a second sheet counted and posted by A; push — the first lands (both names on the cloud document, one Stock Out movement), the second is BLOCKED with STOCKTAKE_NOT_SEGREGATED and logged; re-push creates no second movement (re-performed by the hub-snapshot harness).
controls.evidence: stocktakes.counted_by/posted_by on both ledgers; hub_push_log (doc_type='stocktake'); variance movements

controls.risk: Unauthorized BOM/recipe change mis-states standard cost and every downstream WIP/COGS posting; or the role that maintains the recipe also transacts on it.
controls.description: BOM master maker-checker / SoD: the master BOM library (bom_master) is maintained + distributed by HQ separately from transacting; a tenant BOM submission is reviewed and merged by HQ on approval (a maker-checker over master data). Recipe roll-up (line cost + labor + overhead → cost per unit) is the standard-cost basis. Master-data access is segregated from production (R13).
controls.code_reference: bom master + submissions (/api/bom/master, /api/bom/submissions, /api/portal/bom); manufacturing.service.ts BOM scaling; cutover/cpq.ts + manufacturing.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect BOM maintenance access + the submission→approve maker-checker + the costing roll-up.
controls.toe: Sample BOM change is HQ-approved before use; standard cost rolls up correctly.
controls.evidence: BOM approval log; costing roll-up

controls.risk: Production cost is mis-stated: WIP not relieved, the conversion absorption left stranded, or a yield loss (spoilage/waste) silently capitalised into finished-goods inventory instead of expensed.
controls.description: Production work-order WIP costing with a balanced JE at each step: ISSUE posts Dr 1250 WIP / Cr 1200 raw materials / Cr 2380 manufacturing-costs-applied (idempotent — re-issue is a no-op); COMPLETE posts Dr 1210 Finished Goods at the standard cost of the ACTUAL quantity produced + books the YIELD VARIANCE to 5810 (produced < planned → debit 5810 loss; over-yield → credit) / Cr 1250 WIP — so WIP is always fully relieved and FG is never over-valued on a short yield. State machine Open→Released→Completed (out-of-order → BAD_STATUS). The 2380/1250 clearing balances tie out at close. Scrap/rework write-off of defective output posts to 5810 with CostAccountant authorization.
controls.code_reference: manufacturing.service.ts issue/complete (yield variance to 5810; fmt exposes yield_variance); manufacturing.controller.ts (bom_master/warehouse/exec); schema/manufacturing.ts work_orders; cutover/manufacturing.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the issue/complete JEs + the standard-unit-cost FG valuation + the yield-variance plug + the status guard.
controls.toe: Re-perform: issue (WIP charged), complete at full yield (FG = full cost, no variance) and at a short yield (FG = std×produced, the lost yield to 5810, WIP nets to 0, TB balanced) — re-performed by the harness.
controls.evidence: WO costing register (yield_variance per WO); production JEs

controls.risk: Standard-cost inventory received without isolating the price variance — actual purchase cost buried in inventory, COGS and PPV mis-stated.
controls.description: Goods-receipt capitalisation under the configured costing method (FIFO/AVG/STD per tenant+item): FIFO/AVG → Dr 1200 / Cr 2000 at actual; STD → Dr 1200 at standard, the purchase-price variance booked to 5500 (unfavourable Dr / favourable Cr), Cr 2000 at actual — the PPV (5500) leg is the single balancing plug (= actual − standard, rounded once) so the JE can never reject UNBALANCED on independent per-leg rounding.
controls.code_reference: costing.service.ts (config/valuation, GRV capitalisation + 5500 PPV); costing.controller.ts (masterdata); cutover/costing.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the per-method capitalisation routing + the PPV plug.
controls.toe: Re-perform a STD receipt at actual ≠ standard → PPV isolated to 5500, JE balanced; valuation ties to GL 1200/1210/1250.
controls.evidence: PPV postings; costing valuation

controls.risk: A standard-cost item's standard is set once and never rolled: as real cost drifts, on-hand inventory (valued at the stale standard) is mis-stated and COGS on issue is wrong; OR one person unilaterally re-values inventory by editing the standard, with no independent review of the revaluation that hits the GL.
controls.description: Standard-cost roll / inventory revaluation under MAKER-CHECKER (INV-4). A periodic standard-cost revision is PROPOSED per STD-costed item (a Draft header + per-item lines capturing old_std, the proposed new_std, and an on-hand SNAPSHOT), computing the revaluation = on_hand x (new_std - old_std) with NO posting. A DISTINCT approver (approved_by != prepared_by -> 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) approves it -> for every line the stored standard is rolled forward (so subsequent issues cost at the NEW standard) and a BALANCED revaluation JE posts: standard rose -> Dr 1200 Inventory / Cr 5500 std-cost variance (favourable); standard fell -> Cr 1200 / Dr 5500 (unfavourable) - the two legs are equal by construction so the JE never rejects UNBALANCED. Draft -> Approved only; a non-STD item is rejected (STD_ITEM_REQUIRED); re-approval of an Approved revision is refused (NOT_DRAFT - no double post). The preparer duty is md_config (masterdata); the approver duty is exec - the standing md_config<->exec SoD conflict (MDM-01 rule) already prevents one person holding both, and the in-app self-approval block is the runtime control.
controls.code_reference: modules/costing/std-cost.service.ts (revise snapshots on-hand + STD_ITEM_REQUIRED guard; approve rolls item_costing.standard_cost + posts the 1200<->5500 revaluation JE via LedgerService.postEntry, idempotent on STDREV/rev_no; SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + NOT_DRAFT guards); costing.controller.ts (std-cost/revise @masterdata, std-cost[/:no] @masterdata/exec/planner, std-cost/:no/approve @exec); schema/std-cost.ts std_cost_revisions/std_cost_revision_lines (migration 0341); cutover/std-cost-roll.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the revise->approve maker-checker + the on-hand snapshot + the revaluation formula + the 1200/5500 JE routing + the preparer!=approver check + the roll-forward of the stored standard.
controls.toe: Re-perform: revise a STD item (on-hand snapshotted, nothing posted); the preparer self-approves -> 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a distinct approver approves -> Dr 1200/Cr 5500 (or the reverse on a drop), the standard rolls forward, a subsequent issue costs at the new standard, TB balanced; a non-STD item is rejected (STD_ITEM_REQUIRED) and re-approval refused (NOT_DRAFT) - re-performed by the harness.
controls.evidence: Std-cost revision register (old/new std, on-hand snapshot, revaluation, prepared_by/approved_by); revaluation JEs (STDREV)

controls.risk: A failed quality inspection or a customer/supplier complaint is dispositioned by whoever raised it, and a Scrap/Use-as-is/Return decision writes the defective stock off to loss (Dr 5810) with NO independent approval — a single person can create and dispose of a non-conformance, concealing shrink or writing off good stock.
controls.description: Non-Conformance (NCR) register with maker-checker disposition (QMS-1). A failed inspection (or a customer/supplier complaint) is raised as a first-class NCR (open → pending_disposition → dispositioned → closed); a FINANCIAL disposition (scrap / use-as-is / return) is proposed as pending_disposition and applied — and the scrap write-off posted (Dr 5810 loss / Cr the source inventory account: WO→1250 WIP, GR→1200 raw materials, else→1210 finished goods) — ONLY when a DIFFERENT user approves (dispositioned_by ≠ raised_by → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL). Reject returns the NCR to open. Raising is the `quality` duty; the disposition is the `quality_approve`/exec duty, segregated by SoD R21. Every status transition is written to the doc_status_log audit trail.
controls.code_reference: modules/mfg-depth/ncr.service.ts (raiseNcr/promoteInspection/disposition/reject; scrap GL via LedgerService.postEntry); mfg-depth.controller.ts NcrController (/api/quality/ncr, /api/quality/defect-codes; quality vs quality_approve); schema/quality-ncr.ts non_conformances/defect_codes (migration 0331); permissions.ts SoD R21; cutover/quality-ncr.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the NCR maker-checker (raise → pending → a DIFFERENT approver dispositions) + the scrap write-off JE + the SoD R21 duty split + the status-log trail.
controls.toe: Re-perform: raise an NCR proposing scrap → pending_disposition; the raiser self-dispositions → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a distinct approver dispositions scrap → Dr 5810 / Cr 1250 posted, entry_no recorded, TB balanced; reject → NCR returns to open (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: NCR register (status, dispositioned_by, entry_no); scrap write-off JEs; doc_status_log

controls.risk: Project cost mis-stated: a recoverable cost not captured, or an UN-recoverable (non-billable) cost capitalised into project WIP as an asset that overstates the unbilled balance and inflates margin.
controls.description: Project cost capture with a balanced JE and a billable/non-billable split: a BILLABLE time/expense cost is a recoverable asset → Dr 1260 Project WIP / Cr 2390 Project Costs Applied (accumulates in cost_to_date, relieved to COGS at billing); a NON-BILLABLE cost is unrecoverable → EXPENSED immediately Dr 5800 Project Cost of Services / Cr 2390 (never enters the billable WIP), so the register's total_cost and true margin (billed − recognised − non-billable) reflect the absorbed cost. A non-positive amount → BAD_AMOUNT; first cost flips Open→Active.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts logCost (billable→1260, non-billable→5800; fmt exposes non_billable_cost/total_cost/margin); projects.controller.ts; schema/projects.ts project_entries.billable; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the billable/non-billable GL routing + the WIP vs immediate-expense decision + the margin roll-up.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a billable cost capitalises to 1260; a non-billable cost expenses to 5800 (NOT WIP), cost_to_date unchanged, and the project margin absorbs it (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Project cost JEs; cost register (non_billable_cost)

controls.risk: Revenue billed without matching cost — WIP not relieved, cost of services mis-stated, margin overstated.
controls.description: Customer billing recognises revenue (Dr 1100 AR / Cr 4200 Project Revenue) and relieves the unbilled WIP to cost of services up to the unrecognised billable cost (Dr 5800 / Cr 1260) in the same event — matching cost to recognised revenue. Billing is by raw amount (T&M) or by PERCENT of the contract for Fixed-price milestone billing; a Fixed-price contract is CAPPED at the contract value (cumulative billing can never exceed it — BILL_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT), so the customer is never over-billed (billed_pct/remaining_to_bill on the register). Billing is idempotent on (project, cumulative billed amount).
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts bill (revenue + WIP relief, idempotent); projects.controller.ts (R07 vs cost initiation); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the bill JE + the WIP-relief matching + idempotency.
controls.toe: Re-perform a billing → revenue recognised, WIP relieved to 5800 up to unrecognised cost, 1260 reduced; margin = billed − recognised cost (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Billing JEs; margin/WIP measurement

controls.risk: Unbilled WIP (1260) and the 2390 clearing balance not reviewed at close — stale or unrecoverable WIP carried into the financial statements.
controls.description: Period-end review of unbilled-WIP (1260) aging and the project-costs-applied (2390) clearing tie-out; project margin (billed_to_date − recognised_cost − non-billable) reviewed at close. A PERIOD CLOSE-REVIEW SIGN-OFF (POST /api/projects/close-review) snapshots the WIP(1260) total + the 2390 clearing balance + the open-project count as a Prepared review; an INDEPENDENT approver (SoD: approver ≠ preparer) signs off (Approved) — the auditable record that WIP + clearing were reviewed at close, retained per (tenant, period). SoD: billing authorisation is segregated from cost initiation (R07).
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts (closeSnapshot 1260/2390; prepareCloseReview/approveCloseReview maker-checker); projects.controller.ts (POST /api/projects/close-review[/:period/approve|reject], GET /close-reviews — gated 'exec'); schema/projects.ts project_close_reviews + drizzle/0206_project_close_review.sql; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect 1260 aging + 2390 clearing + margin review evidence + the signed close review.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a close review snapshots WIP(1260)+2390 clearing+open projects (Prepared); the preparer cannot self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION); an independent approver signs off (Approved) — re-performed by the projects harness. Sample projects: 1260 WIP agrees to cost_to_date − recognised; 2390 clears; stale WIP investigated.
controls.evidence: WIP/clearing close-review sign-off

controls.risk: A sales opportunity is delivered without an authorized, traceable hand-off: a project is spun up from a deal that was never won (an open or lost deal), or the same won deal spawns duplicate projects — so project revenue / WIP cannot be traced back to an approved, won opportunity and the deal-to-delivery (win/loss) link is unreliable.
controls.description: Opportunity→project conversion is gated and traceable: POST /api/projects/from-opportunity/:oppNo converts ONLY a WON crm_opportunity (an open/lost deal → OPP_NOT_WON; an unknown deal → OPP_NOT_FOUND), seeds the project contract from the deal value, and stamps customer_no (→ customer_master) + crm_opp_no (→ crm_opportunities.opp_no). Conversion is IDEMPOTENT on crm_opp_no — a given opportunity converts to at most one project, so a re-submit returns the existing project rather than duplicating it. Win/loss integrity (controlled stage machine, mandatory lost reason, terminal won/lost) is enforced upstream by REV-17.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts createFromOpportunity (won-only + idempotent on crm_opp_no); projects.controller.ts POST from-opportunity/:oppNo; schema/projects.ts projects.customer_no/crm_opp_no (migration 0183); crm-pipeline.service.ts (REV-17 stage machine); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the won-only guard, the customer_no/crm_opp_no stamping, and the idempotency key on crm_opp_no.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a won opportunity converts to a project seeded with the deal value + crm_opp_no; re-converting returns the same project (no duplicate); an open deal → OPP_NOT_WON; an unknown deal → OPP_NOT_FOUND (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Conversion audit (project.crm_opp_no → opportunity); won-only / idempotency rejections

controls.risk: Project labor is estimated/charged at an ungoverned or arbitrary rate, or a resource is committed beyond capacity — so planned project cost is unreliable and delivery is over-committed (margin erosion / schedule slip).
controls.description: Resource cost/bill rates are governed by an effective-dated rate card (resource_rates): a project resource assignment resolves and SNAPSHOTS the applicable rate (latest effective_from on/before the assignment date, with effective_to open or on/after), so labor cost/bill estimates trace to an authorized rate (no guessed rate — zero when none is on file). Allocation % is validated to (0,100] (BAD_ALLOC), and a capacity/utilization roll-up sums allocation per resource across projects and flags >100% over-allocation for review.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts addRateCard/resolveRate (effective-dated), assignResource (snapshot + BAD_ALLOC), resourceUtilization (over-allocation); projects.controller.ts (rate-cards, :code/resources, resources/utilization); schema/projects.ts resource_rates/project_resources (migration 0185); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the rate-card effective-dating + the snapshot-at-assignment + the allocation guard + the utilization roll-up.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an assignment snapshots the rate-card rate (zero when none on file); alloc_pct 0 → BAD_ALLOC; a resource booked 60%+60% → utilization 120%, over_allocated (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Rate card; resource assignments (snapshot rate); utilization report

controls.risk: Project labor is charged to a project without independent authorization — a person logs time and the same person posts it to project WIP — so labor cost / unbilled WIP can be inflated or misallocated with no review.
controls.description: Timesheet→project labor is maker-checker: a timesheet is submitted Pending (submitted_by recorded) and a DIFFERENT user must approve it (a submitter approving their own → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin). On approval, if the timesheet targets a project, its labor cost (total hours × the employee's hourly rate) posts ONCE into project WIP through the authorized PRJ-COST path (billable → Dr 1260 / Cr 2390; non-billable → Dr 5800 / Cr 2390); re-approving an already-approved timesheet does not double-post (idempotent). An unknown timesheet → TIMESHEET_NOT_FOUND.
controls.code_reference: hcm.service.ts approveTimesheet (SoD + posts via ProjectsService.logCost); hcm.controller.ts POST timesheets/:id/approve; schema/hcm.ts timesheets.project_id/billable/status/submitted_by/approved_by (migration 0186); projects.service.ts logCost (PRJ-COST); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the submitter≠approver guard + the single-post-on-approval into project WIP.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a submitter self-approving → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an independent approver posts hours×rate to project WIP once; re-approval does not double-post (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Timesheet approved_by/submitted_by; PRJ-COST JE; SOD_SELF_APPROVAL rejection

controls.risk: Project cost / schedule performance is not measured, so an overrun or slippage is detected late — actual cost diverges from earned value with no CPI/SPI signal, and project WIP (1260 actuals) is not reconciled to the work actually earned.
controls.description: Earned-value management: BAC / PV / EV / AC are derived from the project's WBS tasks (planned cost, % complete, planned_end schedule) and its actual cost incurred (cost_to_date + non-billable = the 1260 WIP + 5800 actuals), yielding CPI = EV/AC (cost), SPI = EV/PV (schedule), cost & schedule variance, and EAC/ETC at-completion forecasts. The EVM endpoint reconciles earned value to the project's actual cost (AC ties to the WIP/COGS actuals), giving a detective signal of cost overrun / schedule slip for review.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts evm (BAC/PV/EV/AC → CPI/SPI/EAC; AC = cost_to_date + non-billable); projects.controller.ts GET :code/evm; schema/projects.ts project_tasks (planned_cost/pct_complete/planned_end; depends_on migration 0187); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the EV/PV/AC derivation + the CPI/SPI/variance math + the AC-to-WIP reconciliation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: tasks 1000@100% (scheduled past) + 1000@0% (future) with AC 900 → BAC 2000, EV 1000, PV 1000, CPI 1.1111, SPI 1.0, EAC ~1800 (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: EVM report (CPI/SPI/variances/EAC); WIP (1260) reconciliation

controls.risk: A project silently moves its goalposts: the plan (scope/budget/schedule) is re-baselined with no authorization or audit trail, so variance reporting always looks on-target and scope/cost creep is masked.
controls.description: Change-controlled baselines: a project baseline snapshots the approved BAC + critical-path duration at a point in time. The FIRST baseline is free; RE-baselining REQUIRES a reason (BASELINE_REASON_REQUIRED) and supersedes the prior active baseline (history preserved — status active→superseded, captured_by/at recorded), so the goalposts can't move silently. Variance of the CURRENT plan vs the active baseline (bac_delta / bac_pct / duration_delta) surfaces scope/cost creep for review.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts captureBaseline (reason-gated re-baseline + supersede history) / getBaseline (variance vs active) / currentPlan; projects.controller.ts POST+GET :code/baseline; schema/projects.ts project_baselines (migration 0188); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reason-gated re-baseline, the history (active→superseded), and the current-vs-baseline variance.
controls.toe: Re-perform: capture a baseline (BAC 2000); add scope → variance +500/+25%; re-baseline without a reason → BASELINE_REASON_REQUIRED; with a reason → new active (BAC 2500), variance 0, history 2 (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Baseline history (active/superseded, reason, captured_by); variance report

controls.risk: A project's threats and problems are not governed: an open HIGH risk (or a materialised issue) goes unmitigated and unreviewed, so a foreseeable cost/schedule overrun is buried instead of surfaced for action at review/close.
controls.description: Project risk & issue register: each risk (probability × impact) / issue (5 × impact) is scored 1–25 with a derived RAG, an owner, a mitigation plan and a due date. The register summary and the portfolio top-risks roll-up surface OPEN HIGH (red) items and, specifically, the UNMITIGATED HIGH subset (open high with no mitigation), so an unmitigated exposure is escalated for review — not silently carried.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts addRisk / listRisks (summary high_open + unmitigated_high) / patchRisk (re-score, close) / topRisks (portfolio roll-up); projects.controller.ts POST+GET :code/risks, PATCH risks/:id, GET risks/top; schema/projects.ts project_risks (migration 0192); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the score/RAG derivation, the open-high and unmitigated-high counts, and the portfolio top-risks roll-up.
controls.toe: Re-perform: log a high risk (prob 5 × impact 5 = 25, red) with no mitigation → summary unmitigated_high 1; add a mitigation + close a risk → counts fall; portfolio top-risks ranks the open red item first (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Risk register (RAG, owner, mitigation, due); portfolio top-risks roll-up

controls.fsli: Project Revenue (4200); Contract Asset (1265); Contract Liability (2410); Project WIP (1260); COGS (5800)
controls.risk: Long-duration fixed-price project revenue is recognised only when an invoice is raised (point-in-time), so revenue/cost are mismatched across periods and earnings are mis-stated — or an over-time recognition is computed on an arbitrary/ungoverned basis, letting a single user pull revenue forward.
controls.description: Over-time (percentage-of-completion) revenue recognition on a cost-to-cost basis (TFRS 15 / IFRS 15 / ASC 606 over-time): for a rev_method='poc' fixed-price project, POC% = cost_to_date / estimated_total_cost (EAC), capped at 100%; recognised revenue to date = contract × POC%, and the PERIOD revenue = that minus revenue already recognised — booked Dr 2410 (release any billings-in-excess first) then Dr 1265 Contract Asset / Cr 4200, with the period cost relieved Dr 5800 / Cr 1260. Billing is DECOUPLED from recognition: an invoice on a POC project posts Dr 1100 AR / Cr 1265 (clearing earned-but-unbilled), any excess over recognised revenue parked as a contract liability (2410, billings in excess). So the contract asset/liability position is always recognised-revenue vs billed, revenue is earned as work progresses (not when invoiced), and recognition is idempotent per (project, recognised total). Posts through the PERIOD_LOCKED + GL-audit gates; authorised (gl_post/exec). billing-method projects keep point-in-time recognition unchanged.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts recognizePoc (cost-to-cost %, contract asset/liability mechanics, idempotent) + bill (POC invoice path Dr 1100 / Cr 1265 / Cr 2410) + fmt (poc_pct, contract_asset, billings_in_excess, recognized_revenue); projects.controller.ts POST :code/recognize (gl_post/exec); schema/projects.ts rev_method/estimated_cost/recognized_revenue (migration 0194) + COA 1265; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the cost-to-cost %, the period-revenue delta vs recognised-to-date, the contract asset/liability postings, and that billing on a POC project recognises NO revenue.
controls.toe: Re-perform: POC project (contract 100000, est cost 60000); cost 30000 → recognise @ 50% revenue 50000 / cost 30000 (contract asset 50000); bill 40000 clears the asset (no revenue); cost to 60000 → recognise @ 100% (revenue 100000 total); bill-ahead → billings-in-excess 2410; recognise on a billing-method project → NOT_POC (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Contract asset/liability schedule; period revenue-recognition JEs (4200/1265/2410/5800/1260); POC% reconciliation

controls.risk: A project's contract value / scope is changed unilaterally or without an audit trail — a single person inflates the contract (revenue/billing ceiling) or budget over their own work, and the baseline still reads on-target so scope/price creep is invisible.
controls.description: Change orders are maker-checker: a contract/scope variation is a REQUEST (`pending`) that applies NOTHING; a DIFFERENT user must approve it (the approver ≠ the requester → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), at which point the contract/budget/EAC deltas are applied to the project AND a new baseline is auto-captured (reason = the change order), so the goalposts move only with authorization, segregation, and a preserved variance trail (ties to PROJ-07). An empty change order (no deltas) is rejected (EMPTY_CHANGE_ORDER); a decided change order can't be re-decided (CHANGE_ORDER_DECIDED).
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts createChangeOrder (pending, posts nothing, EMPTY_CHANGE_ORDER) / approveChangeOrder (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, applies deltas + auto re-baseline) / rejectChangeOrder / listChangeOrders; projects.controller.ts POST+GET :code/change-orders, POST change-orders/:id/approve|reject; schema/projects.ts project_change_orders (migration 0195); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the request→approve flow, the approver≠requester check, that a pending CO changes nothing, and the auto re-baseline on approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: request a +20000 contract change (pending, contract unchanged); self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a different user approves → contract 120000 + a new baseline; empty CO → EMPTY_CHANGE_ORDER; re-approve → CHANGE_ORDER_DECIDED (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Change-order register (pending/approved, requester/approver); the re-baseline on approval

controls.risk: Governance exceptions are scattered one project at a time, so a maker-checker approval (change order / project timesheet) sits unactioned, a project drifts red or over budget, a milestone slips, an open HIGH risk stays unmitigated, or an in-flight project runs with no baseline / stale health — buried until a periodic review instead of surfaced for action.
controls.description: PMO action center / exception inbox: a single read-only worklist (GET /api/projects/action-center) aggregates, across all the caller's projects, the exceptions that need a person — pending change orders + pending project timesheets awaiting a DIFFERENT approver (maker-checker, PROJ-04/10), open HIGH risks with no mitigation plan (PROJ-08), red projects (CPI/SPI < 0.9) and over-budget projects, overdue-and-unreached milestones, and governance gaps (an in-flight project with no change-controlled baseline (PROJ-07) or no health snapshot within `stale_days`). Each item carries a severity (high/medium/low) and deep-links to the offending project tab; the list is bounded to the caller's RLS-visible projects. It is PROACTIVE: a red health snapshot or a newly-logged unmitigated-HIGH risk publishes a `project_action` event to the live SSE bus so the inbox wakes without a manual refresh. Posts nothing — a detective surfacing layer over existing controls.
controls.code_reference: projects.service.ts actionCenter (aggregates over_budget/project_red/no_baseline/stale_health + pending change-orders/timesheets + slipping milestones + topRisks unmitigated-high) + emitAction (SSE on red/unmitigated-high, in snapProject + addRisk); projects.controller.ts GET action-center; bi-live.service.ts/bi-live.module.ts (shared BiLiveService bus); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the aggregation across the exception kinds, the severity/sort, the deep-links, and the proactive SSE emit on red/unmitigated-high.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed a project with one of every exception → the worklist surfaces all kinds with the right severity (high first); the red snapshot + the unmitigated-high risk push a project_action SSE event; approving the change order clears change_order_pending + no_baseline from the list (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: PMO action center worklist (severity-ranked, deep-linked); the project_action SSE feed

controls.risk: Material spend on a project exceeds its authorized Bill-of-Quantities budget — staff draw more material against a BoQ line than it is budgeted for, or two concurrent draws each pass an over-permissive check and jointly overrun, so the project silently blows its material budget before anyone reviews it.
controls.description: Commitment/encumbrance control on the BoQ line budget: a project purchase order tagged to a BoQ line RESERVES budget in the `project_commitments` ledger (open+consumed count against the line; released — e.g. a cancelled PO — frees it). A draw is admitted only if `line.budget_amount − Σ(open+consumed) ≥ amount`, checked ATOMICALLY under a FOR UPDATE row-lock on the BoQ line so two concurrent draws cannot jointly overrun (BUDGET_EXCEEDED with the remaining otherwise). The BoQ budget baseline is itself maker-checker (an author-approved BoQ; approver ≠ author → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL syncs the project budget). A cancelled PO releases its encumbrance; a fully-received PO's commitments become consumed (actual). An optional per-project over-budget TOLERANCE (projects.budget_tolerance_pct) lets a draw exceed the line by up to that % of the line budget before it is blocked/needs approval (ceiling = budget × (1+tol%)); 0 = strict. Turns the material budget from OBSERVED into ENFORCED.
controls.code_reference: commitments.service.ts reserve (FOR UPDATE lock on the BoQ line + committed vs budget → BUDGET_EXCEEDED) / release / consume / committedByLine / listForProject; procurement.service.ts createPo (reserve inside the PO tx → over-budget rolls the PO back), cancelPo (release), createGr (consume on full receipt); projects.service.ts getBoq (per-line budget/committed/remaining) + listCommitments; projects.controller.ts GET :code/commitments; schema/projects.ts project_commitments (migration 0237); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reserve→enforce path, the FOR UPDATE lock, the release-on-cancel/consume-on-receipt lifecycle, and the per-line remaining read model.
controls.toe: Re-perform: approve a BoQ line budget; a PO within budget reserves and lowers remaining; a further PO that would exceed the line → BUDGET_EXCEEDED (PO not created); cancel the first PO → budget released, remaining restored (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: BoQ line budget/committed/remaining; the project commitments ledger (open/consumed/released)

controls.risk: Site staff draw material that exceeds the project's authorized BoQ budget without an authorization gate — an over-budget requisition is fulfilled directly (staff self-approve the overage), or the draw silently bypasses the budget with no independent sign-off, so the project overruns before management is asked.
controls.description: Project Material Requisition (PMR) maker-checker over-budget gate: a requisition is evaluated line-by-line against each BoQ line's remaining budget (budget − open/consumed commitments). WITHIN budget it routes to a project-tagged PR for procurement; OVER budget it is parked `pending` and NOT fulfilled until an authoriser (procurement/exec, ≠ the requester → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) approves — routed both through the workflow engine (when a PMR definition is configured) and a one-tap LINE approval card (buildApproveCard postback → the same replay-safe confirm flow). On approval the overage is AUTHORISED and a project-tagged Draft PO is auto-drafted (commitment booked with allowOver) for procurement to review + buy; on rejection nothing is drawn. The pending PMR is surfaced on the PMO action center (`pmr_over_budget`, high).
controls.code_reference: pmr.service.ts submit (per-line remaining check → route pr|po) / approve (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, workflow act, auto-draft Draft PO authorized_over_budget) / reject; pmr.controller.ts POST /api/pmr [+ :pmrNo/approve|reject]; line-webhook.controller.ts chatDecidePmr (PMR- one-tap approval); commitments.service.ts reserve allowOver; procurement.service.ts createPo draft+authorized_over_budget; projects.service.ts actionCenter pmr_over_budget; schema/projects.ts project_material_requisitions+pmr_lines (migration 0239); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the within-vs-over routing, the maker-checker (approver ≠ requester), the LINE approval card, and the auto-drafted Draft PO carrying the authorised overage.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a within-budget PMR raises a project-tagged PR; an over-budget PMR parks pending (action center pmr_over_budget); requester self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an authoriser approves → a project-tagged Draft PO is drafted for the overage and the PMR clears (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: PMR register (routed/pending/approved/rejected); the over-budget approval + auto-drafted PO

controls.risk: Site cash spent on a project — employee advances, expense-reimbursement claims, petty-cash — is not attributed to the project, so project cost is understated: an advance or reimbursement is booked to a generic expense with no project tag and never shows on the project, defeating cost control and the project margin.
controls.description: Project-costed site cash: employee advances (finance.issueAdvance/settleAdvance), expense-reimbursement claims (ess.submitExpense) and petty-cash requests (petty-cash.createRequest/approveRequest) accept an optional project_code, resolve it to project_id (PROJECT_NOT_FOUND on a bad code), and store it on the entity; the GL expense/advance lines are tagged with that project_id (journal_lines.project_id) so the spend is traceable to the project. GET /api/projects/:code/site-cash rolls up all advances + reimbursements + petty-cash raised against the project with per-category + grand totals, so site cash is managed on the project (and reflected alongside EVM). When the site cash is tagged to a BoQ line it also CONSUMES that line's budget (a consumed commitment on advance settlement / petty-cash approval), so material + site cash sit under one budget ceiling. Advance settlement clears the float (Dr expense/Cr 1180) carrying the project dimension. Maker-checker on the underlying flows is unchanged (petty-cash SoD_VIOLATION, ESS self-approval block).
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts issueAdvance/settleAdvance (project_code → project_id, tag 1180/expense lines) + resolveProjectId; ess.service.ts submitExpense (project_id); petty-cash.service.ts createRequest/approveRequest (project_id on the debit line); projects.service.ts siteCash + projects.controller.ts GET :code/site-cash; schema employee_advances/expense_claims/expense_requests project_id (migration 0241); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the project_code resolution, the project_id tagging of the GL expense/advance lines, and the site-cash rollup.
controls.toe: Re-perform: issue a project-tagged advance (Dr 1180 carries project_id) → settle (expense line carries project_id); the project site-cash rollup lists the advance with the correct total; an unknown project_code → PROJECT_NOT_FOUND (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Project site-cash rollup (advances/reimbursements/petty-cash + totals); project_id on the GL lines

controls.fsli: Project material scope change (add item to budget)
controls.risk: A requester expands a project's authorized material budget without independent authorization — an item that is not on the approved Bill-of-Quantities is added to the budget (a new BoQ line) by the same person who wants to buy it, so the project's authorized material budget grows with no maker-checker sign-off and the shop-for-a-project budget restriction is defeated from the inside.
controls.description: Material scope-change authorisation (maker-checker on adding a BoQ line): a requisition-raiser (pr_raise) can only REQUEST that a material item not on the approved BoQ be added to the budget — POST /api/pmr/boq-request parks a `pending` request and posts nothing; it never adds the line itself (an item already on the approved BoQ is refused, ITEM_ALREADY_BUDGETED; a project with no approved BoQ is refused, NO_APPROVED_BOQ). Only an independent authoriser (planner/exec, ≠ the requester → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) may approve (POST …/boq-request/:reqNo/approve, routed through the workflow engine when configured); on approval a new material line is appended to the approved BoQ, the BoQ budget total is re-computed and the project budget synced (the item becomes shoppable and commitment-enforced under PROJ-12), and the requester is notified. Rejection changes nothing. This closes the shop-for-a-project loop: the /shop project surface lists only approved-budget items, and the only way to add one is through this authorised request → so a requester can propose budget but never expand it.
controls.code_reference: pmr.service.ts requestBoqItem (pending request; ITEM_ALREADY_BUDGETED / NO_APPROVED_BOQ guards) / approveBoqRequest (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, workflow act, append BoQ line + re-total budget + sync project budget) / rejectBoqRequest / listBoqRequests; pmr.controller.ts POST /api/pmr/boq-request [+ :reqNo/approve|reject], GET /api/pmr/project/:code/boq-requests; schema/projects.ts project_boq_change_requests (migration 0249); web /shop/project/[code] request-to-add form + planner/exec approve; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the request-only requester path, the ITEM_ALREADY_BUDGETED/NO_APPROVED_BOQ guards, the maker-checker (approver ≠ requester), and the append-line-and-grow-budget on approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a requester requests an off-budget item → pending (nothing added); the same requester approves → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an independent authoriser approves → a new BoQ line appears, the project budget grows, and the item is now shoppable; requesting an already-budgeted item → ITEM_ALREADY_BUDGETED (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Project BoQ change-request register (pending/approved/rejected); the appended BoQ line + grown budget

controls.fsli: Progress billing / retention (งวดงาน)
controls.risk: A construction progress claim bills work not actually done, or is raised and certified by the same person, or retention (เงินประกันผลงาน) is not withheld — so revenue/AR are overstated, the customer is over-billed beyond the contract, and the retained amount is not tracked as a separate receivable to be released later.
controls.description: Progress billing / งวดงาน maker-checker with retention (docs/35 P1): a progress claim values work done to date by BoQ line on a CUMULATIVE basis (value_to_date = boq_line.budget_amount × pct_complete_to_date/100, pct capped 0..100 → cannot certify beyond 100% of a line; value_this_claim = value_to_date − previously certified on prior CERTIFIED claims), so only the genuine movement since the last claim is billed and a line can never be over-certified. Certification is maker-checker — the certifier must differ from the preparer (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; new SoD rule R17, perms proj_billing vs proj_billing_certify) — and a draft-only guard (CLAIM_NOT_DRAFT) prevents re-certifying. On certify the claim posts a single balanced JE carrying the project dimension: Dr 1100 AR (net) + Dr 1170 Retention Receivable (retention = gross × retention_pct) + Cr 4200 Revenue (gross), relieving unbilled WIP Dr 5800 / Cr 1260; the retention is withheld into the shared retention sub-ledger (RetentionService.withhold, party customer, doc CLAIM) atomically in the same transaction. A Fixed-price contract cannot be certified beyond its contract value (BILL_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT), and certification is idempotent per claim (alreadyPosted PRJ-PCLAIM). RELEASE (docs/35 Depth-1): when a retention tranche falls due it is released back out of the retention account to the ordinary control account with a balanced JE (customer 1170 → 1100 AR), guarded against over-release (RETENTION_OVER_RELEASE) under a FOR UPDATE row-lock and idempotent on the cumulative released amount; an overdue tranche surfaces on the PMO action center as retention_due for the controller to action. TAX + REV-REC (docs/35 Depth-2/3): the claim carries OUTPUT VAT (vat_pct → Cr 2100) so AR = net + VAT; and the certification reconciles with the POC engine — on a rev_method='poc' project the claim is a BILLING event only (Dr 1100/1170 · Cr 1265 contract-asset / Cr 2410 billings-in-excess · Cr 2100), recognising NO revenue (recognizePoc does that over time — no double-count), while a billing-method project recognises revenue (Cr 4200) and relieves WIP as before.
controls.code_reference: progress-billing.service.ts createClaim (BoQ-line cumulative valuation, BAD_PERCENT/NOTHING_TO_BILL) / certifyClaim (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, CLAIM_NOT_DRAFT, BILL_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT, JE 1100/1170/4200/5800/1260 + RetentionService.withhold in one tx, idempotent); progress-billing.controller.ts POST /api/progress-billing [+ :claimNo/certify], GET project/:code|:claimNo; schema/projects.ts project_progress_claims+progress_claim_lines (migration 0253); retention_ledger (migration 0252); permissions.ts proj_billing/proj_billing_certify + SoD R17; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the cumulative BoQ-line valuation (no over-certification), the maker-checker certify (certifier ≠ preparer), the AR-net/retention-receivable/revenue split + WIP relief JE, the retention withheld into the sub-ledger, and the Fixed-contract cap.
controls.toe: Re-perform: raise a claim valuing BoQ lines (gross = Σ value_this_claim); preparer self-certify → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an independent certifier certifies → JE posts Dr 1100 net + Dr 1170 retention + Cr 4200 gross (WIP relieved to 5800/1260), retention appears on the project retention sub-ledger, and a second claim's value_this_claim nets off the previously certified value; certifying beyond a Fixed contract → BILL_EXCEEDS_CONTRACT (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Progress-claim register (draft/certified); the certification JE (1100/1170/4200/5800/1260); retention receivable in the sub-ledger

controls.risk: A subcontractor is over-paid — a progress valuation is raised and certified by the same person, work not done is certified, retention (เงินประกันผลงาน) is not withheld, or a subcontract commits more than the project's BoQ budget without an authorization gate — so project cost and AP are overstated and the retained amount is not tracked as a payable to release later.
controls.description: Subcontractor management maker-checker with retention payable + budget commitment (docs/35 P2): a subcontract is a priced scope against BoQ lines that on creation REGISTERS a commitment on each scoped BoQ line (docs/32 CommitmentsService, source SUBCON) so it counts against the works budget like a PO — over budget → BUDGET_EXCEEDED (the whole subcontract rolls back) unless explicitly authorised (allow_over). The subcontractor's periodic VALUATIONS certify the % complete on a CUMULATIVE basis (value_to_date = contract_value × pct/100, pct 0..100; gross_this_val = value_to_date − previously certified → only genuine progress; NOTHING_TO_CERTIFY otherwise; capped at the subcontract value → VAL_EXCEEDS_SUBCONTRACT). Certification is maker-checker — certifier ≠ preparer (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; new SoD rule R18, perms proj_subcon vs proj_subcon_certify) — with a draft-only guard (VALUATION_NOT_DRAFT). On certify a single balanced JE (project-dimensioned) posts: Dr 1260 Project WIP (gross − back_charge) / Cr 2000 AP (net = gross − retention − back_charge) / Cr 2440 Retention Payable (retention); the retention is withheld into the shared retention sub-ledger (RetentionService.withhold, party subcontractor, doc SUBVAL) atomically in the same transaction; back-charges (BAD_BACK_CHARGE if they exceed the net) recover our cost; idempotent per valuation (alreadyPosted PRJ-SUBVAL). RELEASE (docs/35 Depth-1): when a retention tranche falls due it is released out of the retention account to the ordinary control account with a balanced JE (subcontractor 2440 → 2000 AP), over-release-guarded (RETENTION_OVER_RELEASE) and idempotent; an overdue tranche surfaces on the action center as retention_due. WHT (docs/35 Depth-2): the certified valuation withholds Thai construction WITHHOLDING TAX (ภ.ง.ด.53, wht_pct → Cr 2361 Vendor WHT Payable) from the subcontractor's payment, so AP = net − WHT (retention already out) and the WHT is remitted to the RD; a vat_pct also books recoverable INPUT VAT (ภาษีซื้อ, Dr 1300) so AP = net − WHT + VAT — the JE stays balanced (Dr [1260 gross−back + 1300 VAT] = Cr [AP + retention + WHT]).
controls.code_reference: subcontracts.service.ts createSubcontract (scope → commitment reserve SUBCON, BUDGET_EXCEEDED) / createValuation (cumulative pct valuation, NOTHING_TO_CERTIFY/BAD_BACK_CHARGE) / certifyValuation (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, VALUATION_NOT_DRAFT, VAL_EXCEEDS_SUBCONTRACT, JE 1260/2000/2440 + RetentionService.withhold in one tx, idempotent); subcontracts.controller.ts POST /api/subcontracts [+ :subNo/valuations, valuations/:valNo/certify], GET project/:code|:subNo|valuations/:valNo; schema/projects.ts project_subcontracts+subcontract_scope+subcontract_valuations (migration 0254); retention_ledger (migration 0252); permissions.ts proj_subcon/proj_subcon_certify + SoD R18; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the BoQ-budget commitment on subcontract creation, the cumulative valuation (no over-certification), the maker-checker certify (certifier ≠ preparer), the AP-net/retention-payable/WIP JE, the retention withheld into the sub-ledger, and the back-charge handling.
controls.toe: Re-perform: issue a subcontract against BoQ scope (commitment reserved on the line; over budget → BUDGET_EXCEEDED); raise a valuation (gross = value_to_date − previously certified); preparer self-certify → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an independent certifier certifies → JE posts Dr 1260 (gross−back) / Cr 2000 net / Cr 2440 retention, the retention payable appears on the project retention sub-ledger, a 2nd valuation nets off the prior, and certifying beyond the subcontract → VAL_EXCEEDS_SUBCONTRACT (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Subcontract register + valuation JEs (1260/2000/2440); retention payable in the sub-ledger; BoQ commitment

controls.risk: A project is spun up from a lost/unsubmitted tender, or an awarded project's budget baseline is set arbitrarily rather than from the priced winning bid, or a single won tender is awarded twice — so the project starts with an uncontrolled or duplicated cost baseline that later EVM/commitment controls are measured against.
controls.description: Tender → award governance (docs/35 P3): a tender is a pre-award ESTIMATE (a draft BoQ with a cost build-up — bid_rate = unit_cost × (1 + markup%); bid_price = Σ) tracked through a controlled status machine estimating → submitted → won/lost (a lost tender REQUIRES a reason → LOSS_REASON_REQUIRED; a decided tender can't be re-decided → TENDER_DECIDED). AWARD is an AUTHORISED act (proj_tender/exec) allowed ONLY on a WON tender (TENDER_NOT_WON) and IDEMPOTENT — one project per tender (a re-award returns the existing project, never a duplicate). On award the winning bid seeds a project (Fixed-price, contract = bid_price) and a BoQ from the tender lines (bid_rate → BoQ rate) — created as DRAFT so the existing BoQ maker-checker approve (PROJ-12: author ≠ approver, syncs the project budget) sets the controlled budget baseline. Nothing hits GL (estimating is a modelling surface); the seeded BoQ's approval is the segregation point.
controls.owner: Estimator / Project Controller (segregated on the BoQ approve)
controls.code_reference: tenders.service.ts createTender/addLine (cost build-up) / submit (EMPTY_TENDER) / setOutcome (won/lost, LOSS_REASON_REQUIRED, TENDER_DECIDED) / award (TENDER_NOT_WON, idempotent, ProjectsService.create + createBoq draft); tenders.controller.ts POST /api/tenders [+ :no/lines|submit|outcome|award], GET /api/tenders[/:no]; schema/projects.ts project_tenders+tender_boq_lines (migration 0255); permissions.ts proj_tender; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the estimate cost build-up (bid_rate/bid_price), the status machine (won/lost terminal, lost-reason required), the won-only + idempotent award, and the seeded DRAFT BoQ whose maker-checker approve sets the budget baseline.
controls.toe: Re-perform: build a tender (2 lines, 20% markup → bid_price 24000); submit; award before won → TENDER_NOT_WON; mark won; award → a Fixed project (contract 24000) + a DRAFT BoQ (rate = bid_rate); an independent approver approves the BoQ → project budget 24000; re-award → already (no duplicate) (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Tender register + win-rate; the awarded project + its draft-then-approved BoQ baseline

controls.risk: A property developer sells the SAME unit twice — a unit already reserved/contracted is booked or contracted again to another buyer, so two buyers hold a claim on one unit and the availability grid overstates inventory.
controls.description: Unit inventory integrity (docs/35 P4): each re_unit carries a status (available → reserved → contracted → transferred). A BOOKING is admitted only when the unit is AVAILABLE (UNIT_NOT_AVAILABLE otherwise) and flips it to reserved; a CONTRACT is admitted only when the unit is available or reserved (UNIT_NOT_CONTRACTABLE otherwise — a contracted/transferred unit can't be re-sold), re-checked at approval time, and flips it to contracted. The development availability grid (GET /developments/:code/units) reports the live available/reserved/contracted/transferred counts, so inventory is never double-allocated and the grid ties to the unit states. Permission-gated (re_sales) so a non-property tenant never sees the vertical.
controls.code_reference: realestate.service.ts book (UNIT_NOT_AVAILABLE → reserved) / createContract + approveContract (UNIT_NOT_CONTRACTABLE, re-checked on approve → contracted) / listUnits (availability grid); realestate.controller.ts /api/realestate/*; schema/realestate.ts re_projects/re_units/re_bookings (migration 0256); permissions.ts re_sales; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the unit status machine (available→reserved→contracted), the booking/contract admission guards, and the availability grid tie-out.
controls.toe: Re-perform: book an available unit → reserved; re-book it → UNIT_NOT_AVAILABLE; contract it → contracted; contract it again → UNIT_NOT_CONTRACTABLE; the availability summary reflects the counts (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Unit availability grid; booking/contract admission log

controls.risk: A unit is sold at an unauthorised price — a large discount is granted and the contract self-approved by the salesperson (e.g. to a related buyer), with no independent authorisation of the price/discount before the sale is booked and cash is taken.
controls.description: Sale-contract maker-checker (docs/35 P4): a contract is DRAFTED with price = list_price − discount (discount bounded 0..list → BAD_DISCOUNT; down_payment bounded 0..price and ≥ any booking deposit → BAD_DOWN_PAYMENT) and posts NOTHING until approved. An INDEPENDENT approver (re_contract_approve, ≠ the drafter → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; new SoD rule R19) approves it (draft-only → CONTRACT_NOT_DRAFT), which flips the unit to contracted, converts the booking, posts the down-payment to the contract liability (Dr 1000 cash + Dr 2210 reclass booking deposit / Cr 2410 contract liability) and generates the installment schedule. So the price/discount authority is segregated from the salesperson and every sale carries an independent authorisation before cash is recognised.
controls.code_reference: realestate.service.ts createContract (BAD_DISCOUNT/BAD_DOWN_PAYMENT, draft, no GL) / approveContract (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, CONTRACT_NOT_DRAFT, unit→contracted, JE 1000/2210/2410, schedule); realestate.controller.ts contracts[+ :no/approve]; schema/realestate.ts re_contracts+re_installments (migration 0256); permissions.ts re_sales/re_contract_approve + SoD R19; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the price/discount bounds, the draft-then-approve maker-checker (approver ≠ drafter), and the down-payment → contract-liability posting on approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: draft a contract (list 1,000,000 − discount 100,000 = 900,000, down 200,000, 4 installments); drafter self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an independent approver approves → unit contracted, JE posts the down payment to 2410 (booking deposit reclassed from 2210), 4 installments generated (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Contract register (draft/active); the down-payment JE (1000/2210/2410); installment schedule

controls.risk: An installment receipt is misapplied — a paid installment is paid again (double receipt), or an amount other than the scheduled installment is applied, so the buyer's balance and the contract-liability control are wrong.
controls.description: Installment application integrity (docs/35 P4): each installment is paid EXACTLY ONCE (a paid installment → INSTALLMENT_PAID on a second attempt) for EXACTLY its scheduled amount (a different amount → BAD_AMOUNT), posting Dr 1000 cash / Cr 2410 contract liability and marking the installment paid; posting is idempotent per (contract, installment seq) so a retried receipt can't double-post. The contract read model rolls up installments_paid + outstanding = balance − paid, so the buyer's position and the 2410 control reconcile.
controls.code_reference: realestate.service.ts payInstallment (INSTALLMENT_PAID, BAD_AMOUNT, JE 1000/2410, idempotent RE-INSTALL) / getContract (installments_paid/outstanding rollup); realestate.controller.ts installments/:id/pay; schema/realestate.ts re_installments (migration 0256); cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the pay-once / exact-amount guards, the cash → contract-liability posting, and the paid/outstanding rollup.
controls.toe: Re-perform: pay an installment for its exact amount → paid + Dr 1000 / Cr 2410; pay it again → INSTALLMENT_PAID; pay a wrong amount → BAD_AMOUNT; the contract outstanding falls by the paid amount (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Installment schedule (paid/pending); receipt JEs (1000/2410); outstanding rollup

controls.risk: A property sale's revenue is recognised before the buyer has fully paid, or an unauthorised person transfers a unit, or the deferred cash sitting in the contract liability is never recognised at handover — so revenue is misstated (early or missing) and the developer's units/inventory don't clear on transfer.
controls.description: Ownership transfer with revenue recognition (docs/35 P5): revenue on a unit sale is DEFERRED (cash received over the sale sits in the contract liability 2410) and is recognised ONLY at ownership TRANSFER — an AUTHORISED act (re_transfer/exec) allowed ONLY on an ACTIVE, FULLY-SETTLED contract (NOT_FULLY_SETTLED when any installment is outstanding; CONTRACT_NOT_ACTIVE otherwise) and IDEMPOTENT per contract (alreadyPosted RE-TRANSFER). On transfer a balanced JE recognises the price (Dr 2410 Contract Liability / Cr 4200 Revenue) and relieves the unit's construction cost (Dr 5800 COGS / Cr 1200 Inventory), and the unit + contract flip to `transferred`. So revenue lands at the right time (handover, not on cash receipt) for the right amount, cost is matched, and the units clear.
controls.owner: Developer Controller / Legal (segregated on the settled-only gate)
controls.code_reference: realestate.service.ts transferOwnership (NOT_FULLY_SETTLED/CONTRACT_NOT_ACTIVE, idempotent, JE 2410/4200 + 5800/1200, unit+contract → transferred); realestate.controller.ts contracts/:no/transfer (re_transfer); schema/realestate.ts re_units.cost + re_contracts.transfer_entry_no/transferred_at (migration 0258); permissions.ts re_transfer; cutover/projects.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the settled-only + authorised transfer gate, the deferred-revenue recognition (2410 → 4200), and the unit-cost relief (5800 ← 1200).
controls.toe: Re-perform: a fully-settled contract transfers → Dr 2410 / Cr 4200 revenue + Dr 5800 / Cr 1200 cost, unit+contract transferred; a contract with an outstanding installment → NOT_FULLY_SETTLED; a re-transfer → already (idempotent) (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: Transferred contracts + the revenue-recognition JE (2410/4200/5800/1200)

controls.risk: Unbalanced / one-sided journal entries.
controls.description: Double-entry balanced-by-construction — Σdebit=Σcredit enforced; each line single-sided & non-negative.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (postEntry — UNBALANCED / INVALID_LINE)
controls.tod: Inspect balance + line invariants.
controls.toe: Attempt unbalanced JE → rejected.
controls.evidence: Invariant test

controls.risk: Postings to a closed period distort reported results.
controls.description: Period-close lockout — postings to a CLOSED fiscal period are rejected (per-tenant calendar).
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (postEntry PERIOD_CLOSED); fiscal_periods
controls.tod: Inspect close check.
controls.toe: Post to closed period → rejected; review exceptions.
controls.evidence: Close-lock test

controls.risk: Unauthorized year-end close / RE roll.
controls.description: Year-end close restricted to 'exec'; closing entries roll to retained earnings.
controls.code_reference: ledger.controller.ts (close-year @Permissions exec); closeYear
controls.tod: Inspect permission + closing logic.
controls.toe: Attempt close w/o exec → 403; review annual close.
controls.evidence: Close package

controls.risk: Concurrent posting double-books the same source doc.
controls.description: Ledger idempotency — UNIQUE (tenant,source,source_ref,ledger) + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING in postEntry.
controls.code_reference: schema/ledger.ts (ux_je_idem); ledger.service.ts; migration 0058
controls.tod: Inspect unique index + conflict handling.
controls.toe: Concurrent identical post → single entry.
controls.evidence: Dedup test

controls.risk: Manual JE posted without independent review (override risk).
controls.description: Manual journal-entry maker-checker — preparer ≠ approver before a manual JE posts.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (postEntry pendingApproval→Draft; approveEntry preparer≠approver; rejectEntry); ledger.controller.ts (gl_post posts / gl_close approves); cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect JE approval routing.
controls.toe: Sample manual JEs: approver ≠ preparer; Draft excluded from balances until approved (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: JE approvals

controls.risk: Operator mis-posts to another tenant's books.
controls.description: HQ cross-tenant posting gated to Admin (explicit tenant override); others pinned to context (also RLS).
controls.code_reference: ledger.controller.ts (hqTenant)
controls.tod: Inspect Admin gating of tenant_id.
controls.toe: Non-Admin tenant override ignored; Admin audited.
controls.evidence: Override test

controls.risk: Statement of cash flows mis-stated or doesn't tie to the change in cash.
controls.description: Statement of Cash Flows (indirect) reconstructed from the GL: net income + non-cash add-backs + working-capital + investing + financing; year-end CLOSE entries excluded; reconciles to Δcash (1000/1010/1020) by construction with a `reconciled` tie-out flag. A direct-method view presents the same operating cash by receipt/payment nature (receipts from customers, payments to suppliers, tax & payroll) and also reconciles to Δcash; a forward cash-flow forecast projects open AR (inflows) and AP (outflows) by due date from today's cash balance for treasury/collections planning.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (cashFlowStatement, cashFlowDirect, cashFlowForecast); ledger.controller.ts (GET cash-flow, cash-flow-direct, cash-flow-forecast); cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect SCF derivation + reconciliation flag; inspect direct-method bucketing and forecast projection.
controls.toe: Run cash-flow over a seeded period — activities tie to the movement in cash; CLOSE excluded; direct method ties to the same operating cash and Δcash; forecast buckets open AR/AP by due week (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Cash-flow reconciliation; direct-method buckets; forecast schedule

controls.risk: Standing/period accruals missed, posted unbalanced, or posted without approval; manual re-keying error.
controls.description: Recurring/template journal entries: a template's lines are validated balanced (Σdebit=Σcredit) AT SAVE TIME (unbalanced → UNBALANCED), so a broken template can never be persisted. The scheduled job gl_recurring_journals (cron / daily scheduler) posts every due template as a DRAFT JE through the normal maker-checker flow (GL-05) — a different user must approve before it affects balances — and rolls next_run_date forward. Idempotent: next_run_date advanced on posting + the (tenant,source,source_ref,ledger) idempotency key dedupes a same-day re-run. Templates can be paused/resumed without losing history.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (createRecurring balanced-at-save, runDueRecurring→postEntry pendingApproval, setRecurringActive); ledger.controller.ts (gl_post creates/runs); bi.service.ts (gl_recurring_journals); schema/ledger.ts (recurring_journals) + migration 0119; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect template balance validation + scheduled posting as Draft + idempotency.
controls.toe: Unbalanced template → UNBALANCED; due template posts a Draft JE (excluded from TB) into the pending queue; re-run posts nothing; a second user approves → accrual hits the GL (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Recurring-journal templates; scheduled run log; JE approvals

controls.risk: Prepaid expense not amortized over its term — expense mis-stated, prepaid asset overstated.
controls.description: Prepaid amortization schedules: a prepaid asset (insurance/rent paid up front) is registered once with a total + term; the scheduled job gl_prepaid_amortize posts a straight-line slice each period (Dr expense / Cr 1280), the last period taking the remainder so the asset fully clears. Idempotent per (schedule, period) via the JE idempotency key + next_run_date advance.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (createPrepaid, runDuePrepaid); ledger.controller.ts (gl_post); bi.service.ts (gl_prepaid_amortize); schema/ledger.ts (prepaid_schedules) + migration 0120; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect amortization slice + final-period remainder + idempotency.
controls.toe: Register a 12-month prepaid; run amortizes 1/12 to expense; re-run posts nothing (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Prepaid schedules; amortization run log

controls.risk: A new company starts on a chart of accounts with no industry guidance — staff post to wrong/ambiguous accounts; or an industry template drifts from the GL engine's fixed posting codes so a posting targets an account that does not exist (mis-statement / unposted transaction).
controls.description: Industry Chart-of-Accounts templates: the GL engine binds to a FIXED, global account universe (canonical codes are immutable); each tenant additionally gets a per-tenant overlay (tenant_accounts) curating which canonical accounts are active and how they are named/grouped for its industry. At company creation the customer picks an industry (restaurant/retail/distribution/services/general) and the chosen template is materialised into the overlay (provisionTenantCoA) inside the signup transaction, right after fiscal-year provisioning; adopting an industry pack later does the same. Every template code is asserted to exist in the canonical chart at boot (assertTemplatesSubsetOf in seedChartOfAccounts) so a drifted template fails fast and can never reach a tenant. Provisioning is idempotent + additive (never deletes) so re-running only adds missing accounts. The overlay NEVER gates postings — it scopes the picker / Chart-of-Accounts view only; reports surface any account that is active OR carries activity, so a curated-out account that receives a posting still appears.
controls.frequency: At company creation / per pack adopt
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (provisionTenantCoA idempotent+additive, tenant-aware listAccounts, seedChartOfAccounts→assertTemplatesSubsetOf); coa-templates.ts (industry templates + subset assertion); billing.service.ts signup (industry→provisionTenantCoA in-txn); onboarding.service.ts applyPack (pack adopt provisions CoA); schema/ledger.ts tenant_accounts + tenants.industry + migration 0139; cutover/basics.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the boot subset assertion + signup provisioning + the overlay-is-presentation-only read path.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a template referencing an unknown code fails the boot assertion; signup with industry=restaurant materialises a curated, industry-named chart (4000='Food & Beverage Sales') that omits non-F&B accounts; ?all=true still returns the full canonical universe (overlay never gates posting); signup with no industry ⇒ general = the full live chart (re-performed by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Industry templates; tenant_accounts overlay; signup provisioning log

controls.risk: Chart of Accounts changed without authorisation; a tenant's CoA maintainer mutates the GLOBAL, shared canonical account universe that every tenant posts against; account code changed after postings exist; account deactivated while carrying a non-zero balance; manual JE posted directly to a control account (AR/AP/INV/FA), bypassing the sub-ledger and hiding a sub-ledger discrepancy.
controls.description: CoA Change Control — two write surfaces, two duties. (1) CANONICAL universe (accounts table: global, no tenant_id, shared by every tenant): create/update/deactivate require the platform Admin/HQ role IN ADDITION to gl_coa; a tenant gl_coa holder (e.g. FinancialController) is refused with COA_ADMIN_ONLY so it can never silently change the chart other tenants post against. Duplicate code -> DUPLICATE_ACCOUNT; disabling postability on an account with posted entries -> CODE_HAS_POSTINGS; deactivation with a non-zero net balance -> ACCOUNT_HAS_BALANCE (no orphaned balance in a closed account). (2) PER-TENANT curation (tenant_accounts overlay, RLS-scoped): a tenant shapes its OWN chart (active / display name / group / sort order) via PATCH /api/ledger/accounts/:code/overlay under gl_coa; tenant_id is taken from the request context, so a tenant can only ever curate its own chart and never another's, and the overlay never gates postings (references existing canonical codes only -> ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND; missing tenant context -> TENANT_REQUIRED). gl_coa is SoD-separated from gl_post (GlAccountant cannot maintain the CoA). Control accounts 1100 (AR)/2000 (AP)/1200 (INV)/1500 (FA) reject direct JEs without viaSubledger:true (CONTROL_ACCOUNT).
