controls.code_reference: coa.controller.ts (@Controller('api/ledger/accounts'); canonical create/update/deactivate gated to role Admin via assertPlatformAdmin -> COA_ADMIN_ONLY; PATCH :code/overlay curation endpoint, gl_coa); coa.service.ts (createAccount DUPLICATE_ACCOUNT, updateAccount CODE_HAS_POSTINGS, deactivateAccount ACCOUNT_HAS_BALANCE, curateOverlay tenant_accounts upsert TENANT_REQUIRED/ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND); ledger.controller.ts (GET /api/ledger/accounts list read, perms exec/creditors/ar/gl_coa); ledger.service.ts postEntry control-account guard (CONTROL_ACCOUNT, viaSubledger flag) + listAccounts overlay merge; schema/ledger.ts (accounts is_control/control_subledger/is_postable/normal_balance; tenant_accounts overlay + migrations 0155/0139); packages/shared/src/permissions.ts (gl_coa sub-permission, FinancialController default)
controls.tod: Inspect the Admin/HQ gate on canonical writes (COA_ADMIN_ONLY), the RLS-scoped overlay curation, and the CODE_HAS_POSTINGS / ACCOUNT_HAS_BALANCE / CONTROL_ACCOUNT guards.

controls.risk: Account-assignment errors introduced by scattered inline account-code literals in service code; a tenant needing a different account for a given business event has no mechanism to override without a code change.
controls.description: Posting / Account-Determination Engine: two tables — posting_event_types (28 seeded event types, e.g. SALE.FOOD, GR.INVENTORY, DEPRECIATION.FA) and posting_rules (maps event × leg-order to semantic role → account_code + DR/CR side) — drive account assignment instead of hard-coded literals. PostingService.post(eventType, ctx) resolves the applicable rules (global defaults, or shadowed by tenant-specific overrides for the same leg_order), translates role-keyed amounts into DR/CR lines, and delegates to LedgerService.postEntry. If no active rules exist for an event the call fails with NO_POSTING_RULE. Tenant-specific rules require gl_posting_rules permission to create/update. PostingService.preview() provides a dry-run for audit.
controls.code_reference: posting.service.ts (resolveRules, post, preview, listEventTypes, listRules, upsertRule); posting-rules.controller.ts (gl_posting_rules permission gate); schema/posting-rules.ts (posting_event_types, posting_rules tables); migration 0156 (tables + 28 event types + global-default rules); ledger.module.ts (PostingService exported); packages/shared/src/permissions.ts (gl_posting_rules sub-permission); cutover/basics.ts (golden-snapshot ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the rule-resolution logic + NO_POSTING_RULE fast-fail + tenant-shadow logic + gl_posting_rules permission gate.
controls.toe: Re-perform: preview DEPRECIATION.FA with dep_expense:1000 → DR 5200 / CR 1590; preview GR.INVENTORY with inventory:500 → DR 1200 / CR 2000; preview UNKNOWN_EVENT → NO_POSTING_RULE (400); event-type catalogue returns ≥20 entries (re-performed by TC-GL-12-01/02/03/04 UAT cases + basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: Posting-rule change log; preview dry-run; harness golden snapshot

controls.risk: Revenue or expense mis-attributed to the wrong branch / project — per-location P&L is unintelligible or misleading.
controls.description: Multi-dimensional GL postings: journal_lines carries three optional dimension columns (branch_id, project_id, department_id). PostingService.post() stamps all generated lines from PostingContext (branchId/projectId/departmentId). GET /api/ledger/income-statement/by-branch provides a per-branch P&L view; lines without a branch_id are grouped as 'unassigned'. The departments master table holds a tenant-scoped department registry with RLS isolation.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (incomeStatementByBranch); ledger.controller.ts (GET income-statement/by-branch); posting.service.ts (branchId/projectId/departmentId from PostingContext); schema/ledger.ts (branch_id, project_id, department_id on journal_lines); schema/departments.ts (departments table); migration 0157; cutover/basics.ts (GL-13 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect by-branch query + PostingContext stamping + department RLS.
controls.toe: TC-GL-13-01/02/03: smoke + two branches + unassigned bucket (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: By-branch P&L report; dimension completeness

controls.risk: GL control-account balances (AR/AP/INV/FA) drift from the underlying sub-ledger detail without detection — the control account no longer represents the sum of its sub-ledger, masking posting errors, unrecorded items, or fraud.
controls.description: Sub-ledger tie-out / reconciliation: POST /api/ledger/tie-out/run computes, per control account, the GL balance (Σ debit−credit of posted journal_lines on the control account up to the as-of date, tenant-scoped) and the sub-ledger detail balance (AR = Σ outstanding ar_invoices; AP = Σ outstanding ap_transactions; INV = Σ inv_balances.total_value perpetual valuation; FA = Σ acquire_cost − accumulated_depreciation of non-disposed fixed_assets), records the variance (gl − subledger) and a status (Matched if |variance|<0.01 else Variance). Runs upsert per (tenant, subledger, as-of). Certification is maker-checker: POST /api/ledger/tie-out/:id/certify sets status=Certified and records certifier + timestamp, and REJECTS a certifier equal to the runner (SELF_CERTIFY) — the gl_close certifier must differ from the gl_post/gl_close runner (SoD).
controls.code_reference: subledger-tieout.service.ts (runTieOut GL/sub-ledger balance computation + variance/status, certify SELF_CERTIFY maker-checker, list/get); subledger-tieout.controller.ts (gl_close/gl_post run, gl_close certify, gl_close/gl_post/exec/creditors/ar read); schema/subledger-tieout.ts (subledger_tieout_runs table, uq on tenant/subledger/as-of); migration 0160 (table + RLS); ledger.module.ts (service+controller wired); cutover/basics.ts (GL-14 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the GL-vs-sub-ledger balance computation + variance/status + SELF_CERTIFY maker-checker gate + gl_close certify permission.
controls.toe: TC-GL-14-01: run AR tie-out → balances + Matched/Variance; TC-GL-14-02: self-certify by the runner → SELF_CERTIFY (400); TC-GL-14-03: certify by a different gl_close user → Certified (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: Tie-out run log (subledger_tieout_runs) with variance + certification trail; monthly reconciliation sign-off

controls.risk: A period is 'closed' on paper but key close procedures (sub-ledger tie-out, bank rec, depreciation, recurring/prepaid, trial-balance review) were skipped, OR a closed period is silently re-opened and re-posted — the financial statements are produced on an incomplete/altered basis.
controls.description: Hard period close + checklist: POST /api/ledger/close/start opens a close_runs record (status InProgress) per (tenant, period) and seeds a standard checklist of close_run_steps (subledger_tieout, bank_rec, depreciation, recurring, fx_reval[advisory], trial_balance_review). POST /api/ledger/close/step marks a step Done (records completedBy/At); when all REQUIRED steps are Done the run advances to ReadyToLock. POST /api/ledger/close/lock requires ReadyToLock (else STEPS_INCOMPLETE listing the pending required steps) before the period can be locked. Locking writes 'Locked' into fiscal_periods.status; LedgerService.postEntry then REJECTS every posting dated into a Locked period with PERIOD_LOCKED, regardless of the legacy allowClosedPeriod escape (the only exception is the system year-end closing entry, source='CLOSE'). Locked is strictly stronger than the soft 'Closed' status — it is the irreversible hard close.
controls.code_reference: close.service.ts (startClose seeds checklist, completeStep → ReadyToLock when required steps Done, lockPeriod STEPS_INCOMPLETE gate + fiscal_periods Locked write, status/list); close.controller.ts (gl_close start/step/lock, gl_close/gl_post/exec read); schema/close-runs.ts (close_runs + close_run_steps, uq on tenant/period and run/step_key); schema/ledger.ts (period_status enum + 'Locked'); ledger.service.ts (postEntry PERIOD_LOCKED hard gate, source='CLOSE' exemption); migration 0162 (tables + RLS + ALTER TYPE Locked); ledger.module.ts (service+controller wired); cutover/basics.ts (GL-15/GL-16 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the seeded checklist + ReadyToLock gating + STEPS_INCOMPLETE lock rejection + PERIOD_LOCKED hard gate in postEntry (incl. the CLOSE exemption).
controls.toe: TC-GL-15-01: start close seeds ≥4 steps (InProgress); TC-GL-15-02: lock before steps done → STEPS_INCOMPLETE (400); TC-GL-15-03: post into locked period → PERIOD_LOCKED (400) (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: close_runs / close_run_steps with completion trail; fiscal_periods.status=Locked; monthly close sign-off

controls.risk: One person both performs the close and locks the period, defeating segregation of duties — a preparer could conceal a misstatement by closing over their own work without independent review.
controls.description: Segregated period lock (maker-checker): the close_runs.started_by (the user who started the close and ran the checklist steps) is recorded, and POST /api/ledger/close/lock REJECTS a lockedBy equal to startedBy with SELF_LOCK — the gl_close user who locks the period MUST differ from the user who started/ran the close. Both identities and the lock timestamp are retained on the close_runs row (started_by / locked_by / locked_at) as the SoD evidence trail. This complements the existing SoD rule that gl_close is segregated from gl_post (JE posting).
controls.code_reference: close.service.ts (lockPeriod SELF_LOCK maker-checker gate; started_by vs locked_by); close.controller.ts (gl_close lock, acting username from JWT); schema/close-runs.ts (started_by, locked_by, locked_at columns); permissions.ts (gl_post×gl_close SoD pair); cutover/basics.ts (GL-16 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the SELF_LOCK gate (startedBy ≠ lockedBy) + the started_by/locked_by/locked_at evidence trail.
controls.toe: TC-GL-16-01: self-lock by the starter → SELF_LOCK (400); TC-GL-16-02: lock by a different gl_close user → Locked, locked_by recorded (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: close_runs.started_by / locked_by / locked_at SoD trail; monthly close sign-off

controls.risk: A posted journal entry is edited or deleted after the fact — restating history with no trail — instead of being corrected by a transparent reversal; or a correction is made with no record of who/what/why. The general ledger is no longer an immutable, auditable record of record.
controls.description: Posted-entry immutability + reversal-only correction + GL audit trail: a journal entry that has reached Posted status is IMMUTABLE — it can never be edited or deleted. Enforcement is twofold: (1) a production DB trigger (gl_block_posted_mutation) that RAISES on any UPDATE/DELETE of a Posted journal_entries row (permitting only the is_reversed bookkeeping flag flip), and (2) an application-level guard (attemptVoidPosted → GL_IMMUTABLE) that refuses and records any mutation attempt. The ONLY way to correct a posted entry is a REVERSAL: reverseEntry posts a NEW, immediately-Posted contra entry that swaps every line's debit/credit (so original + reversal net to zero on every affected account), dimensions carried over, linked via reversal_of, and flags the original is_reversed. A reversal goes through the normal posting path so the period gates (PERIOD_LOCKED/PERIOD_CLOSED) still apply; only Posted entries can be reversed (NOT_POSTED) and only once (ALREADY_REVERSED). Every POST, APPROVE, REVERSE and blocked mutation (MUTATE_BLOCKED) is recorded with actor + detail + timestamp in gl_audit_log (tenant-scoped, RLS).
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts (postEntry stamps posted_at + logs POST; approveEntry stamps posted_at + logs APPROVE; reverseEntry contra-posting with ENTRY_NOT_FOUND/NOT_POSTED/ALREADY_REVERSED + REVERSE log; attemptVoidPosted GL_IMMUTABLE + MUTATE_BLOCKED log; listGlAudit); ledger.controller.ts (gl_post journal/:id/reverse + journal/:id/attempt-void, gl_post/gl_close/exec audit read); schema/ledger.ts (journal_entries posted_at/reversal_of/is_reversed); schema/gl-audit.ts (gl_audit_log table); migration 0164 (columns + gl_audit_log + RLS + gl_block_posted_mutation trigger); ledger.module.ts (LedgerService owns it); cutover/basics.ts (GL-17 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the DB immutability trigger + the app GL_IMMUTABLE guard + the reversal contra-posting (swapped Dr/Cr, reversal_of, is_reversed) + the gl_audit_log trail (POST/APPROVE/REVERSE/MUTATE_BLOCKED).
controls.toe: TC-GL-17-01: attempt to void a posted entry → GL_IMMUTABLE (400) + MUTATE_BLOCKED audit row; TC-GL-17-02: reverse a posted entry → contra entry nets to zero, original is_reversed + REVERSE audit row; TC-GL-17-03: reverse again → ALREADY_REVERSED (400) (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: gl_audit_log trail (POST/APPROVE/REVERSE/MUTATE_BLOCKED); reversal entries with reversal_of linkage; posted-entry immutability

controls.fsli: Accounts Receivable (1100); Accounts Payable (2000); FX Gain/Loss (5400)
controls.risk: Open foreign-currency monetary balances (AR/AP) are not restated to the period-end closing rate, or a revaluation is computed and posted by one person with no independent review — the unrealized FX gain/loss is omitted/mis-stated and a single user can move earnings through the FX line.
controls.description: Period-end FX revaluation governance (maker-checker, idempotent per (tenant, period)): POST /api/ledger/fx-reval/run computes the unrealized FX on every OPEN foreign-currency AR/AP balance — delta_thb = open_foreign × (closing_rate − booked_rate) — staging an 'Open' fx_reval_runs row (one per tenant/period; re-running an Open period refreshes it; a Posted period → ALREADY_POSTED). Closing rates come from the request `rates` map first, else the latest APPROVED fx_rates row (FX-04); a currency with no rate from either → MISSING_RATE (an unapproved manual rate can never drive the reval). POST /api/ledger/fx-reval/:id/post is segregated — the poster MUST differ from the runner (SELF_POST) — and posts the net to 5400 (net gain → Cr 5400; net loss → Dr 5400) with the AR/AP control restatements (Dr/Cr 1100, Dr/Cr 2000) via LedgerService.postEntry, so the WS2.1 PERIOD_LOCKED gate + the GL-17 audit trail apply. Marks the run Posted. SIGN: net P&L = Σ(AR delta) − Σ(AP delta).
controls.code_reference: fx-reval.service.ts (runReval compute+stage Open, idempotent ALREADY_POSTED, rate resolution MISSING_RATE; postReval SELF_POST maker-checker + 5400/1100/2000 posting; list/get); fx-reval.controller.ts (gl_close/gl_post run + :id/post, gl_close/gl_post/exec read); schema/deferred-tax-fx.ts (fx_reval_runs, uq tenant/period); migration 0168 (tables + RLS); ledger.module.ts (service+controller wired); close.service.ts (fx_reval close-checklist step); reuses fx_rates (FX-04) + LedgerService.postEntry; cutover/basics.ts (GL-18 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the run→post lifecycle, the rate resolution (explicit/approved-only/MISSING_RATE), the SELF_POST maker-checker, and the 5400/1100/2000 posting through the period-lock gate.
controls.toe: TC-GL-18-01: run on an open foreign AR+AP → net unrealized gain/loss with per-doc detail (Open); TC-GL-18-02: runner self-post → SELF_POST (403), a different user posts → 5400 movement, re-post → ALREADY_POSTED (400) (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: fx_reval_runs register (Open→Posted, runner/poster); the FXREVAL-RUN JE to 5400; period-end reval sign-off

controls.risk: A period is locked on the strength of a manual checklist sign-off while the books are not actually clean — an unposted draft journal still sits in the period, or an entry/batch does not balance — so the financial statements are produced on an incomplete or internally-inconsistent basis that the checklist alone could not catch.
controls.description: Programmatic pre-lock validation (read-only, posts nothing): GET /api/ledger/close/validate?period=YYYY-MM asserts the books-are-clean conditions the checklist sign-off cannot by itself — (1) NO unposted Draft journal entries dated in the period, (2) the period's Posted entries balance IN AGGREGATE (Σdebit = Σcredit), (3) EVERY posted entry is individually balanced, and (4) suspense/clearing accounts (2380/2390/1999/9999) net to ~zero through period end (advisory). It returns ready + an explicit blockers/warnings list and a per-check breakdown, surfaced in the period-close screen as advisory blockers BEFORE the GL-16 maker-checker lock — so an unbalanced or draft-laden period is flagged for correction rather than silently locked. Detective and read-only; the hard lock still runs the GL-15 checklist + GL-16 segregation.
controls.code_reference: close.service.ts (validate — draft scan, aggregate + per-entry balance, suspense/clearing balance, ready/blockers/warnings); close.controller.ts (GET close/validate, gl_close/gl_post/exec); reuses journal_entries/journal_lines; no migration (read-only); cutover/basics.ts (GL-19 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the four readiness checks (unposted_drafts, period_balanced, unbalanced_entries, suspense_clearing) and that a draft in the period sets ready=false with the unposted_drafts blocker.
controls.toe: TC-GL-19-CLOSE-01: validate a clean past period → ready=true, no blockers, drafts/balance checks ok; TC-GL-19-CLOSE-02: a manual JE (posts as Draft, GL-05) in the period → ready=false, unposted_drafts blocker count ≥ 1 (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: close-validate readiness report (ready/blockers/warnings + per-check detail)

controls.risk: The trial balance reads the gl_period_balances snapshot (docs/27 R1-2 — maintained transactionally at posting so financial-statement reads stop scanning the full journal); a write path that bypasses LedgerService (direct SQL, ETL) would silently drift the snapshot from the raw ledger, mis-stating the reported balances.
controls.description: Snapshot reconciliation at close: the GL-19 pre-lock validator additionally re-aggregates the period's POSTED journal_lines per account and compares them to the gl_period_balances snapshot — ANY per-account debit/credit mismatch is a HARD close blocker (gl_snapshot_drift, listing the drifted accounts) until the snapshot is resynced (the 0219 backfill recompute). The snapshot itself is written in the SAME transaction as the posting (postEntry → Posted; approveEntry Draft→Posted) and Posted entries are DB-immutable (GL-17/0165), so drift can only come from an out-of-band writer — exactly what this control detects.
controls.code_reference: ledger.service.ts bumpPeriodBalances (transactional maintenance) + trialBalance (snapshot read); close.service.ts validate gl_snapshot_drift; drizzle/0219_gl_period_balances.sql (table + unique key + backfill); cutover/basics.ts TC-GL-20 (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect that the snapshot write shares the posting transaction and that validate compares snapshot vs raw per account.
controls.toe: Re-perform: clean period → gl_snapshot_drift ok; INSERT a bogus snapshot row → validate ready=false with the account named; rebuild → clear (basics harness TC-GL-20).
controls.evidence: Snapshot-reconciliation ToE (basics)

controls.risk: Inventory / COGS posting accounts are hard-coded control literals, so an item (or item family) that should hit a different GL account has no maintainable mechanism; and any per-item account mapping, if introduced, could point at a non-existent or non-postable account — silently mis-stating the sub-ledger or breaking the control-account tie-out.
controls.description: Item-posting account determination (extends GL-12 to the item grain): a per-tenant, opt-in flag (posting_determination, default OFF ⇒ literal parity) lets an item's GL/tax profile drive posting. AccountDeterminationService.resolveItemAccounts resolves each account by precedence item column → its item_categories row → its WAREHOUSE (locations) default → null (caller falls back to the hard-coded control account), so an un-opted-in or unconfigured tenant is byte-for-byte unchanged. The inventory sub-ledger (receive/issue/adjust/issue-to-project) routes its inventory, COGS & adjustment legs through the resolver; reconcile() sums the WHOLE inventory-account set (control 1200 + any item/category/warehouse overrides) so the GL-14-style sub-ledger↔GL tie-out holds under routing. Fail-closed: a resolved override that is not a real, POSTABLE account in the canonical CoA is rejected with INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT before any posting — a typo'd/header/control account can never receive a posting. docs/42 hardening: the SAME fail-closed guard now runs inside postEntry itself for EVERY journal line (any source, subledger or manual) — a line naming a non-existent or non-postable account is rejected with INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT instead of posting silently and vanishing from the typed reports (they INNER JOIN accounts); and a tenant's ACTIVE posting_rules rows (event_type+role, /setup/posting-rules) re-map the recurring system posters' accounts (payroll PAYROLL.*, FA depreciation DEPRECIATION.FA, lease runs LEASE.*/DEPRECIATION.ROU) as override ?? literal — un-configured tenants post byte-identically, and the posting_determination flag is toggleable from the item-categories setup screen. VAT (docs/33 PR6): a configured tax_code drives the VAT rate + output/input VAT GL account on AR invoices (from the order's uniform item vat_code) and AP bills (from an explicit tax_code); an unknown/non-VAT code fails closed (UNKNOWN_TAX_CODE/NOT_A_VAT_CODE), and the PP30↔GL reconciliation (TAX-04) sums the whole VAT-account set so the tie holds under routing. Further item defaults (docs/33 PR7): a stock move with no explicit location defaults to the item's default_location_id (item→category), and an AR invoice's REVENUE account follows the order's uniform item revenue_account — both flag-gated with literal parity (WH-MAIN / 4000 when off/unset). COA-D2 extension: the postEntry account guard additionally enforces, ONLY when declared on the account (fail-closed, byte-identical otherwise): the effective-date window (a line dated outside effective_from/effective_to → ACCOUNT_NOT_EFFECTIVE — the 'retire with an effective-to date' path now binds) and required dimensions (an account flagged require_dimension {branch|project|department|cost_center} rejects a line missing that stamp → REQUIRED_DIMENSION_MISSING, so dimension reporting on the account is complete); a canonical create naming a non-existent parent is refused at request time (PARENT_NOT_FOUND). Both attributes are maintainable on the /chart-of-accounts dialogs (GL-27-staged).
controls.code_reference: account-determination.service.ts (resolveItemAccounts item→category→warehouse precedence + assertPostable INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT fail-closed, enabled flag, resolveTaxCode); inventory-ledger.service.ts (invAccounts resolver + inventoryAccountSet reconcile); posting.service.ts (resolveRules now evaluates the condition jsonb); feature-flags.service.ts (posting_determination CORE flag, default off); item-setup.service.ts (categories/tax-codes/item-profile/warehouse-account CRUD, /api/item-setup/*, gated md_item/md_config); schema/posting-setup.ts (item_categories, tax_codes) + inventory.ts item + locations account columns; migrations 0243/0244 (journaled); ledger.module.ts (AccountDeterminationService exported); ledger-posting.service.ts postEntry account-universe guard (exists + isPostable on every line, INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT); ledger.service.ts postingOverrides (tenant posting_rules role→account map) consumed by payroll.service.ts / assets.service.ts (depreciation) / leases.service.ts (periodic run); cutover/basics.ts + compliance.ts + payroll.ts (GL-21 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the item→category→literal precedence, the OFF-by-default parity, the postable-account fail-closed guard, and that reconcile sums the full inventory-account set.
controls.toe: Re-perform (basics): flag OFF → item COGS override ignored, 5001 untouched (parity); enable posting_determination → goods issue routes COGS to the item's 5001 override (Dr 40) while inventory sub-ledger still reconciles to the GL account set; an override at a non-existent account → INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT (400); a manual JE naming a non-existent account → INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT (compliance); a tenant PAYROLL.GROSS posting-rule re-maps the salaries debit to its override account end-to-end (payroll ToE).
controls.evidence: Feature-flag state + posting-rule/override config; harness ToE (basics GL-21)

controls.risk: A period is locked (or the financial statements are relied upon) without a single, current view of whether the books are actually ready — the sub-ledger↔GL tie-out, the pre-lock readiness checks, and the pending maker-checker queue live on separate screens, so the controller can miss an out-of-balance control account, an unposted draft, or a stale approval and close on an incomplete/inaccurate basis.
controls.description: Controller Close Cockpit — a single read-only period-close readiness board (GET /api/finance/metrics/close/status, perm exec/fin_report/gl_close) that COMPOSES the existing detective controls into one RAG view: the sub-ledger↔GL control-account tie-out (REC-04: AR↔1100, AP↔2000, Inventory↔1200, Gift cards↔2200, Deferred revenue↔2400), the pre-lock readiness checks (GL-19 unposted-draft/aggregate-&-per-entry-balance/suspense + GL-20 snapshot↔raw reconciliation), the pending maker-checker queue with ageing (GOV-01), and the close checklist/state (GL-15/16) — plus a days-to-close metric. Each pillar carries a RAG, and the overall status is RED if any tie-out breaks or a hard readiness blocker exists. Read-only aggregation over the posted GL + sub-ledgers — posts nothing, adds no posting authority (no new SoD conflict). Surfaced on the /finance/close-cockpit web screen so the controller reviews close-readiness in one place before the maker-checker lock.
controls.code_reference: finance/finance-metrics.service.ts (closeStatus — composes close.validate GL-19/20, finance.reconcileControls REC-04, finance.pendingApprovals GOV-01, close.status; RAG + days-to-close); finance-metrics.controller.ts (GET /api/finance/metrics/close/status); apps/web close-cockpit (screen); cutover/finance-kpi.ts (ToE); docs/35-finance-oracle-grade-analytics-plan.md
controls.tod: Inspect the cockpit: the tie-out rows (sub-ledger vs GL control + variance), the readiness checks, the pending-approval ageing, and that the overall RAG turns red on any break.
controls.toe: Re-perform (finance-kpi): a seeded tie-out break (AP/inventory sub-ledger ≠ GL control) makes the cockpit report tie-out RED and overall RED while readiness is GREEN; posting the matching sub-ledgers flips tie-out + overall to GREEN with 0 exceptions; a non-finance role is denied the cockpit (403).
controls.evidence: Close-readiness cockpit (finance/metrics/close/status); harness ToE (finance-kpi)

controls.risk: Shared / overhead cost is not allocated to the consuming cost-centers or departments — or is allocated by an ad-hoc spreadsheet that is unbalanced, non-repeatable, or posted without review — so departmental / project P&L is mis-stated and the allocation is neither auditable nor idempotent.
controls.description: GL allocation engine: a periodic ALLOCATION CYCLE distributes a source POOL (a fixed amount out of a source account / cost-center) to a set of targets by fixed RATIO, a measured DRIVER, or a STATISTICAL KEY (headcount / sqm) — the three methods share one proportional-split engine (target share = pool × basis / Σbasis; the last positive-weight target absorbs the rounding remainder in exact minor-4 units so Σdebits = pool EXACTLY). The cycle is validated UP FRONT at save (bad method/frequency → BAD_METHOD/BAD_FREQUENCY; non-positive pool → BAD_AMOUNT; no targets → NO_TARGETS; zero total basis → NO_BASIS), so a broken cycle can never be persisted and then post an unbalanced/zero JE. The scheduled job gl_allocation_run posts every due cycle as ONE balanced DRAFT JE (Cr the source pool / Dr each target its share) through the normal maker-checker flow (GL-05) — a different user must approve before it affects balances — riding the same recurring rail as GL-08, and rolls next_run_date forward. Idempotent per period: next_run_date advanced on posting + the (tenant,source,source_ref,ledger) idempotency key (source_ref = ALC-<id>-<date>) dedupes a same-day re-run. Cycles can be paused/resumed without losing history.
controls.code_reference: ledger-allocation.service.ts (createCycle balanced-at-save validation, runDueAllocations→postEntry pendingApproval balanced split, setCycleActive); ledger.service.ts (AllocationCycleDto + delegators); ledger.controller.ts (gl_post/exec creates/runs, POST/GET /api/ledger/allocation[/:id/active][/run]); bi.service.ts (gl_allocation_run scheduled job); schema/ledger.ts (allocation_cycles, allocation_targets) + migration 0301; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the up-front validation, the exact proportional split (remainder to last target → balanced JE), the scheduled posting as Draft, and the per-period idempotency.
controls.toe: Zero-basis cycle → NO_BASIS; a ratio cycle (pool 10,000 split 3:1) posts a Draft JE excluded from the TB into the pending queue; a same-day re-run posts nothing; a second user approves → 5710 +7,500 / 5720 +2,500 / 5000 −10,000 (balanced); a driver cycle splits a pool by headcount 2:1 → 600/300 (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: Allocation-cycle definitions; scheduled run log; JE approvals

controls.risk: A tenant posting-rule override re-routes where a recurring system posting (payroll, depreciation, lease runs — and every flow docs/43 wires next) lands in the financial statements, but rule changes were ungoverned: no validation (a typo'd/non-postable account, an unknown role, a sub-ledger control account could be targeted), no second person, no audit trail — a single user could silently repoint expense or liability legs.
controls.description: Posting-rule change governance (docs/43 PR-1): every rule write is validated FAIL-CLOSED against the in-code posting-event REGISTRY (posting-events.ts — 74 events, each role carrying its side, REAL default account and override TIER): unknown event/role → UNKNOWN_POSTING_EVENT/UNKNOWN_POSTING_ROLE, wrong side → POSTING_SIDE_MISMATCH, a 'pinned'/'widen' role (sub-ledger controls incl. the five permanently-pinned REC-04 accounts, equity plugs, the cash set; recon-gated Tier B until PR-7 widens the recon) → OVERRIDE_ROLE_PINNED, a non-existent/non-postable account → INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT. A valid write lands status=PendingApproval and takes effect ONLY when a DIFFERENT user approves (self-approve → SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin); re-editing an approved rule demotes it back to PendingApproval. The resolver (LedgerService.postingOverrides + the per-tenant 5s TtlCache, bust-on-approve) consumes ONLY active+Approved rules, so an unapproved rule can never touch a posting. Every CREATE/APPROVE/REJECT/DEACTIVATE writes an append-only posting_rule_audit row (tenant-scoped, canonical RLS). The registry's role defaults are boot-asserted to exist in the canonical CoA (assertPostingEventDefaults), and the posting_event_types catalogue is seeded FROM the registry (migration 0331) so code and catalogue cannot drift.
controls.code_reference: ledger/posting-events.ts (registry: 74 events / 121 roles, tier free|widen|pinned, assertPostingEventDefaults boot assert from seedChartOfAccounts); ledger/posting.service.ts (validateRule fail-closed, upsertRule→PendingApproval, approveRule SoD + cache bust, rejectRule, deactivateRule, posting_rule_audit rows, resolveRules/preview consume Approved only); ledger/posting-overrides-cache.ts (per-tenant TtlCache, bust-on-write); ledger.service.ts postingOverrides/postingOverridesMany (active+Approved only); posting-rules.controller.ts (Zod body, :id/approve|reject|deactivate, GET registry/audit, gl_posting_rules duty); schema/posting-rules.ts + migration 0331 (status/created_by/approved_by/approved_at, posting_rule_audit + tenant-leading index + canonical RLS); /setup/posting-rules UI (pending badge + approve/reject)
controls.tod: Inspect the registry tiers, the fail-closed validation set, the PendingApproval→distinct-approver flow, the Approved-only resolver + cache bust, and the audit trail.
controls.toe: Re-perform (compliance GL-24 ToE + payroll harness): create a rule for a pinned role → OVERRIDE_ROLE_PINNED; unknown role → UNKNOWN_POSTING_ROLE; bad account → INVALID_POSTING_ACCOUNT; a valid rule lands PendingApproval and a payroll run IGNORES it (posts the default); creator self-approve → 403 SOD_VIOLATION; a different user approves → the next run posts to the override account; audit trail carries CREATE+APPROVE rows.
controls.evidence: posting_rule_audit trail; compliance + payroll harness ToE

controls.risk: Material period-over-period movements in the financial statements are relied upon without a documented management analysis — a large or anomalous swing in a revenue/expense (or balance-sheet) account is neither explained nor independently reviewed, so an error or manipulation that first surfaces as an unexpected fluctuation goes undetected before the books are relied upon / the period is locked.
controls.description: Flux / variance analysis with FORCED explanation + independent sign-off (CLS-01): a preparer GENERATES a period movement analysis from the gl_period_balances snapshot (POST /api/close/flux/generate — basis P&L period-activity or Balance-Sheet cumulative-through-period; comparative prior_period / prior_year / vs approved-budget). Each account line carries the current amount, the comparative amount, Δ$ and Δ%, and is flagged BREACHED when |Δ$| ≥ a configurable absolute threshold AND |Δ%| ≥ a configurable percent threshold (both must trip, so a trivial % on a large base or a large % on a trivial base is not noise). A threshold-breaching line REQUIRES a written explanation (PUT …/lines/:lineId/explain) before the analysis can advance from Draft to Explained; review/sign-off (POST …/review) is REFUSED while any breached line is unexplained (UNEXPLAINED_LINES). Sign-off is maker-checker: the reviewer MUST differ from the preparer (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), and certification locks the analysis (ALREADY_CERTIFIED). Read-only aggregator — posts NOTHING to the GL, adds no posting authority (no new SoD conflict). Surfaced as the advisory 'flux_review' step on the close checklist (GL-15) and as the schedulable flux_analysis BI report.
controls.code_reference: flux/flux.service.ts (generate — signed amounts per account from gl_period_balances, prior_period/prior_year/budget comparatives, abs+pct breach flags; explain forced-explanation + Draft→Explained; review UNEXPLAINED_LINES + SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker → Certified); flux/flux.controller.ts (POST close/flux/generate, GET close/flux[/:id], PUT :id/lines/:lineId/explain, POST :id/review — gl_close/fin_report/exec); schema/flux.ts (flux_analyses + flux_lines, tenant-leading index) + migration 0348 (canonical RLS); ledger/close.service.ts (advisory flux_review checklist step); bi/report-registry.ts + bi-generate.service.ts (flux_analysis report type); apps/web /close/flux; cutover/flux-analysis.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the abs+pct breach logic, the forced-explanation gate before sign-off, the maker-checker reviewer≠preparer rule, and that the analysis posts nothing to the GL.
controls.toe: Re-perform (flux-analysis harness): generate a P&L flux flags the lines breaching abs/pct thresholds and leaves the below-threshold line unflagged; review while a breached line is unexplained → UNEXPLAINED_LINES; the preparer's self-review → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; after every breached line is explained a DISTINCT reviewer certifies; prior_year/budget comparatives resolve correctly; RLS isolates tenants.
controls.evidence: flux_analyses / flux_lines with per-line explanations + reviewer sign-off; flux-analysis harness ToE

controls.risk: Financial statements / the reporting package are issued without a governed disclosure checklist: required TFRS/IFRS + SEC disclosure notes (related parties, revenue disaggregation, leases, income tax, commitments & contingencies, segments, subsequent events, management disclosure-controls sign-off) may be omitted or incomplete, supporting evidence is not pinned to the note, and one person can compile AND release the package with no independent review — a SEC disclosure-controls failure and an incomplete/inaccurate reporting basis.
controls.description: Governed disclosure / close-package checklist (close binder): POST /api/close/disclosure opens a per-period checklist that AUTO-SEEDS the standard TFRS/IFRS + SEC disclosure items — each carries a standard reference (TAS 1/7/12/24, TFRS 8/15/16, TAS 10/37, SEC disclosure controls), an owner and an Open/Complete/NA status with an optional support-doc reference pinned to doc_attachments (docType DISC via assertDocExists). PUT /api/close/disclosure/:id/items/:itemId completes/NAs each item (recording completed_by/at) and attaches its evidence. Review is a MAKER-CHECKER sign-off gate: POST /api/close/disclosure/:id/review is BLOCKED while any item is still Open (ITEMS_INCOMPLETE, listing the open items) and REJECTS a reviewer equal to the preparer (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) — moving the binder Draft → Reviewed — and only a Reviewed binder can be Issued (POST …/issue → Issued; NOT_REVIEWED otherwise) so the financials are released only after an independent review. An advisory disclosure_review step cross-links the binder into the GL-15/16 close run. Detective/monitoring; tenant-scoped (canonical RLS); posts NOTHING to the GL.
controls.code_reference: disclosure.service.ts (open seeds STANDARD_ITEMS, updateItem complete/NA + support_doc_ref, review ITEMS_INCOMPLETE + SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker gate Draft→Reviewed, issue Reviewed→Issued NOT_REVIEWED); disclosure.controller.ts (api/close/disclosure open/list/get/item/review/issue, gl_close mutate + gl_close/gl_post/fin_report/exec read); schema/disclosure-checklists.ts (disclosure_checklists + disclosure_items, tenant-leading index + canonical 0232-form RLS); migration 0347 (tables + RLS + app_user grants); procurement/attachments.service.ts (assertDocExists DISC → disclosure_checklists); common/doc-number.service.ts (DISC daily doc number); ledger/close.service.ts (advisory disclosure_review step); app.module.ts (DisclosureModule wired); cutover/disclosure-checklist.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the seeded standard-item set with standard refs/owners, the item complete/NA + support-doc attach, the ITEMS_INCOMPLETE review gate, the SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker (reviewer ≠ preparer), the Reviewed→Issued release, and the tenant RLS isolation.
controls.toe: TC-CLS-02-01: open seeds ≥10 standard disclosure items (Draft, DISC checklist_no); TC-CLS-02-02: review while an item is Open → ITEMS_INCOMPLETE (400); TC-CLS-02-03: self-review by the preparer → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (403); TC-CLS-02-04: complete/NA all + a DISTINCT reviewer reviews → Reviewed, then issue → Issued; TC-CLS-02-05: attach DISC support evidence to the checklist_no (assertDocExists), a bogus checklist_no → NOT_FOUND; TC-CLS-02-06: another tenant sees 0 checklists (RLS) (re-performed by the disclosure-checklist harness).
controls.evidence: disclosure_checklists / disclosure_items with prepared_by/reviewed_by/issued_by sign-off trail + per-item completed_by/support_doc_ref; harness ToE (disclosure-checklist)

controls.risk: The canonical Chart of Accounts is SHARED by every tenant and the posting engine hard-references its codes, but canonical account changes (create / rename / postability flip / cash-flow-bucket reroute / deactivate) were a single-person action with NO record: GL-11 restricts WHO may change it (platform Admin/HQ + gl_coa) yet nothing records WHAT changed, WHEN, or BY WHOM, and no second person reviews a change that re-routes every tenant's financial statements (the audit gap symmetric to the one GL-24 closed for posting rules).
controls.description: Canonical CoA maker-checker (COA follow-up C): every canonical account write on /api/ledger/accounts (POST create, PATCH :code update, POST :code/deactivate — all still Admin/HQ-gated per GL-11) is validated FAIL-CLOSED at request time (DUPLICATE_ACCOUNT / ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND / ACCOUNT_HAS_BALANCE / CODE_HAS_POSTINGS; one pending request per code — CHANGE_ALREADY_PENDING) and staged as a coa_change_requests row (payload + before-snapshot, created_by); the change applies ONLY when a DIFFERENT Admin approves (POST /api/ledger/accounts/change-requests/:id/approve — creator self-approval → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin; re-validated at approve), or is rejected with a recorded reason. SINGLE-ADMIN EXCEPTION (owner decision 2026-07-12): when the system has exactly one ACTIVE Admin a maker-checker would deadlock, so the change applies immediately and the request row records status AutoApplied with the exception reason — the audit trail survives even where the second pair of eyes cannot. The /chart-of-accounts screen carries the pending queue (approve/reject) and stages its create/edit/deactivate dialogs through the same flow. Bulk imports keep their own standing maker-checker (masterdata staged batches, PR-8).
controls.code_reference: ledger/coa.service.ts (activeAdminCount, validateChange fail-closed, requestChange stage-or-AutoApply, approveChange SOD_VIOLATION + re-validate, rejectChange, listChanges); ledger/coa.controller.ts (writes route through requestChange; GET change-requests + :id/approve|reject, assertPlatformAdmin defence-in-depth); schema/posting-rules.ts coaChangeRequests + migration 0362 (PLATFORM table — created_tenant_id, app_user grants, status index); /chart-of-accounts pending-queue card + staged-write toasts; apps/api/test coa maker-checker unit (single-admin exception)
controls.tod: Inspect the fail-closed request-time validation, the PendingApproval→distinct-Admin flow, the AutoApplied single-admin exception rows, and the append-only request trail (payload + before snapshot).
controls.toe: Re-perform (compliance GL-27 ToE): Admin stages a create → PendingApproval, account does NOT exist yet; creator self-approve → 403 SOD_VIOLATION; a DIFFERENT Admin approves → account exists; duplicate create for the same code → DUPLICATE_ACCOUNT (or CHANGE_ALREADY_PENDING while staged); reject leaves the chart untouched; unit ToE: with exactly ONE active Admin the write applies immediately and the row reads AutoApplied.
controls.evidence: coa_change_requests rows (payload/before/created_by/approved_by/reason); compliance harness + unit ToE

controls.risk: Petty-cash / employee advance issued but never accounted for — cash leakage, expense unrecorded.
controls.description: Employee cash advances: issuing an advance debits the 1180 Employee Advances control (Dr 1180 / Cr 1000); settlement clears it against actual spend + returned cash (Dr expense + Dr 1000 / Cr 1180), enforced to reconcile (settled_expense + returned_cash must equal the advance → SETTLE_MISMATCH). The 1180 balance is the outstanding float subject to review.
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts (issueAdvance, settleAdvance reconcile-or-reject, listAdvances outstanding); finance.controller.ts (creditors/exec); schema/finance.ts (employee_advances) + migration 0120; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect issue/settle GL + settlement reconciliation guard.
controls.toe: Issue an advance (1180 up); settle 700+300 clears 1180; mismatched settle → SETTLE_MISMATCH (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Advance register; outstanding float

controls.risk: Petty-cash disbursed beyond authority / without independent review, or drawn past the fund's limit — cash leakage, unauthorised or unrecorded expense, over-extended float.
controls.description: Petty-cash imprest float + maker-checker on disbursement (SoD): a fund holds cash capped at a credit limit/วงเงิน (1015 Petty Cash, a cash account); each direct EXPENSE or ADVANCE is a REQUEST that posts NOTHING and a DIFFERENT user must approve before the GL posts (expense Dr <expense> / Cr 1015; advance Dr 1180 / Cr 1015) and the fund balance is decremented. Self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin). A draw beyond the fund balance → INSUFFICIENT_FLOAT; establishing/replenishing above the float limit → OVER_FLOAT. Advances settle (Dr expense + Dr 1015 returned / Cr 1180), returning unused cash to the fund. Every request carries a document reference + receipt key + a status trail (StatusLog) and surfaces in the GOV-01 pending-approvals monitor.
controls.code_reference: petty-cash.service.ts (establishFund/replenishFund float guards, createRequest INSUFFICIENT_FLOAT, approveRequest SoD+GL, rejectRequest, settleRequest); petty-cash.controller.ts (creditors/exec); schema/petty-cash.ts (petty_cash_funds, expense_requests) + migration 0139; finance.service.ts pendingApprovals (GOV-01 source 7); cutover/basics.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the float ceiling + the request→approve flow + the approver≠requester check + that the request posts nothing until approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: establish a fund (Dr 1015), request an expense (no GL), self-approve → SOD_VIOLATION, approve as a different user → Dr expense/Cr 1015 + fund down; over-balance draw → INSUFFICIENT_FLOAT; advance approve→settle clears 1180; replenish past float → OVER_FLOAT (re-performed by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Fund register + disbursement approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: A PO-based supplier invoice is paid before it is reconciled to its PO and goods receipt — disbursing cash for goods not received, at the wrong quantity, or above the agreed price (over-payment / duplicate / fictitious-receipt fraud), because the AP-pay flow never consults the 3-way match.
controls.description: 3-way-match PAYMENT GATE: the AP disbursement flow (requestApPayment) calls ThreeWayMatchService.assertPayable BEFORE recording the payment request — a PO-based invoice that was run through match() and did NOT pass (qty/price variance beyond tolerance, over-invoiced, or unmatched line) and was NOT independently overridden is BLOCKED (MATCH_BLOCKED) so no cash can be requested or approved against it. The gate FAILS-OPEN for a non-PO bill (utilities / services / reimbursements never have a match row) so legitimate non-PO payables remain payable; it only blocks invoices subject to the match. Complements EXP-01 (the match + the maker-checked override) by wiring the verdict into the cash path and EXP-06 (the AP disbursement maker-checker).
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts (requestApPayment → matchSvc.assertPayable); match/three-way-match.service.ts (assertPayable fail-open for non-PO, blocks not-payable & not-overridden); finance.controller.ts (ap/transactions/:txnNo/pay); cutover/match.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect that the AP-pay request consults assertPayable and that a non-PO bill is exempt while a failed PO invoice is blocked.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a PO invoice with a qty/price variance over tolerance → pay request MATCH_BLOCKED; an independently-overridden / matched invoice → pay succeeds; a non-PO bill pays unblocked (re-performed by the match harness).
controls.evidence: Blocked-invoice worklist; payment-gate test

controls.risk: A scanned/keyed vendor invoice is booked against the wrong PO (or twice under the same invoice number), or an intake bill bypasses the 3-way match — a mis-mapped or duplicate document becomes a payable and cash is disbursed for it.
controls.description: AP invoice INTAKE pipeline: a scanned invoice — pasted text or a DIRECT IMAGE/PDF UPLOAD (type/size-gated; a digital PDF extracts deterministically from its text layer, a photo/scan via AI vision when keyed, and with no key it queues for human review with empty fields so a blank bill can never book; the source document is stored on the intake for audit) — is extracted (doc-ai), AUTO-MAPPED to its PO (explicit PO number in the document, else vendor + amount scoring) and, on posting, the 3-way match is run in the SAME flow so an intake bill can never exist unmatched. The mapper only auto-maps an UNAMBIGUOUS winner — weak scores or tied candidates queue as NeedsReview for a human; a duplicate vendor invoice number (vs earlier intakes AND booked AP bills) is refused at post (DUPLICATE_INVOICE) and never auto-posted. Header-level matches enforce a CUMULATIVE guard (all invoices matched to a PO consume its received value, so one PO cannot be double-billed under two invoice numbers). A blocked intake invoice is released only by the scheduled auto re-match (ap_automatch_rerun) after the GR catches up, or by the EXP-01 maker-checked override. Booking (post/auto) requires the creditors duty; payment stays behind EXP-09's gate + EXP-06's maker-checker — the pipeline automates the path TO payment-ready, never the disbursement.
controls.code_reference: ap-intake/ap-intake.service.ts (mapToPo, findDuplicate, post→match); ap-intake/ap-intake.controller.ts (post/auto gated creditors); doc-ai/doc-ai.service.ts (extraction incl. po_no); match/three-way-match.service.ts (header amount match + cumulative guard + rematchBlocked); bi.service.ts (ap_automatch_rerun); schema 0229 ap_invoice_intakes; cutover/match.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the mapper's ambiguity/duplicate handling and that posting always runs the match for a PO-mapped intake.
controls.toe: Re-perform: PO-numbered scan auto-posts matched+payable; tied candidates → NeedsReview; duplicate invoice no → 409 DUPLICATE_INVOICE; 2nd invoice on a fully-invoiced PO → over_invoiced blocked; invoice ahead of GR blocked then released by the scheduled re-match (re-performed by the match harness).
controls.evidence: Intake worklist; duplicate/cumulative guard tests

controls.risk: A vendor's payee bank details (bank_name/bank_account) are changed by a single user with no independent review — the classic Business-Email-Compromise / vendor-payment-fraud vector (an attacker who compromises or colludes with one md_vendor holder silently redirects the next disbursement to their own account).
controls.description: Vendor bank-detail maker-checker: a bank_name/bank_account change is STAGED (PendingApproval, carrying the previous values) by an md_vendor holder and applied to the vendors master only when a DIFFERENT exec/approvals user releases it (self-approval → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin). Re-staging supersedes any still-open request so the approval queue always reflects only the latest ask; reject leaves the vendor untouched.
controls.code_reference: procurement.service.ts (stageBankChange/pendingBankChanges/approveBankChange/rejectBankChange); procurement.controller.ts (vendors/:id/bank md_vendor stages; vendor-bank-changes/:reqNo/approve|reject exec/approvals); schema/vendor-bank-change.ts (vendor_bank_change_requests) + migration 0270; cutover/match.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the stage→approve routing + the approver≠requester guard + that vendors only changes on approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: stage a bank change (PendingApproval), self-approval blocked (403 SOD_VIOLATION), a different exec/approvals user approves and vendors.bank_name/bank_account updates; re-staging supersedes the earlier pending request; reject leaves the vendor unchanged (re-performed by the match harness).
controls.evidence: Vendor bank-change approval log

controls.risk: Maker-checker on master data covered only BULK imports (masterdata_import_batches) and a vendor's bank_name/bank_account (EXP-11, 0270). A NORMAL single-record UI/CRUD edit of a SENSITIVE vendor field — its payee account-holder NAME (bank_account_name), its CREDIT LIMIT, or its PAYMENT TERMS — was written to the master directly with no second check, so a single md_vendor holder could redirect a disbursement (substitute the payee name on file), inflate credit exposure, or alter the payment schedule with no independent review — a disbursement-fraud / BEC vector alongside the bank-account number already governed by EXP-11.
controls.description: Sensitive master-data single-record maker-checker (GRC-3, migration 0340): a change to a sensitive vendor field (bank_account / bank_name / bank_account_name / credit_limit / payment_terms) does NOT write the vendor master — it is STAGED as a `pending` masterdata_change_requests row (a GENERIC entity_type vendor|customer|item + field + before/after value; old_value/new_value encrypted at rest via the encryptedText column type so a staged bank value is never plaintext). The master is applied ONLY when a DISTINCT user approves it (POST /api/masterdata/change-requests/:reqNo/approve; approved_by ≠ requested_by → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin), which supersedes the direct-edit path (payment_terms was removed from the vendor-profile direct edit; credit_limit / bank details were already excluded). Reject discards it (the master is untouched). Non-sensitive profile fields keep their direct-edit path (the generic endpoint refuses them → FIELD_NOT_SENSITIVE). The applied change is captured before/after by the DB trigger trg_dcl_vendors (0274) on the master row; the change-request row is the full staging trail (who requested / who released). New SoD rule R22 (master-data maintenance md_vendor/md_item/md_config vs the elevated change-approval authority exec). Tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy); reads/stage gate masterdata/md_vendor/exec, approve/reject gate masterdata/exec.
controls.code_reference: modules/masterdata/masterdata-change.service.ts (stageChange NO-write + supersede; approve applies to the master with SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; reject discards; FIELD_NOT_SENSITIVE / NO_CHANGE guards); masterdata-change.controller.ts (/api/masterdata/change-requests[|/:reqNo/approve|/:reqNo/reject]); schema/masterdata-change.ts masterdata_change_requests + vendors.bank_account_name (migration 0336); procurement.service.ts/.controller.ts (payment_terms removed from the vendor-profile direct edit); permissions.ts SoD R22; apps/web /masterdata/change-requests (reviewer queue + propose form); cutover/masterdata-change.ts (ToE).
controls.tod: Inspect the stage→approve routing, the requester≠approver self-approval block, that the vendor master is written ONLY on approval, that reject/pending leave it unchanged, and that a non-sensitive field is refused by the maker-checker (still edits directly).
controls.toe: Re-perform (masterdata-change harness): stage a vendor bank_account_name change → pending, master unchanged; self-approve → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a DISTINCT approver → master updated; an encrypted bank_account round-trips; stage credit_limit → reject → master unchanged; payment_terms applies ONLY via the maker-checker; a non-sensitive field (category) still edits directly and the generic endpoint refuses it (FIELD_NOT_SENSITIVE); a second tenant sees 0 requests and cannot approve one (RLS).
controls.evidence: Master-data change-request queue (pending/approved/rejected) + before/after; SOD_SELF_APPROVAL / FIELD_NOT_SENSITIVE negatives

controls.risk: Goods 'received' without a physical count (blind confirmation), more stock booked than was ordered (over-receipt → inflated inventory + AP exposure), a short/damaged delivery discovered too late to claim against the supplier, or a short-shipped PO left open indefinitely (stale open commitments).
controls.description: Blind-count goods receiving with an over-receipt tolerance gate, a time-boxed supplier-claim window, and an explicit shortage decision. (1) The /receiving screen loads the selected PO's lines (ordered / already received / outstanding via GET pos/:poNo/receive-lines) but NEVER pre-fills the counted quantity — the receiver must count and key what actually arrived. (2) createGr BLOCKS any line that would exceed the ordered quantity (422 OVER_RECEIPT), checked on the aggregate requested per item; only a WEIGHT-basis UoM (kg/g/ton) may run over, and only within the configurable receiving tolerance (receiving_settings.over_receipt_weight_pct, default 5%). Changing the tolerance is restricted to procurement/exec — the receiver cannot loosen their own gate (mirrors EXP-04). (3) The post-receipt summary shows ordered vs received per line; a supplier claim (photo evidence stored as a GRC doc_attachment) must be opened within receiving_settings.claim_window_hours of goods_receipts.created_at (default 24h) — after that the window auto-closes and the system refuses the claim (422 CLAIM_WINDOW_CLOSED). (4) On a shortage the receiver decides: keep the PO open for a later delivery, or CLOSE IT SHORT (POST pos/:poNo/close-short) — the close releases open project commitments and is binding at line level (po_items.status=Closed → any further receipt is refused, 422 PO_LINE_CLOSED).
controls.code_reference: procurement/procurement-grn.service.ts (receiveLines, createGr OVER_RECEIPT/PO_LINE_CLOSED gates, closePoShort, getReceivingSettings); procurement.controller.ts (receive-lines/close-short/receiving-settings routes); claims/claims.service.ts (createGrClaim CLAIM_WINDOW_CLOSED + GRC photo attachment); schema receiving_settings + goods_receipts.created_at (migration 0290); apps/web /receiving (blind-count form + summary + claim + close-short); cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the over-receipt gate (aggregate per item, weight-UoM tolerance), the claim-window check against the GR timestamp, the close-short line binding, and the tolerance-change permission.
controls.toe: Re-perform: receive 12 on a 10-EA line → 422 OVER_RECEIPT; a kg line accepts up to +5% and refuses beyond; a claim inside the window opens with its photo, the same claim after the window → 422 CLAIM_WINDOW_CLOSED; close-short flips the PO+lines Closed, and a further receipt → 422 PO_LINE_CLOSED (re-performed by the compliance harness).
controls.evidence: GR summary (ordered vs received); OVER_RECEIPT / CLAIM_WINDOW_CLOSED / PO_LINE_CLOSED negatives; claim photo attachment

controls.risk: Supplier defects are captured (gr_claims) and supplier performance is scored (supplier_scorecards), but no FORMAL corrective-action request (8D/SCAR) is issued to the vendor and tracked to closure: a recurring supplier defect is never escalated into a documented root-cause / corrective-action commitment, an issued SCAR is closed on an unverified (or fabricated) 8D response, the SAME person both raises and closes the SCAR (self-sign-off requalifying a defective supplier), or an open corrective action drifts past its due date unactioned — so defective suppliers keep shipping and the quality escape recurs with no evidence of an independent closure review.
controls.description: Supplier Corrective Action Request (SCAR / 8D) register with a closure maker-checker + an overdue detective read (QMS-4, migration 0334). A SCAR is RAISED against a vendor, optionally sourced from a REAL gr_claim (a fabricated claim reference is rejected — CLAIM_NOT_FOUND / CLAIM_VENDOR_MISMATCH), carrying the 8D discipline fields (containment / root_cause / corrective_action / preventive_action). Lifecycle open → supplier_responded → pending_closure → closed | rejected. QC-04 closure gate: a SCAR is CLOSED only (1) after the supplier has responded AND the 8D root_cause + corrective_action are populated (SCAR_INCOMPLETE otherwise) and (2) by a DIFFERENT user than the raiser (closed_by ≠ raised_by → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, binds even Admin; the closure-reviewer duty quality_approve is segregated from the raise duty quality — SoD R21), recording an effectiveness verdict (effective | ineffective). Closing an 'effective' SCAR is the gate that permits a suppressed supplier to be requalified — the verdict is RECORDED here; the scorecard math (procurement.service.ts recomputeScorecard) is never touched. Reject (≠ raiser, reason required) returns the SCAR for re-work. DETECTIVE: GET /api/quality/scar/open?days=N is the overdue supplier-corrective-action worklist (open SCARs past their due_date). Tenant-scoped (RLS, canonical 0232 policy); reads gate quality/quality_approve/creditors/exec, raise quality/creditors, closure quality_approve/exec.
controls.code_reference: quality/scar.service.ts (raise — real-gr_claim gate CLAIM_NOT_FOUND/CLAIM_VENDOR_MISMATCH; respond — 8D fields → supplier_responded; submitClosure SCAR_INCOMPLETE → pending_closure; close — QC-04 SCAR_INCOMPLETE + SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + effectiveness verdict; reject REASON_REQUIRED/SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; openWorklist overdue detective); quality/scar.controller.ts (/api/quality/scar[|/open|/:id|/:id/respond|/submit-closure|/close|/reject]); schema/supplier-scar.ts supplier_scars (migration 0334); permissions.ts quality/quality_approve + SoD R21; cutover/supplier-scar.ts (ToE). Builds on gr_claims/vendors; does NOT recompute supplier_scorecards.
controls.tod: Inspect the raise-from-real-claim gate, the 8D-completeness closure precondition, the raiser≠closer self-approval block, the recorded effectiveness verdict (requalification gate), and the overdue detective worklist.
controls.toe: Re-perform (supplier-scar harness): raise a SCAR from a real gr_claim (a non-existent claim ref → CLAIM_NOT_FOUND); close before the supplier response/8D → SCAR_INCOMPLETE; the supplier responds populating root_cause + corrective_action → supplier_responded → submit-closure → pending_closure; the raiser self-closing → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a DISTINCT reviewer closes effective → closed + requalifies_supplier; an open past-due SCAR surfaces on GET …/open and a closed one does not; reject with no reason → REASON_REQUIRED, a distinct reviewer rejects → rejected; a second tenant sees 0 HQ SCARs and cannot close one (RLS).
controls.evidence: SCAR register (open/responded/pending/closed/rejected) + 8D fields; closure maker-checker log (closed_by/effectiveness); overdue SCAR worklist

controls.risk: A batch vendor-payment run selects and disburses many bills at once: a single user could select, approve and release the whole batch (mass disbursement with no independent review), an unmatched/blocked PO invoice could ride into the batch, the file handed to the bank could differ from the approved run (amounts/beneficiaries altered after approval), or a re-run could double-pay every line.
controls.description: AP payment-run maker-checker with a hash-pinned bank file: (1) a 'creditors' holder PROPOSES the run (open approved AP selected by due-date cutoff; EVERY line must pass the 3-way-match payment gate assertPayable — a blocked PO invoice is skipped at proposal and re-checked at approval, MATCH_BLOCKED); lines are editable only while Draft. (2) A DIFFERENT user with the same authority as a manual AP payment approval (approvals/gl_close) APPROVES the run — proposer self-approval → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin. (3) EXECUTION (also blocked for the proposer) posts each line through the EXISTING per-payment path (requestApPayment→approveApPayment: row-locked paid_amount, identical Dr 2000 / Cr 2361 WHT / Cr 1000 GL, WHT on the pre-VAT base) and is IDEMPOTENT per line (run-scoped idempotency key + Paid-line skip; a partial failure leaves per-line status and a re-execute retries only failed lines — a double-execute pays nothing twice). WHT certificates (50-ทวิ) issue via the existing idempotent batch cert job. (4) The Thai bank bulk-transfer file (generic/scb/kbank/bbl CSV or ISO 20022 pain.001) is generated only from an approved/executed run, FAILS CLOSED on a missing vendor bank account (VENDOR_BANK_MISSING — beneficiaries come from the maker-checked vendor master, EXP-11), and its SHA-256 is pinned on the run + status-logged so the file handed to the bank is provably the approved run's file. (5) Bank-statement auto-match clears run lines (per-payment or bulk-total), giving a detective completeness check that the approved outflow actually left the bank.
controls.code_reference: finance/ap-payment-run.service.ts (propose/editLines/submit/approve SOD/execute idempotent/bankFile sha256); finance.controller.ts (ap/payment-runs routes — creditors proposes, approvals|gl_close approves/executes); match/three-way-match.service.ts assertPayable (per-line gate); tax/tax-jobs.service.ts runWhtCertBatch (50-ทวิ); bank/bank.service.ts autoMatch (line + run-total clearing); schema/finance.ts ap_payment_runs/ap_payment_run_lines + migration 0296; cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the run lifecycle (Draft→PendingApproval→Approved→Executed), the proposer≠approver/executor guards, the per-line match gate, the per-line idempotency, the fail-closed bank-file generation + hash pinning, and the statement-clearing hook.
controls.toe: Re-perform: propose a run (blocked invoice skipped), self-approve → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, approve as a different user, execute posts each line through the manual path (TB balanced; 2361 credited per WHT line; bill Paid), re-execute is a no-op, bank file generated with its SHA-256 recorded + status-logged, missing vendor bank account → 400 VENDOR_BANK_MISSING, statement auto-match flips lines cleared (re-performed by the compliance harness).
controls.evidence: Payment-run approval log; per-line APP/PAY-AP references; bank-file SHA-256 status-log entries; cleared-line progress

controls.risk: Dynamic / early-payment (prompt-payment) discounting on the AP payment run: an unauthorised or manipulated discount schedule could be applied without independent review (a single user sets favourable terms and pays against them), a discount could be taken when the bill was NOT actually paid early (fabricated days-early → understated cash / overstated income), or the discount captured on a run could be mis-posted (cash reduced without recognising the income, or vice-versa) — mis-stating disbursements, purchase-discount income and the AP subledger.
controls.description: Early-payment discount policy CHANGE CONTROL + a controlled discount-taken posting on the FIN-2 AP payment run. (1) A sliding-scale discount schedule (ap_discount_terms — per-vendor or a global default; nominal rate, min-days-early threshold, full-discount-days window, prorate flag, income account) is MAKER-CHECKED: a 'creditors' holder creates it Draft; a DIFFERENT approvals/gl_close user ACTIVATES it (self-activation → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, binds even Admin), and activating one supersedes the prior Active policy for the same vendor scope so at most one Active policy governs a vendor. Only an Active policy is applied. (2) At PROPOSE/EDIT the run computes days_early = due_date − pay_date and the resolved sliding-scale rate (prorated up to the full rate at the window), capping the discount so cash-out (amount − WHT − discount) can never go negative; the projected savings are surfaced on the proposal (projected_discount) for the approver. A bill NOT paid early (days_early < min) earns nothing. (3) At EXECUTION each early line rides the UNCHANGED per-payment maker-checker path (identical Dr 2000 / Cr 2361 WHT / Cr 1000 posting settling the FULL payable) and then books an IDEMPOTENT discount adjustment (Dr 1000 restore the cash not paid / Cr 4600 Early-Payment Discount Income) keyed by a distinct source_ref (AP-DISC:<txn>:<payment>), so the net cash disbursed = amount − WHT − discount, the payable is fully cleared, the discount is recognised as income, and a re-execute never double-books it. Maker-checker on the run itself (proposer≠approver≠executor) is unchanged (EXP-13).
controls.code_reference: finance/ap-payment-run.service.ts (resolveDiscountPolicy/computeDiscount, propose+editLines projected discount, execute AP-DISC adjustment JE idempotent, createDiscountTerm/approveDiscountTerm SOD/rejectDiscountTerm/deactivateDiscountTerm); finance.controller.ts (ap/discount-terms routes — creditors creates, approvals|gl_close activates); ledger.service.ts postEntry/alreadyPosted + COA 4600 (Early-Payment Discount Income); schema/finance.ts ap_discount_terms + ap_payment_run_lines discount cols + migration 0309; cutover/compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the policy maker-checker (creator≠activator, one Active policy per vendor scope), the days-early/sliding-scale computation + the cash-out cap, and the balanced Dr 1000 / Cr 4600 discount posting keyed for idempotency.
controls.toe: Re-perform: creditors drafts a 2/20 policy, self-activation → 403 SOD_VIOLATION, a different approver activates; a bill paid 30 days early projects a ฿20 (2%) discount and net ฿980; execute credits 4600 ฿20, fully pays the bill, sets run total_discount ฿20 and keeps the TB balanced; a vendor with NO active policy earns ฿0 (re-performed by the compliance harness).
controls.evidence: Discount-policy approval log; per-run projected_discount + total_discount; AP-DISC discount-income journal entries

controls.risk: Carrying amount not adjusted for revaluation/impairment — asset over/understated.
controls.description: Asset revaluation / impairment: an upward revaluation credits the revaluation surplus in equity (Dr 1500 / Cr 3200); a downward revaluation (impairment) debits impairment loss (Dr 5820 / Cr 1500). The gross 1500 moves by the delta so the register stays tied to the GL; a no-op revaluation is rejected (NO_CHANGE). Every event is logged for the audit trail.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts (revalue up→3200 / down→5820, listRevaluations; dispose recycles surplus 3200→3100); assets.controller.ts (exec/creditors); schema/assets.ts (asset_revaluations) + migration 0120; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect revaluation/impairment routing + audit log + disposal recycling.
controls.toe: Upward revaluation credits 3200; impairment debits 5820; no-change → NO_CHANGE; both events logged; on disposal the revaluation surplus is recycled to retained earnings (Dr 3200 / Cr 3100), not through P&L (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Revaluation log; disposal recycling JE

controls.risk: Carrying amount changed by fair-value revaluation/impairment without independent review — the preparer revalues the asset and posts to equity/P&L on their own (earnings/equity management).
controls.description: Asset-revaluation maker-checker (SoD): a revalue/impairment request posts its GL entry as a DRAFT (excluded from balances) and records status 'PendingApproval' WITHOUT moving the carrying value; a DIFFERENT user must approve before the JE is effective and the register is re-valued (approver ≠ preparer enforced regardless of permissions, reusing the GL-05 ledger approval). Reject voids the draft; only one revaluation may be pending per asset; the disposal surplus recycle counts only approved revaluations.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts revalue(pendingApproval Draft)/approveRevaluation/rejectRevaluation; assets.controller.ts (:assetNo/revalue/approve|reject); schema/assets.ts asset_revaluations.status + migration 0134; cutover/basics.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect Draft→approve flow + the approver≠preparer check + deferred carrying-value update.
controls.toe: Re-perform: request a revaluation (Draft JE excluded from 3200), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user; confirm the surplus/impairment + carrying value move only after approval (re-performed by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Revaluation approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: Lease not capitalised (IFRS 16 / TFRS 16) — ROU asset + lease liability omitted; interest/depreciation mis-stated.
controls.description: Lease accounting: at commencement a right-of-use asset and lease liability are recognised at the present value of the lease payments (Dr 1600 / Cr 2600). The scheduled job lease_periodic_run posts each period — interest unwinding on the liability (Dr 5900), the cash payment reducing the liability (Dr 2600 / Cr 1000), and straight-line ROU depreciation (Dr 5210 / Cr 1690) — the last period clearing the liability + ROU exactly. Idempotent per (lease, period).
controls.code_reference: leases.service.ts (createLease PV recognition, runDueLeases interest+payment+depreciation, modifyLease remeasurement); leases.controller.ts (gl_post); bi.service.ts (lease_periodic_run); schema/leases.ts (leases, rou_nbv) + migration 0120/0121; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect PV recognition + periodic interest/payment/depreciation + final-period clearing + modification remeasurement.
controls.toe: Commencement recognises ROU=liability=PV; periodic run posts interest+principal(=payment)+ROU depreciation; liability + ROU reduce; a modification remeasures the liability at the revised PV and adjusts the ROU by the same delta (Dr/Cr 1600↔2600), then depreciates over the remaining term (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Lease register; periodic run log; modification remeasurement

controls.fsli: Net investment in lease; Interest income; Rental income
controls.risk: Lessor lease mis-classified or income mis-recognised (IFRS 16 / TFRS 16 lessor) — a finance lease left on the balance sheet as an asset (no net investment / interest income) or an operating lease derecognised in error; classification set without independent review.
controls.description: Lessor-side lease accounting (FIN-10). Each lease is classified finance vs operating per the IFRS 16 lessor criteria (transfer of ownership / bargain purchase / lease term a major part of the asset's economic life ≥75% / PV of the payments ≥90% of fair value). Classification + commencement is MAKER-CHECKER: the lease is created 'pending' and a DIFFERENT user approves it (creator self-approval → 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) before any GL posts. FINANCE lease: at approval the underlying asset is derecognised (Cr 1500) and a net investment in lease / lease receivable is booked at the PV of the payments (Dr 1610), selling profit/loss to 1510; the scheduled periodic run recognises interest income on the running receivable (Cr 4600) and collects the cash (Dr 1000), reducing the receivable by the principal — the last period clearing it exactly. OPERATING lease: the asset stays on the lessor's books, the run recognises straight-line rental income (Dr 1000 / Cr 4610) and continues depreciating the asset (Dr 5200 / Cr 1590). A detective net-investment reconciliation ties GL 1610 to the sum of the finance-lease receivable balances on the schedule.
controls.code_reference: lessor-leases.service.ts (classify, createLease pending, approveLease maker-checker + finance-lease commencement, runDueLeases interest/rental income + depreciation, reconcileReceivable); lessor-leases.controller.ts (gl_post/exec); schema/lessor-leases.ts (lessor_leases) + migration 0309; ledger-constants.ts (COA 1610/4600/4610 + CF_CLASSIFY 1610); cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect classification against the IFRS 16 lessor criteria, the maker-checker (creator ≠ approver) gate, finance-lease derecognition + net-investment recognition, periodic interest/rental income + depreciation, and the 1610 tie-out.
controls.toe: Classification boundary flips finance↔operating at the 75%/90% bright lines; creator self-approval → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a distinct approver books finance-lease commencement (Dr 1610 / Cr 1500); periodic run recognises interest income (finance) or straight-line rental income + continued depreciation (operating); GL 1610 ties to the receivable schedule (reconciled, difference 0) — re-performed by the harness.
controls.evidence: Lessor lease register; classification approvals; periodic income log; net-investment reconciliation

controls.risk: Subledgers diverge from GL undetected.
controls.description: Subledger-to-GL reconciliation per period: import GL, auto-match, certify (sign-off via 'approvals').
controls.code_reference: reconciliation.service.ts (importGlItems/autoMatch/certify)
controls.tod: Inspect recon + certify gate.
controls.toe: Sample periods: reconciled, unmatched cleared, certified.
controls.evidence: Certified recon

controls.risk: Bank balance not reconciled to GL.
controls.description: Bank reconciliation against statements.
controls.code_reference: bank module; drizzle/0015_bank_reconciliation
controls.tod: Inspect bank-rec process.
controls.toe: Sample months reconciled & reviewed.
controls.evidence: Bank rec

controls.risk: A bank fee/interest adjustment is posted straight to the GL by one person during reconciliation — a cash outflow mis-booked as interest income, or a fictitious fee posted, with no independent review (single-person posting to the cash account).
controls.description: Bank adjustment maker-checker (SoD): a fee/interest adjustment on a statement line is a REQUEST that posts a DRAFT JE (no balance/GL effect; the line stays unreconciled) until a DIFFERENT user with approval authority approves (POST /api/bank/lines/:id/adjustment/approve). Self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin); approval flips the line to reconciled and the JE to Posted; reject voids the Draft and frees the line. The Draft JE (source BANKADJ) is also surfaced, aged, by the pending-approvals monitor (GOV-01).
controls.code_reference: bank.service.ts requestAdjustment/approveAdjustment/rejectAdjustment/listPendingAdjustments (reuses ledger.approveEntry SoD); bank.controller.ts (lines/:id/adjustment requests; adjustment/approve|reject gated approvals/gl_close); cutover/bankrec.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the request→approve flow + the approver≠requester check + that the Draft posts nothing until approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: request a fee adjustment (line unreconciled, GL difference unchanged), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user; confirm the difference closes to 0 only after approval (re-performed by the bankrec harness).
controls.evidence: Bank adjustment queue + SoD test

controls.risk: Intercompany balances eliminated without an independent reconciliation sign-off.
controls.description: Per-period intercompany reconciliation sign-off (maker-checker) GATES consolidation elimination: a preparer reconciles the group's IC balances (Due-From 1150 vs Due-To 2150) for the period and signs (Prepared); an independent approver (SoD, approver ≠ preparer) approves only when the balances eliminate (Due-From = Due-To). consolidation.runConsolidation() is hard-gated — it refuses to eliminate until the period's reconciliation is Approved (IC_RECON_NOT_APPROVED).
controls.code_reference: modules/ic-reconciliation/ic-recon.service.ts (prepare/approve, SoD + eliminates gate); consolidation.service.ts runConsolidation (IC_RECON_NOT_APPROVED gate); ic_recon_periods (0205, RLS); cutover/consolidation.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the per-period IC reconciliation sign-off + the consolidation gate.
controls.toe: Re-perform: consolidation is blocked before sign-off (IC_RECON_NOT_APPROVED); the preparer cannot approve their own reconciliation (SOD_VIOLATION); an independent approver signs off (balances eliminate) → consolidation proceeds.
controls.evidence: IC reconciliation sign-off + consolidation gate ToE

controls.risk: A sub-ledger silently drifts from its GL control account at period-end and no single review catches it (incomplete/inaccurate balances reach the financial statements).
controls.description: Period-end control-account reconciliation PACK: one read (GET /api/finance/reconciliation/controls) ties every major sub-ledger to its GL control account and FLAGS any out-of-balance — AR↔1100, AP↔2000, Inventory↔1200, Gift cards/Customer-deposits↔2200, Deferred revenue↔2400 — reporting per line {sub_ledger, gl_control, variance, reconciled} plus an overall all_reconciled flag and an exceptions count. Liability controls are sign-flipped before comparison. The controller's single 'are the books reconciled?' close view; an exception is a control finding to investigate before sign-off.
