controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts reconcileControls; finance.controller.ts GET reconciliation/controls (exec/ar/creditors); reuses ledger.trialBalance + the AR/AP/inventory/gift-card/deferred sub-ledgers; cutover/giftcards.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the pack: each control account's sub-ledger query + the GL tie + the exceptions roll-up.
controls.toe: Re-perform: with a clean set, every line reconciled + all_reconciled=true; introduce a known sub-ledger/GL difference and confirm it surfaces as an exception (re-performed: gift-card 2200 + inventory 1200 ties asserted by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Reconciliation pack; exceptions list

controls.risk: Till cash dropped to the safe never reaches the bank, or a deposit isn't matched to the statement — cash sits unbanked (theft/loss exposure) or the bank balance can't be tied to the books.
controls.description: Cash banking — safe-drop → bank deposit → reconciliation: till 'drop's move cash from the drawer to the safe (drawer-only, no GL). Banking BATCHES the undeposited drops into a deposit and posts the GL (Dr <bank account, e.g. 1010> / Cr 1000 Cash), stamping each drop with the deposit id; the deposit is then RECONCILED to the bank statement (status Deposited→Reconciled). A detective read (GET /api/bank/deposits/undeposited-drops) surfaces the CASH STILL IN THE SAFE (drops with deposit_id NULL) — the unbanked exposure to chase. SoD: banking is a treasury duty (exec/ar) — segregated from the cashier (pos_till) who drops the cash, so the person who removes cash from the drawer is not the one who banks it.
controls.code_reference: bank.service.ts undepositedDrops/createDeposit (Dr bank/Cr 1000, stamps deposit_id)/reconcileDeposit/listDeposits; bank.controller.ts (deposits, deposits/undeposited-drops, deposits/:id/reconcile; exec/ar); schema/cash.ts bank_deposits + cash_movements.deposit_id + migration 0152; /cash-banking UI; cutover/cash-banking.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the undeposited-drop exposure read + the deposit GL (bank↔1000) + the reconcile step + the SoD (cashier vs treasury).
controls.toe: Re-perform: two till drops → cash-in-safe shows the total; a cashier (pos_till) cannot bank (403); treasury banks → Dr bank / Cr 1000, cash-in-safe back to 0; reconcile the deposit; trial balance balanced (re-performed by the cash-banking harness).
controls.evidence: Bank-deposit register; undeposited-drop (cash-in-safe) exposure

controls.risk: The business runs into a liquidity squeeze because no one holds a forward view of cash — the current cash/bank position, the projected trough over the coming weeks, and the FX exposure sit on separate screens, so a shortfall (or an unhedged currency position) is noticed only when it bites.
controls.description: Treasury / Cash Command — a single read-only liquidity board (GET /api/finance/metrics/cash/position, perm exec/fin_report/ar) that COMPOSES the cash view: the GL cash/bank position per account (ties to the trial balance) + the house-bank accounts, the 13-week direct cash forecast (open AR inflows − AP outflows by due date, reuses the GL-07 forecast) with the projected closing balance and the liquidity TROUGH (lowest projected balance + the week it hits), the liquidity KPI subset (current/quick/cash ratio, working capital, days-cash — same canonical definitions as ELC-07), and FX exposure by non-THB currency (open AR/AP face amounts, net position). Read-only aggregation over the posted GL + sub-ledgers — posts nothing, adds no posting authority (no SoD change). Surfaced on the /finance/treasury web screen so treasury reviews the forward cash position + FX on a cadence.
controls.code_reference: finance/finance-metrics.service.ts (cashPosition — cash/bank position from the trial balance, cashFlowForecast GL-07 13-week, liquidity KPI subset, fxExposure by currency); finance-metrics.controller.ts (GET /api/finance/metrics/cash/position); apps/web treasury (screen); cutover/finance-kpi.ts (ToE); docs/35-finance-oracle-grade-analytics-plan.md
controls.tod: Inspect the cash/bank position (ties to the trial balance), the 13-week forecast + projected trough, the liquidity KPIs, and the FX exposure by currency.
controls.toe: Re-perform (finance-kpi): on a seeded ledger total_cash ties to the GL cash account (100k), the 13-week forecast opens at 100k, layers the open-AR inflow (50k) and closes at 150k over 14 buckets, the liquidity subset carries cash_ratio 2.5, and a USD payable surfaces as an FX exposure (payable 1000, net −1000); a non-finance role is denied (403).
controls.evidence: Cash-position / treasury board (finance/metrics/cash/position); harness ToE (finance-kpi)

controls.fsli: Short-term Borrowings (2500); Long-term Borrowings (2550); Accrued Interest Payable (2450); Interest Expense (5900); Bank (1010)
controls.risk: A borrowing is recorded, drawn or accrued without control — a debt facility is set up and drawn down by one person with no independent authorisation (unauthorised leverage / cash booked to the drawer), the drawdown mis-posts principal (wrong short-/long-term borrowings control, or omitted), or the interest expense is mis-stated because the effective-interest (EIR) amortized-cost accrual is computed at the wrong rate, applied to the wrong carrying amount, or double-posted for a period — so borrowings, interest expense and the accrued-interest liability are misstated.
controls.description: Debt & Borrowings register with maker-checker + an idempotent EIR amortized-cost engine (Track C Wave 1). A facility is CREATED as PendingApproval (POST /api/treasury/facilities, maker duty `treasury`); a DIFFERENT user APPROVES it (POST .../:id/approve, checker duty `treasury_approve`) — a self-approve is rejected 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (creator ≠ approver, binds even Admin, mirroring FX-04 / TAX-11). Only an APPROVED facility may be DRAWN (POST .../:id/drawdown), which checks the drawdown against the available limit (LIMIT_EXCEEDED) and posts Dr 1010 Bank / Cr 2500 (short-term) or 2550 (long-term) Borrowings via LedgerService.postEntry (so PERIOD_LOCKED + the GL audit trail apply). Each drawdown carries an amortized-cost carrying amount (= its outstanding principal at par) and a periodic-run cursor (next_run_date / periods_posted) mirroring the lease engine (LSE-01). The EIR accrual (POST .../:id/accrue, checker duty) posts ONE month of effective interest = carrying x EIR/12 (Dr 5900 Interest Expense / Cr 2450 Accrued Interest Payable) per due drawdown and advances the cursor; it is IDEMPOTENT (the cursor moves a month per run AND alreadyPosted('DEBT-ACCR', drawdown+period) guards the JE, so re-running the same as-of posts nothing). Repayment (POST .../:id/repay) clears principal (Dr 2500/2550) + accrued interest (Dr 2450) against cash (Cr 1010), guarded (REPAY_EXCEEDS_PRINCIPAL/INTEREST). A maturity ladder (GET .../maturity-ladder) buckets outstanding principal by time-to-maturity. Tenant-scoped (RLS) so the register never leaks across companies; reads gate treasury/treasury_approve/fin_report/exec, the maker/checker split is enforced by SoD R23.
controls.code_reference: treasury-debt/debt.service.ts (createFacility PendingApproval / approveFacility SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker / drawdown LIMIT_EXCEEDED + Dr 1010 / Cr 2500|2550 / accrue idempotent EIR Dr 5900 / Cr 2450 + alreadyPosted cursor / repay Dr 2500|2550 + 2450 / Cr 1010 / maturityLadder); debt.controller.ts (api/treasury/facilities[/:id/{approve,reject,drawdown,repay,accrue,covenants}], maturity-ladder, covenants[/test,/breaches]); schema/treasury-debt.ts (debt_facilities/debt_drawdowns/debt_covenants/debt_covenant_tests, tenant-leading indexes); migration 0353 (tables + RLS + DEBT.* posting-events + treasury role_enum); ledger-constants 2450/2500/2550 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY 2500/2550 financing, 2450 operating); posting-events.ts DEBT.DRAWDOWN/INTEREST/REPAY; permissions.ts treasury/treasury_approve + SoD R23 + TreasuryAnalyst/TreasuryManager; debt.module.ts (wired, imports LedgerModule); cutover/treasury-debt.ts (TRE-01 ToE, 36 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the facility create→approve maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), the drawdown limit gate + Dr 1010 / Cr 2500|2550 posting, the EIR accrual (carrying x EIR/12 → Dr 5900 / Cr 2450) tie to a hand-computed amortization table, the accrual idempotency (cursor + alreadyPosted), and the repayment legs.
controls.toe: TC-TRE-01-01: create a facility, self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (403), a distinct approver approves; a pre-approval drawdown → FACILITY_NOT_APPROVED; TC-TRE-01-02: draw 600,000 → Dr 1010 / Cr 2550 balanced, an over-limit drawdown → LIMIT_EXCEEDED; TC-TRE-01-03: accrue @ EIR 12% (1%/mo) → interest 6,000 Dr 5900 / Cr 2450, re-accrue same period posts 0 (idempotent), a second month totals 12,000; repay 100,000 principal + 12,000 interest → Dr 2550/Dr 2450/Cr 1010, over-repay → REPAY_EXCEEDS_PRINCIPAL; TC-TRE-01-04: a sibling tenant never sees the facilities (RLS) (re-performed by the treasury-debt harness, 36 checks).
controls.evidence: Debt & borrowings register (facility Open→Approved, drawdown/repayment, EIR amortization schedule + cursor); the DEBT-DRAW/DEBT-ACCR/DEBT-REPAY JEs; the maturity ladder

controls.risk: A financial-covenant breach on a borrowing (a minimum DSCR / current ratio, a maximum leverage) goes unnoticed between reporting cycles — the covenant test is done ad-hoc on a spreadsheet with no persisted result, so a breach that could trigger an event-of-default / reclassification of long-term debt to current is not surfaced, owned or disclosed on time.
controls.description: Covenant tracking + breach detection (TRE-02): each facility carries financial covenants (POST /api/treasury/facilities/:id/covenants — metric, operator gte/lte/gt/lt, threshold, cadence). A covenant test (POST /api/treasury/covenants/test) evaluates each supplied period reading against its threshold/operator, PERSISTS a debt_covenant_tests row with a breached flag (recording the breach IS the detective control), and returns the breaches; GET /api/treasury/covenants/breaches is the outstanding-breach worklist (the latest breached test per covenant) the controller reviews each cycle. Read-mostly monitor over the debt register; tenant-scoped (RLS) so breaches never cross companies. Gated treasury_approve/exec to run tests, treasury/fin_report to read.
controls.code_reference: treasury-debt/debt.service.ts (createCovenant / testCovenants passes()+persist breached / covenantBreaches worklist); debt.controller.ts (facilities/:id/covenants, covenants/test, covenants/breaches); schema/treasury-debt.ts debt_covenants + debt_covenant_tests (tenant-leading indexes); migration 0353; cutover/treasury-debt.ts (TRE-02 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the covenant definition (metric/operator/threshold), the pass/breach evaluation, the persisted debt_covenant_tests row with the breached flag, and the breach worklist.
controls.toe: TC-TRE-02-01: define a DSCR ≥ 1.25 covenant; a test at 1.40 passes (not breached); a test at 1.10 breaches and is persisted; the breach worklist surfaces the outstanding DSCR breach; a sibling tenant never sees it (RLS) (re-performed by the treasury-debt harness).
controls.evidence: debt_covenants register + debt_covenant_tests (breached readings) + the covenant-breach worklist

controls.fsli: Investments — Amortized Cost (1350); Investments — FVOCI (1360); Investments — FVTPL (1370); Allowance for Investment ECL (1355); FVOCI Reserve — OCI (3500); Investment Income (4700); Fair-value Gain/Loss FVTPL (5430); Investment Impairment (5440); Bank (1010)
controls.risk: A marketable security is classified, marked-to-market or impaired by one person with no approved prices or independent review — a holding is bought and self-approved (unauthorised investment / cash booked to the drawer), MIS-CLASSIFIED (a fair-value holding parked at amortized cost, or the reverse, so remeasurement is routed to the wrong place — P&L instead of OCI or vice versa), marked to market against an UN-APPROVED / fat-fingered price, or impaired at a manager's discretion — so investment assets, the OCI equity reserve and earnings are misstated.
controls.description: Investment & Securities register with classification + valuation maker-checker and the reusable OCI-reserve primitive (Track C Wave 2; Wave 3 hedge accounting reuses the OCI reserve). A security is CREATED as PendingApproval (POST /api/treasury/investments, maker duty `treasury`) carrying its classification AMORTIZED_COST | FVOCI | FVTPL; a DIFFERENT user APPROVES it (POST .../:id/approve, checker duty `treasury_approve`) — self-approve is rejected 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (creator ≠ approver, binds even Admin, mirroring FX-04 / TRE-01) — and the buy posts Dr 1350 (amortized cost) | 1360 (FVOCI) | 1370 (FVTPL) / Cr 1010 Bank via LedgerService.postEntry (GL-05 balanced + period-lock). A maker-checker market-PRICE register (POST /api/treasury/prices → PendingApproval; POST .../prices/approve by a DIFFERENT user, SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; mirrors fx_rates / FX-04) drives mark-to-market: MTM (POST .../:id/revalue) values the holding at quantity x the LATEST APPROVED price — an UN-approved price is rejected NO_APPROVED_PRICE — and routes the fair-value delta by classification: FVOCI → the OCI equity RESERVE 3500 (the reusable OCI-reserve primitive), FVTPL → P&L 5430 fair-value gain/loss; an AMORTIZED_COST holding is measured at amortized cost, not marked (MTM_NOT_APPLICABLE). AMORTIZED_COST interest income accretes on the effective-interest carrying (POST .../:id/accrue → Dr 1350 / Cr 4700 Investment Income), reusing the Wave-1 EIR periodic cursor + alreadyPosted idempotency (a re-run of the same period posts nothing). ECL impairment (POST .../:id/impair) posts Dr 5440 Investment Impairment / Cr 1355 Allowance (contra-asset). Tenant-scoped (RLS) so the register never leaks across companies; reads gate treasury/treasury_approve/fin_report/exec, the maker/checker split is enforced by SoD R23.
controls.code_reference: treasury-invest/investment.service.ts (createInvestment PendingApproval / approveInvestment SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker + buy Dr 1350|1360|1370 / Cr 1010 / postPrice+approvePrice maker-checker price register (FX-04 shape) / revalue MTM from the latest APPROVED price → ociReserveLines 3500 (FVOCI) | plGainLossLines 5430 (FVTPL), MTM_NOT_APPLICABLE for amortized cost, NO_APPROVED_PRICE gate / accrue EIR interest Dr 1350 / Cr 4700 idempotent + alreadyPosted / impair ECL Dr 5440 / Cr 1355 / portfolio); investment.controller.ts (api/treasury/investments[/:id/{approve,reject,revalue,impair,accrue}], prices[/approve,/reject], portfolio); schema/treasury-invest.ts (investments/investment_prices/investment_valuations, tenant-leading indexes); migration 0354 (tables + RLS + INVEST.* posting-events); ledger-constants 1350/1355/1360/1370/3500/4700/5430/5440 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY 1350/1355/1360/1370 investing, 3500 financing); posting-events.ts INVEST.BUY/INCOME/MTM.PL/MTM.OCI/IMPAIR; permissions.ts treasury/treasury_approve + SoD R23; investment.module.ts (wired, imports LedgerModule); cutover/treasury-invest.ts (TRE-03 ToE, 40 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the investment create→approve maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) + classification routing of the buy (1350/1360/1370), the price-register maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) + the MTM-only-from-Approved-price gate (NO_APPROVED_PRICE), the FVOCI-to-OCI-3500 vs FVTPL-to-P&L-5430 remeasurement split, MTM_NOT_APPLICABLE for amortized cost, the idempotent EIR interest accrual (Dr 1350 / Cr 4700), and the ECL impairment (Dr 5440 / Cr 1355).
controls.toe: TC-TRE-03-01: create an investment, self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (403), a distinct approver approves → the buy posts to the class asset; a non-treasury role is denied. TC-TRE-03-02: buy AMORTIZED_COST/FVOCI/FVTPL → Dr 1350/1360/1370 / Cr 1010 balanced. TC-TRE-03-03: an unapproved price cannot drive MTM (NO_APPROVED_PRICE); a self-approved price → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a distinct approver approves; FVOCI MTM lands in OCI 3500 (not P&L) and FVTPL MTM lands in P&L 5430; AMORTIZED_COST MTM → MTM_NOT_APPLICABLE. TC-TRE-03-04: AMORTIZED_COST interest ties a hand-computed EIR schedule (1,000 then 1,010), re-run same period posts 0 (idempotent). TC-TRE-03-05: ECL impairment → Dr 5440 / Cr 1355; a sibling tenant never sees the register (RLS) (re-performed by the treasury-invest harness, 40 checks).
controls.evidence: Investment & securities register (holding PendingApproval→Approved, classification, MTM/ECL valuation ledger); the INVEST-BUY/MTM/ACCR/DIV/ECL JEs; the maker-checker market-price register; the portfolio roll-up

controls.fsli: Derivative Asset (1380); Derivative Liability (2460); Cash-Flow Hedge Reserve — OCI (3550); Hedge Ineffectiveness / FV-hedge P&L (5450); the hedged-item carrying account (e.g. Inventory 1200) and its reclassification revenue/P&L line (e.g. 4000)
controls.risk: Hedge accounting is applied without an approved designation/documentation or a passing effectiveness test → OCI/earnings misclassified and the IFRS 9 / TFRS 9 / ASC 815 hedge criteria are unmet (TFRS 9 is Thailand's verbatim adoption of IFRS 9, effective 2020 for PAEs; effectiveness is the principles-based assessment — economic relationship, credit risk not dominant, hedge ratio consistent with actuals — NOT the retired IAS 39 80–125% bright line). A derivative's fair-value change is deferred in the OCI hedge reserve (kept out of earnings) although the relationship was never designated/approved or its effectiveness test failed; the effective/ineffective split is mis-stated so too much (or too little) is parked in OCI vs P&L; a fair-value hedge basis-adjusts the hedged item with no effective hedge; or the deferred OCI is never recycled to earnings when the hedged cash flow occurs — so the OCI equity reserve, the hedged-item carrying amount and earnings are misstated.
controls.description: Hedge accounting register with designation + effectiveness + valuation maker-checker (Track C Wave 3; IFRS 9 / TFRS 9 · ASC 815; reuses the Wave-2 OCI-reserve primitive at a new Cash-Flow Hedge Reserve 3550). A hedge RELATIONSHIP is DESIGNATED as PendingApproval (POST /api/treasury/hedges, maker duty `treasury`) carrying the hedged item, the hedging instrument (derivative), the hedge TYPE (CASH_FLOW | FAIR_VALUE), the hedge ratio and the formal documentation (required, BAD_DOCUMENTATION); a DIFFERENT user APPROVES it (POST .../:id/approve, checker duty `treasury_approve`) — self-approve is rejected 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (creator ≠ approver, binds even Admin, mirroring FX-04 / TRE-03). THE CONTROL GATE: NO hedge/OCI accounting until the relationship is Approved (designation) AND its LATEST effectiveness test (POST .../:id/effectiveness — prospective/retrospective, method, offset ratio %, effective bool) is effective=true. Remeasuring the derivative (POST .../:id/measure) posts the fair-value change Dr 1380 Derivative Asset (gain) / Cr 2460 Derivative Liability (loss) and routes the offset: for a CASH_FLOW hedge the EFFECTIVE portion defers in the Cash-Flow Hedge Reserve 3550 (OCI equity) and the INEFFECTIVE portion hits P&L 5450 — but ONLY when Approved+effective; otherwise the OCI path is refused (HEDGE_NOT_EFFECTIVE) and the whole change is routed to P&L (to_pl). For a FAIR_VALUE hedge the derivative change hits P&L 5450 and the hedged item is BASIS-ADJUSTED (its carrying account) with an offsetting P&L leg (Approved+effective, else HEDGE_NOT_EFFECTIVE). Accounting on an undesignated/unapproved relationship is rejected HEDGE_NOT_DESIGNATED. When the hedged cash flow occurs the deferred OCI is RECLASSIFIED to earnings (POST .../:id/reclassify → Dr 3550 / Cr the hedged-item revenue/P&L line; OCI_INSUFFICIENT guards over-release). Rebalancing (POST .../:id/rebalance) adjusts the hedge ratio/notional of an approved relationship. Every posting routes through LedgerService.postEntry (GL-05 balanced + period-lock). Tenant-scoped (RLS) so the register never leaks across companies; reads gate treasury/treasury_approve/fin_report/exec, the maker/checker split is enforced by SoD R23.
controls.frequency: Per designation / month-end remeasurement / on the hedged cash flow
controls.code_reference: treasury-hedge/hedge.service.ts (designate PendingApproval + BAD_DOCUMENTATION / approve SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker / recordEffectiveness (HEDGE_NOT_DESIGNATED before approval) / measure — the CONTROL GATE: HEDGE_NOT_DESIGNATED if unapproved, CASH_FLOW OCI refused HEDGE_NOT_EFFECTIVE unless Approved+effective else to_pl routes to P&L, effective portion → 3550 / ineffective → 5450, FAIR_VALUE basis-adjusts the hedged item + P&L 5450 / reclassify Dr 3550 / Cr revenue with OCI_INSUFFICIENT guard / rebalance); hedge.controller.ts (api/treasury/hedges[/:id/{approve,reject,effectiveness,measure,rebalance,reclassify}]); schema/treasury-hedge.ts (hedge_relationships/hedge_derivatives/hedge_effectiveness_tests/hedge_oci_movements, tenant-leading indexes); migration 0356 (tables + RLS + HEDGE.* posting-events); ledger-constants 1380/2460/3550/5450 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY 3550 financing, 1380/2460 operating); posting-events.ts HEDGE.DERIVATIVE.MTM/CF.OCI/RECLASSIFY/FV.BASIS; permissions.ts treasury/treasury_approve + SoD R23; hedge.module.ts (wired, imports LedgerModule); cutover/treasury-hedge.ts (TRE-04 ToE, 38 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the designation create→approve maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), the two-part control gate (Approved designation AND a passing effectiveness test) before any OCI treatment, the CASH_FLOW effective→OCI-3550 / ineffective→P&L-5450 split vs the not-effective route-to-P&L (HEDGE_NOT_EFFECTIVE), the HEDGE_NOT_DESIGNATED bar on unapproved relationships, the FAIR_VALUE hedged-item basis adjustment, and the OCI→P&L reclassification (Dr 3550 / Cr revenue).
controls.toe: TC-TRE-04-01: designate a hedge, self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (403), a distinct approver approves; measuring an unapproved relationship → HEDGE_NOT_DESIGNATED; a non-treasury role is denied. TC-TRE-04-02: with the latest effectiveness test effective=false the CASH_FLOW OCI attempt → HEDGE_NOT_EFFECTIVE and the whole change routes to P&L 5450 (nothing to OCI); with effective=true the effective portion lands in OCI 3550 and only the ineffective portion in P&L 5450. TC-TRE-04-03: reclassification recycles the deferred OCI to revenue (Dr 3550 / Cr 4000) on the hedged cash flow; over-release → OCI_INSUFFICIENT. TC-TRE-04-04: a FAIR_VALUE hedge basis-adjusts the hedged item (Cr 1200 / Dr 5450) with the derivative gain to P&L (net ineffectiveness); a sibling tenant never sees the register (RLS) (re-performed by the treasury-hedge harness, 38 checks).
controls.evidence: Hedge accounting register (relationship PendingApproval→Approved designation, effectiveness tests, derivative FV, OCI movement ledger); the HEDGE-MTM / HEDGE-RECLASS JEs; the Cash-Flow Hedge Reserve 3550; the FV-hedge basis adjustment

controls.fsli: Intercompany Loan Receivable (1155); Intercompany Loan Payable (2155); Investment/Interest Income (4700); Interest Expense (5900); Bank (1010); the pool header/member bank accounts (e.g. 1010/1020)
controls.risk: An intercompany loan, cash-pool sweep or pool interest is posted or allocated without approval, or is NOT eliminated on consolidation — so group balances and group finance cost/income are double-counted. An IC loan is registered and drawn down by one person with no independent authorisation (unauthorised intercompany lending / cash mis-booked), the mirrored drawdown or EIR interest mis-posts one side (a receivable without its payable, or interest booked to only one company), a notional pool 'allocates' interest that does NOT sum to zero (value created or destroyed out of thin air), or — the core risk — the IC-loan receivable/payable (1155/2155) and the IC interest (4700 creditor income / 5900 debtor expense) are NOT eliminated on consolidation, so the group balance sheet grosses up an intra-group loan and the group P&L double-counts finance income against finance cost.
controls.description: Cash pooling / in-house bank / intercompany-loan register with maker-checker + consolidation-elimination integrity (Track C Wave 4, FINAL; reuses the Wave-1 EIR periodic cursor + alreadyPosted idempotency and extends the consolidation IC-elimination loop). An IC LOAN is REGISTERED as PendingApproval (POST /api/treasury/ic-loans, maker duty `treasury`) carrying creditor/debtor tenants, principal and EIR; a DIFFERENT user APPROVES it (POST .../:id/approve, checker duty `treasury_approve`) — self-approve is rejected 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (creator ≠ approver, binds even Admin, mirroring TRE-01/03/04) — and the mirrored drawdown posts Dr 1155 IC-Loan Receivable / Cr 1010 Bank (creditor) AND Dr 1010 Bank / Cr 2155 IC-Loan Payable (debtor) via LedgerService.postEntry (GL-05 balanced + period-lock). EIR interest (POST .../:id/accrue) accrues on the amortized-cost carrying → creditor Dr 1155 / Cr 4700 Investment/Interest Income and debtor Dr 5900 Interest Expense / Cr 2155, idempotently (cursor + alreadyPosted → a re-run of the same period posts nothing). A CASH POOL (POST /api/treasury/pools; notional | physical) models the in-house bank: a PHYSICAL pool SWEEPS cash member→header (POST .../:id/sweep → Dr header-bank / Cr member-bank), a NOTIONAL pool ALLOCATES the pooled interest across members (POST .../:id/allocate-interest) — an allocation that MUST sum to zero (ALLOCATION_NOT_ZERO otherwise; surplus members earn 4700 income, deficit members bear 5900 expense, group net P&L = 0). THE CONTROL CORE: consolidation.runConsolidation is extended so that for a loan whose BOTH legs are in the group the 1155/2155 receivable/payable pair AND the 4700/5900 IC interest ELIMINATE for the period (mirroring the trade-IC 1150/2150 pair), so the consolidated group balance sheet and group finance cost/income net to zero and CON-03's balanced-TB integrity holds. The IC-loan row's tenant_id = the creditor (RLS owner, mirroring ic_transactions); tenant-scoped (RLS + explicit tenant filter) so the register never leaks across companies; reads gate treasury/treasury_approve/fin_report/exec, the maker/checker split is enforced by SoD R23.
controls.frequency: Per loan / month-end accrual / on consolidation
controls.code_reference: treasury-pool/pool.service.ts (registerLoan PendingApproval / approveLoan SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker + mirrored drawdown Dr 1155 / Cr 1010 (creditor) + Dr 1010 / Cr 2155 (debtor) / accrue EIR Dr 1155 / Cr 4700 (creditor) + Dr 5900 / Cr 2155 (debtor) idempotent + alreadyPosted / definePool / sweep Dr header / Cr member NOT_PHYSICAL_POOL gate / allocateInterest zero-sum ALLOCATION_NOT_ZERO gate NOT_NOTIONAL_POOL / poolPosition); pool.controller.ts (api/treasury/pools[/:id/{sweep,allocate-interest,position}], ic-loans[/:id/{approve,reject,accrue}]); consolidation/consolidation.service.ts runConsolidation Step 2b (IC-loan 1155/2155 + 4700/5900 elimination per period → ic_loan_eliminations); schema/treasury-pool.ts (cash_pools/cash_pool_members/ic_loans/ic_loan_accruals, tenant-leading indexes); migration 0357 (tables + RLS + ICLOAN.*/POOL.* posting-events); ledger-constants 1155/2155 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY 1155 investing / 2155 financing); posting-events.ts ICLOAN.DRAWDOWN/INTEREST + POOL.SWEEP/INTEREST; permissions.ts treasury/treasury_approve + SoD R23; pool.module.ts (wired, imports LedgerModule); cutover/treasury-pool.ts (TRE-05 ToE, 46 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the IC-loan register→approve maker-checker (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL), the mirrored drawdown posting in BOTH tenants (1155 creditor / 2155 debtor), the idempotent EIR interest accrual (4700 creditor income / 5900 debtor expense), the notional zero-sum allocation gate (ALLOCATION_NOT_ZERO), the physical sweep (Dr header / Cr member), and — the control core — the consolidation elimination of the 1155/2155 pair AND the 4700/5900 IC interest (group nets to zero, CON-03 balanced).
controls.toe: TC-TRE-05-01: register an IC loan, self-approve → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (403), a distinct approver approves → the mirrored drawdown posts Dr 1155 / Cr 1010 (creditor) + Dr 1010 / Cr 2155 (debtor); a non-treasury role is denied; accrue before approval → LOAN_NOT_APPROVED. TC-TRE-05-02: accrue @ EIR 12% on 120,000 → interest 1,200 Dr 1155 / Cr 4700 (creditor) + Dr 5900 / Cr 2155 (debtor); re-accrue same period posts 0 (idempotent). TC-TRE-05-03: a notional allocation that does not sum to zero → ALLOCATION_NOT_ZERO; a zero-sum allocation posts Cr 4700 / Dr 5900 (net group P&L 0); a physical sweep → Dr header 1010 / Cr member 1020; wrong-mode calls → NOT_PHYSICAL_POOL / NOT_NOTIONAL_POOL. TC-TRE-05-04: on consolidation the group 1155/2155 AND 4700/5900 ELIMINATE to 0 (balanced, ic_loan_eliminations=1); a sibling tenant never sees the register (RLS) (re-performed by the treasury-pool harness, 46 checks).
controls.evidence: Cash-pool + in-house-bank register (pool header/members, physical sweep, notional zero-sum interest allocation); the intercompany-loan register (loan PendingApproval→Approved, mirrored drawdown, EIR interest accrual ledger); the ICLOAN-DRAW/ICLOAN-ACCR/POOL-SWEEP/POOL-ALLOC JEs; the consolidation IC-loan elimination lines (1155/2155 + 4700/5900)

controls.risk: A maker-checker approval sits un-actioned and is forgotten — a transaction stalls before it is effective, or (worse) a control is quietly bypassed and no one notices an item never got its independent review.
controls.description: Pending-approvals MONITOR (COSO monitoring): a single worklist of EVERY item awaiting independent approval across the system, each with its AGE in days and an overdue roll-up — spanning the manual-JE (GL-05), bank-adjustment (BANK-02), AP-disbursement (EXP-06), payroll (PAY-03), asset-revaluation (FA-08), asset-disposal (FA-09), inventory-write-off (INV-07), till-close cash over/short (REV-13), FX-rate (FX-04), budget (BUD-01) and large-refund (REV-16) maker-checkers. The controller reviews it before close; a stale item (age beyond the threshold) is a control finding to chase or escalate. The /approvals screen also lets a manager approve/reject a material till variance or a large refund inline (the pending types with no dedicated module screen). Read-only worklist; tenant-scoped via the caller's RLS.
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts pendingApprovals; finance.controller.ts GET approvals/pending (exec/approvals/creditors); aggregates journalEntries(Draft incl. BANKADJ→BANK-02)/apPayments/payruns/asset_revaluations/fixed_assets(disposal_pending)/inv_writeoff_requests/till_sessions(PendingApproval)/fx_rates(PendingApproval)/budgets(PendingApproval); cutover/compliance.ts (ToE); COA-D1: + postingRules PendingApproval (GL-24), coaChangeRequests PendingApproval (GL-27, platform-global), masterdataImportBatches PendingApproval (MDM-03), masterdataChangeRequests pending (MDM-01) — with /approvals per-item + batch approve routing for all four
controls.tod: Inspect the aggregation across all pending sources + the age/overdue roll-up.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a pending item (e.g. a Draft JE) and confirm it appears in the worklist with its control ID, amount and age; the overdue count flags items past the threshold (re-performed by the harness).
controls.evidence: Pending-approvals worklist

controls.risk: Control exceptions surfaced by continuous monitoring are noted but never owned or closed: an anomaly the scanner flags (duplicate invoice/payment, ghost/duplicate vendor, split PO under the approval ceiling, weekend manual JE, dormant-vendor reactivation) is reviewed ad-hoc with no accountable owner, no remediation due date, no documented root cause and no link to the RCM control it relates to, so a real red flag drifts unresolved and management has no key-control-indicator (KCI) view of exception volume, ageing or mean-time-to-remediate.
controls.description: Managed continuous-monitoring program (GRC-4): the read-only scanner (POST /api/controls/scan) runs six tenant-scoped detectors — duplicate vendor invoice (EXP-10), possible duplicate payment (EXP-01), ghost/duplicate vendor via decrypted-tax-ID grouping (EXP-02), split PO under the approval threshold (EXP-02: ≥2 non-Draft POs to one vendor within a 7-day window each below the THB 50,000 ceiling but summing over it), weekend manual JE (GL-05: source=Manual with entry_date on Sat/Sun), and dormant-vendor reactivation (EXP-05: a >180-day gap between a vendor's AP transactions) — each upserting a control_findings row by a stable fingerprint (idempotent; an already-dispositioned finding is never reset) that carries the RCM control it relates to (rcm_ref). Every exception is then DISPOSITIONED (POST /api/controls/findings/:id/disposition): an accountable owner, a remediation due_date and a documented root_cause, with a lifecycle open → investigating → remediated | accepted | false_positive; a closing disposition stamps remediated_by/at so the exception is tracked to closure. GET /api/controls/kci rolls the population up into KEY-CONTROL-INDICATORS — open exceptions by detector/severity/RCM family, an overdue count (open past due_date) and the mean time-to-remediate — the management scorecard reviewed each monitoring cycle. Read-only vs the GL; reads + disposition gate exec/users/creditors; tenant-scoped via the caller's RLS (findings never cross tenants).
controls.code_reference: modules/controls/controls.service.ts (scan 6 detectors + disposition + kci); controls.controller.ts (POST scan, GET findings, POST findings/:id/{review,disposition}, GET kci); schema/controls.ts control_findings (rcm_ref/disposition/owner/due_date/root_cause/remediated_by/at) + migration 0339_control_exceptions; cutover/control-exceptions.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the six detectors + fingerprint idempotency, the disposition lifecycle (owner/due/root-cause + closing stamp), the KCI roll-up (overdue + MTTR) and the RLS scoping.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed data triggering each detector, scan surfaces the findings each with its rcm_ref; disposition a finding (owner/due/root-cause set, still open on the KCI) then remediate it (remediated_by/at stamped, dropped from the open KCI); the KCI returns open-by-detector/severity/family, an overdue count and MTTR; another tenant cannot see the findings (RLS) — re-performed by the control-exceptions harness.
controls.evidence: Control-exception disposition register + KCI roll-up

controls.risk: The control inventory (this RCM), each control's status, and its test-of-effectiveness (ToE) results live only in an offline spreadsheet + scattered CI harness scripts — an external auditor has NO in-product way to browse the control catalogue, see which ToE passed, or pull the underlying evidence, so management's ongoing self-monitoring of ICFR operating effectiveness is neither centralised nor reviewable in-system.
controls.description: In-app CONTROL CONSOLE (auditor-facing): the full ~240-control RCM catalogue (all 17 RCM fields — risk, assertion, description, COSO principle, code reference, TOD/TOE, evidence, status) is surfaced read-only in the product from a build-time census artifact (compliance/rcm-catalog.json, regenerated alongside the RCM xlsx from build_rcm.py so it can never silently drift), grouped by control family with a live census summary. Against each control a tenant records a ToE test-run (pass/fail/na, harness name, checks passed/total, evidence reference, notes); the control-detail view then shows the latest + historical test-runs, any linked continuous-controls-monitoring (CCM) findings, and the recent tamper-evident audit-log evidence for that control. Reads + test-run recording are gated to the compliance/exec function and every test-run row is tenant-isolated by the canonical RLS policy (a tenant sees only its own ToE evidence). Read-heavy monitor; posts NOTHING to the GL.
controls.code_reference: modules/control-console/control-console.service.ts (loadCatalog reads compliance/rcm-catalog.json; rcm()/rcmDetail()/recordTestRun()); control-console.controller.ts GET /api/controls/rcm + GET /api/controls/rcm/:id + POST /api/controls/rcm/:id/test-run (exec/users); schema/control-console.ts (control_test_runs, tenant-scoped RLS + (tenant_id,…) index); drizzle/0336_control_console.sql; compliance/build_rcm.py --json -> compliance/rcm-catalog.json (catalogue source of truth); apps/web /controls/rcm (server-shell + rcm-client.tsx island); cutover/control-console.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect that the catalogue is generated from the same build_rcm.py source as the xlsx (no drift), that reads + test-run recording are permission-gated, and that control_test_runs carries a leading (tenant_id,…) index + the canonical tenant_isolation RLS policy.
controls.toe: Re-perform (control-console harness): the catalogue endpoint returns the full control inventory (>=240 controls) + the census summary; a control-detail read returns the 17 RCM fields + its test-run history; a ToE test-run is recorded against a control and read back with its result/harness/checks; and a second tenant cannot see the first tenant's recorded test-runs (RLS isolation).
controls.evidence: Control catalogue + recorded ToE test-runs + linked CCM findings / audit evidence

controls.risk: VAT computed incorrectly → filing/penalty risk.
controls.description: VAT via pluggable provider (TH 7%); unit-tested; no hard-coded rate.
controls.code_reference: tax/tax-providers.ts; tax.service.ts; test/unit.test.ts
controls.tod: Inspect rate provider + tests.
controls.toe: Re-perform VAT on sample; tie to return.
controls.evidence: VAT tests

controls.risk: Non-compliant e-Tax invoice / not transmitted to ETDA, or a failed SP submission silently lost.
controls.description: e-Tax invoice (ETDA UBL 2.1 XML) generation + email with CC to ETDA timestamp mailbox; pluggable SP adapter (configurable auth scheme + bounded retry); every SP submission attempt (accepted/rejected/error) is persisted (autocommit, survives the request rollback) and swept for retry; PDF/A-3-oriented archival embeds the XML in the human-readable PDF.
controls.code_reference: tax-docs/etax-xml.ts; etax-email.service.ts; pos-fiscal/etax.service.ts + etax-providers.ts (submit/retryFailed, tested); tax-docs/pdfa3.ts
controls.tod: Inspect XML schema + email composer + submission log.
controls.toe: Sample invoices: valid XML, correct CC/escaping; induce a submission failure and confirm it is retryable, not lost.
controls.evidence: e-Tax samples; submission log

controls.risk: Withholding tax mis-computed, not withheld at payment, or not tied to the GL.
controls.description: WHT withheld at AP payment time and posted to the GL, then tied out: when a vendor payment is approved (maker-checker), WHT is computed on the PRE-VAT base and posted Dr 2000 AP / Cr 2361 Vendor-WHT-Payable / Cr 1000 (vendor paid net), so the liability to the RD is on the books. A monthly ภ.ง.ด.3/53 → GL tie-out (pndTieOut) reconciles the GL 2361 movement to the WHT withheld on approved AP payments AND to the 50-ทวิ certificates issued — flagging out-of-process JEs (gl≠ap) and un-certificated withholdings. Payroll PND1 WHT stays a separate account (2360, PAY-02). Automation (docs/33 PR4): a schedulable, idempotent BI action job **tax_wht_cert_batch** auto-issues the 50-ทวิ certificate (มาตรา 50 ทวิ) for every AP-payment WHT in the period that does not yet have one — keyed on the payment_no so a re-run skips already-certificated payments — CLOSING the pndTieOut un-certificated-WHT gap without manual re-keying (labour/service withholding ค่าจ้างทำของ/ค่าบริการ). A payment whose vendor lacks a valid 13-digit tax ID is skipped (surfaced in the run count), not silently certificated wrong. WHT tax codes (docs/33 PR7): a payment request may carry a WHT `tax_code` that DEFAULTS the income type + rate (from the configured WHT code, ค่าจ้างทำของ/ค่าบริการ) when the operator omits them — an explicit rate/type still wins, and a non-WHT/inactive code is rejected (INVALID_WHT_TAX_CODE).
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts approveApPayment (WHT calc + GL 2361 post); tax/tax-jobs.service.ts runWhtCertBatch (auto-issue 50-ทวิ, idempotent per payment_no); bi.service.ts REPORT_TYPES tax_wht_cert_batch; tax/documents/wht.service.ts issue; tax-reports.service.ts pndTieOut (ภ.ง.ด.→GL tie-out); ap_payments wht_* (0204); ledger-constants 2361; cutover/taxdocs.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the WHT-at-payment posting + the ภ.ง.ด.→GL tie-out + the scheduled auto-cert batch.
controls.toe: Re-perform: approve a vendor payment with a WHT rate → vendor paid net, GL 2361 credited the WHT; run the tie-out → GL 2361 ties to the AP-withheld total (tied_gl_ap); run tax_wht_cert_batch → a 50-ทวิ is issued for that payment (issued 1), a re-run skips it (issued 0, no duplicate).
controls.evidence: WHT GL postings + ภ.ง.ด.→GL tie-out + scheduled 50-ทวิ auto-cert ToE

controls.risk: Output/input VAT on the filed ภ.พ.30 return does not agree to the GL — VAT liability mis-stated, a filing/penalty exposure, or a posting omission goes undetected before submission.
controls.description: Periodic VAT-return ↔ GL reconciliation (ภ.พ.30): the monthly return (GET /api/tax-reports/pp30) is built from the sales/output-VAT and purchase/input-VAT sub-reports, computes net VAT = output − input, and RECONCILES it to the **GL account 2100 (Tax Payable) net movement** for the same period (Σ credit − Σ debit over Posted entries) — returning `reconciliation.tied` = true only when the two agree within rounding. A non-tie is a detective finding to investigate and clear BEFORE filing (the form also carries the ม.157 deadline = the 15th of the next month). Output VAT is posted to 2100 on every sale (Cr) and reversed on returns (Dr); input VAT is posted on AP bills (Dr) — so 2100's net is the period's VAT payable. Read-only report; exportable to a ภ.พ.30 PDF/HTML. VAT-account routing (docs/33 PR6): when a tenant routes VAT to its own output/input accounts via a tax_code, the reconciliation sums the WHOLE VAT-account set (2100 + the configured tax-code accounts), so the ภ.พ.30↔GL tie stays exact under routing (no tax codes ⇒ set is {2100} ⇒ unchanged).
controls.code_reference: tax-reports.service.ts pp30 (outputVat/inputVat + 2100 net-movement tie); tax-reports.controller.ts (GET pp30, pp30/export, exec); tax-reports-pdf.ts; dine-in.service.ts/returns.service.ts/finance.service.ts post 2100 on sale/return/AP; /tax/reports UI; cutover/taxdocs.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the pp30 build + the 2100 net-movement query + the tie flag + the filing deadline.
controls.toe: Re-perform: the pp30 return computes net VAT (output − input), the GL 2100 net movement reflects the posted input VAT, and the reconciliation block exposes the gl_account (2100), the report net VAT and a boolean tie verdict (tied iff the two agree within rounding); the ภ.พ.30 export renders (re-performed by the taxdocs harness).
controls.evidence: ภ.พ.30 return + GL-2100 reconciliation tie

controls.risk: A return is computed but there is no evidence it was actually FILED — no record of the figures as filed, the submission date or the Revenue-Department reference — so a missed or late filing (penalty/surcharge exposure) is not detected, and a filed return can be silently recomputed/altered after submission.
controls.description: Thai tax filing register + remittance calendar: a computed ภ.พ.30 (PP30) or ภ.ง.ด.3/53 (PND) return is SNAPSHOTTED into a thai_tax_filings record (one per tenant/type/period) carrying the figures as filed (output/input/net VAT, tax withheld) and a DRAFT→SUBMITTED→ACCEPTED lifecycle. Filing is idempotent per period; submitting REQUIRES a Revenue-Department submission reference (SUBMISSION_REF_REQUIRED) and stamps SUBMITTED + the timestamp; an already-filed (SUBMITTED/ACCEPTED) period is NOT silently overwritten when re-filed (returned as-is, already_filed=true). A remittance calendar (GET /api/tax-reports/remittance-calendar) lists every monthly obligation (ภ.พ.30 by the 15th, ภ.ง.ด. by the 7th of the following month) with its current status (NOT_FILED/DRAFT/SUBMITTED/ACCEPTED), so the controller sees what is due/filed/late before the deadline. Tenant-scoped (RLS); gated exec. Automation (docs/33 PR4): schedulable BI action jobs **tax_pp30_draft** / **tax_pnd_draft** register the period's return as a DRAFT filing (via fileReturn — idempotent, never overwriting a SUBMITTED/ACCEPTED period), and **tax_remittance_reminder** surfaces the period's amounts due + statutory deadlines (ภ.พ.30 15th, ภ.ง.ด. 7th) for a proactive nudge — a human still submits.
controls.code_reference: tax-reports.service.ts fileReturn (snapshots pp30/pnd into a DRAFT, idempotent per period; no overwrite of a filed return) / submitFiling (SUBMISSION_REF_REQUIRED → SUBMITTED) / acceptFiling / listFilings / remittanceCalendar; tax-reports.controller.ts (POST filings[/:id/submit|accept], GET filings, remittance-calendar, exec); schema/tax-docs.ts thai_tax_filings + migration 0165; /tax/reports filings & calendar tab; cutover/taxdocs.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the filing snapshot + the DRAFT→SUBMITTED→ACCEPTED lifecycle + the mandatory submission reference + the no-overwrite-of-a-filed-return rule + the deadline calendar.
controls.toe: Re-perform: file PP30 → DRAFT with the period figures; re-file refreshes the same DRAFT (idempotent); submit without a reference → 400; submit with a reference → SUBMITTED + timestamp; accept → ACCEPTED; re-filing an accepted period returns it (already_filed); the remittance calendar shows the period ACCEPTED with the 15th-of-next-month deadline (re-performed by the taxdocs harness).
controls.evidence: thai_tax_filings register + remittance calendar

controls.fsli: Deferred Tax Asset (1700); Deferred Tax Liability (2700); Deferred Tax Expense (5950)
controls.risk: Book-vs-tax TEMPORARY differences (the AR allowance, accelerated depreciation, accruals) are not recognised as deferred tax, or the deferred-tax entry is computed and posted by one person with no review — income tax expense and the deferred tax asset/liability are mis-stated (TAS 12 / TFRS).
controls.description: Deferred tax (TAS 12, maker-checker, idempotent per (tenant, period)): POST /api/ledger/deferred-tax/run gathers temporary differences — the latest POSTED AR allowance (a DEDUCTIBLE temp diff → DTA = allowance × CIT) and accelerated depreciation (book NBV vs an assumed tax NBV; a TAXABLE temp diff → DTL = (bookNBV − taxNBV) × CIT) — computes net_deferred = DTA − DTL and the DELTA vs the prior posted run, staging an 'Open' deferred_tax_runs row (one per tenant/period; a Posted period → ALREADY_POSTED). CIT rate defaults to 0.20 (Thai). SIMPLIFICATION: no parallel tax-depreciation ledger exists, so tax depreciation is assumed FASTER than book by a documented factor (default 1.5×, capped at the depreciable base) — overridable per run; a deliberate approximation until a tax-dep ledger is modelled. POST /api/ledger/deferred-tax/:id/post is segregated (poster ≠ runner → SELF_POST) and posts the period DELTA via LedgerService.postEntry (delta>0 net asset up = deferred tax benefit → Dr 1700 / Cr 5950; delta<0 → Dr 5950 / Cr 1700), so PERIOD_LOCKED + the GL audit trail apply. Marks the run Posted.
controls.code_reference: deferred-tax.service.ts (runDeferredTax gather temp diffs [AR allowance, accelerated dep] + delta-vs-prior + stage Open, idempotent ALREADY_POSTED; postDeferredTax SELF_POST maker-checker + 1700/5950 delta posting; list/get); deferred-tax.controller.ts (gl_close/gl_post run + :id/post, gl_close/gl_post/exec read); schema/deferred-tax-fx.ts (deferred_tax_runs, uq tenant/period); migration 0168 (tables + RLS); COA 1700/2700/5950 (seedChartOfAccounts); ledger.module.ts (wired); close.service.ts (deferred_tax close-checklist step); reuses ar_allowance (REV-18) + fixed_assets NBV; cutover/basics.ts (TAX-06 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the temp-difference gathering (allowance×rate DTA + accelerated-dep DTL with the documented tax-dep simplification), the delta-vs-prior-run, the SELF_POST maker-checker, and the 1700/5950 posting through the period-lock gate.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-06-01: run with a posted AR allowance of 1100 at 20% → DTA 220, net deferred asset 220, delta 220 (Open); TC-TAX-06-02: runner self-post → SELF_POST (403), a different user posts → Dr 1700 / Cr 5950 (220), re-post → ALREADY_POSTED (400) (re-performed by the basics.ts harness).
controls.evidence: deferred_tax_runs register (Open→Posted, temp-difference schedule, runner/poster); the DEFTAX JE to 1700/5950; quarterly/year-end deferred-tax sign-off

controls.fsli: Deferred Tax Asset (1700); Deferred Tax Expense (5950)
controls.risk: Two mandatory ASC 740 income-tax disclosures are omitted or prepared by one person with no review: (a) the gross deferred tax asset is carried at full value with no more-likely-than-not (MLTN) recoverability assessment, so a DTA that will not be realised against future taxable income is overstated (no valuation allowance); (b) uncertain tax positions (transfer pricing, deductions at risk) are not registered with a recognised benefit / unrecognised reserve, so the tax provision and the FIN 48 disclosure are understated — and a single user could book/release a valuation allowance or close a position to flatter earnings.
controls.description: ASC 740 disclosures on top of the deferred-tax engine (TAX-06), maker-checker: (1) DTA valuation allowance — POST /api/tax/valuation-allowance/run resolves the GROSS DTA (explicit, else the latest deferred_tax_runs.dta for the tenant) and an MLTN-recoverable amount (management judgment) and computes allowance = max(0, dta_gross − mltn_recoverable) + the DELTA vs the prior posted allowance, staging an 'Open' dta_valuation_allowances row (one per tenant/period; a Posted period → ALREADY_POSTED). POST …/valuation-allowance/:id/post is segregated (poster ≠ runner → SELF_POST) and posts the DELTA via LedgerService.postEntry (allowance increase = a charge → Dr 5950 / Cr 1700; a release → Dr 1700 / Cr 5950), so PERIOD_LOCKED + the GL audit trail apply and the net DTA on the balance sheet is the recoverable portion. (2) Uncertain Tax Positions (FIN 48) — POST /api/tax/utp registers a position (tax year, gross exposure, recognized MLTN-sustainable benefit, reserve = gross − recognized, interest/penalty accrual; BENEFIT_EXCEEDS_EXPOSURE guard) as a MEMO register (no GL leg — the reserve is a disclosure). POST /api/tax/utp/:id/settle is segregated (settler ≠ creator → SELF_SETTLE; only an Open position → NOT_OPEN) and closes it Settled | Lapsed. Both surfaces are tenant-isolated (RLS).
controls.code_reference: tax/tax-utp.service.ts (runValuationAllowance gross-DTA-from-TAX-06 + allowance=max(0,gross−MLTN) + delta-vs-prior + stage Open, idempotent ALREADY_POSTED; postValuationAllowance SELF_POST maker-checker + 5950/1700 delta posting; createUtp reserve=gross−recognized + BENEFIT_EXCEEDS_EXPOSURE; settleUtp SELF_SETTLE/NOT_OPEN maker-checker; list*); tax/tax-utp.controller.ts (valuation-allowance run + :id/post gl_close/gl_post, utp create + :id/settle gl_close/gl_post, reads gl_close/gl_post/exec); tax/tax-utp.module.ts (wired into TaxModule, imports LedgerModule); schema/tax-utp.ts (dta_valuation_allowances uq tenant/period, uncertain_tax_positions uq tenant/position_no) + migration 0351 (tables + leading tenant index + 0232-form RLS); reuses deferred_tax_runs (TAX-06) + COA 1700/5950 + DocNumberService (UTP-…); cutover/tax-utp.ts (TAX-12 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the allowance computation (gross DTA sourced from TAX-06, max(0, gross − MLTN)), the delta-vs-prior-run, the SELF_POST maker-checker and the 5950/1700 posting; and the UTP register's reserve = gross − recognized, the BENEFIT_EXCEEDS_EXPOSURE guard, and the SELF_SETTLE create→settle segregation.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-12-01: run a valuation allowance with gross DTA 1000 vs MLTN 600 → allowance 400, delta 400 (Open); TC-TAX-12-02: runner self-post → SELF_POST (403), a different user posts → Dr 5950 / Cr 1700 (400) + trial balance balanced, re-post → ALREADY_POSTED (400); TC-TAX-12-03: run without an explicit gross DTA sources it (500) from deferred_tax_runs; TC-TAX-12-04: register a UTP (gross 100000, recognized 60000 → reserve 40000, Open), recognized > gross → BENEFIT_EXCEEDS_EXPOSURE (400); TC-TAX-12-05: creator self-settle → SELF_SETTLE (403), a different user settles → Settled, re-settle → NOT_OPEN (400); TC-TAX-12-06: a second tenant's positions are not visible cross-tenant (RLS) (re-performed by the tax-utp harness).
controls.evidence: dta_valuation_allowances register (Open→Posted, gross/MLTN/allowance, runner/poster); the DTAVA JE to 5950/1700; uncertain_tax_positions register (Open→Settled/Lapsed, exposure/recognized/reserve, creator/settler); quarterly/year-end deferred-tax & UTP sign-off

controls.fsli: Tax (VAT); Tax Payable (2100); Revenue (4000); Accounts Receivable (1100)
controls.risk: A sales adjustment (return / price change / defect / undercharge) is not documented on a proper ใบลดหนี้ (ม.86/10) / ใบเพิ่มหนี้ (ม.86/9), so output VAT is not adjusted in the correct filing period; OR the person who issues the note also posts its GL/VAT reversal with no independent review — revenue, output VAT and the ภ.พ.30 return are mis-stated, or a fictitious/over-stated credit note conceals a diversion of receivables.
controls.description: Credit/Debit-note maker-checker + VAT adjustment (ม.86/10 / 86/9): a seller (ar/pos) issues a credit note (ใบลดหนี้ — reduces the sale) or debit note (ใบเพิ่มหนี้ — increases it) that MUST reference the original issued full tax invoice (ORIGINAL_NOT_FOUND / ORIGINAL_NOT_ISSUED / CANNOT_ADJUST_A_NOTE) and state a REASON (REASON_REQUIRED, ม.86/10(4)); a credit note cannot exceed the original net value (CREDIT_EXCEEDS_ORIGINAL). The note is issued as status 'PendingApproval' and posts a DRAFT GL entry (credit: Dr 4000 + Dr 2100 / Cr 1100; debit: Dr 1100 / Cr 4000 + Cr 2100, via the AR sub-ledger). A DIFFERENT user (approvals/gl_close/exec) approves — approver ≠ issuer enforced regardless of permissions (SOD_VIOLATION, reusing the GL-05 ledger approval) — which posts the GL AND flips the note to 'Issued'. Only an 'Issued' note feeds the ภ.พ.30 output-VAT report, which SIGNS the note by type (credit − / debit +) into the note's issue period, so the VAT effect and the GL 2100 movement land together and the TAX-04 report↔GL tie holds. Reject voids the note + its draft entry.
controls.code_reference: tax/documents/tax-invoice.service.ts issueAdjustment (reference + reason + credit-cap guards, Draft GL via LedgerService.postEntry pendingApproval)/approveAdjustment (SOD_VIOLATION + approveEntry → Issued)/rejectAdjustment; tax/documents/tax-docs.controller.ts (credit-note/debit-note @ar/pos; :docNo/approve-note|reject-note @approvals/gl_close/exec); tax/reports/tax-reports.service.ts outputVat (sign by type); tax-docs-pdf.service.ts creditDebitNoteHtml; schema/tax-docs.ts (tax_invoice_type credit_note/debit_note + PendingApproval + original_doc_no/reason/gl_entry_no) + migration 0248; cutover/taxdocs.ts (TAX-07 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reference/reason/credit-cap validation, the PendingApproval + Draft-GL issuance, the approver≠issuer SoD, and the signed output-VAT aggregation into the issue period.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-07-01: issue a credit note (net 100) against an issued TIV → PendingApproval + a Draft GL entry, and it is EXCLUDED from output VAT while pending; TC-TAX-07-02: issuer self-approves → SOD_VIOLATION (403); a different user approves → Issued + GL Posted and output VAT falls by 7; TC-TAX-07-03: a debit note (net 50) approved raises output VAT by 3.5; TC-TAX-07-04: a credit exceeding the original → CREDIT_EXCEEDS_ORIGINAL, and a missing reason → 400 (re-performed by the taxdocs harness).
controls.evidence: ใบลดหนี้/ใบเพิ่มหนี้ register (PendingApproval→Issued/Voided, original ref + reason, issuer/approver); the CN/DN JE to 4000/2100/1100; the signed output-VAT report row

controls.fsli: Input VAT (1300); PP36 VAT Payable (2120); Expense (5100)
controls.risk: VAT on imported services (จ่ายค่าบริการให้ผู้ประกอบการต่างประเทศ) is not self-assessed and remitted via ภ.พ.36 (ม.83/6) — the reverse-charge output VAT the payer owes the RD is omitted (a filing/penalty exposure), or the self-assessed VAT is not tied to the GL before filing.
controls.description: Reverse-charge / self-assessed VAT on imported services (ม.83/6): an AP bill for an offshore/non-VAT-registered supplier is flagged reverse_charge — it carries NO vendor input VAT, so createApTxn books it at net (Dr 5100 / Cr 2000) and appends the self-assessment pair Dr 1300 Input VAT / Cr 2120 PP36 VAT Payable (7% of net), a wash on the P&L that records the ภ.พ.36 remittance obligation on a DEDICATED account 2120 kept OUT of the ภ.พ.30/2100 set (a separate return). The ภ.พ.36 report (GET /api/tax-reports/pp36) lists the imported-service bills, computes the self-assessed VAT to remit, and reconciles it to the GL 2120 net credit movement (tie verdict) so a manual JE touching 2120 out-of-process is caught; the filing is registered/submitted/accepted through the same TAX-05 lifecycle and shown on the remittance calendar due the 7th of the following month. DEFAULT-inert: the reverse_charge flag defaults false, so every existing/domestic bill is byte-for-byte unchanged.
controls.code_reference: finance.service.ts createApTxn (reverse_charge → net booking + Dr 1300 / Cr 2120 self-assessment); tax/reports/tax-reports.service.ts pp36 (report + GL 2120 tie) + fileReturn/remittanceCalendar (PP36); tax/reports/tax-reports.controller.ts GET pp36; schema/finance.ts ap_transactions.reverse_charge + migration 0280; ledger-constants 2120 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY); cutover/taxdocs.ts (TAX-08 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the reverse_charge net booking + the Dr 1300 / Cr 2120 self-assessment leg, the ภ.พ.36 report + GL 2120 tie, and the filing/remittance-calendar wiring.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-08-01: book a ฿5,000 imported-service bill flagged reverse_charge → ภ.พ.36 self-assesses ฿350 (7%), the line appears, and it ties to the GL 2120 movement (฿350); TC-TAX-08-02: the reverse-charge bill claims 0 vendor input VAT on ภ.พ.30; TC-TAX-08-03: file ภ.พ.36 → DRAFT remitting ฿350, and the remittance calendar lists ภ.พ.36 due the 7th of next month (re-performed by the taxdocs harness).
controls.evidence: ภ.พ.36 report + GL 2120 reconciliation tie; the self-assessment JE (1300/2120); the ภ.พ.36 filing register row + remittance calendar

controls.fsli: SBT Payable (2130); SBT Expense (5840); Project Revenue (4200)
controls.risk: A commercial immovable-property sale (ขายอสังหาริมทรัพย์เป็นทางค้าหรือหากำไร, ม.91/2(6)) is subject to SPECIFIC BUSINESS TAX — 3% + 10% local tax = 3.3% effective — INSTEAD of VAT, filed monthly on ภ.ธ.40 by the 15th; if the SBT is not accrued at ownership transfer or not filed, the developer under-states its tax liability (filing/penalty exposure) and the RE vertical books transfers tax-free.
controls.description: SBT accrual at ownership transfer, driven by an explicit per-project rate: re_projects.sbt_rate (NULL = accrual OFF — legacy books unchanged until a controller opts the project in; set 3.3 for the statutory effective rate). transferOwnership then appends Dr 5840 SBT Expense / Cr 2130 SBT Payable (3.3% of the sale price) to the SAME revenue-recognition JE (atomic with Dr 2410 / Cr 4200) and stamps the contract (re_contracts.sbt_rate/sbt_amount — the ภ.ธ.40 register line). The ภ.ธ.40 report (GET /api/tax-reports/pt40) lists the period's SBT-stamped transfers, computes the SBT to remit, and reconciles it to the GL 2130 net credit movement (tie verdict) so a manual JE touching the SBT payable out-of-process is caught; the return files through the same TAX-05 register lifecycle and shows on the remittance calendar due the 15th. Idempotent via the existing RE-TRANSFER alreadyPosted guard.
controls.code_reference: realestate.service.ts transferOwnership (SBT accrual + contract stamp); tax/reports/tax-reports.service.ts pt40 (report + GL 2130 tie) + fileReturn/remittanceCalendar (PT40); tax-reports.controller.ts GET pt40; schema/realestate.ts sbt_rate/sbt_amount + migration 0285; ledger-constants 2130/5840 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY); cutover/projects.ts (TAX-09 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the per-project opt-in rate, the atomic Dr 5840 / Cr 2130 accrual inside the transfer JE, the ภ.ธ.40 report + GL 2130 tie, and the filing/remittance-calendar wiring.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-09-01: transfer a ฿500,000 unit under a 3.3% project → sbt_amount ฿16,500 accrued in the transfer JE and the contract stamped; TC-TAX-09-02: ภ.ธ.40 lists the transfer, totals ฿16,500, and ties to the GL 2130 movement; TC-TAX-09-03: a project WITHOUT sbt_rate accrues nothing (default-inert); TC-TAX-09-04: file ภ.ธ.40 → DRAFT remitting ฿16,500 and the remittance calendar lists it due the 15th (re-performed by the projects harness).
controls.evidence: ภ.ธ.40 report + GL 2130 reconciliation tie; the transfer JE SBT lines; the ภ.ธ.40 filing register row + remittance calendar

controls.risk: A buyer asks the counter to convert an abbreviated tax invoice (ม.86/6 slip) into a FULL tax invoice (ม.86/4) — if the conversion re-keys or recomputes the amounts, issues a SECOND full invoice for the same slip, skips the buyer Tax-ID validation, or leaves BOTH documents counted as Issued, then output VAT is double-counted on the ภ.พ.30 (or drifts from the original supply), a duplicated full invoice lets a buyer over-claim input VAT, and the conversion leaves no audit evidence.
controls.description: ABB→full conversion (POST /api/tax-invoices/abbreviated/:docNo/convert-full, gated cust_pos/pos/ar like the sibling issuance endpoints): the buyer block is MANDATORY — name + address (ม.86/4(3)), a checksum-VALIDATED 13-digit Tax ID (INVALID_BUYER_TAXID) and a 5-digit branch code defaulted 00000. The full invoice COPIES the ABB's amounts/VAT/lines and frozen seller snapshot VERBATIM (never recomputed) and keeps the ABB's issue/tax-point dates so the VAT stays in the original filing period; linkage is recorded (full.replaces_doc_no = ABB doc_no) and the ABB flips to status 'Replaced', so the ภ.พ.30 output-VAT report (status='Issued') counts the supply EXACTLY once. IDEMPOTENT — one full invoice per ABB: a re-call returns the same document (already_converted), DB-enforced by the partial unique index uq_tiv_converted_from (migration 0291); a Voided ABB (ABB_VOIDED) or a non-ABB document (NOT_ABBREVIATED) cannot convert. The conversion lands on the hash-chained audit_log (appendAuditMeta: ABB + full doc numbers + buyer Tax ID) and flows into the existing e-Tax XML/PDF path.
controls.code_reference: tax/documents/tax-invoice.service.ts convertAbbreviatedToFull; tax-docs.controller.ts POST abbreviated/:docNo/convert-full; dto.ts ConvertAbbBody; schema/tax-docs.ts uq_tiv_converted_from + migration 0291; web /tax/invoices conversion card; cutover/taxdocs.ts (TAX-10 ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the buyer Tax-ID validation, the verbatim amount copy, the Replaced-status single-count, the idempotency backstop index and the audit metadata.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-10-01: convert an issued ABB with a valid buyer block → a full TIV with identical amounts/VAT, linked to the ABB, ABB → Replaced, ภ.พ.30 output VAT unchanged (single count); TC-TAX-10-02: re-convert the same ABB → the SAME full invoice returned (already_converted, no second document); TC-TAX-10-03: an invalid 13-digit Tax ID (bad checksum) or a missing Tax ID is rejected 400; TC-TAX-10-04: a Voided ABB → ABB_VOIDED, a non-ABB document → NOT_ABBREVIATED, cross-tenant → 404 (re-performed by the taxdocs harness).
controls.evidence: Converted-invoice pair (ABB Replaced + linked full TIV); ภ.พ.30 single-count assertion; audit_log tax_abb_convert row; taxdocs harness ToE

controls.fsli: Corporate Income Tax Expense — current (5960); CIT Payable (2110); Deferred Tax (1700/2700/5950)
controls.risk: The CURRENT income-tax provision is mis-stated — pretax book income is not bridged to taxable income (permanent + temporary book-to-tax adjustments omitted or double-counted), the current CIT is computed at the wrong statutory rate, the temporary difference is re-derived inconsistently with the deferred-tax module (TAX-06), or the provision entry is computed and posted by one person with no review — so income-tax expense, the CIT payable and the effective-tax-rate (ETR) disclosure are misstated (ASC 740 / IAS 12).
controls.description: Current income-tax provision + ETR reconciliation (ASC 740 / IAS 12, maker-checker, idempotent per (tenant, period)): POST /api/tax/provision/run reads pretax book income from the income statement EXCLUDING income-tax postings (DEFTAX + CITPROV so the base is genuinely pre-tax), applies caller-supplied PERMANENT book-to-tax adjustments (non-deductible expenses +, tax-exempt income −), and REUSES the deferred-tax run's period delta for the TEMPORARY adjustment (temporaryAdjustment = deltaNetDeferred / deferred-rate) rather than re-deriving temporary differences — so the current and deferred modules never disagree. taxable income = pretax + permanent + temporary; current tax = taxable × statutory rate (default 0.20 Thai CIT); it stages an 'Open' income_tax_provisions row (a Posted period → ALREADY_POSTED) and computes the ETR schedule (statutory → effective: permanent differences × rate, rate-change effect, valuation allowance, prior-year deferred), which ties by construction (current + deferred = statutory + permanent×rate). GET /api/tax/provision[/:id] and /:id/etr expose the register + reconciliation. POST /api/tax/provision/:id/post is segregated (poster ≠ runner → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) and posts the CURRENT tax via LedgerService.postEntry (Dr 5960 Corporate Income Tax Expense / Cr 2110 CIT Payable; a tax benefit reverses the legs), so PERIOD_LOCKED + the GL audit trail apply; the deferred leg is posted by TAX-06. Marks the row Posted.
controls.code_reference: tax-provision.service.ts (runProvision: pretax from incomeStatement excl DEFTAX/CITPROV + permanent M-1 + temporary reused from deferred_tax_runs delta, taxable/current-tax, ETR schedule, stage Open, idempotent ALREADY_POSTED; postProvision SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker + 5960/2110 posting; list/get/etr); tax-provision.controller.ts (gl_close/gl_post run + :id/post, gl_close/gl_post/exec read + :id/etr); schema/income-tax-provision.ts (income_tax_provisions, uq tenant/period); migration 0349 (table + RLS + TAX.PROVISION posting-event); ledger-constants 5960/2110 (COA + CF_CLASSIFY 2110 operating); posting-events.ts TAX.PROVISION; tax-provision.module.ts (wired, imports LedgerModule); reuses deferred-tax (TAX-06) + ledger.incomeStatement; cutover/tax-provision.ts (TAX-11 ToE, 24 checks)
controls.tod: Inspect the pretax-book-income basis (income-tax postings excluded), the permanent + REUSED-temporary book-to-tax bridge to taxable income, the statutory-rate current tax, the statutory→effective ETR reconciliation tie, the SOD_SELF_APPROVAL maker-checker, and the Dr 5960 / Cr 2110 posting through the period-lock gate.
controls.toe: TC-TAX-11-01: run for a period with pretax book income 600,000, a 50,000 non-deductible permanent add-back and a deferred-tax DTL originating 100,000 → taxable income 550,000, current CIT 110,000 (20%), total income-tax expense 130,000, ETR ≈ 21.67%, and the ETR schedule reconciles statutory 120,000 + permanent 10,000 = 130,000; TC-TAX-11-02: runner self-post → SOD_SELF_APPROVAL (403), a different user posts → Dr 5960 / Cr 2110 (110,000) balanced, re-post → ALREADY_POSTED (400); TC-TAX-11-03: a sibling tenant never sees the provision (RLS) (re-performed by the tax-provision.ts harness, 24 checks).
controls.evidence: income_tax_provisions register (Open→Posted, taxable-income bridge + ETR schedule, runner/poster, deferred-tax link); the CITPROV JE to 5960/2110; quarterly/year-end income-tax provision + ETR sign-off

controls.risk: Payroll, SSO and PIT mis-computed.
controls.description: Payroll engine — SSO 5% (cap 750), progressive PIT, payslip net; unit-tested.
controls.code_reference: payroll/payroll-calc.ts; test/unit.test.ts
controls.tod: Inspect calc + tests.
controls.toe: Re-perform sample payslips.
controls.evidence: Payslip tests

controls.risk: Statutory withholdings (SSO/WHT/PF) mis-stated or not remitted, so the liability owed to the authorities diverges from the books.
controls.description: Payroll-liability schedule (GET /api/payroll/liabilities): each statutory account — SSO 2350, WHT/PND1 2360, PF 2370 — shows accrued (GL credits) − remitted (GL debits) = outstanding, tied back to the INDEPENDENT payrun accrual with a `reconciled` flag (a divergence flags a manual JE that touched a payroll-liability account outside the payroll process). Treasury remits the cash (POST .../remit → Dr liability / Cr 1000) which clears the outstanding and keeps the books reconciled to the statutory filings; a remittance beyond the outstanding is blocked (REMIT_EXCEEDS_OUTSTANDING).
controls.code_reference: payroll/payroll.service.ts liabilities/remitLiability
controls.tod: Inspect the schedule + the accrued/remitted/outstanding tie-out and the reconciled flag.
controls.toe: Re-perform: run a payroll, confirm the SSO/WHT outstanding equals the payrun accrual; remit part and confirm the outstanding falls and the TB stays balanced; attempt an over-remit (REMIT_EXCEEDS_OUTSTANDING).
controls.evidence: Liability schedule + remittance JEs tied to filings

controls.risk: Payroll run posted to the GL without independent review — the preparer both computes and posts their own payroll (ghost employees, inflated rate/hours).
controls.description: Payroll maker-checker (SoD): a run posts its GL entry as a DRAFT (excluded from balances) with the run record 'PendingApproval'; a DIFFERENT user must approve before it becomes effective (approver ≠ preparer enforced regardless of permissions, reusing the GL-05 ledger approval). Reject voids the draft; idempotent per (tenant, period).
controls.code_reference: payroll/payroll.service.ts approvePayroll/rejectPayroll; migration 0133
controls.tod: Inspect Draft→approve flow + the approver≠preparer check.
controls.toe: Re-perform: run as A, attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as B; confirm the JE hits balances only after approval.
controls.evidence: Approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: Overtime is paid at a single flat multiplier with no holiday/night tiers and no statutory cap (Thai LPA OT is mis-paid — too little on holidays, or beyond the 48h/week limit), AND labor cost runs over target as a % of sales with nothing flagging it — margin erosion goes unnoticed until payroll posts.
controls.description: Tiered OT rules engine + labor-% alerting: a per-tenant labor_ot_rules table overrides the Thai LPA statutory defaults (REGULAR_OT 1.5x beyond 8h/day, HOLIDAY 2x, HOLIDAY_OT 3x, NIGHT 1.0x tracked; 48h/week cap) — GET/PUT /api/pos/labor/ot-rules. computeOtPay (POST /api/pos/labor/ot-pay) applies the tier's multiplier and CAPS paid hours at the 48h weekly limit, flagging any over-cap hours (over_cap=true) rather than silently paying them. Separately, POST /api/pos/labor/labor-alert/check computes labor % of sales for a period and, when it exceeds the target (default 35%), persists a LABOR_PCT_EXCEEDED alert (idempotent per period) surfaced on GET /api/pos/labor/alerts for the manager to act on / resolve. Tenant-scoped (RLS); gated pos/users/exec. Operational (no GL).
controls.code_reference: pos-loyalty-labor/schedule.service.ts getOtRules/upsertOtRule (Thai defaults overlay) + computeOtPay (tier multiplier + 48h cap, over_cap flag) + checkLaborAlert/listAlerts/resolveAlert; pos-loyalty-labor.controller.ts (ot-rules, ot-pay, labor-alert/check, alerts[/:id/resolve]); schema/pos-scale.ts labor_ot_rules + labor_alerts + migration 0167; /scheduling UI; cutover/scheduling.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the statutory-default overlay, the tier multipliers, the 48h cap + over_cap flag, and the labor-% threshold alert (raise/idempotent/resolve).
controls.toe: Re-perform: OT rules expose Thai defaults (1.5/2/3/1.0, cap 48); override REGULAR_OT to 2.0; HOLIDAY_OT 2h x 100 x 3 = 600; a 5h OT entry at 47h already-worked pays only 1h + flags 4h over-cap; labor 8% vs a 5% target raises an alert (idempotent), vs a 50% target raises none; resolve clears it (re-performed by the scheduling harness).
controls.evidence: labor_ot_rules + labor_alerts register; OT/labor-% test

controls.risk: Time-and-attendance is unverified — one worker clocks a colleague in/out (buddy punching), or punches off-site, so paid labor hours overstate hours actually worked (payroll fraud / inflated labor cost), and there is no attributable override trail when a manager fixes a punch.
controls.description: Clock-in integrity (anti-buddy-punch): a punch records its capture METHOD (PIN/QR/FACE_HASH/SUPERVISOR) and optional GPS; a re-punch within 15 minutes of the same employee's last clock-out is REJECTED (DUPLICATE_PUNCH) unless a supervisor overrides. A per-branch geofence_zone (lat/lng/radius) lets the clock-in compute geofence_pass when GPS is supplied; an out-of-fence punch is accepted but FLAGGED (geofence_pass=false) for review rather than hard-blocked (kiosks may lack GPS -> null), and a SUPERVISOR override REQUIRES a reason (REASON_REQUIRED), stamps method=SUPERVISOR + the reason on the punch note, and is captured by the append-only audit_log so every override is attributable. The labor report surfaces method + geofence_pass so anomalies are visible. Tenant-scoped (RLS); gated pos/users/exec.
controls.code_reference: pos-loyalty-labor/timeclock.service.ts clockIn (DUPLICATE_PUNCH window, method capture, haversine geofence_pass) + supervisorOverride (REASON_REQUIRED, audited) + setGeofenceZone/listGeofenceZones; pos-loyalty-labor.controller.ts (clock-in, clock-in/override, geofence-zones); schema/pos-scale.ts time_clock.clock_in_method/lat/lng/geofence_pass + geofence_zones + migration 0169; cutover/pos-p2.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the 15-min duplicate-punch guard, the method capture, the geofence pass/fail computation, and the supervisor-override reason + audit trail.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a re-punch within 15 min of clock-out -> DUPLICATE_PUNCH; a supervisor override (with a reason) bypasses it and stamps method SUPERVISOR; an override with no reason -> 400; an in-fence GPS punch -> geofence_pass true; an out-of-fence punch is accepted but flagged geofence_pass false (re-performed by the pos-p2 harness).
controls.evidence: time_clock method/geofence columns + override note; anti-buddy test

controls.risk: An employee's payslip (salary, citizen ID, statutory deductions) is exposed to someone other than the employee or authorised HR/payroll — a PDPA confidentiality breach: a self-service user pulls a colleague's payslip by guessing an id, or the full 13-digit citizen ID is printed in the clear on the slip.
controls.description: PDPA-scoped payslip access: the employee self-service payslip download (GET /api/ess/payslips/:id/pdf, perm `ess`) resolves the caller to their OWN employee from the JWT username (NEVER a body/query param) and ANDs an employee_id predicate onto the lookup, so a slip id that isn't the caller's own returns 404 — an employee can only ever retrieve their own slip. HR/payroll printing (GET /api/payroll/slips/:id/pdf + POST .../send-email, gate exec/users/creditors) may print/email any slip in the tenant; an ess-only user is denied the HR route (403). The citizen ID is MASKED to the last 4 digits on the face of the slip (data-minimisation), and RLS scopes every lookup to the caller's tenant. Same shared HTML->PDF renderer as the external documents (HTML fallback when Chromium absent).
controls.code_reference: payroll/payslip-pdf.service.ts (payslipHtml + maskNationalId); payroll.service.ts getSlipForPrint(restrictEmployeeId)/emailPayslip; payroll.controller.ts slips/:id/pdf|send-email; ess.service.ts myPayslipForPrint (me()-scoped); ess.module.ts ess payslips/:id/pdf; cutover/payroll.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the me()->employee_id predicate on the ESS route, the HR-route permission gate, and the last-4 citizen-ID mask.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an ESS employee downloads their OWN slip (200) but a colleague's slip id -> 404; HR prints any slip (200) while an ess-only user is denied the HR route (403); the slip shows only the masked citizen ID (no full 13-digit); the email path reaches the transport (503 EMAIL_NOT_CONFIGURED, SMTP unset) - re-performed by the payroll harness.
controls.evidence: PDPA payslip-access test (own-200/other-404/HR-403) + masked-ID render

controls.risk: Staff are hired into positions beyond the approved establishment — an employee is assigned to a position whose budgeted headcount is already full, so the organisation carries unbudgeted headcount (over-establishment) and the resulting salary/labour cost is committed with no independent authorisation; the org chart and the payroll base drift above the approved plan unnoticed.
controls.description: Headcount-governance control on the HR org structure (docs/42 HR-1): each hr_position carries a budgeted_headcount, and creating an hr_assignment (an effective-dated employee→position link) counts the position's currently-active assignments (end_date IS NULL). When that count has already reached the budgeted headcount the assignment is BLOCKED (HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED, 403) for an ordinary HR maintainer (perm hr_admin) — the establishment cannot be exceeded from the inside. Only a user holding `exec` may OVERRIDE and add the over-establishment seat; the override is AUDIT-LOGGED (a doc_status_log HRASSIGN row carrying HEADCOUNT_OVERRIDE, the position, the count/budget and the reason) in addition to the append-only audit_log the global AuditInterceptor writes for every mutation. A budgeted_headcount of 0 means an unbudgeted seat (no cap). Reads are gated hr/hr_admin/exec and writes hr_admin/exec; the tables are tenant-scoped (RLS) so headcount never leaks across companies. So an HR maintainer can fill the approved plan but never grow it, and every over-establishment hire is an attributable exec decision.
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-org.service.ts createAssignment (active-headcount count vs budgeted_headcount → HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED; exec-only override via canOverride; StatusLogService HRASSIGN HEADCOUNT_OVERRIDE audit) / createDepartment / createPosition / orgChart; hcm-org.controller.ts /api/hcm/org/{departments,positions,assignments,chart}; schema/hcm-org.ts hr_departments/hr_positions/hr_assignments (migration 0320, RLS + tenant-leading indexes); permissions.ts hr/hr_admin; cutover/hcm-org.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the active-headcount count against budgeted_headcount, the HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED block for a non-exec, the exec-only override path, and the HEADCOUNT_OVERRIDE audit row on the doc status log.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a position with budgeted_headcount=1; assign one employee (within budget, no override); assign a second as a non-exec hr_admin → HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED (403); assign the second as an exec → succeeds with headcount_overridden=true and a doc_status_log HRASSIGN HEADCOUNT_OVERRIDE row; a budgeted_headcount=0 position accepts multiple assignments with no override (re-performed by the hcm-org harness, incl. RLS tenant isolation).
controls.evidence: Position establishment register (budgeted vs current headcount); the HEADCOUNT_OVERRIDE audit trail on over-establishment hires

controls.risk: Asset disposed without independent review — one person writes an asset off the books and records the proceeds (asset-stripping / theft: dispose a still-held asset, or to a related party below value).
