controls.description: Asset-disposal maker-checker (SoD): a disposal REQUEST posts its GL entry as a DRAFT (excluded from balances) and flags the asset disposal_pending WITHOUT marking it disposed or moving the register; a DIFFERENT user must approve before it is effective (status→disposed, revaluation surplus recycled 3200→3100) (approver ≠ requester enforced regardless of permissions, reusing the GL-05 ledger approval). Reject voids the draft and the asset stays in service; only one disposal may be pending per asset; a disposal-pending asset is frozen from depreciation.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts dispose(pendingApproval Draft)/approveDisposal/rejectDisposal; assets.controller.ts (:assetNo/dispose/approve|reject); schema/assets.ts fixed_assets.disposal_pending + migration 0135; cutover/basics.ts + worldclass.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect Draft→approve flow + the approver≠requester check + deferred status/recycle.
controls.toe: Re-perform: request a disposal (Draft JE excluded), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user; confirm status→disposed + surplus recycled only after approval (re-performed by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Disposal approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: Capital purchases mis-capitalised or capitalised without independent review — the person who receives the goods also decides what enters the asset register and at what cost/useful life (fictitious or over-valued assets; capex/opex mis-classification).
controls.description: Procure-to-Capitalize maker-checker (SoD): a goods-receipt line flagged capital (item-master is_fixed_asset or per-PO-line override) is excluded from inventory capitalisation (1200) at receipt and instead becomes ELIGIBLE for the asset register. Capitalisation is a REQUEST that posts NOTHING to the GL; a DIFFERENT user must approve before the fixed_assets row + acquisition JE (Dr 1500 / Cr 2000) are created (approver ≠ requester enforced regardless of permissions). The created asset carries its source GR/PO for end-to-end PR→PO→GR→FA traceability; a GR line cannot be capitalised twice.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts registerFromGr/approveRegistration/rejectRegistration/eligibleFromGr; assets.controller.ts (registrations*); procurement.service.ts createGr (is_capital routing, costing excluded); schema/assets.ts asset_registration_requests + fixed_assets.source_gr_no/po_no + migration 0137; cutover/basics.ts + compliance.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the eligible→request→approve flow + approver≠requester check + the inventory-vs-asset routing of capital lines.
controls.toe: Re-perform: receive a capital line, raise a registration (no GL), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user; confirm the asset + Dr 1500/Cr 2000 post only after approval and the line cannot be re-registered (re-performed by the harnesses).
controls.evidence: Capitalization approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: Asset moved/reassigned without independent review — a custodian relabels an asset's physical location or holder (or an audit finds it elsewhere) and the register is changed on one person's say-so, enabling misappropriation (reassign to self/related party) or concealment of a missing asset; existence/custody assertions unreliable.
controls.description: Asset custody-change maker-checker (SoD) + audit-by-scan. A tag scan that CHANGES an asset's location/holder no longer writes the register: it raises a PendingApproval custody-change REQUEST (no GL effect); a DIFFERENT user must approve before the register moves and a 'Scan Update' movement is logged (approver != requester enforced regardless of permissions — binds even Admin; reject leaves the asset in place; only one request pending per asset). Confirming the current location is logged immediately as a non-approval 'Scan Verify' movement (existence evidence). A physical AUDIT (count by scan) classifies each scan Found/Misplaced/Unknown and reconciles Missing against the register; closing the audit raises custody-change requests for the misplaced assets — so no floor-level move rewrites the register without a second person's approval.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts scanUpdate(verify vs request)/approveCustody/rejectCustody/openAudit/scanAudit/getAudit/closeAudit; assets.controller.ts (scan-update, custody*, audits*); schema/assets.ts asset_scan_requests + asset_audits + asset_audit_scans + migration 0251; web /assets Audit + Custody-approvals tabs; cutover/module-qr.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the scan->request->approve flow + the approver!=requester check + the audit reconcile->custody-request path.
controls.toe: Re-perform: scan a move (PendingApproval, register unmoved), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user (register moves + movement logged); run an audit that finds a misplaced asset and confirm closing raises a pending custody request (re-performed by the module-qr harness).
controls.evidence: Custody-change approval log + audit reconciliation + SoD test

controls.risk: Assets exist on the register but are never physically confirmed — a missing/stolen/scrapped asset stays on the books indefinitely because no periodic existence check flags it, overstating PPE and the existence assertion.
controls.description: Periodic asset-existence exception monitoring. A schedulable BI report (report_type asset_verification_exceptions) lists every ACTIVE asset not physically verified within N days (default 90) — 'verified' = a Scan Verify or approved Scan Update movement (FA-11), with a never-verified asset falling back to its acquisition date as the implicit receipt baseline. Each exception carries days-since-last-seen so the oldest surface first. Scheduled monthly, it drives a recurring existence count before period-end; the companion asset_audit report surfaces recent audit results (found/missing/misplaced/unknown) and the outstanding custody-change queue. Read-only aggregation over fixed_assets + asset_movements — posts nothing, adds no authority (no SoD conflict); complements the FA-11 audit-by-scan (which supplies the verification movements) and the FA-05 register-to-GL tie-out.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts unverifiedAssets/auditReport; assets.controller.ts (unverified, audit-report); bi.service.ts REPORT_TYPES asset_verification_exceptions + asset_audit + generateReport blocks; bi.module.ts imports AssetsModule; cutover/module-qr.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the exception query (last Scan Verify/Update vs N-day threshold, acquisition-date fallback) and that the report is a registered, schedulable BI type.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed an old never-scanned asset, run GET /api/assets/unverified?days=90 (asset flagged) + a recently-scanned asset is NOT flagged; schedule + run the asset_verification_exceptions report and confirm the summary counts the exception (re-performed by the module-qr harness).
controls.evidence: Scheduled exception report runs + exception list + verification-movement log

controls.risk: Construction-in-progress (CIP/AUC) is capitalised into the productive asset register without independent review — the person who accumulates the construction cost also decides when it is 'complete', at what cost/useful life it enters service, and starts depreciation, so premature/over-valued capitalisation (or deferring capitalisation to delay depreciation) goes unchecked; PPE existence/valuation and the depreciation charge are mis-stated.
controls.description: CIP/AUC settlement maker-checker (SoD): a CIP asset accumulates cost lines (GR/manual/project) into the CIP GL account (Dr 1520 / Cr 2000 AP | 1000 Cash) and is NOT depreciated while under construction. Capitalising it is a settlement REQUEST that posts NOTHING and requires a MANDATORY reason; a DIFFERENT user must approve before the fixed_assets row + reclassification JE (Dr 1500 / Cr 1520, for the accumulated construction cost) are created and depreciation begins (approver != requester enforced regardless of permissions — binds even Admin; the created asset carries its source CIP for traceability and gets its own parallel tax book). Only an Open CIP accepts cost (CIP_NOT_OPEN); a CIP with no accumulated cost cannot be settled (CIP_NO_COST); reject re-opens the CIP. Pairs with the FIN-6a parallel TAX depreciation book, which keeps a memo tax basis (Thai caps + first-year initial allowance) feeding the book-vs-tax temporary difference straight into deferred tax (TAX-06) instead of a manual GAAP adjustment.
controls.code_reference: assets.service.ts openCip/addCipCost/settleCip/approveCipSettlement/rejectCipSettlement + runTaxDepreciation + acquire(taxBookInit, creditAccount 1520); assets.controller.ts (cip*, tax-depreciation/run); deferred-tax.service.ts (prefers the stored tax NBV); ledger-constants.ts COA 1520 + CF_CLASSIFY; schema/assets.ts cip_assets + cip_cost_lines + fixed_assets tax-book/source_cip_no cols + migration 0299; cutover/basics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the accumulate->settle->approve flow + the approver!=requester check + the Dr 1500/Cr 1520 reclassification, and that deferred tax consumes the real tax NBV.
controls.toe: Re-perform: open a CIP, accumulate cost into 1520 (not 1500), raise a settlement (no GL), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user (asset + Dr 1500/Cr 1520 post); confirm CIP_NOT_OPEN/CIP_NO_COST guards and that the parallel tax book feeds the deferred-tax difference (re-performed by the basics harness).
controls.evidence: CIP settlement approval log + SoD test + tax-book/deferred-tax tie-out

controls.tod: Inspect consolidation logic + gate.

controls.risk: Intercompany balances/transactions are not eliminated on consolidation, OR eliminations are mis-stated, so the consolidated group trial balance does not balance and group financials are overstated (IC double-counted).
controls.description: Elimination integrity (CON-03): runConsolidation combines member-entity TBs, generates reciprocal IC eliminations (Dr 2150 Due-To / Cr 1150 Due-From for each in-group IC transaction) at the GROUP layer (consolidation_run_lines — NOT pushed into any operating entity's GL), and ASSERTS the consolidated TB still balances (Σ signed nets ~0 and eliminations net to 0, NCI presentation reclass excluded) — failure throws CONSOL_UNBALANCED and rolls back the run. A balanced run is finalised, then frozen by a DIFFERENT user via run→post maker-checker (self-post → SELF_POST; re-running a Posted period → ALREADY_POSTED; one run per (group, period)). HQ/Admin-only; reads across member tenants via app.bypass_rls. Configurable elimination rules (consol_elimination_rules).
controls.code_reference: consolidation.service.ts runConsolidation (balanced check, CONSOL_UNBALANCED)/postConsolidation (SELF_POST, ALREADY_POSTED); consolidation.controller.ts (run @approvals, runs/:id/post @approvals); schema/consolidation.ts (consolidation_runs.balanced/posted_by, consol_elimination_rules) + migration 0170; cutover/consolidation.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the combine→eliminate→balance flow, the CONSOL_UNBALANCED guard, and the run→post approver≠runner check.
controls.toe: Re-perform: run a group consolidation, confirm IC 1150/2150 eliminate and the consolidated TB balances; attempt self-post (SELF_POST); post as a different user (Posted); re-run the posted period (ALREADY_POSTED). Re-performed by the consolidation harness.
controls.evidence: Consolidated TB + elimination lines + post log

controls.description: Segment reporting (CON-04, IFRS 8): segmentReport produces P&L (revenue/expense/net) grouped by reportable segment, sourced from the dimension columns on journal_lines (branch_id/project_id/department_id) and mapped through configurable segment_definitions (member_keys → segment); unmapped dimension values surface as their own/Unassigned bucket. HQ/Admin-only, gated 'exec'.
controls.code_reference: consolidation.service.ts defineSegment/listSegments/segmentReport; consolidation.controller.ts (segments, segment-report @exec); schema/consolidation.ts segment_definitions + migration 0170; cutover/consolidation.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the segment mapping + the dimension-grouped P&L aggregation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: define a segment over branches, run the segment report for a period, confirm revenue/expense/net tie to the underlying dimensioned postings. Re-performed by the consolidation harness.

controls.risk: A wrong manually-entered FX rate (fat-fingered, e.g. USD 36 keyed as 63) flows straight into a period-end revaluation JE and the unrealized-FX report with no independent review — mis-stating earnings/equity and FX-translated balances.
controls.description: FX rate maker-checker (SoD): a MANUALLY-entered rate lands as PendingApproval and is EXCLUDED from rate resolution — revaluation, the unrealized-FX report, and consolidation translation all use APPROVED rates only — until a DIFFERENT user approves it (POST /api/fx/rates/approve). Self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin); reject marks the rate Rejected (never usable); re-entering a rate sends it back to PendingApproval. Externally-sourced (feed) rates carrying an explicit non-manual source are auto-approved. Pending rates are also surfaced, aged, by the pending-approvals monitor (GOV-01).
controls.code_reference: fx.service.ts setRate(manual→PendingApproval)/approveRate/rejectRate/rateAsOf(Approved only); fx.controller.ts (rates/approve|reject|pending gated approvals/gl_close); consolidation.service.ts (translation reads Approved rates); schema/fx.ts fx_rates.status + migration 0141; cutover/fxreval.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the request→approve flow + the approver≠requester check + that revaluation/reporting resolve Approved rates only.
controls.toe: Re-perform: enter a manual rate (PendingApproval), confirm revalue is blocked NO_RATE while pending, attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user, then revalue succeeds (re-performed by the fxreval harness).
controls.evidence: FX rate approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: A foreign subsidiary is translated at a single closing rate for BOTH its income statement and its balance sheet, so no cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) ever arises — mis-stating group equity/OCI and hiding the exchange effect on the group. The consolidated statement of cash flows is not produced post-elimination, so group cash flows cannot be reviewed.
controls.description: Dual-rate translation + CTA/OCI (CON-05, IAS 21 / TAS 21): runConsolidation translates each foreign entity's P&L (revenue 4xxx / expense 5xxx) at the PERIOD AVERAGE rate and its balance sheet at the CLOSING rate, then parks the residual translation difference in a CTA / OCI translation-reserve equity line (3400, lineType FX_CTA) — the plug that makes each foreign entity's translated trial balance balance again (a base-currency THB entity yields no CTA). Each run line records the fx_rate + rate_type (average/closing/cta) used, so the translation is auditable, and CON-03's balanced-TB assertion covers Entity+FX_CTA (only NCI is excluded). A consolidated statement of cash flows (indirect, POST-elimination) is derived from the run lines (GET /api/consolidation/runs/:id/cash-flow), showing the CTA as a dedicated effect-of-exchange-rate-changes-on-cash section and reconciling to the movement in the consolidated cash accounts. HQ/Admin-only, gated 'exec'.
controls.code_reference: consolidation.service.ts runConsolidation (average/closing dual-rate translation, FX_CTA 3400 plug, cta_total)/consolidatedCashFlow; consolidation.controller.ts (runs/:id/cash-flow @exec); schema/consolidation.ts consolidation_run_lines.fx_rate/rate_type + migration 0301 (account 3400 CTA/OCI reserve); cutover/consolidation.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the average-vs-closing rate selection, the CTA plug that rebalances each foreign entity, and the post-elimination consolidated SCF derivation/reconciliation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: consolidate a foreign (non-THB) subsidiary, confirm P&L translates at the average rate and the balance sheet at the closing rate, that the CTA/OCI line (3400) equals the translation difference and the consolidated TB still balances, and that the consolidated cash-flow statement reconciles to the change in cash (re-performed by the consolidation harness).
controls.evidence: Consolidated TB with CTA/OCI line + consolidated SCF

controls.risk: A budget figure is entered/changed by one person and immediately drives the budget-vs-actual variance report — the basis for spend authorization and performance review — with no independent approval; a wrong or self-serving budget makes overspending look 'on budget'.
controls.description: Budget maker-checker (SoD): an upserted budget (POST /api/ledger/budgets) lands as PendingApproval and is EXCLUDED from budget-vs-actual until a DIFFERENT user approves it (POST /api/ledger/budgets/approve for the fiscal_year/account/cost-centre[/period]). Self-approval → SOD_VIOLATION (binds even Admin); reject marks it Rejected (excluded). DEFAULT 'Approved' keeps existing rows + direct planning seeds usable. Pending budgets are also surfaced, aged, by the pending-approvals monitor (GOV-01).
controls.code_reference: budget.service.ts upsertBudget(→PendingApproval)/approveBudget/rejectBudget/budgetVsActual(Approved only); budget.controller.ts (budgets/approve|reject|pending gated approvals/gl_close); schema/budgets.ts budgets.status + migration 0142; cutover/budget.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the request→approve flow + the approver≠requester check + that budget-vs-actual counts Approved budgets only.
controls.toe: Re-perform: upsert a budget (PendingApproval, excluded from variance), attempt self-approve (SOD_VIOLATION), approve as a different user, confirm it then appears in budget-vs-actual (re-performed by the budget harness).
controls.evidence: Budget approval log + SoD test

controls.risk: Budgets are report-only: a PR/PO that exceeds the approved budget is approved and committed with no availability check, so overspend is only discovered after the fact in the variance report — commitments (open POs / approved PRs) are invisible to spend authorization.
controls.description: Budgetary control / encumbrance gate (FIN-3): at PR and PO APPROVAL (never at request — asking is free) the gate computes availability = approved budget (fiscal-YTD) − GL actuals (YTD) − OPEN commitments per resolved budget account (item.cogs_account → item_categories.cogs_account → budget_control_settings.default_expense_account; project/BoQ lines are excluded — PROJ-12/13 encumber them — as are is_capital lines), per the tenant policy (budget_control_settings.policy): off (default, report-only) | advise (approve + annotate) | warn (approver must resubmit with confirm_over_budget) | block (422 BUDGET_EXCEEDED unless an EXEC override with a mandatory reason — BUDGET_OVERRIDE_DENIED for a non-exec approver, BUDGET_OVERRIDE_REASON_REQUIRED without one; audited on the commitment row + doc status log). The final approval RECORDS the commitment in budget_commitments (a PR reserves its item-master estimate, released when it converts to POs; a PO reserves the ordered amount, consumed on full receipt, released on cancel/close-short). Scope: only accounts WITH an approved budget for the year are enforced (unbudgeted spend is caught by ELC-06). Policy change is restricted to exec/gl_close (mirrors EXP-04 change control).
controls.code_reference: commitments.service.ts glGate/glReserve/glRelease/glConsume/glAvailability (one encumbrance engine with PROJ-12); procurement-pr.service.ts approvePr + procurement-po.service.ts approvePo (gate + reserve), convertPrToPo/cancelPo/closePoShort/createGr (lifecycle); budget-control.controller.ts (/api/budget availability|commitments|control-settings); schema/budgets.ts budget_control_settings + budget_commitments + migration 0298; cutover/budget.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the availability formula (budget − actuals − open commitments), the policy switch + fail-open default, the exec-only override with mandatory reason, and the commitment lifecycle (reserve→consume/release).
controls.toe: Re-perform: default off (response unchanged); advise (approve + exceeded flag); warn (BUDGET_CONFIRM_REQUIRED then confirm); block (BUDGET_EXCEEDED; non-exec override 403; exec override without reason 400; with reason approved + audited row); receipt consumes; PR→PO conversion releases the PR estimate (re-performed by the budget harness).
controls.evidence: Budget-control policy + budget_commitments audit rows (override_by/override_reason)

controls.risk: A performance appraisal is rated and signed off by the employee under review (self-review), or the review is signed off without an independent manager rating — inflating ratings that drive pay rises/bonuses/promotions (payroll cost + fraud exposure), and goal weightings are set incoherently (weights totalling over 100%) so the appraisal cannot be scored fairly.
controls.description: Performance review sign-off SoD (HR-03): the appraisal runs self -> manager -> calibration -> sign-off. The manager rating (POST /api/hcm/performance/reviews/:id/manager) is REJECTED when manager_emp_code equals the reviewee (SOD_SELF_REVIEW), and sign-off (POST .../reviews/:id/sign, gate hr_admin/exec) is REJECTED when the signer's own linked employee equals the reviewee (SOD_SELF_REVIEW) or when the review carries no manager rating yet (NO_MANAGER_RATING) — so a review can only be finalised by someone OTHER than the person being reviewed, after an independent rating. Goal weights per employee/cycle are validated <= 100% on create (WEIGHT_EXCEEDED). Tenant-scoped (RLS); reads hr/hr_admin/exec (ess self-scoped), writes hr/hr_admin.
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-perf.service.ts managerRate/signReview (SOD_SELF_REVIEW + NO_MANAGER_RATING) + createGoal/assertWeight (WEIGHT_EXCEEDED) + closeCycle; hcm/hcm-perf.controller.ts (cycles/goals/reviews[/:id/manager|/sign]); schema/hcm-perf.ts perf_cycles/perf_goals/perf_reviews + migration 0322; /hcm/performance UI; cutover/hcm-perf.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the manager-rating self-review guard, the sign-off signer-vs-reviewee guard + manager-rating precondition, and the <=100% goal-weight check.
controls.toe: Re-perform: a self-review then self-sign -> 403 SOD_SELF_REVIEW; a manager rating by a DIFFERENT employee succeeds; a sign-off with no manager rating -> 400 NO_MANAGER_RATING; a calibrated review signed by HR succeeds; goal weights summing over 100% -> 400 WEIGHT_EXCEEDED; a cross-tenant read returns nothing (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-perf harness.
controls.evidence: Review workflow log (self/manager/calibrated/signed) + SoD test

controls.risk: Leave entitlement is unmanaged — balances are static (no accrual), so a leave request beyond an employee's earned entitlement is granted (over-paid absence / understated leave liability), and accrual is applied inconsistently by hand (favouritism, mis-stated leave provision).
controls.description: Leave accrual engine + entitlement gate (HR-2, docs/42): a per-tenant leave_types master carries the accrual method (monthly/anniversary/none) + carryover cap + max balance; leave_policies override the base rate by job-grade/tenure. An idempotent accrual RUN (POST /api/hcm/leave/accrual/run, or the schedulable hr_leave_accrual BI action job) resolves each active employee's effective rate (policy by grade/tenure, else the type default), credits `accrued` on the (employee, type, year) balance clamped to max_balance_days, and — at the year boundary — rolls the prior year's remaining balance into `carryover` capped at carryover_cap_days (the excess recorded as `expired`); a re-run of the same (tenant, period) is a NO-OP (guarded by leave_accrual_runs). requestLeave then BLOCKS a paid request exceeding the available balance (entitled+accrued+carryover-used-expired) with INSUFFICIENT_LEAVE_BALANCE unless the type allows a negative balance (unpaid leave is not gated). Approval stays maker-checker (approver != requester, SOD_SELF_APPROVAL). Tenant-scoped (RLS); config + accrual run gated hr_admin/exec, reads hr/hr_admin/exec/ess.
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-leave.service.ts runAccrual/availableBalance/createType/createPolicy; hcm/hcm.service.ts requestLeave (INSUFFICIENT_LEAVE_BALANCE gate); hcm/hcm-leave.controller.ts (leave/types|policies|balances|accrual/run); bi/bi-generate.service.ts hr_leave_accrual + report-registry.ts; schema/hcm-leave.ts leave_types/leave_policies/leave_accrual_runs + schema/hcm.ts leave_balances.accrued/carryover/expired + migration 0321; cutover/hcm-leave.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the effective-rate resolution (policy grade/tenure -> type default), the per-(tenant,period) idempotency guard, the carryover-cap + max-balance clamps, and the requestLeave available-balance gate.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a monthly 1.25 d/period type + a grade-M2 policy at 2.0; run accrual and confirm an M2 employee accrues 2.0 while a default employee accrues 1.25; re-run the same period is a no-op (no double-accrual); a prior-year 8-day balance carries min(8,cap 5)=5 with 3 expired; a paid request within balance is Pending while one beyond it is rejected INSUFFICIENT_LEAVE_BALANCE; a second tenant's run counts only its own employees (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-leave harness (17 checks).
controls.evidence: leave_types/leave_policies/leave_accrual_runs register + accrual/gate test

controls.risk: A salary change is set outside any approved pay band and/or waved through by the person who requested it — an employee's monthly salary is raised beyond the sanctioned grade range (over-market/favouritism, inflated labour cost) or a raise is self-approved, so the payroll base grows with no independent authorisation and no band discipline; benefit enrolments are administered inconsistently.
controls.description: Compensation-change maker-checker within band (HR-6, docs/42 Wave 2): a per-tenant pay_grades register carries each grade's [min, mid, max] band. A comp change (POST /api/hcm/comp/changes) whose new_salary falls OUTSIDE the target grade's [min,max] band is BLOCKED at request time (OUT_OF_BAND) unless an hr_admin/exec sets an explicit override flag — the override is AUDIT-LOGGED (a doc_status_log COMPCHG row carrying OUT_OF_BAND_OVERRIDE, the employee, the salary and the grade) in addition to the append-only audit_log. Approval is maker-checker: approved_by MUST differ from requested_by (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, 403) and only hr_admin/exec approve (POST .../changes/:id/approve|reject); the employee master (payroll.employees.monthly_salary + job_grade) is written ONLY on approval — a request posts nothing to the employee record, and a reject leaves the salary unchanged. Benefit plans (employer/employee cost) + effective-dated enrolments (end-datable) round out the comp record; enrolment reads are ess own-scoped. Tenant-scoped (RLS); config/requests hr/hr_admin, approvals hr_admin/exec.
controls.owner: HR / People Ops; HR Admin / Exec (approve)
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-comp.service.ts createChange (OUT_OF_BAND band check + override audit)/approveChange (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + employee-master write on approval)/rejectChange/createGrade/createBenefitPlan/createEnrollment/endEnrollment; hcm/hcm-comp.controller.ts /api/hcm/comp/{grades,changes[/:id/approve|reject],benefit-plans,enrollments[/:id/end]}; schema/hcm-comp.ts pay_grades/comp_changes/benefit_plans/benefit_enrollments (migration 0325, RLS + tenant-leading indexes); permissions.ts hr/hr_admin; /hcm/comp UI; cutover/hcm-comp.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the new_salary-within-[min,max] band check, the OUT_OF_BAND block + hr_admin/exec override audit row, the approved_by != requested_by self-approval guard, and that the employee monthly_salary is updated only on approval.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a pay grade; a within-band comp change is accepted; an out-of-band change -> 400 OUT_OF_BAND; an exec override allows it (audit-logged); the requester approving their own change -> 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; a DISTINCT approver approves -> employees.monthly_salary updated; a rejected change leaves salary unchanged; create a benefit plan + enrol + end enrolment; an ess user sees only their own enrolments; a cross-tenant read returns nothing (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-comp harness.
controls.evidence: pay_grades band register + comp-change approval log (OUT_OF_BAND override + SoD test) + benefit enrolment register

controls.fsli: Human Resources (employee master data); Payroll expense
controls.risk: An employee edits their own master data with no independent check — a self-service change to a SENSITIVE field (bank account, national ID, name, tax ID) is applied straight to the employee record, so payroll pay is diverted to a substituted bank account, statutory identifiers are silently altered, or an employee waves through their own change; and one employee can view or amend ANOTHER employee's profile or personal documents (privacy breach / unauthorised master-data change).
controls.description: ESS profile-change maker-checker (HR-8, docs/42 Wave 3): a self-service change to a SENSITIVE profile field (name / national_id / bank_account / tax_id via POST /api/hcm/ess/profile-requests) is parked `pending` in ess_profile_change_requests and the payroll.employees master is written ONLY when a DIFFERENT hr/hr_admin user approves (POST .../profile-requests/:id/approve) — approved_by MUST differ from requested_by (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, 403) so an employee CANNOT self-approve their own sensitive change; reject leaves the master unchanged. Applying an approved (or auto-applied) request audit-logs the before/after on doc_status_log (docType ESSPROFILE) in addition to the append-only audit_log. A LOW-RISK field (phone / address / emergency_contact) auto-applies at request time (still recorded). OWN-SCOPE is enforced server-side: an `ess` caller may only see/modify their OWN requests + personal documents (emp_code resolved from the JWT, never a body param) — a second employee gets an empty/404 result, and hr-visibility documents are hidden from the employee. Object-storage document keys are isSafeObjectKey-validated (no traversal / host redirect). Tenant-scoped (RLS) so profiles/documents never leak across companies; reads gate ess/hr/hr_admin/exec, approvals hr/hr_admin. So no employee can silently repoint their own pay or amend a statutory identifier, and no one can read or change a colleague's record.
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-ess.service.ts createRequest (sensitive→pending vs low-risk auto-apply; own-scope me())/approveRequest (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL + employee-master write on approval + ESSPROFILE audit)/rejectRequest/listRequests (own-scope)/uploadDocument (isSafeObjectKey)/listDocuments (own-scope + hr-visibility hiding)/teamDirectory; hcm/hcm-ess.controller.ts /api/hcm/ess/{profile-requests[/:id/approve|reject],documents,team}; schema/hcm-ess.ts ess_profile_change_requests/employee_documents + payroll.employees ESS columns (migration 0331, RLS + tenant-leading indexes); permissions.ts ess/hr/hr_admin; /hcm/ess UI; cutover/hcm-ess.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the sensitive-field pending gate, the approved_by != requested_by self-approval guard, the employee-master write only on approval, the ESSPROFILE before/after audit row, own-scope emp_code resolution from the JWT, and the isSafeObjectKey validation.
controls.toe: Re-perform: an ess user requests a sensitive change (bank_account) -> pending, employee master untouched; a low-risk change (phone) auto-applies; the requester approving their own -> 403 SOD_SELF_APPROVAL; an ess user cannot reach the approve endpoint (403); a DISTINCT hr_admin approves -> employees.bank_account written + ESSPROFILE audit; a reject leaves the master unchanged; a second employee sees neither the request nor the document (own-scope); an unsafe object key -> 400 BAD_OBJECT_KEY; a cross-tenant read returns nothing (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-ess harness.
controls.evidence: ess_profile_change_requests approval log (SoD + before/after ESSPROFILE audit) + employee_documents register + own-scope isolation test

controls.risk: A terminated employee's access is not removed on departure — offboarding is an ad-hoc checklist with no enforced completion, so the person who runs the exit can close it out while email/SSO/VPN/production access is still live (dormant credentials, insider/ex-employee data exfiltration), and there is no independent register of which terminations still carry unrevoked access.
controls.description: Onboarding/offboarding lifecycle with access-revocation completeness (HR-5, docs/42): per-tenant checklist TEMPLATES (kind onboarding|offboarding) carry ordered tasks by category (it_access|payroll|equipment|docs|training); a template task can be flagged is_access_revocation. Starting a template for an employee (POST /api/hcm/lifecycle/start) INSTANTIATES a per-employee lifecycle with a copy of the tasks (pending). The HR-05 control lives at completion: an OFFBOARDING lifecycle CANNOT be marked complete (POST .../lifecycle/:id/complete) while any is_access_revocation task is still pending — ACCESS_REVOCATION_INCOMPLETE (400) — so the exit stays open until every access-removal step is done or explicitly waived. Skipping an access-revocation task is privileged: it requires hr_admin/exec (SKIP_REQUIRES_HR_ADMIN for a plain hr maintainer) AND a mandatory reason (SKIP_REASON_REQUIRED), and is AUDIT-LOGGED (a doc_status_log EMPLIFECYCLE row carrying ACCESS_REVOCATION_SKIP, the task and the reason) on top of the append-only audit_log. A detective read (GET .../lifecycle/offboarding-exceptions?days=N) lists every open offboarding whose access-revocation tasks are still pending past N days (oldest first) so stale terminations with live access surface for follow-up. Reads gate hr/hr_admin/exec; writes hr/hr_admin; tables are tenant-scoped (RLS) so lifecycles never leak across companies. So a leaver's access can only be signed off as removed — or waived by a named authoriser with a reason on the record.
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-lifecycle.service.ts start (task instantiation)/patchTask (skip authorisation: SKIP_REQUIRES_HR_ADMIN + SKIP_REASON_REQUIRED, ACCESS_REVOCATION_SKIP audit)/complete (ACCESS_REVOCATION_INCOMPLETE gate)/offboardingExceptions (detective read); hcm/hcm-lifecycle.controller.ts (/api/hcm/lifecycle/{templates,templates/:id/tasks,start,tasks/:id,:id/complete,offboarding-exceptions}); schema/hcm-lifecycle.ts onboarding_templates/onboarding_template_tasks/employee_lifecycle/employee_lifecycle_tasks (migration 0324, RLS + tenant-leading indexes); /hcm/onboarding UI; cutover/hcm-onboarding.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the access-revocation-pending count vs the completion gate, the hr_admin/exec + reason skip authorisation with its ACCESS_REVOCATION_SKIP audit row, and the offboarding-exceptions detective query.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create an onboarding + an offboarding template (one task flagged is_access_revocation), start onboarding for an employee (tasks instantiated), mark them done and complete; start offboarding, complete with an access-revocation task pending -> 400 ACCESS_REVOCATION_INCOMPLETE; an hr-only user skip -> 403 SKIP_REQUIRES_HR_ADMIN; hr_admin skip without a reason -> 400 SKIP_REASON_REQUIRED; hr_admin skip with a reason (audit-logged) or mark done -> completion succeeds; the offboarding-exceptions read lists the open offboarding with unrevoked access; a cross-tenant read returns nothing (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-onboarding harness (27 checks).
controls.evidence: Offboarding checklist with the access-revocation completion gate + the ACCESS_REVOCATION_SKIP audit trail + the offboarding-exceptions exception register

controls.fsli: Human Resources (headcount / labour cost); Payroll expense
controls.risk: A hire is made with no independent authorisation of the establishment or the offer — a hiring manager raises a job requisition AND approves it (self-authorised headcount), or extends and self-approves an employment offer, so people are hired (and payroll cost committed) beyond the approved plan or at un-reviewed terms; recruiting also hires more heads than the requisition authorised (over-establishment) or converts a candidate to an employee with no approved offer at all.
controls.description: Recruiting/ATS maker-checker (docs/42 HR-4): the requisition -> candidate pipeline -> offer -> hire flow enforces control HR-04 at three points. (1) A job_requisition is raised `pending` (requested_by stamped) and must be APPROVED by a DIFFERENT user (approve gate hr_admin/exec) — self-approval is REJECTED (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL); an application cannot advance to the offer/hired stages, nor an offer be created, until its requisition is `approved` (REQUISITION_NOT_APPROVED). (2) An employment offer is created `pending` (created_by stamped) and must be authorised by a DIFFERENT user (hr_admin/exec) before it can convert — self-approval is REJECTED (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) and an unapproved offer cannot convert (OFFER_NOT_APPROVED). (3) Converting an accepted+approved offer creates the payroll.employees row from the candidate (the hire) and is HEADCOUNT-BOUND: once the count of hired applications reaches the requisition headcount, a further hire is BLOCKED (HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED) and the requisition flips to `filled`. Reads gate hr/hr_admin/exec, writes hr/hr_admin, approvals hr_admin/exec; the four tables are tenant-scoped (RLS) so requisitions/candidates/offers never leak across companies. So no one can both open a requisition and staff it, no candidate becomes an employee without an authorised offer, and the establishment cannot be exceeded from the inside.
controls.frequency: Per requisition / per offer / per hire
controls.owner: HR / People Ops; Hiring Manager; Exec
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-recruiting.service.ts createRequisition/approveRequisition (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL) + createApplication/advanceStage (REQUISITION_NOT_APPROVED, HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED) + createOffer/approveOffer (SOD_SELF_APPROVAL)/convertOffer (OFFER_NOT_APPROVED, HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED, payroll.employees hire); hcm-recruiting.controller.ts /api/hcm/recruiting/{requisitions[/:reqNo/approve|reject],candidates,applications[/:id/stage],offers[/:id/approve|convert]}; schema/hcm-recruiting.ts job_requisitions/candidates/applications/offers (migration 0323, RLS + tenant-leading indexes); StatusLogService JOBREQ/OFFER audit; permissions.ts hr/hr_admin; /hcm/recruiting UI; cutover/hcm-recruiting.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the requisition approve maker-checker (approver != requested_by), the requisition-approved gate on the offer/hire stages, the offer authorization (approver != created_by) before convert, and the headcount-bound hire that mints the employee.
controls.toe: Re-perform: raise a requisition (headcount 1), self-approve -> SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, approve as a different user; add a candidate + application, advance applied->interview; advance to offer against an unapproved requisition -> REQUISITION_NOT_APPROVED; create an offer, convert before approval -> OFFER_NOT_APPROVED, self-approve the offer -> SOD_SELF_APPROVAL, approve as a different user, convert -> a payroll.employees row is created; a second hire against the headcount-1 requisition -> HEADCOUNT_EXCEEDED; a cross-tenant read returns nothing (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-recruiting harness (20 checks).
controls.evidence: Requisition + offer approval log (JOBREQ/OFFER) + the recruiting register (requisitions/candidates/applications/offers); SoD + headcount test

controls.risk: Workforce cost, headcount and leave liability drift with no periodic, independent review — establishment creep (heads outside the approved plan), attrition running above tolerance, salaries paid outside the sanctioned pay-grade bands (over-market/favouritism) and an understated accrued-leave liability all go unnoticed because there is no scheduled, tenant-scoped analytical review of the HR base that management (and audit) can rely on to detect these exceptions.
controls.description: Workforce-analytics review (HR-9, docs/42 Wave 3): a set of five read-only, schedulable, tenant-scoped BI report types on the HCM spine gives management a recurring analytical-review control over the HR base. hr_headcount_trend (active headcount by department/position from the CURRENT org assignments + a hire-cohort trend), hr_turnover (attrition % over a window — separations = completed HR-5 OFFBOARDING lifecycles / average headcount), hr_tenure_distribution (tenure buckets from hire date), hr_comp_ratio (each active employee's salary vs the HR-6 pay_grades [min,mid,max] band — flags employees paid OUT-OF-BAND above max / below min) and hr_leave_liability (accrued-but-untaken leave days = entitled+accrued+carryover-used-expired, valued at salary / working-days) surface the exceptions for review. Each report is an idempotent read-aggregation (no writes), runs through the existing BI subscription scheduler (create a daily/weekly/monthly subscription -> each run persists to report_runs, deliverable by email/LINE/in-app), and is tenant-scoped (explicit tenant filter + RLS) so one company's workforce metrics never leak to another. Reads gate the BI exec surface (exec). So headcount, turnover, pay-band compliance and leave liability are reviewed on a cadence rather than only on an ad-hoc pull.
controls.code_reference: modules/bi/report-registry.ts (hr_headcount_trend/hr_turnover/hr_tenure_distribution/hr_comp_ratio/hr_leave_liability report types); modules/bi/bi-generate.service.ts generateReport branches (read-only aggregations over payroll.employees + hr_assignments/hr_positions/hr_departments + employee_lifecycle + pay_grades + leave_balances); modules/bi/bi.controller.ts /api/bi/report-types + subscriptions (exec); /scheduled-reports UI (data-driven from the report-type catalog); cutover/hcm-analytics.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect that each report type is an idempotent read (no writes), is tenant-scoped (a T2 run returns only T2's workforce), and that the comp-ratio out-of-band flag and the leave-liability valuation reconcile to the pay_grades bands and leave_balances.
controls.toe: Re-perform: seed employees / org assignments / pay grades / an offboarding lifecycle / a leave balance; run each report type via a BI subscription; confirm headcount by department, the turnover %, a tenure bucket, the comp-ratio out-of-band flag (salary above band max) and the accrued-leave-liability sum; a second tenant's run sees only its own rows (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-analytics harness (23 checks).
controls.evidence: The persisted report_runs payloads for the five workforce-analytics report types (headcount/turnover/tenure/comp-ratio/leave-liability) + the scheduled-report subscriptions

controls.risk: Mandatory or safety-critical training lapses unnoticed — an employee's required certification (safety induction, first-aid, compliance) expires with no recert and no independent register flags it, so people work without the certification the business or regulator requires (safety / regulatory exposure), and training completions are recorded inconsistently (a 'completed' course with no evidence of the required assessment) so the competency record cannot be relied on.
controls.description: Training & certification compliance (docs/42 HR-7): the course catalogue -> session -> enrollment -> completion flow enforces control HR-07 at three points. (1) PREVENTIVE — an enrollment on a course flagged `requires_score` cannot be marked `completed` without a score (SCORE_REQUIRED); completing an absent/foreign enrollment is REJECTED (ENROLLMENT_NOT_FOUND, 404). (2) AUTOMATED — completing an enrollment for a course that carries a recert cadence (`validity_months`) or is mandatory MINTS/renews a certifications row with expiry_date = completed_date + validity_months (a mandatory course with no cadence mints a non-expiring credential); a renewal supersedes the prior active credential so only the freshest is evaluated, and the mint is audit-logged (StatusLogService TRAINCERT). (3) DETECTIVE — GET /api/hcm/training/compliance?days=N returns every employee whose MANDATORY-course certification is expired or expiring within N days (default 30), the periodic recurring-training evidence. Reads gate hr/hr_admin/exec (an employee's own training/certifications also ess, own-scoped); writes hr/hr_admin; the four tables are tenant-scoped (RLS) so courses/sessions/enrollments/certifications never leak across companies. So a mandatory certification cannot silently lapse and a course cannot be booked complete without its assessment.
controls.owner: HR / People Ops; HR Admin / Exec
controls.code_reference: hcm/hcm-training.service.ts createCourse/createSession/enroll + complete (SCORE_REQUIRED gate, ENROLLMENT_NOT_FOUND, certification mint expiry=completed_date+validity_months, supersede prior active) + listCertifications/compliance (expired/expiring detective, ess own-scope); hcm-training.controller.ts /api/hcm/training/{courses,sessions,enrollments[/:id/complete],certifications,compliance}; schema/hcm-training.ts training_courses/training_sessions/training_enrollments/certifications (migration 0326, RLS + tenant-leading indexes); StatusLogService TRAINCERT audit; permissions.ts hr/hr_admin/ess; /hcm/training UI; cutover/hcm-training.ts (ToE)
controls.tod: Inspect the SCORE_REQUIRED completion gate on a requires-score course, the certification mint on completion (expiry = completed_date + validity_months) and the supersede of the prior active credential, and the compliance read that lists expired/expiring mandatory certifications.
controls.toe: Re-perform: create a mandatory recert course + a requires-score course; enroll an employee; complete the recert course -> a certification is minted with expiry = completed_date + validity_months; complete the requires-score course with no score -> SCORE_REQUIRED, then with a score -> completes; complete an absent enrollment -> 404 ENROLLMENT_NOT_FOUND; seed an expired + a soon-expiring mandatory cert and call the compliance read -> both surface within the window (a 5-day window drops the ~30-day-out one, keeps the expired one); a non-expiring mandatory cert is never flagged; an ess user sees only their own certifications; a cross-tenant read returns nothing (RLS) - re-performed by the hcm-training harness (27 checks).
controls.evidence: Training register (courses/sessions/enrollments) + certification register with expiry + the TRAINCERT mint log + the expired/expiring compliance report
